column8: deTagsüber bleibt Zubat an einem dunklen Ort und bewegt sich kaum. Wenn es dem Sonnenlicht längere Zeit ausgesetzt wird, zieht es sich Verbrennungen an seinem Körper zu.
column9: enIt forms colonies in perpetually dark  places and uses ultrasonic waves to   identify and approach targets.
column10: enWhile living in pitch-black caverns, their eyes gradually grew shut and deprived them of vision. They use ultrasonic waves to detect obstacles.
column11: enIt has no eyes. Instead, it relies on its ultrasonic cries for echo location to flit about in darkness.
column12: deEs hat keine Augen. Zur Orientierung nutzt es seine Ultraschallwellen-Schreie, um in der Dunkelheit zu fliegen.
column13: enIt has no eyes. Instead, it relies on its ultrasonic cries for echolocation to flit about in darkness.
column14: enIt has no eyeballs, so it can’t see. It checks its surroundings via the ultrasonic waves it emits from its mouth.
column15: enDisliking sunlight, it sleeps deep in forests and caves until sundown.
column16: enWhen exposed to sunlight, they suffer burns. The frequency of their ultrasonic waves can differ slightly from colony to colony.
column17: enZUBAT avoids sunlight because exposure causes it to become unhealthy. During the daytime, it stays in caves or under the eaves of old houses, sleeping while hanging upside down.
column18: deÜber den Mund stößt es Ultraschallwellen aus, um seine Umgebung zu erkunden. So kann es selbst in engen Höhlen geschickt umherfliegen.
column19: deZubat stößt während des Fluges Ultraschalllaute aus, um Hindernisse zu orten.
column20: enEmits ultrasonic cries while it flies. They act as a sonar used to check for objects in its way.
column21: enZubat live in caves, down where the sun’s light won’t reach. In the morning, they gather together to keep each other warm as they sleep.
column22: enForms colonies in perpetually dark places. Uses ultrasonic waves to identify and approach targets.
column23: enMakes its home in gloomy caves. Atrophied eyes have left this Pokémon blind, so it scans its surroundings via sound waves that it emits from its mouth as it flies.
column24: enTheir skin is so thin that they’ll be burned if sunlight hits them. When it gets cold out, they gather together to warm one another’s bodies.
column25: enZubat remains quietly unmoving in a dark spot during the bright daylight hours. It does so because prolonged exposure to the sun causes its body to become slightly burned.
column26: deSeine dünne Haut erleidet bei Sonnenlicht Verbrennungen. Bei Kälte schmiegt es sich an seine Artgenossen, um sich zu wärmen.
column27: deZubat meidet Sonnenlicht, da es dadurch krank würde. Am Tage hält es sich in Höhlen oder unter den Dachrinnen alter Häuser auf. Dort schläft es mit dem Kopf nach unten.
column28: enIt checks its surroundings and location using reflections of the ultrasonic waves from its mouth.
column29: enIt does not need eyes, because it emits ultrasonic waves to check its surroundings while it flies.
column30: deDa es keine Augäpfel hat, ist es blind. Mithilfe von Ultraschallwellen, die es aus dem Mund aussendet, erkundet es seine Umgebung.
column31: deZubat leben tief in Höhlen, wo das Sonnenlicht sie nicht erreicht. Bei Tagesanbruch rücken sie zusammen, um sich im Schlaf zu wärmen.
column32: deTagsüber schläft es in Höhlen. Da es keine Augen hat, erschließt es sich seine Umgebung im Flug durch das Aussenden von Schallwellen.
column33: enIt sleeps in caves during the day. It has no eyes, so to check its surroundings while flying, it emits ultrasonic waves.
column34: enZubat avoids sunlight because exposure causes it to become unhealthy. During the daytime, it stays in caves or under the eaves of old houses, sleeping while hanging upside down.
column35: enEven though it has no eyes, it can sense obstacles using ultrasonic waves it emits from its mouth.
column36: deSonnenlicht verursacht bei ihm Verbrennungen. Die Frequenz seiner Ultraschallwellen variiert von Schwarm zu Schwarm.
column37: enCapable of flying safely in dark places, it emits ultrasonic cries to check for any obstacles.
column38: deObwohl es keine Augen hat, kann es Hindernisse mithilfe von Ultraschallwellen wahrnehmen.
column39: enWhile flying, it constantly emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings.
column40: enZUBAT remains quietly unmoving in a dark spot during the bright daylight hours. It does so because prolonged exposure to the sun causes its body to become slightly burned.
column41: enIt emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.
column42: enDuring the day, it gathers with others and hangs from the ceilings of old buildings and caves.

column8: deDieses Pokémon saugt dem Gegner selbst dann noch Energie ab, wenn es zu schwer zum Fliegen wird.
column9: deGolbat beißt seine Beute mit seinen vier Reißzähnen und trinkt das Blut seiner Opfer. Es wird in Nächten aktiv, in denen nicht einmal der Mond scheint. Es fliegt dann durch die Dunkelheit und greift Menschen und Pokémon an.
column10: enIts feet are tiny, but this Pokémon walks skillfully. It sneaks up on sleeping prey before sinking in its fangs and slurping up blood.
column11: enIt loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.
column12: enHowever hard its victim’s hide may be, it punctures with sharp fangs and gorges itself with blood.
column13: enWhen it plunges its fangs into its prey, it instantly draws and gulps down more than ten ounces of blood.
column14: deGolbat nähert sich schnell und lautlos an. Mit seinen spitzen Zähnen beißt es zu und saugt seinen Opfern Blut ab.
column15: enThey can suck down over 10 ounces of blood in one go. They have been known to drink so much blood that they can no longer fly.
column16: enIt attacks in a stealthy manner, without warning. Its sharp fangs are used to bite and to suck blood.
column17: enOnce it starts sucking blood, it does not stop until it is full. It flies at night in search of prey.
column18: deHat es einmal angefangen Blut zu saugen, hört es erst wieder auf, wenn es satt ist. Es jagt nachts.
column19: deIn einem Schluck kann es bis zu 300 ml Blut absaugen. Manchmal trinkt es so viel, dass es nicht mehr fliegen kann.
column20: deGolbat kann trotz seiner kleinen Beine geschickt laufen. Es schleicht sich an schlafende Beute an, stößt seine Zähne in sie und schlürft ihr Blut.
column21: enIt attacks in a stealthy manner, without warning. Its sharp fangs are used to bite and suck blood.
column22: enIts fangs easily puncture even thick animal hide. It loves to feast on the blood of people and POKéMON. It flits about in darkness and strikes from behind.
column23: enOnce it strikes, it will not stop draining energy from the victim even if it gets too heavy to fly.
column24: enGOLBAT bites down on prey with its four fangs and drinks the victim’s blood. It becomes active on inky dark moonless nights, flying around to attack people and POKéMON.
column25: deSeine dicken Reißzähne sind im Inneren hohl wie Strohhalme und zerbrechlich. Sie sind das Produkt seiner Entwicklung zum Blutsauger.
column26: enIts thick fangs are hollow like straws, making them unexpectedly fragile. These fangs are specialized for sucking blood.
column27: enOnce it bites, it will not stop draining energy from the victim even if it gets too heavy to fly.
column28: deUm seinen Hunger zu stillen, versucht es mitunter auch, in Stahl-Pokémon zu beißen, und bricht sich dabei die Reißzähne ab.
column29: deGolbat hat es auf das Blut von Lebewesen abgesehen. Im Schutze der Nacht wird es besonders aktiv. Dieses Pokémon fliegt durch die Nacht, um nach frischem Blut zu suchen.
column30: enGolbat loves to drink the blood of living things. It is particularly active in the pitch black of night. This Pokémon flits around in the night skies, seeking fresh blood.
column31: enIts sharp fangs puncture the toughest of hides and have small holes for greedily sucking blood.
column32: enIt can drink more than 10 ounces of blood at once. If it has too much, it gets heavy and flies clumsily.
column33: enGolbat bites down on prey with its four fangs and drinks the victim’s blood. It becomes active on inky dark moonless nights, flying around to attack people and Pokémon.
column34: deDas Blut anderer Lebewesen ist seine Leibspeise. Man sagt, dass es das abgesaugte Blut manchmal mit hungrigen Artgenossen teilt.
column35: enSometimes they drink so much blood, they can’t fly anymore. Then they fall to the ground and become food for other Pokémon.
column36: enIt loves the blood of humans and Pokémon. It flies around at night in search of neck veins.
column37: enIt clamps down on its prey with needle-sharp fangs and drains over 10 ounces of blood in one gulp.
column38: deManchmal saugt es so viel Blut, dass es hilflos zu Boden fällt und leichte Beute für andere Pokémon wird.
column39: enHowever hard its victim's hide may be, it punctures with sharp fangs and gorges itself with blood.
column40: enFlitting around in the dead of night, it sinks its fangs into its prey and drains a nearly fatal amount of blood.
column41: enGOLBAT loves to drink the blood of living things. It is particularly active in the pitch black of night. This POKéMON flits around in the night skies, seeking fresh blood.
column42: enIt sinks its sharp fangs into other creatures and slurps up their blood. A closer look at the fangs reveals that they are hollow and akin to straws.
column43: enEvery once in a while, you’ll see a Golbat that’s missing some fangs. This happens when hunger drives it to try biting a Steel-type Pokémon.

column8: deEs wird oft mit Unkraut verwechselt. Versucht man, es aus der Erde zu ziehen, stößt es einen fürchterlichen Schrei aus.
column9: deMyrapla sucht nach fruchtbarem, nahrhaftem Boden und pflanzt sich selbst darin ein. Solange es eingepflanzt ist, nehmen seine Füße tagsüber die Gestalt von Baumwurzeln an.
column10: enIf exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.
column11: enODDISH searches for fertile, nutrient-rich soil, then plants itself. During the daytime, while it is planted, this POKéMON’s feet are thought to change shape and become similar to the roots of trees.
column12: enIt may be mistaken for a clump of weeds. If you try to yank it out of the ground, it shrieks horribly.
column13: enIts scientific name is “Oddium Wanderus.” At night, it is said to walk nearly 1,000 feet on its two roots.
column14: deIm warmen Klima Alolas gedeiht Myrapla besonders gut. Hier lebende Exemplare sind rund und schwer.
column15: deWenn sich der Mond zeigt, taucht es aus dem Boden auf. Über fruchtbarer Erde schüttelt es seinen Kopf und verstreut Samen.
column16: deSein wissenschaftlicher Name lautet: „Oddium Wanderus“. Nachts soll es knapp 300 m auf seinen zwei Wurzeln zurücklegen.
column17: enIt often plants its root feet in the ground during the day and sows seeds as it walks about at night.
column18: enThis POKéMON grows by absorbing moonlight. During the daytime, it buries itself in the ground, leaving only its leaves exposed to avoid detection by its enemies.
column19: enAwakened by moonlight, it roams  actively at night. In the day, it stays quietly underground.
column20: enAwakened by moonlight, it roams actively at night. In the day, it stays quietly underground.
column21: enDuring the day, it stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.
column22: deTagsüber versteckt es sich in der kalten Erde, um die Sonne zu meiden. Es wächst im Mondschein.
column23: enDuring the daytime, Oddish buries itself in soil to absorb nutrients from the ground using its entire body. The more fertile the soil, the glossier its leaves become.
column24: enOddish searches for fertile, nutrient-rich soil, then plants itself. During the daytime, while it is planted, this Pokémon’s feet are thought to change shape and become similar to the roots of trees.
column25: deWird es vom Mondschein getroffen, bewegt es sich. Nachts wandert es weite Wege, um seine Samen zu verstreuen.
column26: deAm Tage vergräbt sich Myrapla im Boden, um mit seinem ganzen Körper Nahrung aufzunehmen. Je fruchtbarer der Boden ist, desto glänzender werden seine Blätter.
column27: enDuring the daytime, ODDISH buries itself in soil to absorb nutrients from  the ground using its entire body. The more fertile the soil, the glossier its leaves become.
column28: enDuring the day, it keeps its face buried in the ground. At night, it wanders around sowing its seeds.

column8: deDieses Pokémon sondert einen übelriechenden Geruch ab. Trotzdem halten einige Leute es im Haus.
column9: enSmells incredibly foul! However, around one out of a thousand people enjoy sniffing its nose-bending stink.
column10: enThe smell from its drool-like syrup and the pollen on its petals is so bad, it may make opponents faint.
column11: enWhat appears to be drool is actually sweet honey. It is very sticky and clings stubbornly if touched.
column12: enThe fluid that oozes from its mouth isn’t drool. It is a nectar that is used to attract prey.
column13: deAus Duflors Mund tropft übelst riechender Honig. Diesen Gestank scheint es zu lieben, denn es schnieft die giftigen Dämpfe ein und sabbert danach noch mehr Honig.
column14: deDer Honig, der er aus seinem Mund tropft, riecht zwar übel, lässt sich aber durch Trocknen und Erwärmen zu wirkungsvoller Medizin verarbeiten.
column15: enThe fluid that oozes from its mouth isn't drool. It is a nectar that is used to attract prey.
column16: deDuflor produziert einen ekelerregenden Gestank aus dem Stempel seiner Blume. Wenn es in Gefahr gerät, wird der Gestank noch unerträglicher. Wenn sich dieses Pokémon in Sicherheit befindet, stößt es keinen Gestank aus.
column17: enThe honey it drools from its mouth smells so atrocious, it can curl noses more than a mile away.
column18: deSein Stempel sondert einen unglaublich faulen Gestank ab. Dieser stechende Geruch kann selbst bis zu 2 km entfernte Gegner bewusstlos machen.
column19: enSmells incredibly foul! However, around 1 out of 1,000 people enjoy sniffing its nose-bending stink.
column20: enIt secretes a sticky, drool-like honey. Although sweet, it smells too repulsive to get very close.
column21: enGLOOM releases a foul fragrance from the pistil of its flower. When faced with danger, the stench worsens. If this POKéMON is feeling calm and secure, it does not release its usual stinky aroma.
column22: enIts pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.
column23: deWas wie Speichel aussieht, ist eigentlich Honig. Er ist sehr klebrig und wenn man ihn berührt, bekommt man ihn nicht mehr ab.
column24: enFrom its mouth Gloom drips honey that smells absolutely horrible. Apparently, it loves the horrid stench. It sniffs the noxious fumes and then drools even more of its honey.
column25: enFrom its mouth GLOOM drips honey that smells absolutely horrible. Apparently, it loves the horrid stench. It sniffs the noxious fumes and then  drools even more of its honey.
column26: enGloom releases a foul fragrance from the pistil of its flower. When faced with danger, the stench worsens. If this Pokémon is feeling calm and secure, it does not release its usual stinky aroma.
column27: enA horribly noxious honey drools from its mouth. One whiff of the honey can result in memory loss. Some fans are said to enjoy this overwhelming stink, however.
column28: deDer Honig, den es abgibt, riecht so entsetzlich, dass sich sogar Nasen in 2 km Entfernung rümpfen.
column29: deEs riecht unglaublich widerlich. Trotzdem gibt es unter tausend Menschen mindestens einen, dem der Gestank gefällt.
column30: deAus seinem Mund tropft übelriechender Honig. Bekommt man etwas davon ab, haftet sein Gestank selbst nach 10 Waschgängen noch an.

column8: deGiflor hat die größten Blätter der Welt. Sie ziehen Beute an und bestäuben sie mit giftigen Sporen. Wenn die Beute sich nicht mehr rührt, fängt dieses Pokémon sie und frisst sie auf.
column9: enFlaps its broad flower petals to scatter its poisonous pollen. The flapping sound is very loud.
column10: enIts petals are the largest in the world. As it walks, it scatters extremely allergenic pollen.
column11: enIts petals are the largest in the world. It fiendishly scatters allergy-causing pollen from its petals.
column12: enThe bud bursts into bloom with a bang. It then starts scattering allergenic, poisonous pollen.
column13: enBy shaking its big petals, it scatters toxic pollen into the air, turning the air yellow.
column14: enThe larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.
column15: enVileplume has the world’s largest petals. They are used to attract prey that are then doused with toxic spores. Once the prey are immobilized, this Pokémon catches and devours them.
column16: deWenn es seine großen Blütenblätter schüttelt, um giftige Pollen zu verteilen, entsteht dabei ein unglaublich großer Lärm.
column17: deGiflors giftige Pollen rufen böse allergische Anfälle hervor. Deshalb ist es ratsam, sich keiner schönen Blume im Dschungel zu nähern, so anziehend sie auch sein mag.
column18: enVileplume’s toxic pollen triggers atrocious allergy attacks. That’s why it is advisable never to approach any attractive flowers in a jungle, however pretty they may be.
column19: enIn seasons when it produces more pollen, the air around a VILEPLUME turns yellow with the powder as it walks. The pollen is highly toxic and causes paralysis.
column20: enIt has the world's largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.
column21: deEs besitzt die größten Blätter der Welt. Bei jedem Schritt streut es Giftpollen zu Boden.
column22: deJe größer die Blütenblätter, desto mehr giftige Pollen sind in der Blüte enthalten.
column23: enVILEPLUME has the world’s largest petals. They are used to attract prey that are then doused with toxic spores. Once the prey are immobilized, this POKéMON catches and devours them.
column24: enIt has the world’s largest petals. With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.
column25: deGiflors Pollen sind hochgiftig. Auf so manch eigentümliches Käfer-Pokémon scheinen sie aber eine anziehende Wirkung zu haben.
column26: deEs verstreut hochgiftige Pollen. Man erkennt Giflors Spur am leicht gelblichen Nebel, den es hinterlässt.
column27: deEs besitzt die größten Blütenblätter der Welt. Bei jedem Schritt streut es große Mengen an Giftpollen zu Boden.
column28: enVILEPLUME’s toxic pollen triggers atrocious allergy attacks. That’s why it is advisable never to approach any attractive flowers in a jungle, however pretty they may be.
column29: deJe größer die Blütenblätter, desto mehr giftige Pollen enthält die Blüte. Aber es ist auch umso erschöpfter, da sein Kopf so schwer wird.

column8: enMushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host PARAS.
column9: enBurrows under the ground to gnaw on tree roots. The mushrooms on its back absorb most of the nutrition.
column10: deAuf Paras’ Rücken wachsen parasitäre Pilze, die Tochukaso genannt werden. Sie wachsen, indem sie dem Käfer-Pokémon Nährstoffe entziehen. Sie sind sehr wertvoll als lebensverlängernde Medizin.
column11: enWhether it’s due to a lack of moisture or a lack of nutrients, in Alola the mushrooms on Paras don’t grow up quite right.
column12: enPARAS has parasitic mushrooms growing on its back called tochukaso. They grow large by drawing nutrients from the BUG POKéMON host. They are highly valued as a medicine for extending life.
column13: deEs gräbt sich in den Boden, um Baumwurzeln Nährstoffe zu entziehen. Den Großteil davon absorbieren jedoch die Pilze auf seinem Rücken.
column14: enA PARAS has parasitic tochukaso mushrooms growing on its back. They grow by drawing nutrients from the host. They are valued as a medicine for long life.
column15: enBurrows to suck tree roots. The mushrooms on its back grow by drawing nutrients from the bug host.
column16: enMushrooms called tochukaso sprout from its back. They can be dried and powdered to make a medicine used to extend life.
column17: enSometimes seen at the foot of trees in humid forests. The mushrooms on its back—called tochukaso—are not present on infant specimens and instead emerge as Paras matures.
column18: deDieses Pokémon gräbt sich in den Boden und frisst Wurzeln. Die Pilze auf seinem Rücken sind Parasiten.
column19: deDie Pilze auf seinem Rücken heißen Tochukaso. Sie lassen sich als lebensverlängernde Medizin verwerten, wenn man sie trocknet und zermahlt.
column20: enAs its body grows, large mushrooms named tochukaso start sprouting out of its back.
column21: enMushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host Paras.
column22: enThe mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.
column23: enNo matter how much it eats, the mushrooms growing on its back steal away most of the nutrients it consumes.
column24: enIt is doused with mushroom spores when it is born. As its body grows, mushrooms sprout from its back.
column25: deAuf seinem Rücken wachsen Pilze, die man Tochukaso nennt. Die Pilze wachsen mit ihrem Wirt.
column26: enAs its body grows large, oriental mushrooms named tochukaso start sprouting out of its back.
column27: enPARAS has parasitic mushrooms growing on its back called tochukaso. They grow large by drawing nutrients from this BUG POKéMON host. They are highly valued as a medicine for extending life.
column28: enParas has parasitic mushrooms growing on its back called tochukaso. They grow large by drawing nutrients from this Bug Pokémon host. They are highly valued as a medicine for extending life.
column29: enThe tochukaso growing on this POKéMON's back orders it to extract juice from tree trunks.
column30: enGrowing out of the bug’s back are mushrooms called tochukaso. The mushrooms grow with the bug host.
column31: deEgal, wie viel Nahrung es auch zu sich nimmt, die Pilze, die auf seinem Rücken wachsen, entziehen ihm so gut wie alle Nährstoffe.
column32: deIn Alola sind Paras’ Pilze etwas unterentwickelt. Vielleicht mangelt es ihm hier an Feuchtigkeit oder Nährstoffen.
column33: deParas’ Körper kann nicht nach seinem eigenen Willen handeln, da er von Tochukaso, den Pilzen auf seinem Rücken, kontrolliert wird.

column8: enIt is controlled by a mushroom grown larger than the bug body. It is said to prefer damp places.
column9: enIt scatters toxic spores from the mushroom cap. In China, the spores are used as herbal medicine.
column10: enThe bug host is drained of energy by the mushrooms on its back. They appear to do all the thinking.
column11: enWhen nothing's left to extract from the bug, the mushrooms on its back leave spores on the bug's egg.
column12: enPARASECT are known to infest the roots of large trees en masse and drain nutrients. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.
column13: deSeine Giftsporen werden als natürliches Heilmittel verwendet, doch bei Parasek aus Alola ist die Qualität etwas minderwertig.
column14: deParasek wird von dem Pilz auf seinen Rücken kontrolliert. Es ist oft mit Lamellux in Revierstreitigkeiten verstrickt.
column15: enA host-parasite pair in which the parasite mushroom has taken over the host bug. Prefers damp places.
column16: enA mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls Parasect. It scatters poisonous spores.
column17: enIt stays mostly in dark, damp places, the preference not of the bug, but of the big mushroom on its back.
column18: enIts poisonous spores are also used in traditional medicine. Apparently, spores produced in Alola are not of very good quality.
column19: deJe größer der Pilz auf seinem Rücken wird, desto stärker werden auch die Sporen, die es verteilt.
column20: enIt stays mostly in dark, damp places, the preference not of the bug, but of the big mushrooms on its back.
column21: deDer Pilz auf seinem Rücken ist sein wichtigstes Körperteil. Der Rest ist quasi leblos und kann sich ohne den Pilz nicht bewegen.
column22: deParasek sucht große Bäume heim und entzieht Nährstoffe aus Stamm und Wurzeln. Wenn ein befallener Baum stirbt, sucht es sich zusammen mit seinen Artgenossen einen neuen Wirt.
column23: enA mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls PARASECT. It scatters poisonous spores.
column24: deParasek wird von einem Pilz, der größer als das Pokémon ist, kontrolliert. Er gibt Giftsporen ab.
column25: enThe large mushroom on its back controls it. It often fights over territory with Shiinotic.
column26: enThe bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.
column27: enThe larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mushroom spores it scatters.
column28: enParasect is known to infest large trees en masse and drain nutrients from the lower trunk and roots. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.
column29: enIt scatters toxic spores from its mushroom cap. Once harvested, these spores can be steeped and boiled down to prepare herbal medicines.
column30: enPARASECT is known to infest large trees en masse and drain nutrients from the lower trunk and roots. When an infested tree dies, they move onto another tree all at once.
column31: deDer Pilz auf seinem Rücken stößt Giftsporen aus. Kocht man ihn ab, lässt er sich als natürliches Heilmittel verwerten.
column32: deDer Pilz auf Paraseks Rücken nutzt das Pokémon als Wirt und entzieht ihm Energie. Es scheint, als habe er die Kontrolle über das Pokémon.
column33: enMushroom-lacking specimens of this Pokémon lie unmoving in the forest, lending credence to the hypothesis that the large mushroom is in control of Parasect's actions.
column34: enThe bug host is drained of energy by the mushroom on its back. The mushroom appears to do all the thinking.
column35: enA host-parasite pair in which the parasite mushroom has taken over the host bug. Prefers damp places.

column8: enIts big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At night, its kind is drawn by light.
column9: enIts eyes act as radar, enabling it to be active in darkness. The eyes can also shoot powerful beams.
column10: enLives in the shadows of tall trees where it eats bugs. It is attracted by light at night.
column11: enVenonat is said to have evolved with a coat of thin, stiff hair that covers its entire body for protection. It possesses large eyes that never fail to spot even minuscule prey.
column12: deDieses Pokémon lebt im Schatten großer Bäume. Es frisst Insekten und wird von Licht angezogen.
column13: enThe small bugs it eats appear only at night, so it sleeps in a hole in a tree until night falls.
column14: enVENONAT is said to have evolved with a coat of thin, stiff hair that covers its entire body for protection. It possesses large eyes that never fail to spot even miniscule prey.
column15: enIts large eyes act as radars. In a bright place, you can see that they are clusters of many tiny eyes.
column16: deSeine großen Augen fungieren als Radar. Im Licht kann man erkennen, dass sie aus unzähligen, sehr kleinen Augen bestehen.
column17: enPoison oozes from all over its body. It catches and eats small bugs at night that are attracted by light.
column18: deSeine großen Augen bestehen aus vielen kleinen Augen. Nachts wird es von Licht angezogen.
column19: enIts large eyes act as radar. In a bright place, you can see that they are clusters of many tiny eyes.
column20: enIts coat of thin, stiff hair that covers its entire body is said to have evolved for protection. Its large eyes never fail to spot even miniscule prey.
column21: enIts eyes also function as radar units. It catches and eats small bugs that hide in darkness.
column22: enLives in the shadows of tall trees where it eats insects. It is attracted by light at night.
column23: deBluzuk hat einen Pelz aus dünnen Borsten entwickelt, der dem Schutz seines Körpers dient. Es hat große Augen, denen auch winzig kleine Beute nicht entgeht.

column8: deWer die von ihm verstreuten Schuppen berührt, wird gelähmt und muss sich setzen.
column9: deSeine Flügel sind mit staubähnlichen Schuppen überzogen. Mit jedem Flügelschlag verliert es hochgiftigen Staub.
column10: enThe powdery scales on its wings are hard to remove. They also contain poison that leaks out on contact.
column11: deOmot ist ein nachtaktives Pokémon. Kleine Insekten, die vom Licht in der Dunkelheit angezogen Straßenlaternen umschwärmen, sind seine bevorzugte Beute.
column12: enVENOMOTH is nocturnal - it is a POKéMON that only becomes active at night. Its favorite prey are small insects that gather around streetlights, attracted by the light in the darkness.
column13: enThe wings are covered with dustlike scales. Every time it flaps its wings, it looses highly toxic dust.
column14: enThe powder on its wings is poisonous if it is dark in hue. If it is light in hue, it causes paralysis.
column15: enVENOMOTH are nocturnal--they only are active at night. Their favorite prey are insects that gather around streetlights, attracted by the light in the darkness.
column16: enThe scales it scatters will paralyze anyone who touches them, making that person unable to stand.
column17: enWhen it attacks, it flaps its large wings violently to scatter its poisonous powder all around.
column18: enThe dustlike scales covering its wings are color-coded to indicate the kinds of poison it has.
column19: enVenomoth is nocturnal—it is a Pokémon that only becomes active at night. Its favorite prey are small insects that gather around streetlights, attracted by the light in the darkness.
column20: enIt flutters its wings to scatter dustlike scales. The scales leach toxins if they contact skin.
column21: enThe powder on its wings is poisonous if it is dark in color. If it is light, it causes paralysis.
column22: enThe powdery scales on its wings are hard to remove from skin. They also contain poison that leaks out on contact.
column23: enThe dust-like scales covering its wings are color coded to indicate the kinds of poison it has.
column24: deBerührt man die staubartigen Schuppen auf seinen Flügeln, heften diese sich hartnäckig am Körper an und sondern darüber hinaus Gift ab.

column8: enIts skin is very thin. If it is exposed to light, its blood heats up, causing it to grow weak.
column9: enAround their crops, farmers plant the kind of tree that Diglett like to eat as a way of getting Diglett to plow the fields for them.
column10: deDieses Pokémon lebt 1 m unter der Erde. Es frisst Wurzeln und kommt selten an die Oberfläche.
column11: enIf a Diglett digs through a field, it leaves the soil perfectly tilled and ideal for planting crops.
column12: deEs knabbert im Erdreich die Wurzeln von Bäumen und Pflanzen an. Bei manchen Gemüsesorten kann dies zu Ernteschäden führen.
column13: enIf a DIGLETT DIGS through a field, it leaves the soil perfectly tilled and ideal for planting crops.
column14: enIt burrows through the ground at a shallow depth. It leaves raised earth in its wake, making it easy to spot.
column15: deSeine Haut ist sehr dünn. Wird es Sonnenlicht ausgesetzt, erhitzt sich sein Blut und es wird schwach.
column16: enIf a DIGLETT digs through a field, it leaves the soil perfectly tilled and ideal for planting crops.
column17: enLives about one yard underground where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears above ground.
column18: enIt digs underground and chews on tree roots, sticking its head out only when the sun isn't bright.
column19: enDIGLETT are raised in most farms. The reason is simple - wherever this POKéMON burrows, the soil is left perfectly tilled for planting crops. This soil is made ideal for growing delicious vegetables.
column20: enIt prefers dark places. It spends most of its time underground, though it may pop up in caves.
column21: deDigda bevorzugt dunkle Orte. Es hält sich meist unter der Erdoberfläche auf, kommt aber auch oft in Höhlen zum Vorschein.
column22: enIt travels through tunnels that it digs underground. It hates sunlight, so it comes out only after the sun goes down.
column23: enDIGLETT are raised in most farms. The reason is simple--wherever they burrow, the soil is left perfectly tilled for growing delicious crops.
column24: enMany farmers cherish and nurture Diglett because its droppings enrich the soil it lives in.
column25: deAuf Farmen werden Digdas Lieblingsbäume um die Feldfrüchte herum gepflanzt. Im Gegenzug durchpflügen sie dafür den Boden.
column26: enIt chews on plant roots underground. Farmers whose vegetables are vulnerable find it can be quite a pest.
column27: enDiglett are raised in most farms. The reason is simple— wherever this Pokémon burrows, the soil is left perfectly tilled for planting crops. This soil is made ideal for growing delicious vegetables.
column28: deEs gräbt sich in geringer Tiefe durch den Erdboden. Da es dabei durchwühlte Erde an der Oberfläche hinterlässt, ist es leicht zu finden.
column29: deBewegt sich ein Digda unterirdisch fort, pflügt es dabei den Erdboden gleich mit und bereitet so den Anbau von Feldfrüchten vor.
column30: deDigda wird zumeist auf Farmen gezüchtet. Denn wo auch immer dieses Pokémon zu graben beginnt, hinterlässt es Erde, die für das Anpflanzen von Feldfrüchten perfekt geeignet und für den Anbau köstlichen Gemüses ideal ist.
column31: enLives about one yard underground where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.
column32: enA Pokémon that lives underground. Because of its dark habitat, it is repelled by bright sunlight.
column33: deEs gräbt unterirdische Tunnel und bewegt sich darin fort. Da es Licht verabscheut, taucht es erst nachts wieder an der Oberfläche auf.
column34: deDigda wird gerne auf Farmen gezüchtet, da es das Erdreich, in dem es wohnt, gut durchpflügt und mit seinen Ausscheidungen düngt.

column8: deEs ist so stark, dass es sich mit Schaufler sogar durch steinharten Boden bis in 100 km Tiefe gräbt.
column9: enThese DIGLETT triplets dig over 60 miles below sea level. No one knows what it's like underground.
column10: enDUGTRIO are actually triplets that emerged from one body. As a result, each triplet thinks exactly like the other two triplets. They work cooperatively to burrow endlessly.
column11: deDigdri sind Drillinge und normalerweise ein Herz und eine Seele. Nur ganz selten streiten sie sich, welcher Kopf zuerst ans Futter darf.
column12: enA team of Diglett triplets. It triggers huge earthquakes by burrowing 60 miles underground.
column13: enExtremely powerful, they can DIG through even the hardest ground to a depth of over 60 miles.
column14: deMit der vereinten Kraft von drei Digda gräbt es sich bis zu 100 km tief in die Erde. Berichten zufolge löst dies mitunter Erdbeben aus.
column15: enA team of triplets that can burrow to a depth of 60 miles. It’s reported that this triggers an earthquake.
column16: enThis set of triplets moves cooperatively. It’s the power of teamwork that enables this Pokémon to dig to a depth of 60 miles.
column17: deDigdri sind Drillinge, die aus einem Körper entstanden sind. Daher denken alle Köpfe gleich. Sie arbeiten so gut zusammen, dass sie endlos graben können.
column18: enA team of DIGLETT triplets. It triggers huge earthquakes by burrowing 60 miles underground.
column19: enDespite the closeness between this Pokémon and farmers and other people, no one has ever seen the parts of it concealed underground.
column20: enBecause the triplets originally split from one body, they think exactly alike. They work cooperatively to burrow endlessly through the ground.
column21: enIts three heads bob separately up and down to loosen the soil nearby, making it easier for it to burrow.
column22: deTrotz seiner Beliebtheit bei Farmern und vielen anderen Menschen, hat noch niemand den unter der Erde versteckten Teil von Digdri gesehen.
column23: enIts three heads move alternately, driving it through tough soil to depths of over 60 miles.
column24: enThese Diglett triplets dig over 60 miles below sea level. No one knows what it’s like underground.
column25: deDie drei arbeiten und handeln stets zusammen. Durch Teamwork gelingt es dem Pokémon, sich bis zu 100 km tief ins Erdreich zu graben.
column26: deDiese kraftvollen Digda-Drillinge graben bis zu 100 km tief. Keiner weiß, wie sie unter der Erde aussehen.
column27: deIm Kampf gräbt es sich ein und attackiert den Gegner aus einer unvorhersehbaren Richtung.
column28: deDiese Digda-Drillinge graben sich manchmal bis zu 100 km tief in den Erdboden und lösen dadurch Erdbeben aus.
column29: enDugtrio’s heads are sleek and smooth and incredibly hard. It can dig through any soil with its headbutts.
column30: enWhile the three of them normally get along splendidly, on rare occasions a huge fight will break out over which head gets to eat first.
column31: enDugtrio are actually triplets that emerged from one body. As a result, each triplet thinks exactly like the other two triplets. They work cooperatively to burrow endlessly.
column32: enA team of triplets that can burrow over 60 MPH. Due to this, some people think it's an earthquake.
column33: deSeine Köpfe sind vollkommen glatt und steinhart. Mit Kopfnüssen gräbt es sich mühelos durch jeden Untergrund.
column34: enExtremely powerful, they can dig through even the hardest ground to a depth of over 60 miles.
column35: enIn battle, it digs through the ground and strikes the unsuspecting foe from an unexpected direction.
