column8: deEs treibt durch seichte Gewässer in der Nähe von Stränden. Durch seine Tentakel verursachte Verletzungen müssen sofort untersucht werden.
column9: deDieses Pokémon findet man in flachen Gewässern. Unachtsame Gegner empfängt es mit brennendem Gift.
column10: enTentacool’s body is largely composed of water. If it is removed from the sea, it dries up like parchment. If this Pokémon happens to become dehydrated, put it back into the sea.
column11: enIts eyes are as transparent as crystals. From them, it shoots mysterious beams of light.
column12: deTentachas Körper setzt sich zum größten Teil aus Wasser zusammen. Wenn es das Meer verlässt, vertrocknet es. Wenn dieses Pokémon auszutrocknen beginnt, sollte man es schnell wieder ins Wasser zurückbringen.
column13: enThey fire beams from the glassy, magenta orbs that resemble eyes atop their heads, and they drift in shallow seas. During low tide, they can sometimes be found on beaches, desiccated.
column14: enThis Pokémon is mostly made of water. A Tentacool out in the ocean is very hard to spot, because its body blends in with the sea.
column15: enDrifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by its stinging acid.
column16: enIts body is 99% water. The remaining 1% contains the organ that makes its poison.
column17: deTentacha nimmt Sonnenlicht auf und bricht es in seinem Körper mithilfe von Wasser, um es in Strahlenenergie umzuwandeln. Dieses Pokémon verschießt Strahlen aus dem kleinen, runden Organ über seinen Augen.
column18: enBecause its body is almost entirely composed of water, it shrivels up if it is washed ashore.
column19: enIt drifts through the sea searching for prey. Its poisonous tentacles break off sometimes, but after a while, they grow back.
column20: enWhen the tide goes out, dehydrated TENTACOOL remains can be found washed up on the shore.
column21: enDrifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by their stingers.
column22: deSein Körper besteht aus Wasser. Aus seinen kristallartigen Augen verschießt es eigenartige Strahlen.
column23: enIts body is almost entirely composed of water. It ensnares its foe with its two long tentacles, then stabs with the poison stingers at their tips.
column24: enAs it floats along on the waves, it uses its toxic feelers to stab anything it touches.
column25: enTentacool is not a particularly strong swimmer. It drifts across the surface of shallow seas as it searches for prey.
column26: deFindet man ein schwaches, ausgetrocknetes Tentacha am Strand und bringt es ins Wasser zurück, so wird es wieder topfit.
column27: enIt can sometimes be found all dry and shriveled up on a beach. Toss it back into the sea to revive it.
column28: enIt drifts in shallow seas, such as the areas near beaches. If you get bitten or stabbed by its toxic tentacles, rush to the hospital.
column29: deAm Strand kann man oft stark ausgetrocknete Exemplare finden, die aber noch leben. Taucht man sie in Wasser, werden sie wieder topfit.
column30: enIt drifts aimlessly in waves. Very difficult to see in water, it may not be noticed until it stings.
column31: enTentacool absorbs sunlight and refracts it using water inside its body to convert it into beam energy. This Pokémon shoots beams from the small round organ above its eyes.
column32: enIts body is virtually composed of water. It shoots strange beams from its crystal-like eyes.
column33: deDa Tentacha kein besonders guter Schwimmer ist, treibt es in seichten Gewässern an der Wasseroberfläche und sucht nach Beute.
column34: deEs besteht zu 99 % aus Wasser. Das restliche 1 % wird von einem Organ für die Giftproduktion eingenommen.
column35: enTENTACOOL absorbs sunlight and refracts it using water inside its body to convert it into beam energy. This POKéMON shoots beams from its crystal-like eyes.
column36: enIt drifts in sea currents. Countless fishermen are hurt by its poison stingers.
column37: enThey can be found lying dehydrated on beaches, but they are often still alive. When soaked in water, they will revive.
column38: enTENTACOOL’s body is largely composed of water. If it is removed from the sea, it dries up like parchment. If this POKéMON happens to become dehydrated, put it back into the sea.
column39: deTentacha besteht fast vollständig aus Wasser, wodurch man es im Meer nur sehr schwer erkennen kann.
column40: deEs treibt in Küstennähe im Wasser und sucht nach Beute. Seine giftigen Tentakel fallen ab und zu ab, wachsen aber mit der Zeit wieder nach.

column8: deEs hat für gewöhnlich 80 giftige Tentakel. Mit dem Alter verliert es diese jedoch nach und nach.
column9: deMit Ultraschallwellen aus seinen roten Kugeln schwächt es zunächst seine Beute, um sie dann mit seinen 80 Tentakeln fest zu umwickeln.
column10: enWhen its 80 feelers absorb water, it stretches to become like a net to entangle its prey.
column11: enWhen the red orbs on Tentacruel’s head glow brightly, watch out. The Pokémon is about to fire off a burst of ultrasonic waves.
column12: enWith 80 tentacles for ensnaring victims, it prevents escape until the prey is weakened by poison.
column13: deTentoxa hat große, rote Kugeln auf seinem Kopf. Sie glühen auf, bevor sie seine Gegner mit Ultraschall erschüttern. Ein Ausbruch dieses Pokémon erzeugt starke Wellen in seiner Umgebung.
column14: deDieses Pokémon kann die Tentakel bei der Jagd ausfahren, um die Beute leichter zu fangen.
column15: enIt has 80 tentacles, each with a venomous tip. These tentacles are also extendible, lengthening when Tentacruel attempts to catch prey. Use caution.
column16: enTentacruel has tentacles that can be freely elongated and shortened at will. It ensnares prey with its tentacles and weakens the prey by dosing it with a harsh toxin. It can catch up to 80 prey at the same time.
column17: deBeute umwickelt es mit seinen 80 Tentakeln, die es nach Belieben ausfahren oder einziehen kann. Danach schwächt es sie mit Gift.
column18: enTentacruel has large red orbs on its head. The orbs glow before lashing the vicinity with a harsh ultrasonic blast. This Pokémon’s outburst creates rough waves around it.
column19: enIt extends its 80 tentacles to form an encircling poisonous net that is difficult to escape.
column20: enTENTACRUEL has large red orbs on its head. The orbs glow before lashing the vicinity with a harsh ultrasonic blast. This POKéMON’s outburst creates rough waves around it.
column21: enIts 80 tentacles can stretch and contract freely. They wrap around prey and weaken it with poison.
column22: enIt alerts others to danger by stridently flashing the red orbs on its head.
column23: enIn battle, it extends all 80 of its tentacles to entrap its opponent inside a poisonous net.
column24: enIts 80 tentacles can stretch and shrink freely. Tentacruel ensnares prey in a net of spread-out tentacles, delivering venomous stings to its catch.
column25: enIts 80 tentacles absorb water and stretch almost endlessly to constrict its prey and enemies.
column26: enIt communicates with others of its kind by lighting up the red orbs on its head. When the orbs are blinking, it’s a warning sign.
column27: deTentoxa besitzt Tentakel, die es jederzeit ausfahren und einziehen kann. Es fängt damit Beute und schwächt diese, indem es ihr ein starkes Gift verabreicht. Es kann bis zu 80 Opfer gleichzeitig fangen.
column28: deWenn Tentoxa untereinander kommunizieren, glühen ihre roten Kugeln. Ein wiederholtes Aufleuchten steht für ein Warnsignal.
column29: deEs verfügt über 80 Tentakel, die es frei bewegen kann. Damit kann es zustechen und so Vergiftung und starke Schmerzen verursachen.
column30: deLeuchten die roten Kugeln auf seinem Kopf stark auf, ist Vorsicht geboten, da es kurz danach Ultraschallwellen ausstoßen wird.
column31: enIt has 80 tentacles that move about freely. They can sting, causing poisoning and sharp, stabbing pain.
column32: enIt fires off ultrasonic waves from its red orbs to weaken its prey, and then it wraps them up in its 80 tentacles.
column33: enNormally, it has 80 poisonous tentacles. The longer one has been alive, the fewer tentacles it will have.
column34: deTentoxa kann seine 80 Tentakel beliebig ausfahren und einziehen. Es breitet sie netzartig aus, um Beute zu fangen und dann zu vergiften.
column35: enIts 80 tentacles absorb water and stretch almost endlessly to CONSTRICT its prey and enemies.
column36: enAlthough these Pokémon are rare, when a large outbreak of them occurs, all fish Pokémon disappear from the surrounding sea.
column37: enTENTACRUEL has tentacles that can be freely elongated and shortened at will. It ensnares prey with its tentacles and weakens the prey by dosing it with a harsh toxin. It can catch up to 80 prey at the same time.
column38: enThe tentacles are normally kept short. On hunts, they are extended to ensnare and immobilize prey.
column39: enIt lives in complex rock formations on the ocean floor and traps prey using its 80 tentacles. Its red orbs glow when it grows excited or agitated.
column40: deIn äußerst seltenen Fällen bilden sie einen Schwarm und rotten alle Fisch-Pokémon in der näheren Umgebung aus.

column8: enGeodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they’re totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.
column9: enThe longer a GEODUDE lives, the more its edges are chipped and worn away, making it more rounded in appearance. However, this POKéMON’s heart will remain hard, craggy, and rough always.
column10: deWanderer stolpern in den Bergen häufig über dieses Pokémon, da es wie ein Stein aussieht.
column11: enIt uses both hands to climb precipitous cliffs. People who see it in action have been known to take up bouldering.
column12: deMit dem Alter verliert es zunehmend seine Kantigkeit, bis es vollkommen rund und auch charakterlich immer ausgeglichener wird.
column13: enIt is impossible to distinguish from rocks. It slams against others in contests of hardness.
column14: enIt climbs mountain paths using only the power of its arms. Because they look just like boulders lining paths, hikers may step on them without noticing.
column15: enIts round body makes it easy to pick up, but it is hard and heavy. It’s dangerous to play with them as if they were snowballs.
column16: deMan findet sie in rauen Mengen am Straßenrand. Ein Forscher hat in seiner Freizeit auf einer Straße einmal 100 Exemplare gezählt.
column17: deWenn Kleinstein tief und fest schläft, gräbt es sich selbst zur Hälfte im Boden ein. Selbst wenn Wanderer versehentlich auf Kleinstein treten, wacht es nicht auf. Am Morgen rollt es auf der Suche nach Nahrung den Berg herunter.
column18: deTrotz seiner griffigen, runden Form ist Kleinstein hart und schwer. Bewirft man sich damit wie mit Schneebällen, besteht Verletzungsgefahr!
column19: enMakes its home in mountainous regions, using its arms to climb along harsh mountain roads. Can be troublesome—carelessly kicking one will cause it to fly into a rage and chase after you.
column20: deMit den Armen erklimmt es selbst steile Hänge. Unter den Menschen soll diese Klettermethode Vorbild für den Bouldersport gewesen sein.
column21: enIt uses its arms to steadily climb steep mountain paths. It swings its fists around if angered.
column22: deJe länger ein Kleinstein lebt, desto mehr wird seine raue Oberfläche abgeschliffen, sodass es runder wird. Das Herz dieses Pokémon bleibt aber für immer versteinert, hart und rau.
column23: enAt rest, it looks just like a rock. Carelessly stepping on it will make it swing its fists angrily.
column24: enMany live on mountain trails and remain half buried while keeping an eye on climbers.
column25: enThe longer a Geodude lives, the more its edges are chipped and worn away, making it more rounded in appearance. However, this Pokémon’s heart will remain hard, craggy, and rough always.
column26: enCommonly found near mountain trails and the like. If you step on one by accident, it gets angry.
column27: enIts round form makes it easy to pick up. Some people have used them to hurl at each other in a snowball fight.
column28: deEs hält sich gern in der Nähe von Gebirgspfaden auf. Tritt man versehentlich auf eins, wird es sehr wütend. Deshalb ist höchste Vorsicht geboten.
column29: enWhen GEODUDE sleeps deeply, it buries itself halfway into the ground. It will not awaken even if hikers step on it unwittingly. In the morning, this POKéMON rolls downhill in search of food.
column30: enProud of their sturdy bodies, they bash against each other in a contest to prove whose is harder.
column31: enMost people may not notice, but a closer look should reveal that there are many GEODUDE around.
column32: deMan kann es nicht von Felsbrocken unterscheiden. Es rempelt andere an, um sich zu beweisen.
column33: enCommonly found near mountain trails, etc. If you step on one by accident, it gets angry.
column34: enFound in fields and mountains. Mistaking them for boulders, people often step or trip on them.
column35: enThere are plenty of them to be found along any road. A scholar with too much free time once counted a hundred of them along a single route.
column36: enWhen Geodude sleeps deeply, it buries itself halfway into the ground. It will not awaken even if hikers step on it unwittingly. In the morning, this Pokémon rolls downhill in search of food.

column8: deDa sich seine Beine nicht zum Gehen eignen, bewegt es sich rollend fort. Kommt es richtig in Fahrt, erreicht es dabei bis zu 100 km/h.
column9: enIt travels by rolling on mountain paths. If it gains too much speed, it stops by running into huge rocks.
column10: deEs klettert Berghänge bis zum Gipfel empor. Einmal oben angekommen, rollt es über den Bergpfad sogleich wieder hinunter.
column11: enThey descend from mountains by tumbling down steep slopes. They are so brutal, they smash aside obstructing trees and massive boulders with thunderous tackles.
column12: enBe careful while hiking on mountain trails. GRAVELER may come rolling down the path without slowing.
column13: enA slow walker, it rolls to move. It pays no attention to any object that happens to be in its path.
column14: deGeorok wächst, indem es sich von Steinen ernährt. Am liebsten mag es Steine, die mit Moos überzogen sind. Dieses Pokémon frisst sich täglich durch eine riesige Menge von Steinen.
column15: deEs ist ihm völlig gleichgültig, wenn Stücke aus ihm herausbrechen, während es Berge hinabrollt.
column16: enGraveler grows by feeding on rocks. Apparently, it prefers to eat rocks that are covered in moss. This Pokémon eats its way through a ton of rocks on a daily basis.
column17: enIt travels by rolling down cliffs. If it falls into a river, it will explode with its last gasp.
column18: enMossy rocks are its favorite food. It can devour a ton a day while crunching loudly.
column19: enDwells in holes dug into sheer walls of stone. It enjoys rolling down slopes as though it were a boulder during a rockfall, so keep an eye upward while traversing mountain roads.
column20: deMit Moos überzogene Steine sind seine absolute Leibspeise. Es vertilgt täglich laut knirschend eine ganze Tonne davon.
column21: deEs bewegt sich rollend über Abhänge fort. Stürzt es versehentlich in einen Fluss, sprengt es sich in seiner Verzweiflung selbst in die Luft.
column22: enGRAVELER grows by feeding on rocks. Apparently, it prefers to eat rocks that are covered in moss. This POKéMON eats its way through a ton of rocks on a daily basis.
column23: enOften seen rolling down mountain trails. Obstacles are just things to roll straight over, not avoid.
column24: deSteine sind Georoks Lieblingsspeise. Dieses Pokémon klettert vom Fuße eines Berges zu seinem Gipfel empor und zermalmt dabei das Gestein. Wenn es den Gipfel erreicht hat, rollt es wieder herunter.
column25: enRocks are Graveler’s favorite food. This Pokémon will climb a mountain from the base to the summit, crunchingly feasting on rocks all the while. Upon reaching the peak, it rolls back down to the bottom.
column26: enThis slow-footed Pokémon moves by curling up and rolling instead of walking. With enough momentum, its speed can exceed 60 mph.
column27: enRolls down slopes to move. It rolls over any obstacle without slowing or changing its direction.
column28: enIt tumbles down slopes, heedless of any body parts chipping off. It eats a ton of rocks daily.
column29: enIt rolls on mountain paths to move. Once it builds momentum, no Pokémon can stop it without difficulty.
column30: enWith a free and uncaring nature, it doesn't mind if pieces break off while it rolls down mountains.
column31: enRocks are GRAVELER’s favorite food. This POKéMON will climb a mountain from the base to the summit, crunchingly feasting on rocks all the while. Upon reaching the peak, it rolls back down to the bottom.
column32: enGRAVELER make their homes on sheer cliff faces by gouging out numerous horizontal holes.
column33: deEs rollt sich gerne Gebirgspfade hinunter. Dabei ist es ihm egal, wer oder was sich in seinem Weg befindet.
column34: enIt climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.
column35: deDieses Pokémon bewegt sich nur rollend fort. Kein Hindernis kann es vom Kurs abbringen.
column36: enWith a free and uncaring nature, it doesn’t mind if pieces break off while it rolls down mountains.

column8: enGOLEM is known for rolling down from mountains. To prevent them from rolling into the homes of people downhill, grooves have been dug into the sides of mountains to serve as guideways for diverting this POKéMON’s course.
column9: deEs kann sich in die Luft sprengen. Diese Explosionen setzt es ein, um selbst steile Bergpfade in rasantem Tempo zu meistern.
column10: deSelbst Sprengstoff kann seinem harten Körper nichts anhaben. Es verabscheut Feuchtigkeit und Regen.
column11: enIt sheds its skin once a year. The discarded shell immediately hardens and crumbles away.
column12: enIts boulder-like body is extremely hard. It can easily withstand dynamite blasts without damage.
column13: enIt is capable of blowing itself up. It uses this explosive force to jump from mountain to mountain.
column14: enOnce it sheds its skin, its body turns tender and whitish. Its hide hardens when it’s exposed to air.
column15: deSie rollen Berge hinunter und hinterlassen Spurrillen. Halte dich von diesen Rillen fern.
column16: deIm Alter hört es auf, sich zu häuten. Der Panzer betagter Geowaz ist mit dichtem, grünem Moos bewachsen.
column17: deGeowaz ist dafür bekannt, dass es Berghänge herunterrollt. Damit es nicht in die Häuser der Menschen hineinrollt, wurden Gräben so an den Bergen angelegt, dass der Weg dieses Pokémon umgeleitet wird.
column18: enEven dynamite can’t harm its hard, boulderlike body. It sheds its hide just once a year.
column19: deNachdem es sich gehäutet hat, wird sein Körper weich und hell. Durch den Kontakt mit der Luft erhärtet er aber sofort wieder.
column20: enGolem live up on mountains. If there is a large earthquake, these Pokémon will come rolling down off the mountains en masse to the foothills below.
column21: enIt tumbles down mountains, leaving grooves from peak to base. Stay clear of these grooves.
column22: enGolem is known for rolling down from mountains. To prevent them from rolling into the homes of people downhill, grooves have been dug into the sides of mountains to serve as guideways for diverting this Pokémon’s course.
column23: enIt is said to live in volcanic craters on mountain peaks. Once a year, it sheds its hide and grows larger. The shed hide crumbles and returns to the soil.
column24: enThe rocklike shell is shed each year. The cast-off shell then crumbles, reverting to a mass of soil, which can be spread across fields to promote crop growth.
column25: enIt detonates its own body. The power from that explosion can propel it up steep mountain paths with amazing speed.
column26: deSelbst Sprengstoff kann seinem harten Körper nichts anhaben. Häutet sich nur einmal jährlich.
column27: deEinmal im Jahr häutet es sich. Die abgestreifte Haut wird oft von Bauern eingesammelt, da sie sich ausgezeichnet als Dünger verwenden lässt.
column28: enIts rock-like body is so durable, even high-powered dynamite blasts fail to scratch its rugged hide.
column29: enOnce a year, this Pokémon molts, and its shed shell returns to the soil. This process creates enriched soil, so farmers collect the shells.
column30: enEven dynamite can’t harm its hard, boulder-like body. It sheds its hide just once a year.
column31: enGOLEM live up on mountains. If there is a large earthquake, these POKéMON will come rolling down off the mountains en masse to the foothills below.
column32: enIt sheds its hide once a year. Its boulderlike body is so tough, even dynamite can’t harm it.
column33: deGeowaz lebt hoch in den Bergen. Gibt es ein starkes Erdbeben, rollt dieses Pokémon so lange den Berg hinunter, bis es schließlich unten angekommen ist.
column34: enIts body can survive dynamite blasts without a single scratch, but it hates rain and humidity.
column35: enOnce it sheds its skin, its body turns tender and whitish. Its hide hardens when it's exposed to air.
column36: enIt is enclosed in a hard shell that is as rugged as slabs of rock. It sheds skin once a year to grow larger.
column37: enWhen Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.
column38: enIts boulder-like body is extremely hard. It can easily withstand dynamite blasts without taking damage.

column8: enAbout an hour after birth, its fiery mane and tail grow out, giving it an impressive appearance.
column9: enAs a newborn, it can barely stand. However, through galloping, its legs are made tougher and faster.
column10: enIts hooves are 10 times harder than diamonds. It can trample anything completely flat in little time.
column11: enCapable of jumping incredibly high. Its hooves and sturdy legs absorb the impact of a hard landing.
column12: deSeine Beine werden kräftig, da es seinen Eltern hinterherläuft. Es rennt den ganzen Tag umher.
column13: enIts hind legs, which have harder-than-diamond hooves, kick back at any presence it senses behind it.
column14: enIf you’ve been accepted by Ponyta, its burning mane is mysteriously no longer hot to the touch.
column15: deNeugeboren kann es kaum stehen. Durch das Galoppieren werden seine Beine aber schneller und kräftiger.
column16: deNach seiner Geburt ist Ponita sehr schwach. Es braucht sehr lange, bis es auf allen Vieren steht. Dieses Pokémon wird stärker, da es bei dem Versuch, mit seinen Eltern Schritt zu halten, immer wieder stolpert und hinfällt.
column17: enIts body is light, and its legs are incredibly powerful. It can clear Ayers Rock in one leap.
column18: enIt can’t run properly when it’s newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.
column19: enPonyta is very weak at birth. It can barely stand up. This Pokémon becomes stronger by stumbling and falling to keep up with its parent.
column20: enA PONYTA is very weak at birth. It can barely stand up. Its legs become stronger as it stumbles and falls while trying to keep up with its parent.
column21: deEs kann unglaublich hoch springen. Die Wucht der Landung absorbiert es mit seinen robusten Hufen und Beinen.
column22: enIts hind legs, which have hooves that are harder than diamond, kick back at any presence it senses behind it.
column23: deNach seiner Geburt ist Ponita so schwach, dass es kaum von alleine aufstehen kann. Dieses Pokémon wird dadurch schnell stärker, dass es sich beim Versuch, mit seinen Eltern Schritt zu halten, immer wieder aufrappeln muss.
column24: enTraining by jumping over grass that grows longer every day has made it a world-class jumper.
column25: deHat man erst einmal das Vertrauen eines Ponitas gewonnen, kann man seltsamerweise sogar seine feurige Mähne anfassen, ohne sich zu verbrennen.
column26: enIt is a weak runner immediately after birth. It gradually becomes faster by chasing after its parents.
column27: enIts legs grow strong while it chases after its parent. It runs in fields and mountains all day.
column28: enThese Pokémon live in herds out in the grassland. Newborn foals lack their fiery manes, which will develop about an hour after birth.
column29: deNach der Geburt fällt ihm das Laufen schwer. Die Wettrennen, die es sich mit seinen Freunden liefert, stärken jedoch seine Beinmuskulatur.
column30: enIts hooves are ten times harder than diamond. It can trample anything completely flat in little time.
column31: enPONYTA is very weak at birth. It can barely stand up. This POKéMON becomes stronger by stumbling and falling to keep up with its parent.

column8: enWith incredible acceleration, it reaches its top speed of 150 mph after running just 10 steps.
column9: enIt has astounding acceleration. From a standstill, it can reach top speed within 10 steps.
column10: enWith incredible acceleration, it reaches its top speed of 150 mph after running just ten steps.
column11: enIt gallops at nearly 150 mph. With its mane blazing ferociously, it races as if it were an arrow.
column12: enAt full gallop, its four hooves barely touch the ground because it moves so incredibly fast.
column13: deMan kann Gallopa dabei beobachten, wie es fröhlich über Felder und Wiesen galoppiert. Wenn dieses Pokémon jedoch Ernst macht, erreicht es bis zu 240 km/h. Dann beginnt seine Mähne zu lodern.
column14: enFiery mane aglow, Rapidash darts like an arrow across the land. This prodigiously swift creature can traverse the vast region of Hisui in a day and a half.
column15: deDas schnellste Exemplar wird zum Anführer bestimmt und gibt als solcher den Zielort und die Geschwindigkeit der Herde vor.
column16: enRAPIDASH usually can be seen casually cantering in the fields and plains. However, when this POKéMON turns serious, its fiery manes flare and blaze as it gallops its way up to 150 mph.
column17: deDie lodernde Mähne dieses Pokémon flattert im Wind, wenn es mit einer Geschwindigkeit von 240 km/h über Felder und Wiesen galoppiert.
column18: deEs galoppiert für sein Leben gern. Entdeckt es etwas, das schneller ist als es selbst, nimmt es blitzschnell die Verfolgung auf.
column19: deDieses Pokémon verfolgt schnelle Objekte in der Hoffnung, ein Wettrennen gegen sie zu gewinnen.
column20: enThe fastest runner becomes the leader, and it decides the herd’s pace and direction of travel.
column21: enRapidash usually can be seen casually cantering in the fields and plains. However, when this Pokémon turns serious, its fiery manes flare and blaze as it gallops its way up to 150 mph.
column22: enIt usually canters casually in the fields and plains. But once a RAPIDASH turns serious, its fiery manes flare and blaze as it gallops its way up to 150 mph.
column23: enVery competitive, this Pokémon will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.
column24: enIt just loves to gallop. The faster it goes, the longer the swaying flames of its mane will become.
column25: deSeine Mähne lodert auf, wenn es mit 240 km/h pfeilschnell galoppiert.
column26: enJust loves to run. If it sees something faster than itself, it will give chase at top speed.
column27: enVery competitive, this POKéMON will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.
column28: deMan kann Gallopa dabei beobachten, wie es lässig über Felder und Wiesen galoppiert. Wenn dieses Pokémon jedoch Ernst macht, erreicht es bis zu 240 km/h. Dann beginnt seine Mähne zu lodern.
column29: enIt can gallop at a top speed of 150 miles per hour. It can race as fast as a bullet train while ablaze.
column30: enThis Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.
column31: enWhen at an all-out gallop, its blazing mane sparkles, enhancing its beautiful appearance.

column8: deVon selbst abgefallene Flegmon-Ruten scheinen überall herumzuliegen. Dennoch gilt dieses einfache Nahrungsmittel als hochwertig.
column9: enAlthough slow, it is skilled at fishing with its tail. It does not feel pain if its tail is bitten.
column10: deTaucht es seine Rute ins Wasser, strömt sie ein süßes Aroma aus. Mit diesem Köder lockt es andere Pokémon an, die es dann an Land zieht.
column11: enSlow-witted and oblivious, this Pokémon won’t feel any pain if its tail gets eaten. It won’t notice when its tail grows back, either.
column12: deDieses Pokémon ist unglaublich faul und träge. Es faulenzt den ganzen Tag lang und schert sich nicht um die verstrichene Zeit.
column13: enA sweet sap leaks from its tail’s tip. Although not nutritious, the tail is pleasant to chew on.
column14: enWhen this Pokémon’s tail is soaked in water, sweetness seeps from it. Slowpoke uses this trait to lure in and fish up other Pokémon.
column15: enIt lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won’t even notice for a whole day.
column16: deIn Alola bereiten viele Familien Flegmon-Ruten zu, indem sie diese zuerst trocknen und dann in Salzwasser kochen.
column17: enIt is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.
column18: enAlolan home cooking involves drying Slowpoke tails and then simmering them into a salty stew.
column19: enIncredibly slow and sluggish. It is quite content to loll about without worrying about the time.
column20: enIncredibly slow and dopey. It takes 5 seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.
column21: enIt is always so absent-minded that it won't react, even if its flavorful tail is bitten.
column22: enA sweet sap leaks from its tail's tip. Although not nutritious, the tail is pleasant to chew on.
column23: enIts long tail often breaks off. It doesn’t really feel any pain, though, and the tail grows back, so Slowpoke isn’t particularly bothered.
column24: deSeine Rute bricht sehr leicht ab. Dies kümmert es jedoch wenig, da es dabei keine Schmerzen spürt und sie sofort wieder nachwächst.
column25: deEs faulenzt am Wasser. Wenn es in den Schweif gebissen wird, bemerkt es das erst am nächsten Tag.
column26: enSLOWPOKE uses its tail to catch prey by dipping it in water at the side of a river. However, this POKéMON often forgets what it’s doing and often spends entire days just loafing at water’s edge.
column27: enIt catches prey by dipping its tail in water at the side of a river. But it often forgets what it is doing and spends entire days just loafing at water’s edge.
column28: deEiner Legende zufolge soll Flegmons Gähnen Regen herbeiführen. In manchen Regionen wird ihm deswegen gehuldigt.
column29: deEs ist so träge und unempfindlich, dass es keinen Schmerz verspürt, wenn seine Rute gegessen wird. Es bemerkt nicht einmal, dass sie nachwächst.
column30: enSlowpoke uses its tail to catch prey by dipping it in water at the side of a river. However, this Pokémon often forgets what it’s doing and often spends entire days just loafing at water’s edge.
column31: deEs ist stets in Gedanken versunken und niemand weiß, worüber es nachdenkt. Es kann mit seiner Rute gut angeln.
column32: enAlthough their tails, which fall off naturally, can easily be found lying around, they’re a precious ingredient for cooking.
column33: deFlegmon benutzt seinen Schweif, um Beute zu fangen, indem es ihn an einem Flussufer ins Wasser taucht. Dieses Pokémon vergisst häufig, was es gerade tun wollte, und verbringt ganze Tage damit, am Ufer herumzutrödeln.
column34: enThere are some places where Slowpoke is worshiped because of a long-standing belief that whenever Slowpoke yawns, it rains.
column35: enIncredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.
column36: enIt lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won't even notice for a whole day.

column8: enLives lazily by the sea. If the Shellder on its tail comes off, it becomes a Slowpoke again.
column9: enShellder, in its greed to suck out more and more sweetness from Slowbro’s tail, has metamorphosed into a spiral-shaped shell.
column10: deAn Lahmus’ Rute hat sich ein Muschas festgebissen. Daher kann es ihn nicht mehr zum Angeln benutzen und es ist dazu gezwungen, umherzuschwimmen und nach Beute Ausschau zu halten.
column11: enSlowpoke became Slowbro when a Shellder bit on to its tail. Sweet flavors seeping from the tail make the Shellder feel as if its life is a dream.
column12: enThe SHELLDER that latches onto SLOWPOKE’s tail is said to feed on the host’s leftover scraps.
column13: deEin von Grund auf begriffsstutziges Pokémon. Verliert es seine Rute mitsamt Muschas, entwickelt es sich zu Flegmon zurück.
column14: deEs betrachtet oft mit leerem Blick das Meer. Das Gift, das Muschas in seinen Körper injiziert, erhöht seine Begriffsstutzigkeit noch weiter.
column15: enIts tail has a SHELLDER firmly attached with a bite. As a result, the tail can’t be used for fishing anymore. This forces it to reluctantly swim and catch prey.
column16: enAn attached Shellder won’t let go because of the tasty flavor that oozes out of its tail.
column17: enThough usually dim witted, it seems to become inspired if the Shellder on its tail bites down.
column18: enIt spaces out while gazing at the sea. With Shellder’s poison flowing through its body, it becomes even spacier.
column19: enThe SHELLDER that is latched onto SLOWPOKE's tail is said to feed on the host's left over scraps.
column20: enNaturally dull to begin with, it lost its ability to feel pain due to SHELLDER’s seeping poison.
column21: enSpacing out is basically all it does. It turns back into Slowpoke if its tail, along with Shellder, breaks off.
column22: enIf the tail-biting SHELLDER is thrown off in a harsh battle, it reverts to being an ordinary SLOWPOKE.
column23: deAn Lahmus’ Schweif hat sich ein Muschas festgebissen. Daher kann es ihn nicht mehr zum Fischen benutzen und ist dazu gezwungen, umherzuschwimmen und Ausschau nach Beute zu halten.
column24: deEs führt ein ruhiges Leben in der Nähe des Meeres. Verliert es das Muschas an seinem Schwanz, wird es wieder zu einem Flegmon.
column25: enWhenever Shellder bites down hard on its tail, it gives Slowbro a flash of inspiration...which it forgets a moment later.
column26: deDas Muschas an seiner Rute bekommt von dem süßen Geschmack einfach nicht genug und mutiert für mehr Biss zu einer Spiralform.
column27: enNaturally dull to begin with, it lost its ability to feel pain due to SHELLDER's seeping poison.
column28: enThough usually dim witted, it seems to become inspired if the SHELLDER on its tail bites down.
column29: deDer Schock, den Muschas’ Biss verursachte, ließ es auf zwei Beinen stehen. Löst das Muschas sich von ihm, wird es wieder zu einem Flegmon.
column30: deDas Muschas an seiner Rute lässt nicht locker, da ein leckerer Geschmack aus seiner Rute strömt.
column31: enSlowbro’s tail has a Shellder firmly attached with a bite. As a result, the tail can’t be used for fishing anymore. This causes Slowbro to grudgingly swim and catch prey instead.
column32: enSLOWBRO’s tail has a SHELLDER firmly attached with a bite. As a result, the tail can’t be used for fishing anymore. This causes SLOWBRO to grudgingly swim and catch prey instead.
column33: enWhen a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.
column34: enWhen a SLOWPOKE went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a SHELLDER. That made it evolve into SLOWBRO.
column35: deBeißt das Muschas an seinem Schweif beherzt zu, hat es eine geniale Idee, die es aber sofort wieder vergisst.
column36: deEs entwickelte sich, als ein Muschas in seine Rute biss. Das süße Aroma, das aus der Rute strömt, hat Muschas in eine Art Trance versetzt.
column37: deAls Flegmon im Wasser jagen ging, wurde es von einem Muschas gebissen. Dadurch entwickelte es sich zu Lahmus.
column38: enBeing bitten by a Shellder shocked this Pokémon into standing on two legs. If the Shellder lets go, it seems Slowbro will turn back into a Slowpoke.
column39: enAn attached SHELLDER won't let go because of the tasty flavor that oozes out of its tail.
column40: enLives lazily by the sea. If the SHELLDER on its tail comes off, it becomes a SLOWPOKE again.
