column29: deDer Schock, den Muschas’ Biss verursachte, ließ es auf zwei Beinen stehen. Löst das Muschas sich von ihm, wird es wieder zu einem Flegmon.
column30: deDas Muschas an seiner Rute lässt nicht locker, da ein leckerer Geschmack aus seiner Rute strömt.
column31: enSlowbro’s tail has a Shellder firmly attached with a bite. As a result, the tail can’t be used for fishing anymore. This causes Slowbro to grudgingly swim and catch prey instead.
column32: enSLOWBRO’s tail has a SHELLDER firmly attached with a bite. As a result, the tail can’t be used for fishing anymore. This causes SLOWBRO to grudgingly swim and catch prey instead.
column33: enWhen a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.
column34: enWhen a SLOWPOKE went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a SHELLDER. That made it evolve into SLOWBRO.
column35: deBeißt das Muschas an seinem Schweif beherzt zu, hat es eine geniale Idee, die es aber sofort wieder vergisst.
column36: deEs entwickelte sich, als ein Muschas in seine Rute biss. Das süße Aroma, das aus der Rute strömt, hat Muschas in eine Art Trance versetzt.
column37: deAls Flegmon im Wasser jagen ging, wurde es von einem Muschas gebissen. Dadurch entwickelte es sich zu Lahmus.
column38: enBeing bitten by a Shellder shocked this Pokémon into standing on two legs. If the Shellder lets go, it seems Slowbro will turn back into a Slowpoke.
column39: enAn attached SHELLDER won't let go because of the tasty flavor that oozes out of its tail.
column40: enLives lazily by the sea. If the SHELLDER on its tail comes off, it becomes a SLOWPOKE again.

column8: enIt moves while constantly hovering. It discharges THUNDER WAVE and so on from the units at its sides.
column9: enThe units at its sides generate electromagnetic waves that keep it airborne. It feeds on electricity.
column10: enThe faster the units at its sides rotate, the greater the magnetic force they generate.
column11: enThe electricity emitted by the units on each side of its body cause it to become a strong magnet.
column12: enThe units at its sides are extremely powerful magnets. They generate enough magnetism to draw in iron objects from over 300 feet away.
column13: enMagnemite floats in the air by emitting electromagnetic waves from the units at its sides. These waves block gravity. This Pokémon becomes incapable of flight if its internal electrical supply is depleted.
column14: enIt’s frequently the cause of power outages, which is why some power plants send out electrical signals that it can’t stand.
column15: enUses anti-gravity to stay suspended. Appears without warning and uses THUNDER WAVE and similar moves.
column16: enIt is born with the ability to defy gravity. It floats in air on powerful electromagnetic waves.
column17: deDie Magneten an seinem Körper erzeugen ein Antigravitationsfeld, um es ständig in der Schwebe zu halten.
column18: enThe units at the sides of its body generate antigravity energy to keep it aloft in the air.
column19: deSie scharen sich um Orte, durch die Elektrizität fließt. Deshalb klammern sie sich zum Beispiel sehr oft in Massen an Strommasten fest.
column20: enA bizarre Pokémon with but a single eye embedded in an iron sphere. I suspect this creature levitates due to the magnetism it emits from its arms, which resemble horseshoe-shaped magnets.
column21: enThe electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.
column22: enIt is attracted by electromagnetic waves. It may approach trainers if they are using their POKéGEAR.
column23: deSeit Stromleitungen zunehmend unterirdisch verlegt werden, stürzen sich mehr und mehr Magnetilo direkt auf Kraftwerke.
column24: enMagnemite attaches itself to power lines to feed on electricity. If your house has a power outage, check your circuit breakers. You may find a large number of this Pokémon clinging to the breaker box.
column25: deDa Magnetilo Stromausfälle verursacht, werden in vielen Kraftwerken Radiowellen ausgesendet, die es nicht leiden kann, um es zu vertreiben.
column26: deMagnetilo segelt durch die Luft und sendet dabei elektromagnetische Wellen an beiden Seiten seines Körpers aus. Dieses Pokémon wird flugunfähig, wenn die Stromquelle in seinem Inneren erschöpft ist.
column27: enMAGNEMITE attaches itself to power lines to feed on electricity. If your house has a power outage, check your circuit breakers. You may find a large number of this POKéMON clinging to the breaker box.
column28: deEs trotzt von Geburt an der Schwerkraft und schwebt auf elektromagnetischen Wellen durch die Luft.
column29: enIt moves while constantly hovering. It discharges Thunder Wave and so on from the units at its sides.
column30: enPerhaps because electrical lines are often buried these days, the number of Magnemite attacks on power plants has increased.
column31: deMagnetilo ernährt sich von Elektrizität aus Stromleitungen. Dieses Pokémon hängt sich gern an Sicherungskästen. Wenn es in deinem Haus zu einem Stromausfall kommt, solltest du die Sicherungen überprüfen.
column32: enThey gather in places where electricity is available. They can be found clinging to the steel towers used to support power lines.
column33: deEs sendet elektromagnetische Wellen aus und segelt durch die Luft. Berührt man es, während es Strom saugt, bekommt man einen Schlag.
column34: enAt times, Magnemite runs out of electricity and ends up on the ground. If you give batteries to a grounded Magnemite, it’ll start moving again.
column35: deEs bewegt sich schwebend. Seine Magnete an den Seiten entladen eine Donnerwelle nach der nächsten.
column36: enIt is attracted by electromagnetic waves. It may approach Trainers if they are using their Pokégear.
column37: deManchmal geht die Elektrizität in seinem Körper zur Neige, woraufhin es zu Boden fällt. Eine Batterie bringt es wieder in Bewegung.
column38: enUses antigravity to stay suspended. Appears without warning and uses THUNDER WAVE and similar moves.
column39: enMAGNEMITE floats in the air by emitting electromagnetic waves from the units at its sides. These waves block gravity. This POKéMON becomes incapable of flight if its internal electrical supply is depleted.
column40: enIt subsists on electricity. As Magnemite flies, it emits electromagnetic waves from the units on each side of its body.
column41: deEs ernährt sich von Strom und segelt durch die Luft, indem es mit den Magneten an seinem Körper elektromagnetische Wellen aussendet.
column42: enIt is hatched with the ability to defy gravity. It floats while emitting powerful electromagnetic waves.
column43: enIt sends out electromagnetic waves, which let it float through the air. Touching it while it’s eating electricity will give you a full-body shock.

column8: deZiehen Regenwolken auf, versammeln sich Magneton in großer Höhe und warten darauf, dass der Blitz einschlägt.
column9: enMany mysteriously appear when more sunspots dot the sun. They stop TV sets from displaying properly.
column10: enThe stronger electromagnetic waves from the three linked Magnemite are enough to dry out surrounding moisture.
column11: enThis Pokémon is three Magnemite that have linked together. Magneton sends out powerful radio waves to study its surroundings.
column12: enA linked cluster formed of several MAGNEMITE. It discharges powerful magnetic waves at high voltage.
column13: enDelicate equipment can malfunction in areas inhabited by Magneton, which send out mysterious electrical signals.
column14: enMAGNETON emits a powerful magnetic force that is fatal to mechanical devices. As a result, large cities sound sirens to warn citizens of large-scale outbreaks of this POKéMON.
column15: deEs sondert stetig seine starken Magnetkräfte ab, sodass bei den meisten Computern Störungen auftreten, wenn es sich in deren Nähe aufhält.
column16: enIt is actually three MAGNEMITE linked by magnetism. It generates powerful radio waves that raise temperatures by 3.6 degrees F within a 3,300-foot radius.
column17: enGenerates strange radio signals. It raises the temperature by 3.6F degrees within 3,300 feet.
column18: deMagneton strahlt eine starke magnetische Energie ab, die für elektronische Geräte und Präzisionsinstrumente sehr schädlich ist. In einigen Städten werden die Bewohner gebeten, dieses Pokémon in einem Pokéball zu halten.
column19: deMagneton erzeugt ein sehr starkes Magnetfeld, das Maschinen zerstören kann. Deshalb hört man in großen Städten oft Sirenen, die die Bürger vor Scharen dieser Pokémon warnen sollen.
column20: enThree MAGNEMITE are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.
column21: deSchließen sich mehrere Magnetilo zusammen, entsteht dieses Pokémon. Es entlädt kräftige Hochspannungsmagnetwellen.
column22: enMagneton emits a powerful magnetic force that is fatal to mechanical devices. As a result, large cities sound sirens to warn citizens of large-scale outbreaks of this Pokémon.
column23: enMagneton emits a powerful magnetic force that is fatal to electronics and precision instruments. Because of this, it is said that some towns warn people to keep this Pokémon inside a Poké Ball.
column24: enThe MAGNEMITE are united by a magnetism so powerful, it dries all moisture in its vicinities.
column25: enIt is actually three MAGNEMITE linked by magnetism. A group can set off a magnetic storm.
column26: enFormed by several MAGNEMITEs linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.
column27: deEs erzeugt seltsame Radiowellen, die Störungen an Präzisionsinstrumenten in seiner Umgebung verursachen.
column28: deSeine Stromstärke ist fast dreimal so hoch wie die eines Magnetilos. Es erscheint vermehrt, wenn Flecken auf der Sonne auftauchen.
column29: deDrei Magnetilo schließen sich zusammen und vereinigen ihre Gehirne. Das heißt aber nicht, dass dieses Pokémon dreimal so schlau wird.
column30: deEs erzeugt seltsame Radiowellen, durch welche die Lufttemperatur in einem Radius von 1 km um 2 şC steigt.
column31: deEs entsteht durch den Zusammenschluss dreier Magnetilo und sendet starke Radiowellen aus, um seine Umgebung zu untersuchen.
column32: enGenerates strange radio signals. It raises the temperature by 3.6 degrees Fahrenheit within 3,300 feet.
column33: enWhen three Magnemite link together, their brains also become one. They do not become three times more intelligent.
column34: enWhen many MAGNETON gather together, the resulting magnetic storm disrupts radio waves.
column35: enThis Pokémon is constantly putting out a powerful magnetic force. Most computers go haywire when a Magneton approaches.
column36: enMAGNETON emits a powerful magnetic force that is fatal to electronics and precision instruments. Because of this, it is said that some towns warn people to keep this POKéMON inside a POKé BALL.
column37: enA linked cluster formed of several Magnemite. It discharges powerful magnetic waves at high voltage.
column38: enThe MAGNEMITE are united by a magnetism so powerful, it dries all moisture in its vicinity.
column39: enFormed by several MAGNEMITE linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.
column40: enThree Magnemite gathered to evolve into this Pokémon. The source of much vexation on my part, as its powerful magnetism destroys my research equipment.
column41: enIt has about three times the electrical power of Magnemite. For some reason, outbreaks of this Pokémon happen when lots of sunspots appear.
column42: deDieses Pokémon erzeugt seltsame Radiowellen und kann die Lufttemperatur geringfügig anheben.
column43: enWhen rain clouds form, many Magneton gather in high places to wait for lightning to strike.

column8: enIf anyone tries to disturb where the essential plant stalks grow, it uses its own stalk to thwart them.
column9: enIt always walks about with a plant stalk clamped in its beak. The stalk is used for building its nest.
column10: deDieses Pokémon nutzt eine Lauchstange als Waffe. Es setzt sie wie ein Schwert ein.
column11: deEs läuft stets mit einer Lauchstange umher. Damit baut es sein Nest.
column12: enFarfetch’d is always seen with a stalk from a plant of some sort. Apparently, there are good stalks and bad stalks. This Pokémon has been known to fight with others over stalks.
column13: deUnter seinem Flügel trägt es eine Lauchstange, die es wie ein Schwert gegen Feinde einsetzt und welche ihm im Bedarfsfall auch als Nahrung dient.
column14: enIf it eats the plant stalk it carries as emergency rations, it runs off in search of a new stalk.
column15: enThey use a plant stalk as a weapon, but not all of them use it in the same way. Several distinct styles of stalk fighting have been observed.
column16: enIf anyone tries to disturb where the essential plant sticks grow, it uses its own stick to thwart them.
column17: enIn order to prevent their extinction, more people have made an effort to breed these POKéMON.
column18: deIm Kampf verwendet es eine Lauchstange als Waffe. Es gibt verschiedene Meinungen dazu, wie diese korrekt zu schwingen sei.
column19: enIf it eats the plant stick it carries as emergency rations, it runs off in search of a new stick.
column20: deSie leben in Gebieten, in denen stangenförmige Gewächse gedeihen. Da diese seltener werden, scheint es auch weniger Porenta zu geben.
column21: enThe plant stalk it holds is its weapon. The stalk is used like a sword to cut all sorts of things.
column22: enIt is always seen with a stick from a plant. Apparently, there are good sticks and bad sticks. This POKéMON occasionally fights with others over choice sticks.
column23: enIt can’t live without the stalk it holds. That’s why it defends the stalk from attackers with its life.
column24: dePorenta trifft man immer mit einer Lauchstange an. Es gibt gute und schlechte Stangen. Dieses Pokémon kämpft mit Hilfe seiner Stange gegen seine Gegner.
column25: enThe sprig of green onions it holds is its weapon. It is used much like a metal sword.
column26: enThey live where reedy plants grow. Farfetch’d are rarely seen, so it’s thought their numbers are decreasing.
column27: dePorenta trifft man immer mit einem Pflanzenstängel an, dessen Qualität unterschiedlich ausfallen kann. Von diesem Pokémon wird behauptet, es kämpfe gegen die eigenen Artgenossen um die besten Stängel.
column28: enFARFETCH’D is always seen with a stick from a plant of some sort. Apparently, there are good sticks and bad sticks. This POKéMON has been known to fight with others over sticks.
column29: enLives where reedy plants grow. They are rarely seen, so it's thought their numbers are decreasing.
column30: enThe stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

column8: enBy alternately raising and lowering its two heads, it balances itself to be more stable while running.
column9: enIts short wings make flying difficult. Instead, this POKéMON runs at high speed on developed legs.
column10: enA bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.
column11: enDoduo’s two heads never sleep at the same time. Its two heads take turns sleeping, so one head can always keep watch for enemies while the other one sleeps.
column12: enDODUO’s two heads never sleep at the same time. Its two heads take turns sleeping, so one head can always keep watch for enemies while the other one sleeps.
column13: enA two-headed POKéMON that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.
column14: deDodu hat zwei Köpfe, die nie zur selben Zeit, sondern abwechselnd schlafen. Während ein Kopf schläft, hält der andere Ausschau nach Feinden.
column15: enDoduo’s two heads contain completely identical brains. A scientific study reported that on rare occasions, there will be examples of this Pokémon possessing different sets of brains.
column16: enDODUO’s two heads contain completely identical brains. A scientific study reported that on rare occasions, there will be examples of this POKéMON possessing different sets of brains.
column17: deEs kann nicht gut fliegen, weil seine Flügel zu klein sind. Dafür kann es mit seinen starken Beinen sehr schnell laufen.
column18: deDodu hat zwei Köpfe mit absolut identischen Gehirnen. Eine wissenschaftliche Studie hat allerdings nachgewiesen, dass es auch Exemplare dieses Pokémon gibt, die zwei unterschiedliche Gehirne haben.
column19: deDieses zweiköpfige Pokémon gilt als plötzliche Mutation. Es rennt bis zu 100 km/h schnell.
column20: enIts short wings make flying difficult. Instead, this Pokémon runs at high speed on developed legs.
column21: enThe brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.
column22: enIt lives on a grassy plain where it can see a long way. If it sees an enemy, it runs away at 60 mph.
column23: enA two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.
column24: enIt races through grassy plains with powerful strides, leaving footprints up to four inches deep.
column25: enEven while eating or sleeping, one of the heads remains always vigilant for any sign of danger. When threatened, it flees at over 60 miles per hour.
column26: deDie Gehirne der beiden Köpfe kommunizieren ihre Gefühle über Telepathie.

column8: enWhen Doduo evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.
column9: enWatch out if Dodrio’s three heads are looking in three separate directions. It’s a sure sign that it is on its guard. Don’t go near this Pokémon if it’s being wary—it may decide to peck you.
column10: enIt collects data and plans three times as wisely, but it may think too much and fall into a state of immobility.
column11: enApparently, the heads aren’t the only parts of the body that DODRIO has three of. It has three sets of hearts and lungs as well, so it is capable of running long distances without rest.
column12: enIf one of the heads gets to eat, the others will be satisfied, too, and they will stop squabbling.
column13: enApparently, the heads aren’t the only parts of the body that Dodrio has three of. It has three sets of hearts and lungs as well, so it is capable of running long distances without rest.
column14: enUses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head stays awake.
column15: enOne of Doduo’s two heads splits to form a unique species. It runs close to 40 mph in prairies.
column16: deBei der Entwicklung zu Dodri hat sich einer von Dodus Köpfen in zwei geteilt. Es flitzt mit einer Geschwindigkeit von 60 km/h über Wiesen.
column17: enAn odd species that is rarely found. The three heads respectively represent joy, sadness, and anger.
column18: enUses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head is said to stay awake.
column19: deLässt ein Feind auch nur einen der drei Köpfe für nur eine Sekunde aus den Augen, wird er sofort gepickt.
column20: deDodri hat nicht nur drei Köpfe, auch andere Körperteile sind dreifach vorhanden. Es besitzt drei Herzen und drei Lungen, sodass es lange Strecken ohne Pause rennen kann.
column21: enAn enemy that takes its eyes off any of the three heads--even for a second--will get pecked severely.
column22: enWhen DODUO evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.
column23: deEin seltsames Exemplar, das nur selten gefunden wird. Die drei Köpfe repräsentieren Freude, Trauer und Wut.
column24: enWatch out if DODRIO’s three heads are looking in three separate directions. It’s a sure sign that it is on its guard. Don’t go near this POKéMON if it’s being wary - it may decide to peck you.
column25: enA peculiar POKéMON species with three heads. It vigorously races across grassy plains even in arid seasons with little rainfall.
column26: deDodris drei Köpfe blicken in drei verschiedene Richtungen. Es ist immer auf der Hut. Du solltest dich diesem Pokémon nicht nähern, wenn es bereits misstrauisch ist. Es könnte nach dir hacken.
column27: enIt collects data  and plans three times as wisely, but it may think too much and become immobilized.
column28: enOne of DODUO's 2 heads splits to form a unique species. It runs close to 40 MPH in prairies.

column8: deDieses Pokémon lebt auf Eisbergen. Es schwimmt im Eiswasser und bricht das Eis mithilfe seines Horns.
column9: enLoves freezing-cold conditions. Relishes swimming in a frigid climate of around 14 degrees Fahrenheit.
column10: deKalte Meere machen ihm dank seiner dicken Fettschicht nichts aus. Wärmere Gewässer setzen ihm hingegen sehr zu.
column11: enA Pokémon that lives on icebergs. It swims in the sea using the point on its head to break up ice.
column12: enIt has always been supposed that Seel live only in cold seas. Their having shown up in Alola is a mystery.
column13: enThe protruding horn on its head is very hard. It is used for bashing through thick icebergs.
column14: enSEEL hunt for prey in frigid, ice-covered seas. When it needs to breathe, it punches a hole through the ice with the sharply protruding section of its head.
column15: enSeel hunts for prey in the frigid sea underneath sheets of ice. When it needs to breathe, it punches a hole through the ice with the sharply protruding section of its head.
column16: enAlthough it can’t walk well on land, it is a graceful swimmer. It especially loves being in frigid seas.
column17: enLoves freezing cold conditions. Relishes swimming in a frigid climate of around 14F degrees.
column18: deUnter seinem hellblauen Pelz liegt eine dicke und feste Haut. Es ist bei frostigen -40 °C noch aktiv.
column19: deEs bevorzugt eiskalte Lebensräume und schwimmt mit Vorliebe in -10 şC kaltem Wasser.
column20: deBisher wurde vermutet, dass es ausschließlich in kalten Gewässern lebe. Warum es auch in Alola auftaucht, ist noch ein Rätsel.
column21: enThanks to its thick fat, cold seas don’t bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.
column22: enThe light blue fur that covers it keeps it protected against the cold. It loves iceberg-filled oceans.
column23: enCovered with light blue fur, its hide is thick and tough. It is active in bitter cold of minus 40 degrees Fahrenheit.
column24: enThe protruding horn on its head is very hard. It is used for bashing through thick ice.
column25: enCovered with light blue fur, its hide is thick and tough. It is active in bitter cold of -40 degrees Fahrenheit.
column26: enThe colder it gets, the better it feels. It joyfully swims around oceans so cold that they are filled with floating ice.
column27: deJurob jagt unter der eisigen Oberfläche des Eismeeres nach Beute. Zum Luftholen schlägt es mit dem vorstehenden Teil seines Kopfes ein Loch ins Eis.
column28: enIn daytime, it is often found asleep on the seabed in shallow waters. Its nostrils close while it swims.
column29: enAlthough it can't walk well on land, it is a graceful swimmer. It especially loves being in frigid seas.
column30: deJurob jagt unter der frostigen Oberfläche des Eismeeres nach Beute. Zum Luftholen schlägt es mit dem vorstehenden Teil seines Kopfes ein Loch ins Eis.
column31: enSEEL hunts for prey in the frigid sea underneath sheets of ice. When it needs to breathe, it punches a hole through the ice with the sharply protruding section of its head.

column8: deEs verspürt kaum Widerstand im Wasser. Je niedriger die Temperatur, desto verspielter verhält es sich.
column9: enIt swims through the ocean at a speed of eight knots, searching for Pokémon that will become its prey. It’s especially fond of Wishiwashi.
column10: deJugong liebt es, auf bitterkalten Eisschollen ein Nickerchen zu machen. Ein Seemann, der ein solches Pokémon eines Tages auf einem Eisberg schlafen sah, hielt es für eine Meerjungfrau.
column11: enIts streamlined body has low resistance, and it swims around cold oceans at a speed of eight knots.
column12: enIts entire body is a snowy-white. Unharmed by even intense cold, it swims powerfully in icy waters.
column13: enIts body is covered with a pure white fur. The colder the weather, the more active it becomes.
column14: enIt sunbathes on the beach after meals. The rise in its body temperature helps its digestion.
column15: enIt loves to snooze on bitterly cold ice. The sight of this POKéMON sleeping on a glacier was mistakenly thought to be a mermaid by a mariner long ago.
column16: deSein ganzer Körper ist schneeweiß. Da ihm Kälte nichts ausmacht, kann es selbst in Eiswasser kraftvoll schwimmen.
column17: deEs schwimmt mit bis zu acht Knoten im Meer und sucht nach Beute. Für Lusardin hat es eine besondere Vorliebe.
column18: enIts entire body is a snowy white. Unharmed by even intense cold, it swims powerfully in icy waters.
column19: enDewgong loves to snooze on bitterly cold ice. The sight of this Pokémon sleeping on a glacier was mistakenly thought to be a mermaid by a mariner long ago.
column20: enIt sleeps under shallow ocean waters during the day, then looks for food at night when it's cold.
column21: enIt stores thermal energy in the body. It swims at a steady eight knots even in intensely cold waters.
column22: deSein Körper ist mit reinem, weißem Fell überzogen. Je kälter es wird, desto aktiver wird dieses Pokémon.
column23: deJugong liebt es, auf bitterkalten Eisschollen ein Nickerchen zu machen. Ein Seemann, der einmal ein solches Pokémon schlafend auf einem Eisberg sah, hielt es irrtümlicherweise für eine Meerjungfrau.
column24: enIts streamlined body has little drag in water. The colder the temperature, the friskier it gets.
column25: enIt loves frigid seas with ice floes. It uses its long tail to change swimming direction quickly.
column26: enIn snow, the pure white coat covering its body obscures it from predators.
column27: enDEWGONG loves to snooze on bitterly cold ice. The sight of this POKéMON sleeping on a glacier was mistakenly thought to be a mermaid by a mariner long ago.
column28: deNach einer Mahlzeit genießt es ein Sonnenbad am Strand. Durch die erhöhte Körpertemperatur wird seine Verdauung unterstützt.
column29: enStores thermal energy in its body. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters.

column8: enIt was born from sludge on the ocean floor. In a sterile environment, the germs within its body can’t multiply, and it dies.
column9: enGRIMER emerged from the sludge that settled on a polluted seabed. This POKéMON loves anything filthy. It constantly leaks a horribly germ-infested fluid from all over its body.
column10: deDieses Pokémon lebt vorwiegend in schmutzigen Gebieten und ernährt sich von Industrieabfällen.
column11: deSleima ist aus erhärtetem Schlamm entstanden. Es riecht sehr penetrant und man sollte es nicht berühren. Wo es hintritt, wächst kein Gras mehr.
column12: enWherever GRIMER has passed, so many germs are left behind that no plants will ever grow again.
column13: deSchlamm, der Röntgenstrahlung vom Mond ausgesetzt war, wurde zu Sleima. Es isst am liebsten dreckige Dinge.
column14: deEs ernährt sich primär von Industrieabfällen. In letzter Zeit sieht man es viel seltener als früher.
column15: enAppears in filthy areas. It thrives by sucking up polluted sludge that is pumped out of factories.
column16: enThe wastewater coming from factories is clean these days, so Grimer have nothing to eat. They’re said to be on the verge of extinction.
column17: deSchlamm, der Röntgenstrahlung vom Mond ausgesetzt war, wurde zu Sleima. Sinkt die Zahl der Bakterien in seinem Körper, stirbt es.
column18: enWhen two of these POKéMON's bodies are combined together, new poisons are created.
column19: enMade of congealed sludge. It smells too putrid to touch. Even weeds won’t grow in its path.
column20: enBorn from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.
column21: deSleimas glitschiger, gummiartiger Körper passt durch jede noch so kleine Öffnung. Dieses Pokémon steigt in die Kanalisation hinab, um fauliges Abwasser zu trinken.
column22: deSleima ist aus dem Schlamm am Meeresgrund entstanden. An sterilen Orten kann es seine Bakterien nicht kultivieren und stirbt.
column23: enIts main source of food is the slimy industrial waste of factories. This Pokémon’s numbers have been decreasing in recent years.
column24: enAs it moves, it loses bits of its body, from which new GRIMER emerge. This worsens the stench around it.
column25: enAppears in filthy areas. Thrives by sucking up polluted sludge that is pumped out of factories.
column26: enBorn from polluted sludge in the sea, GRIMER’s favorite food is anything filthy. They feed on wastewater pumped out from factories.
column27: enGrimer’s sludgy and rubbery body can be forced through any opening, however small it may be. This Pokémon enters sewer pipes to drink filthy wastewater.
column28: enSludge exposed to X rays from the moon transformed into GRIMER. It loves feeding on filthy things.
column29: enAs it moves, it loses bits of its body from which new GRIMER emerge. This worsens the stench around it.
column30: enGRIMER’s sludgy and rubbery body can be forced through any opening, however small it may be. This POKéMON enters sewer pipes to drink filthy wastewater.
column31: deEs ernährt sich von Industrieabfällen, die durch umweltfreundliche Methoden nun aber knapp werden. Es ist daher vom Aussterben bedroht.
column32: enIt was born from sludge transformed by exposure to X-rays from the moon. When its internal load of germs decreases, it dies.
column33: enGrimer emerged from the sludge that settled on a polluted seabed. This Pokémon loves anything filthy. It constantly leaks a horribly germ-infested fluid from all over its body.
column34: enIt was born when sludge in a dirty stream was exposed to the moon’s X-rays. It appears among filth.
column35: enSludge exposed to X-rays from the moon transformed into Grimer. It loves feeding on filthy things.
column36: enMade of hardened sludge. It smells too putrid to touch. Even weeds won't grow in its path.
column37: deSleima ist aus dem Schlamm eines verseuchten Flussbetts entstanden. Dieses Pokémon liebt verschmutzte Dinge. Es scheidet ständig eine mit Bakterien verseuchte Flüssigkeit aus seinem ganzen Körper aus.

column8: enFrom Muk’s body seeps a foul fluid that gives off a nose-bendingly horrible stench. Just one drop of this Pokémon’s body fluid can turn a pool stagnant and rancid.
column9: enIt’s so stinky! Muk’s body contains toxic elements, and any plant will wilt when it passes by.
column10: deEs wird immer seltener gesehen, da zunehmend mehr Wert auf Umweltschutz gelegt wird. Man vermutet, dass es irgendwann ganz ausstirbt.
column11: enA toxic fluid seeps from its body. The fluid instantly kills plants and trees on contact.
column12: enIts body is made of a powerful poison. Touching it accidentally will cause a fever that requires bed rest.
column13: enIt is usually undetectable because it blends in with the ground. Touching it can cause terrible poisoning.
column14: deAufgrund von Futtermangel sieht man es immer seltener. Um es vom Aussterben zu bewahren, werden nun künstliche Schlammteiche angelegt.
column15: enThickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.
column16: enSmells so awful, it can cause fainting. Through degeneration, it lost its sense of smell.
column17: enAfter recent environmental improvements, this Pokémon is now hardly seen at all. People speculate that it may go extinct at some point.
column18: enSmells so awful, it can cause fainting. Through degeneration of its nose, it lost its sense of smell.
column19: enBecause they scatter germs everywhere, they’ve long been targeted for extermination, leading to a steep decline in their population.
column20: deDa es Krankheitskeime verbreitet, hat man lange versucht, es auszurotten. Heute sieht man es daher nur noch selten.
column21: enFrom MUK’s body seeps a foul fluid that gives off a nose-bendingly horrible stench. Just one drop of this POKéMON’s body fluid can turn a pool stagnant and rancid.
column22: deSleimok scheidet eine widerlich stinkende Substanz aus. Ein einziger Tropfen dieser Substanz reicht aus, um ein Gewässer zu verseuchen.
column23: enThe stench it gives off will make your nose scrunch up. Still, there are Muk fans who think that’s a good thing.
column24: deSein fürchterlicher Gestank kann zur Ohnmacht führen. Da seine Nase so verkümmert ist, kann es selbst nichts mehr riechen.
column25: deEs sondert einen widerlichen Gestank ab. In Fankreisen wird jedoch ausgerechnet diese Eigenschaft besonders geschätzt.
column26: enIt prefers warm and humid habitats. In the summertime, the toxic substances in its body intensify, making MUK reek like putrid kitchen garbage.
column27: enThey love to gather in smelly areas where sludge accumulates, making the stench around them worse.
column28: enThis POKéMON’s favorite food is anything that is repugnantly filthy. In dirty towns where people think nothing of throwing away litter on the streets, MUK are certain to gather.
column29: enAs it moves, a very strong poison leaks from it, making the ground there barren for three years.
column30: deSein Körper sondert eine giftige Substanz ab, die bei Kontakt Pflanzen und Bäume vernichtet.
column31: deSie treffen sich an stinkenden Orten, an denen der Schleim sich türmt. Dies merkt man am Gestank.
column32: deSleimoks Lieblingsspeise ist alles, was widerlich verschmutzt ist. In dreckigen Städten, in denen sich Menschen nichts dabei denken, wenn sie Müll auf die Straße werfen, lassen sich diese Pokémon mit Vorliebe in Scharen nieder.
column33: enThis Pokémon’s favorite food is anything that is repugnantly filthy. In dirty towns where people think nothing of throwing away litter on the streets, Muk are certain to gather.
column34: enTheir food sources have decreased, and their numbers have declined sharply. Sludge ponds are being built to prevent their extinction.

column8: enGrains of sand trapped in its shells mix with its body fluids to form beautiful pearls.
column9: enThis Pokémon’s tongue is always hanging out. It uses its tongue with great dexterity to dig up sand from the seabed in its search for food.
column10: deIhre Schalen sind härter als Diamanten. Vor langer Zeit sammelte man die Schalen von Muschas und machte Schilde daraus.
column11: deSeine Zunge lässt es selbst bei geschlossener Schale immer heraushängen. Zieht man fest daran, öffnet es vor Schreck seine Schale.
column12: enIt is encased in a shell that is harder than diamond. Inside, however, it is surprisingly tender.
column13: deSeine Schale ist hart genug, um allen Attacken standzuhalten. Wenn sie offen ist, kommt sein weiches Inneres zum Vorschein.
column14: enIt swims facing backward by opening and closing its two-piece shell. It is surprisingly fast.
column15: enIt swims backward by opening and closing its two shells. Its large tongue is always kept hanging out.
column16: enEven when its shell is closed, its tongue still hangs out. If you give its tongue a good yank, the shock will cause Shellder to open its shell.
column17: enAt night, this Pokémon uses its broad tongue to burrow a hole in the seafloor sand and then sleep in it. While it is sleeping, Shellder closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.
column18: deNachts benutzt dieses Pokémon seine breite Zunge, um ein Loch in den Meeresboden zu graben. Darin schläft es. Dabei schließt es seine Schale, aber die Zunge hängt heraus.
column19: deSeine Zunge hängt immer aus seiner Schale. Es sucht nach Beute, indem es mit ihr gezielt den Sand am Meeresboden aufwirbelt.
column20: enIts hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.
column21: enThe shell can withstand any attack. However, when it is open, the tender body is exposed.
column22: enAt night, this POKéMON uses its broad tongue to burrow a hole in the seafloor sand and then sleep in it. While it is sleeping, SHELLDER closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.
column23: enClamping on to an opponent reveals its vulnerable parts, so it uses this move only as a last resort.
column24: deNachts verwendet dieses Pokémon seine breite Zunge, um ein Loch in den Meeresboden zu graben. Darin schläft es. Dabei schließt es seine Schale, lässt aber seine Zunge heraushängen.
column25: deDer Sand, der sich in Muschas’ Schale sammelt, entwickelt sich zu Perlen. Da es diese als störend empfindet, spuckt es sie einfach aus.
column26: deEs schwimmt rückwärts, indem es seine Schale öffnet und schließt, und ist damit überraschend schnell.
column27: enThe sand that accumulates inside its shell eventually becomes a pearl. But the pearl gets in the way, so it spits it out and discards it.
column28: deDa es seine verwundbarste Stelle beim Klammern preisgibt, tut es dies nur in wirklichen Notfällen.
column29: deEine harte Schale schützt dieses Pokémon. Nur wenn es diese öffnet, wird es verwundbar.
column30: enThe hardness of its shell surpasses the hardness of a diamond. In days gone by, people used the shells to make shields.
column31: enAt night, it burrows a hole in the seafloor with its broad tongue to make a place to sleep. While asleep, it closes its shell, but leaves its tongue hanging out.

column8: enCLOYSTER that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.
column9: enCloyster is capable of swimming in the sea. It does so by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. This Pokémon shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.
column10: enExcavation of the tombs of ancient hunting tribes has turned up many spears tipped with spikes that had fallen off this Pokémon’s shell.
column11: deEs wird von einer Schale geschützt, die härter als Diamant ist. Dieses gefährliche Pokémon feuert die Stacheln an seiner Schale auf Gegner ab.
column12: enFor protection, it uses its harder-than-diamond shell. It also shoots spikes from the shell.
column13: enIt swims in the sea by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. The CLOYSTER shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.
column14: enOnce it slams its shell shut, it is impossible to open, even by those with superior strength.
column15: enWhen attacked, it launches its horns in quick volleys. Its innards have never been seen.
column16: enSlowpoke tails are its favorite food. It has even been known to come up on land to look for Slowpoke from time to time.
column17: enIt fights by keeping its shell tightly shut for protection and by shooting spikes to repel foes.
column18: deIst seine Schale erst geschlossen, ist es selbst mit viel Kraft nicht mehr möglich, diese zu öffnen.
column19: deAustos kann im Meerwasser schwimmen, indem es Wasser schluckt und wieder herausspritzt. Dieses Pokémon kann sich auch verteidigen, indem es spitze Stacheln mithilfe des Wasserdrucks verschießt.
column20: deAustos kann im Meerwasser schwimmen, indem es Wasser schluckt und mit Druck wieder herausspritzt. Auf dieselbe Weise verschießt dieses Pokémon scharfe Stacheln.
column21: deFlegmon-Ruten sind seine Leibspeise. Manchmal kriecht es sogar an Land, nur um nach Flegmon zu suchen.
column22: enCloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.
column23: deSein Panzer ist extrem hart. Es kann nicht zerschmettert werden, auch nicht durch eine Bombe. Der Panzer öffnet sich nur, wenn es angreift.
column24: enIts hard shell cannot be shattered—not even by a bomb. The contents of the shell remain unknown.
column25: deIn Gräbern alter Jägervölker werden oft Speere ausgegraben, welche aus abgebrochenen Stacheln seiner Schale gefertigt wurden.
column26: deSeine Schale ist extrem hart. Schäden an der Schale schwellen zunächst an und entwickeln sich allmählich zu großen, scharfen Stacheln.
column27: enIf areas of Cloyster’s very hard shell get damaged, those areas swell, gradually growing into large sharp spikes.
column28: enEven a missile can't break the spikes it uses to stab opponents. They're even harder than its shell.
column29: deSeine Schale ist extrem hart und kann nicht einmal durch eine Bombe zerschmettert werden. Sie öffnet sich nur, wenn es angreift.
column30: enCLOYSTER is capable of swimming in the sea. It does so by swallowing water, then jetting it out toward the rear. This POKéMON shoots spikes from its shell using the same system.
column31: enIts shell is extremely hard. It cannot be shattered, even with a bomb. The shell opens only when it is attacking.
column32: deSeine Schale ist so hart, dass nicht einmal eine Bombe sie zerschmettern kann. Bis heute weiß niemand, wie das Innere seiner Schale aussieht.
column33: enFor protection, it uses its harder-than-diamonds shell. It also shoots spikes from the shell.
column34: deAustos, die im Meer mit starker Strömung leben, werden groß und entwickeln scharfe Stacheln.

column8: deNebulak hat einen gasförmigen Körper. Wenn es starkem Wind ausgesetzt wird, kann es davongeweht werden. Scharen dieser Pokémon sammeln sich unter Dachrinnen, um sich vor dem gefährlichen Wind zu schützen.
column9: deEs heißt, dieses Pokémon sei aus einem tiefen Groll entstanden, der Gasen innewohnte, die auf Friedhöfen ausgeströmt waren.
column10: enSaid to appear in decrepit, deserted buildings. It has no real shape as it appears to be made of a gas.
column11: deEs besteht zu 95 % aus Giftgas. Die restlichen 5 % bestehen aus Seelen von Opfern giftiger Gase.
column12: enIts body is made of gas. Despite lacking substance, it can envelop an opponent of any size and cause suffocation.
column13: deEs hat einen gasförmigen Körper. Es kann jeden Gegner mit Giftgas einnebeln und dadurch ersticken.
column14: enA being that exists as a thin gas. It can topple an Indian elephant by enveloping the prey in two seconds.
column15: enGaseous and completely impalpable. Also highly dangerous— inhaling part of its poisonous body will cause one to faint instantly.
column16: enAlthough Gastly is barely visible, when it’s near, a faint sweet smell lingers.
column17: deDass sich ein Nebulak in der Nähe aufhält, erkennt man hauptsächlich an einem süßlichen Duft. Sehen kann man es fast gar nicht.
column18: deFlackert in einem verlassenen alten Gebäude plötzlich ein rätselhaftes Licht auf, hält sich dort ein Nebulak versteckt.
column19: enSaid to appear in decrepit, deserted buildings. It has no real shape, as it appears to be made of a gas.
column20: enIt wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.
column21: enShould a strange light be seen flickering in an abandoned building, Gastly is lurking there.
column22: deEs ist aus Gasen entstanden. Wird jemand in seinen gasförmigen, giftigen Körper gehüllt, so fällt er in Ohnmacht.
column23: enA Pokémon born from poison gases. It defeats even the largest foes by enveloping them in gas.
column24: enAlmost invisible, this gaseous POKéMON cloaks the target and puts it to sleep without notice.
column25: enWhen exposed to a strong wind, a GASTLY’s gaseous body quickly dwindles away. They cluster under the eaves of houses to escape the ravages of wind.
column26: enPoisonous gas comprises 95% of its body. It’s said that the remaining 5% is made up of the souls of those who died from the gas.
column27: deEingehüllt in seinen gasförmigen, Gift enthaltenden Körper würde jeder in Ohnmacht fallen.
column28: enBorn from gases, anyone would faint if engulfed by its gaseous body, which contains poison.
column29: enWith its gas-like body, it can sneak into any place it desires. However, it can be blown away by wind.
column30: enGASTLY is largely composed of gaseous matter. When exposed to a strong wind, the gaseous body quickly dwindles away. Groups of this POKéMON cluster under the eaves of houses to escape the ravages of wind.
column31: enIts thin body is made of gas. It can envelop an opponent of any size and cause suffocation.
column32: enIt’s said that gas emanating from a graveyard was possessed by the grievances of the deceased and thus became a Pokémon.
column33: enGastly is largely composed of gaseous matter. When exposed to a strong wind, the gaseous body quickly dwindles away. Groups of this Pokémon cluster under the eaves of houses to escape the ravages of wind.
column34: deAufgrund seines an Gas erinnernden Körpers kommt es an jeden Ort. Es kann jedoch vom Wind davongeweht werden.
column35: enThis Pokémon’s body is 95% made up of gases, which are blown away by strong gusts of wind.
column36: deEs heißt, es erscheine in alten, verlassenen Gebäuden. Sein Körper hat keine feste Form und erinnert an Gas.

column8: enBy licking, it saps the victim’s life. It causes shaking that won’t stop until the victim’s demise.
column9: enHAUNTER is a dangerous POKéMON. If one beckons you while floating in darkness, you must never approach it. This POKéMON will try to lick you with its tongue and steal your life away.
column10: deAlpollo ist ein gefährliches Pokémon. Wenn es dir ein Zeichen gibt, während es durch die Dunkelheit schwebt, darfst du dich ihm nicht nähern. Dieses Pokémon wird versuchen, an dir zu lecken und dein Leben zu stehlen.
column11: enIn total darkness, where nothing is visible, HAUNTER lurks, silently stalking its next victim.
column12: deFalls du im Dunkeln das Gefühl hast, beobachtet zu werden, und niemand ist zu sehen, ist es bestimmt Alpollo.
column13: enIt likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.
column14: enIt can slip through any obstacle. It lurks inside walls to keep an eye on its foes.
column15: enIt strikes at humans from total darkness. Those licked by its cold tongue grow weaker with each passing day until they die.
column16: enIf a HAUNTER beckons you while it is floating in darkness, don’t approach it. This POKéMON will try to lick you with its tongue and steal your life away.
column17: enBy licking, it saps the victim's life. It causes shaking that won't stop until the victim's demise.
column18: deAlpollo ist ein gefährliches Pokémon. Wenn es dich zu sich lockt, während es durch die Dunkelheit schwebt, darfst du ihm keinesfalls zu nahe kommen. Dieses Pokémon wird versuchen, an dir zu lecken und dein Leben zu stehlen.
column19: deSeine Zunge besteht aus Gas. Schleckt es seine Opfer damit ab, zittern diese unentwegt, bis sie schließlich entschlafen.
column20: enIt fears the light and revels in the dark. It may be on the verge of extinction in cities that stay brightly lit at night.
column21: enIt licks with its gaseous tongue to steal the victim’s life force. It lurks in darkness for prey.
column22: enIt’s dangerous to go outside alone on nights when you’re feeling sad. Haunter will catch you, and you won’t be able to go back home.
column23: enIf you get the feeling of being watched in darkness when nobody is around, Haunter is there.
column24: enBecause of its ability to slip through block walls, it is said to be from another dimension.
column25: deIn mondlosen Nächten sucht es nach Opfern, die es mit einem Fluch belegen kann. Zu Hause ist man daher am sichersten aufgehoben.
column26: enHaunter is a dangerous Pokémon. If one beckons you while floating in darkness, you must never approach it. This Pokémon will try to lick you with its tongue and steal your life away.
column27: deEs ist lichtscheu und bevorzugt die Dunkelheit. Aufgrund der hellen Lichter der Großstädte ist es dort vom Aussterben bedroht.
column28: deEs lauert Menschen im Dunkeln auf und leckt sie mit seiner kalten Zunge. Danach werden sie von Tag zu Tag schwächer, bis sie dahinscheiden.
column29: enIts tongue is made of gas. If licked, its victim starts shaking constantly until death eventually comes.
column30: enIf you get the feeling of being watched in darkness when nobody is around, HAUNTER is there.
column31: enOn moonless nights, Haunter searches for someone to curse, so it’s best not to go out walking around.
column32: deEs beleckt seine Opfer mit seiner Zunge und stiehlt ihnen so Lebenskraft. Es lauert im Dunkeln auf sie.
column33: deFalls du im Dunkeln das Gefühl hast, beobachtet zu werden und niemand ist zu sehen, ist es bestimmt Alpollo.
column34: enIt hides in the dark, planning to take the life of the next living thing that wanders close by.
column35: deVon Kummer Geplagte sollten nachts nicht allein herumspazieren. Ansonsten lauert ihnen Alpollo auf und lässt sie nie mehr nach Hause zurück.
column36: enThis frightful, malevolent spirit can glide through walls, appearing wherever it likes. According to rumor, victims of a Haunter's lick will wither to death day by day.
column37: deEs saugt seinen Opfern das Leben aus, indem es sie abschleckt. Man sagt, sie würden daraufhin unentwegt zittern, bis sie schließlich entschlafen.

column8: enOn the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be GENGAR’s doing.
column9: enYou can hear tales told all over the world about how Gengar will pay a visit to children who are naughty.
column10: deNachts kann es passieren, dass dich dein Schatten im Licht einer Straßenlaterne plötzlich überholt. Dann ist es ein Gengar, das an dir vorbeiläuft und sich als dein Schatten ausgibt.
column11: enTo steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.
column12: deLäuft es einem plötzlich eiskalt den Rücken hinunter, ist ein Gengar in der Nähe. Womöglich belegt es einen dann mit einem Fluch.
column13: enThe leer that floats in darkness belongs to a Gengar delighting in casting curses on people.
column14: enPossesses potential victims' shadows in an effort to steal away the victims' lives. If your shadow begins to laugh, you must take hold of a protective charm posthaste!
column15: enLurking in the shadowy corners of rooms, it awaits chances to steal its prey’s life force.
column16: enA GENGAR is close by if you feel a sudden chill. It may be trying to lay a curse on you.
column17: enTo steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey's shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.
column18: enIt apparently wishes for a traveling companion. Since it was once human itself, it tries to create one by taking the lives of other humans.
column19: enDeep in the night, your shadow cast by a streetlight may suddenly overtake you. It is actually a GENGAR running past you, pretending to be your shadow.
column20: deLäuft es einem plötzlich eiskalt den Rücken hinunter, wurde man von Gengar als nächstes Opfer auserkoren. Widerstand ist zwecklos.
column21: deEs versteckt sich im Schatten der Menschen und absorbiert deren Wärme, sodass die Menschen frieren.
column22: enShould you feel yourself attacked by a sudden chill, it is evidence of an approaching Gengar. There is no escaping it. Give up.
column23: enThe leer that floats in darkness belongs to a GENGAR delighting in casting curses on people.
column24: deWenn Schatten bei Vollmond plötzlich anfangen, sich zu bewegen und zu lachen, steckt zweifellos Gengar dahinter.
column25: enHiding in people's shadows at night, it absorbs their heat. The chill it causes makes the victims shake.
column26: deMan sagt, es sei aus Dunkelheit entstanden, um denjenigen, die sich in den Bergen verirrt haben, das Leben zu rauben.
column27: enIt is said to emerge from darkness to steal the lives of those who become lost in mountains.
column28: enHiding in people’s shadows at night, it absorbs their heat. The chill it causes makes the victims shake.
column29: enEven your home isn’t safe. Gengar will lurk in whatever dark corner of a room it can find and wait for its chance to catch its prey.
column30: deSelbst zu Hause ist man vor Gengar nicht sicher. In den kleinsten Schatten der engsten Winkel lauert es auf Beute.
column31: enOn the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be Gengar’s doing.
column32: enIt hides in shadows. It is said that if Gengar is hiding, it cools the area by nearly 10 degrees Fahrenheit.
column33: deGengar raubt Menschen das Leben, um einen Begleiter an seiner Seite zu haben, der wie es selbst einmal ein Mensch war.
column34: deEs versteckt sich im Schatten. Man sagt, wenn sich ein Gengar versteckt, kühlt es sich um 5 °C ab.
column35: enIt steals heat from its surroundings. If you feel a sudden chill, it is certain that a GENGAR appeared.
column36: enIt hides in shadows. It is said that if GENGAR is hiding, it cools the area by nearly 10 degrees F.
column37: enA Gengar is close by if you feel a sudden chill. It may be trying to lay a curse on you.
column38: enSometimes, on a dark night, your shadow thrown by a streetlight will suddenly and startlingly overtake you. It is actually a GENGAR running past you, pretending to be your shadow.
column39: enUnder a full moon, this POKéMON likes to mimic the shadows of people and laugh at their fright.
column40: deUnartige Kinder holt sich Gengar. So heißt es weltweit in mündlichen Überlieferungen.
column41: enSometimes, on a dark night, your shadow thrown by a streetlight will suddenly and startlingly overtake you. It is actually a Gengar running past you, pretending to be your shadow.

column33: deGengar raubt Menschen das Leben, um einen Begleiter an seiner Seite zu haben, der wie es selbst einmal ein Mensch war.
column34: deEs versteckt sich im Schatten. Man sagt, wenn sich ein Gengar versteckt, kühlt es sich um 5 °C ab.
column35: enIt steals heat from its surroundings. If you feel a sudden chill, it is certain that a GENGAR appeared.
column36: enIt hides in shadows. It is said that if GENGAR is hiding, it cools the area by nearly 10 degrees F.
column37: enA Gengar is close by if you feel a sudden chill. It may be trying to lay a curse on you.
column38: enSometimes, on a dark night, your shadow thrown by a streetlight will suddenly and startlingly overtake you. It is actually a GENGAR running past you, pretending to be your shadow.
column39: enUnder a full moon, this POKéMON likes to mimic the shadows of people and laugh at their fright.
column40: deUnartige Kinder holt sich Gengar. So heißt es weltweit in mündlichen Überlieferungen.
column41: enSometimes, on a dark night, your shadow thrown by a streetlight will suddenly and startlingly overtake you. It is actually a Gengar running past you, pretending to be your shadow.

column8: enOn the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be GENGAR’s doing.
column9: enYou can hear tales told all over the world about how Gengar will pay a visit to children who are naughty.
column10: deNachts kann es passieren, dass dich dein Schatten im Licht einer Straßenlaterne plötzlich überholt. Dann ist es ein Gengar, das an dir vorbeiläuft und sich als dein Schatten ausgibt.
column11: enTo steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.
column12: deLäuft es einem plötzlich eiskalt den Rücken hinunter, ist ein Gengar in der Nähe. Womöglich belegt es einen dann mit einem Fluch.
column13: enThe leer that floats in darkness belongs to a Gengar delighting in casting curses on people.
column14: enPossesses potential victims' shadows in an effort to steal away the victims' lives. If your shadow begins to laugh, you must take hold of a protective charm posthaste!
column15: enLurking in the shadowy corners of rooms, it awaits chances to steal its prey’s life force.
column16: enA GENGAR is close by if you feel a sudden chill. It may be trying to lay a curse on you.
column17: enTo steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey's shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.
column18: enIt apparently wishes for a traveling companion. Since it was once human itself, it tries to create one by taking the lives of other humans.
column19: enDeep in the night, your shadow cast by a streetlight may suddenly overtake you. It is actually a GENGAR running past you, pretending to be your shadow.
column20: deLäuft es einem plötzlich eiskalt den Rücken hinunter, wurde man von Gengar als nächstes Opfer auserkoren. Widerstand ist zwecklos.
column21: deEs versteckt sich im Schatten der Menschen und absorbiert deren Wärme, sodass die Menschen frieren.
column22: enShould you feel yourself attacked by a sudden chill, it is evidence of an approaching Gengar. There is no escaping it. Give up.
column23: enThe leer that floats in darkness belongs to a GENGAR delighting in casting curses on people.
column24: deWenn Schatten bei Vollmond plötzlich anfangen, sich zu bewegen und zu lachen, steckt zweifellos Gengar dahinter.
column25: enHiding in people's shadows at night, it absorbs their heat. The chill it causes makes the victims shake.
column26: deMan sagt, es sei aus Dunkelheit entstanden, um denjenigen, die sich in den Bergen verirrt haben, das Leben zu rauben.
column27: enIt is said to emerge from darkness to steal the lives of those who become lost in mountains.
column28: enHiding in people’s shadows at night, it absorbs their heat. The chill it causes makes the victims shake.
column29: enEven your home isn’t safe. Gengar will lurk in whatever dark corner of a room it can find and wait for its chance to catch its prey.
column30: deSelbst zu Hause ist man vor Gengar nicht sicher. In den kleinsten Schatten der engsten Winkel lauert es auf Beute.
column31: enOn the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be Gengar’s doing.

column8: enBurrows at high speed in search of food. The tunnels it leaves are used as homes by DIGLETTs.
column9: deEs lebt gewöhnlich unter der Erde. Während es sich mit 80 km/h durchs Erdreich bohrt, sucht es nach Nahrung.
column10: deOnix hat einen Magneten in seinem Gehirn. Er dient ihm als Kompass, sodass es beim Graben von Tunneln nicht die Orientierung verliert. Wenn es älter wird, wird sein Körper immer runder und glatter.
column11: enIt burrows through the ground at a speed of 50 mph while feeding on large boulders.
column12: enBurrows at high speed in search of food. The tunnels it leaves are used as homes by Diglett.
column13: deEs bohrt sich mit 80 km/h durch das Erdreich, indem es seinen massiven, rauen Körper dreht und windet.
column14: enONIX has a magnet in its brain. It acts as a compass so that this POKéMON does not lose direction while it is tunneling. As it grows older, its body becomes increasingly rounder and smoother.
column15: enIt twists and squirms through the ground. The thunderous roar of its tunneling echoes a long way.
column16: enOpening its large mouth, it ingests massive amounts of soil and creates long tunnels.
column17: enThis chain of immense stones resembles a giant serpent. Tremors shake the earth above as it burrows deep beneath the ground, feeding on boulders as it goes.
column18: enIt usually lives underground. It searches for food while boring its way through the ground at 50 miles per hour.
column19: deWenn es sich durch die Erde gräbt, nimmt es viele harte Gegenstände auf, die seinen Körper erhärten.
column20: deDieses Pokémon gräbt auf seiner Suche nach Futter lange Tunnel, in denen sich später Digda einnisten.
column21: enThere is a magnet in its brain that prevents an ONIX from losing direction while tunneling. As it grows older, its body becomes steadily rounder and smoother.
column22: enIt burrows through the ground at a speed of 50 mph while feeding on large boulders.
column23: enOnix has a magnet in its brain. It acts as a compass so that this Pokémon does not lose direction while it is tunneling. As it grows older, its body becomes increasingly rounder and smoother.
column24: deEs gräbt auf seiner Suche nach Futter mit irrsinniger Geschwindigkeit lange Tunnel, in denen sich später Digda einnisten.
column25: enAs it grows, the stone portions of its body harden to become similar to black-colored diamonds.
column26: enWhen it travels underground, it causes rumbling and tremors. It can move at 50 mph.
column27: enAs it digs through the ground, it absorbs many hard objects. This is what makes its body so solid.
column28: enIt squirms through the ground using its long and rugged body. It always eats while burrowing.
column29: enIt rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.
column30: enAs it grows, the stone portions of its body harden to become similar to a diamond, but colored black.

column8: deEs ernährt sich von Träumen. Man geht davon aus, dass es von demselben Vorfahren wie Somniam und Somnivora abstammt.
column9: enIf you sleep by it all the time, it will sometimes show you dreams it had eaten in the past.
column10: enPuts enemies to sleep then eats their dreams. Occasionally gets sick from eating bad dreams.
column11: enIf your nose becomes itchy while you are sleeping, it’s a sure sign that one of these POKéMON is standing above your pillow and trying to eat your dream through your nostrils.
column12: enIt finds really fun dreams tasty. When it makes friends with people, it may show them the most delicious dreams it’s ever eaten.
column13: enIt puts its prey to sleep and devours their dreams. It seems that bad dreams taste sour, so Drowzee doesn’t particularly like eating them.
column14: deLustige Träume schmecken ihm am besten. Ist man nett zu ihm, zeigt es einem ab und an Träume, die ihm besonders gemundet haben.
column15: deTraumato versetzt Gegner in den Schlaf und frisst deren Träume. Von Alpträumen wird ihm aber übel.
column16: deWenn dir im Schlaf die Nase juckt, ist das ein sicheres Zeichen dafür, dass eines dieser Pokémon über deinem Kissen schwebt, um deinen Traum durch deine Nasenlöcher hindurch zu verspeisen.
column17: enWhen it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.
column18: enIf you think that you had a good dream but you can’t remember it, a Drowzee has probably eaten it.
column19: enA descendent of the legendary animal baku, which is said to eat dreams. It is skilled at hypnotism.
column20: deHast du gut geträumt und kannst dich nicht erinnern, hat ein Traumato deinen Traum gefressen.
column21: enIf you think that you had a good dream but you can’t remember it, a DROWZEE has probably eaten it.
column22: enIf you think that you had a good dream, but you can't remember it, a DROWZEE has probably eaten it.
column23: enIt remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children’s are much tastier.
column24: enIt remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children's are much tastier.
column25: deWenn dir im Schlaf die Nase juckt, ist das ein sicheres Zeichen dafür, dass eines dieser Pokémon auf deinem Kissen sitzt, um deinen Traum durch deine Nasenlöcher hindurch zu verspeisen.
column26: deWenn man immer in der Nähe eines Traumato schläft, zeigt es einem nachts manchmal Träume, die es vor langer Zeit verspeist hat.
column27: enIt can be spotted near recreational facilities, intending to eat the pleasant dreams of children who enjoyed themselves there that day.
column28: deTraumato hat es auf die lustigen Träume von Kindern abgesehen. Man sieht es daher oft in der Nähe von Freizeiteinrichtungen.
column29: enIf you sleep by it all the time, it will sometimes show you dreams it has eaten in the past.
column30: enIf your nose becomes itchy while you are sleeping, it’s a sure sign that a DROWZEE is standing above your pillow and trying to eat your dream through your nostrils.
column31: enPuts enemies to sleep, then eats their dreams. Occasionally gets sick from eating only bad dreams.
column32: enIf your nose becomes itchy while you are sleeping, it’s a sure sign that one of these Pokémon is standing above your pillow and trying to eat your dream through your nostrils.
column33: deTraumato versetzt seine Beute in den Schlaf und frisst ihre Träume. Alpträume mag es aufgrund ihres säuerlichen Geschmacks nicht so gern.
column34: enIt can tell what people are dreaming by sniffing with its big nose. It loves fun dreams.
column35: enA Pokémon that nourishes itself by eating dreams, it is thought to share common ancestry with Munna and Musharna.

column8: deLäuft man einem Hypno über den Weg, sollte man wegsehen, da es andere mit dem Pendel in seiner Hand hypnotisieren kann.
column9: enThe longer it swings its pendulum, the longer the effects of its hypnosis last.
column10: deDieses Pokémon ist äußerst gefährlich. Für Menschen, die an Schlaflosigkeit leiden, ist es aber ein wahrer Retter in der Not.
column11: enWhen it is very hungry, it puts humans it meets to sleep, then it feasts on their dreams.
column12: enWhen it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of PSI moves such as HYPNOSIS and CONFUSION.
column13: enHYPNO holds a pendulum in its hand. The arcing movement and glitter of the pendulum lull the foe into a deep state of hypnosis. While this POKéMON searches for prey, it polishes the pendulum.
column14: enAlways holding a pendulum that it rocks at a steady rhythm, it causes drowsiness in anyone nearby.
column15: deEs trägt ein Pendel. Man berichtet von einem Vorfall, bei dem es ein Kind mitnahm, das es zuvor hypnotisiert hatte.
column16: enHypno holds a pendulum in its hand. The arcing movement and glitter of the pendulum lull the foe into a deep state of hypnosis. While this Pokémon searches for prey, it polishes the pendulum.
column17: enSeeing its swinging pendulum can induce sleep in three seconds, even in someone who just woke up.
column18: deEin Blick auf das Pendel versetzt einen in drei Sekunden in Schlaf, selbst wenn man gar nicht müde ist.
column19: enAs a matter of course, it makes anyone it meets fall asleep and has a taste of their dreams. Anyone having a good dream, it carries off.
column20: enIn Alola, Komala is Hypno’s main target. It rarely harms people.
column21: deManchmal unterstützt es das medizinische Personal in Krankenhäusern, wenn Patienten an Schlaflosigkeit leiden.
column22: enIt carries a pendulum-like device. There once was an incident in which it took away a child it hypnotized.
column23: enIt carries a pendulum-like device. There once was an incident in which it took  away a child it hypnotized.
column24: enAvoid eye contact if you come across one. It will try to put you to sleep by using its pendulum.
column25: enWhile it is an extremely dangerous Pokémon, people who are in need of a good, sound sleep call it their savior.
column26: enThere are some Hypno that assist doctors with patients who can’t sleep at night in hospitals.
column27: enThe arcing movement and glitter of the pendulum in a HYPNO’s hand lull the foe into deep hypnosis. While searching for prey, it polishes the pendulum.
column28: deIn Alola hat es Hypno hauptsächlich auf Koalelu abgesehen. Menschen schadet es kaum.
column29: deHypno hält ein Pendel in der Hand. Das Schwingen und Glitzern des Pendels versetzt seine Feinde in eine tiefe Hypnose. Während dieses Pokémon auf der Suche nach Beute ist, poliert es sein Pendel.
column30: enAlways holding a pendulum that it swings at a steady rhythm, it causes drowsiness in anyone nearby.
column31: deEs versetzt jeden, dem es begegnet, sofort in Schlaf und kostet seine Träume. Schmeckt ihm ein Traum, nimmt es die Person mit.

column8: deKrabby lebt an Stränden, in Löchern im Sand. An Sandstränden, an denen es wenig Nahrung findet, sieht man dieses Pokémon mit Artgenossen um sein Revier streiten.
column9: enThe pincers break off easily. If it loses a pincer, it somehow becomes incapable of walking sideways.
column10: enKRABBY live on beaches, burrowed inside holes dug into the sand. On sandy beaches with little in the way of food, these POKéMON can be seen squabbling with each other over territory.
column11: deEs kommt vor allem nahe des Meeres vor. Die großen Scheren wachsen nach, wenn sie vom Körper abgetrennt wurden.
column12: enKRABBY live in holes dug into beaches. On sandy shores with little in the way of food, they can be seen squabbling with each other over territory.
column13: enIf it senses danger approaching, it cloaks itself with bubbles from its mouth so it will look bigger.
column14: deEs kommt vor allem nahe dem Meer vor. Die großen Scheren wachsen nach, wenn sie vom Körper abgetrennt wurden.
column15: enIts pincers are superb weapons. They sometimes break off during battle, but they grow back fast.
column16: deKrabby lebt an Stränden, in Löchern im Sand. An Sandstränden, an denen es wenig Nahrung findet, sieht man dieses Pokémon mit Artgenossen streiten.
column17: enIt can be found near the sea. The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.
column18: enIts pincers are not only powerful weapons, they are used for balance when walking sideways.
column19: enIt lives in burrows dug on sandy beaches. Its pincers fully grow back if they are broken in battle.
column20: enKrabby live on beaches, burrowed inside holes dug into the sand. On sandy beaches with little in the way of food, these Pokémon can be seen squabbling with each other over territory.
column21: deDie Scheren dieses Pokémon bilden perfekte Waffen. Im Kampf brechen sie zwar manchmal ab, wachsen aber schnell wieder nach.
column22: enIf it is unable to find food, it will absorb nutrients by swallowing a mouthful of sand.
column23: deWittert es Gefahr, hüllt es sich in Blasen aus seinem Maul, um größer zu erscheinen.

column9: deSeine Riesenschere ist sehr stark, aber wenn es nicht kämpft, kommt sie ihm häufig in die Quere.

column13: deSeine Scheren werden sehr groß. Hebt es sie zu schnell, verliert es die Balance und strauchelt.
