column8: enIt was discovered when Poké Balls were introduced. It is said that there is some connection.
column9: enDuring the study of this POKéMON, it was discovered that its components are not found in nature.
column10: enIt was discovered when POKé BALLS were introduced. It is said that there is some connection.
column11: deEs wurde entdeckt, als man Pokébälle einführte. Es scheint, als gäbe es da einen Zusammenhang.
column12: enAn enigmatic Pokémon that happens to bear a resemblance to a Poké Ball. When excited, it discharges the electric current it has stored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.
column13: enUsually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a POKé BALL, it has zapped many people.
column14: enIt bears an uncanny and unexplained resemblance to a POKé BALL. Because it explodes at the slightest shock, even veteran TRAINERS treat it with caution.
column15: deMan sagt, es tarne sich als Pokéball, um sich zu schützen. Allerdings zerstört es sich schon bei der kleinsten äußeren Einwirkung selbst.
column16: enA life-form whose identity is unknown. It is said to SCREECH or suddenly SELFDESTRUCT.
column17: enVOLTORB is extremely sensitive - it explodes at the slightest of shocks. It is rumored that it was first created when a POKé BALL was exposed to a powerful pulse of energy.
column18: enIt is said to camouflage itself as a Poké Ball. It will self-destruct with very little stimulus.
column19: enIt is said to camouflage itself as a POKé BALL. It will self-destruct with very little stimulus.
column20: deVoltobal wurde zuerst bei einer Firma beobachtet, die Pokébälle herstellt. Der Zusammenhang zwischen dieser Beobachtung und der Tatsache, dass dieses Pokémon wie ein Pokéball aussieht, ist noch ungeklärt.
column21: enUsually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.
column22: enIt rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.
column23: enIt looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on touch.
column24: deVoltobal ist äußerst empfindlich, es explodiert schon bei der leichtesten Erschütterung. Man munkelt, es sei entstanden, als ein Pokéball einen starken elektrischen Schlag bekommen hat.
column25: enVoltorb was first sighted at a company that manufactures Poké Balls. The link between that sighting and the fact that this Pokémon looks very similar to a Poké Ball remains a mystery.
column26: deDieses Pokémon wird oftmals mit einem Pokéball verwechselt. Es lebt vorwiegend in Kraftwerken.
column27: enIt looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on contact.
column28: enUsually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a POKé BALL, they have zapped many people.
column29: enVOLTORB was first sighted at a company that manufactures POKé BALLS. The link between that sighting and the fact that this POKéMON looks very similar to a POKé BALL remains a mystery.
column30: enVoltorb is extremely sensitive—it explodes at the slightest of shocks. It is rumored that it was first created when a Poké Ball was exposed to a powerful pulse of energy.

column8: deEs speichert elektrische Energie in seinem Körper. Schon die geringste Erschütterung lässt es explodieren.
column9: enThe more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.
column10: deEs explodiert schon bei kleinsten Reizen. Sein Spitzname „Die Bombenkugel“ spiegelt die Furcht der Menschen wider.
column11: enOne of ELECTRODE’s characteristics is its attraction to electricity. It is a problematical POKéMON that congregates mostly at electrical power plants to feed on electricity that has just been generated.
column12: deLektrobal ernährt sich von Elektrizität aus der Atmosphäre. An Tagen, an denen es blitzt, explodiert dieses Pokémon dauernd, da es zu viel Elektrizität zu sich nimmt.
column13: deEs speichert eine riesige Menge Elektrizität in seinem Körper. Es explodiert beim kleinsten Ruck.
column14: deTypisch für Lektrobal ist seine große Faszination für Elektrizität. Es ist ein problematisches Pokémon, das sich meistens in Kraftwerken niederlässt, wo es sich von frisch erzeugtem Strom ernähren kann.
column15: enIt explodes in response to even minor stimuli. It is feared, with the nickname of “The Bomb Ball.”
column16: enIt stores electric energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.
column17: enOne of Electrode’s characteristics is its attraction to electricity. It is a problematical Pokémon that congregates mostly at electrical power plants to feed on electricity that has just been generated.
column18: enIt is dangerous. If it has too much electricity and has nothing to do, it amuses itself by exploding.
column19: enIt is known to drift on winds if it is bloated to bursting with stored electricity.
column20: enELECTRODE eats electricity in the atmosphere. On days when lightning strikes, you can see this POKéMON exploding all over the place from eating too much electricity.
column21: enElectrode eats electricity in the atmosphere. On days when lightning strikes, you can see this Pokémon exploding all over the place from eating too much electricity.
column22: enThe tissue on the surface of its body is curiously similar in composition to an Apricorn. When irritated, this Pokémon lets loose an electric current equal to 20 lightning bolts.
column23: enThey appear in great numbers at electric power plants. Because they feed on electricity, they cause massive and chaotic blackouts in nearby cities.
column24: enIt stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.
column25: enStores electrical energy inside its body. Even the slightest shock could trigger a huge explosion.

column8: deDieses Pokémon besteht aus sechs Eiern, die ein engmaschiges Netz bilden. Die sechs Eier ziehen sich gegenseitig an und drehen sich. Wenn die Eier Risse bekommen, steht Owei kurz vor seiner Entwicklung.
column9: enAlthough they are the same size as other Exeggcute, the ones produced in Alola are quite heavy. Their shells are packed full.
column10: enSix of them form a single Pokémon. Should one of the six be lost, the next morning there will once more be six.
column11: deDie sechs Eier kommunizieren telepathisch miteinander. Sie tragen das Erbgut von Pflanzen sowie Pokémon eines bestimmten Typs in sich.
column12: enThese Pokémon get nervous when they’re not in a group of six. The minute even one member of the group goes missing, Exeggcute become cowardly.
column13: enThis Pokémon consists of six eggs that form a closely knit cluster. The six eggs attract each other and spin around. When cracks increasingly appear on the eggs, Exeggcute is close to evolution.
column14: enIt is often mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.
column15: deOwei in Alola sind trotz gleicher Größe schwerer als ihre Artgenossen in anderen Regionen, da ihr Inneres eine besonders hohe Dichte hat.
column16: enUsing telepathy only they can employ, they always form a cluster of six EXEGGCUTE.
column17: enIts six eggs use telepathy to communicate among themselves. It is believed to carry plant genes and the genes of other species.
column18: deEs muss immer aus sechs Mitgliedern bestehen, sonst herrscht Unruhe. Fehlt auch nur eines, steigt bei den anderen die Bereitschaft zur Flucht.
column19: enTheir shells are very durable. Even if they crack, they can survive without spilling their contents.
column20: enIt consists of six eggs that care for each other. The eggs attract each other and spin around. When cracks increasingly appear, it is close to evolution.
column21: deOweis kleine Köpfe ziehen sich gegenseitig an und drehen sich. Es braucht sechs von ihnen, um die Balance zu halten.
column22: deOwei mag zwar Eiern ähneln, ist aber ein echtes Pokémon, das aus sechs Individuen besteht, die wohl telepathisch miteinander kommunizieren.
column23: deEs besteht aus sechs Eiern. Krabbox versucht oft, es sich einzuverleiben, doch wehrt es sich tapfer mit seinen Psycho-Kräften.
column24: deDieses Pokémon besteht aus sechs Individuen. Geht eines verloren, sind es am nächsten Morgen trotzdem auf jeden Fall wieder sechs.
column25: enThis POKéMON consists of six eggs that form a closely knit cluster. The six eggs attract each other and spin around. When cracks increasingly appear on the eggs, EXEGGCUTE is close to evolution.
column26: enThe shell is very durable. Even if it cracks, it can survive without spilling the contents.
column27: deAuch wenn sein Erscheinungsbild eher Eiern ähnelt, wurde entdeckt, dass es mehr mit pflanzenähnlichen Wurzeln in Zusammenhang steht.
column28: enThe heads attract each other and spin around. There must be 6 heads for it to maintain balance.
column29: enOften mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.
column30: enThe heads attract each other and spin around. There must be six heads for it to maintain balance.
column31: enUsing telepathy only they can receive, they always form a cluster of six EXEGGCUTE.
column32: enEven though it appears to be eggs of some sort, it was discovered to be a life-form more like plant seeds.
column33: enIf even one is separated from the group, the energy bond between the six will make them rejoin instantly.
column34: deDie sechs Eier kommunizieren telepathisch. Werden sie getrennt, finden sie sich schnell wieder.
column35: enIts six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.
column36: enThough it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.
column37: enSix of them together form a full-fledged Pokémon. It’s often hunted by Crabrawler, but uses psychokinesis to drive it off.

column8: enIts cries are very noisy. This is because each of the 3 heads thinks about whatever it likes.
column9: enLegend has it that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an EXEGGCUTE.
column10: enEach of its heads has its own will. They use telepathy to discuss their plans before coming to a joint decision.
column11: enIt is said that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an EXEGGCUTE.
column12: enIts cries are very noisy. This is because each of the three heads thinks about whatever it likes.
column13: deJeder der drei Köpfe hat einen eigenen Willen und scheint sich nur für sich selbst zu interessieren.
column14: enIts three heads think independently. However, they are friendly and never appear to squabble.
column15: deKokowei stammt ursprünglich aus den Tropen. Seine Köpfe wachsen stetig, wenn es starkem Sonnenlicht ausgesetzt ist. Wenn seine Köpfe abfallen, bilden sie zusammen ein Owei.
column16: deZu einer bestimmten Jahreszeit fällt einer seiner drei Köpfe ab. Dieser wird schließlich zu einem neuen Owei.
column17: deSeine drei Köpfe denken unabhängig voneinander. Dennoch sind sie nett zueinander und streiten nie.
column18: enIt is called “The Walking Jungle.” If a head grows too big, it falls off and becomes an EXEGGCUTE.
column19: deSeine drei Köpfe setzen mächtige Psycho-Kräfte frei, wenn sie als Einheit agieren. Zieht sich der Himmel zu, bewegt es sich nur noch schwerfällig.
column20: deJeder seiner Köpfe hat einen eigenen Willen. Sie kommunizieren telepathisch miteinander, um gemeinsam Entscheidungen zu treffen.
column21: deJeder seiner drei Köpfe hat einen eigenen Willen. Können sie sich nicht auf eine Richtung einigen, bleibt es wie angewurzelt auf der Stelle stehen.
column22: enOriginally from the tropics, EXEGGUTOR’s heads grow larger from exposure to strong sunlight. It is said that when the heads fall, they group to form an EXEGGCUTE.
column23: deMan nennt es den „Laufenden Dschungel“. Wird ein Kopf zu groß, fällt er ab und wird zu einem Owei.
column24: enExeggutor originally came from the tropics. Its heads steadily grow larger from exposure to strong sunlight. It is said that when the heads fall off, they group together to form Exeggcute.
column25: enEach of its three heads has its own thoughts. When they want to go in different directions, Exeggutor becomes unable to move.
column26: enIf a head drops off, it emits a telepathic call in search of others to form an EXEGGCUTE cluster.
column27: enIt is called “The Walking Tropical Rainforest.” Each of the nuts has a face and a will of its own.
column28: enEach of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.
column29: deDer Schrei dieses Pokémon ist ohrenbetäubend, weil jeder seiner drei Köpfe sagt, was er denkt.
column30: deGegen Feinde setzt es Psycho-Kräfte ein. Seine drei Köpfe greifen unabhängig voneinander an, wodurch sich der Gesamtangriff verdreifacht.
column31: enWhen they work together, Exeggutor’s three heads can put out powerful psychic energy. Cloudy days make this Pokémon sluggish.
column32: enIt is called “The Walking Jungle.” If a head grows too big, it falls off and becomes an Exeggcute.
column33: enIt engages its enemies using psychic powers. Each of its three heads fires off psychokinetic energy, tripling its power.
column34: enLiving in a good environment makes it grow lots of heads. A head that drops off becomes an EXEGGCUTE.
column35: enWhen the time comes, one of its three heads falls off. Before long, the fallen head grows into an Exeggcute.
column36: enEXEGGUTOR originally came from the tropics. Its heads steadily grow larger from exposure to strong sunlight. It is said that when the heads fall off, they group together to form EXEGGCUTE.

column8: enThis Pokémon wears the skull of its deceased mother. Sometimes Cubone’s dreams make it cry, but each tear Cubone sheds makes it stronger.
column9: deDer Schädel auf seinem Kopf gehörte seiner verstorbenen Mutter. Überwindet es diesen Verlust, setzt angeblich die Entwicklung ein.
column10: enIt lost its mother after its birth. It wears its mother's skull, never revealing its true face.
column11: deEs trägt den Schädel seiner verstorbenen Mutter auf seinem Kopf. Fühlt es sich einsam, soll es laut weinen.
column12: enWhen the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.
column13: enIf it is sad or lonely, the skull it wears shakes, and emits a plaintive and mournful sound.
column14: enWhen it thinks of its dead mother, it cries. Its crying makes the skull it wears rattle hollowly.
column15: enIt always wears the skull of its dead mother, so no one has any idea what its hidden face looks like.
column16: enBecause it never removes its skull helmet, no one has ever seen this POKéMON’s real face.
column17: enIt wears the skull of its dead mother on its head. When it becomes lonesome, it is said to cry loudly.
column18: deNachts weint es laut, weil es seine verstorbene Mutter vermisst. Dadurch wird jedoch sein natürlicher Feind Grypheldis auf es aufmerksam.
column19: enBecause it never removes its skull helmet, no one has ever seen this POKéMON's real face.
column20: enAt night, it weeps loudly for its dead mother, but those cries only attract its natural enemy—Mandibuzz.
column21: enWears the skull of its deceased mother. Its cries echo inside the skull and come out as a sad melody.
column22: deEs trägt den Schädel seiner verstorbenen Mutter. Manchmal weint Tragosso, während es träumt, doch jede vergossene Träne macht es stärker.
column23: deEs trägt immer den Schädel seiner verstorbenen Mutter. Darum weiß niemand, wie sein Gesicht aussieht.
column24: deEs trägt den Schädel seiner Mutter als Maske. Sein Weinen hallt darin wider und wird zu einer traurigen Melodie.
column25: enIt wears its mother’s skull on its head, so no one knows what its bare face looks like. However, it’s clear that it’s always crying.
column26: enThe skull it wears on its head is that of its dead mother. According to some, it will evolve when it comes to terms with the pain of her death.
column27: enWhen it thinks of its deceased mother, it weeps loudly. Mandibuzz that hear its cries will attack it from the air.
column28: enIt pines for the mother it will never see again. Seeing a likeness of its mother in the full moon, it cries. The stains on the skull it wears are from its tears.
column29: deDa es den Schädel seiner Mutter auf dem Kopf trägt, weiß man nicht, wie sein Gesicht aussieht. Bekannt ist nur, dass es ständig weint.
column30: deTragosso sehnt sich nach seiner Mutter, die nicht mehr da ist. Wenn es im Vollmond das Ebenbild seiner Mutter erblickt, muss es weinen. Die Flecken auf dem Totenkopf, den es trägt, stammen von vergossenen Tränen.
column31: deDenkt Tragosso an seine verstorbene Mutter, so weint es. Sein Schluchzen hallt dabei in dem Schädel auf seinem Kopf klagend wider.
column32: deDenkt es an seine verstorbene Mutter, weint es laut. Hört dies ein Grypheldis, nutzt dieses die Gelegenheit, um es aus der Luft anzugreifen.
column33: enCubone pines for the mother it will never see again. Seeing a likeness of its mother in the full moon, it cries. The stains on the skull the Pokémon wears are made by the tears it sheds.
column34: enCUBONE pines for the mother it will never see again. Seeing a likeness of its mother in the full moon, it cries. The stains on the skull the POKéMON wears are made by the tears it sheds.
column35: enIf it is sad or lonely, the skull it wears shakes and emits a plaintive and mournful sound.

column8: deDurch die Entwicklung wurde der Schädel seiner Mutter, den es stets trug, zu einem Teil von ihm und es bekam einen aggressiven Charakter.
column9: enA MAROWAK is the evolved form of a CUBONE that has grown tough by overcoming the grief of losing its mother. Its tempered and hardened spirit is not easily broken.
column10: enThis Pokémon is out for vengeance on its natural enemy, Mandibuzz. It throws bones like boomerangs to try to take it down.
column11: enSmall and weak, this Pokémon is adept with its bone club. It has grown more vicious over the ages.
column12: deEs kommuniziert mit seinen Kameraden durch rhythmisches Knochenklappern. Fast 50 Muster sind bisher bekannt.
column13: deEs wirft seinen Knochen nach Grypheldis, um es vom Himmel zu holen. Man nimmt an, dass es sich für seine Mutter an ihm rächen will.
column14: enIt is small and was originally very weak. Its temperament turned ferocious when it began using bones.
column15: enSomewhere in the world is a cemetery just for MAROWAK. It gets its bones from those graves.
column16: enMarowak is the evolved form of a Cubone that has overcome its sadness at the loss of its mother and grown tough. This Pokémon’s tempered and hardened spirit is not easily broken.
column17: enOriginally, it was weak and timid. After evolution, its temperament becomes violent, and it begins to wield bones as weapons.
column18: deVor der Entwicklung war es schwach und ängstlich. Jetzt ist es hitzköpfig und schlägt wild mit einem Knochen um sich.
column19: enIt collects bones from an unknown place. A MAROWAK graveyard exists somewhere in the world, rumors say.
column20: enIt has been seen pounding boulders with the bone it carries in order to tap out messages to others.
column21: enSmall and weak, this POKéMON is adept with its bone club. It has grown more vicious over the ages.
column22: enThis Pokémon overcame its sorrow to evolve a sturdy new body. Marowak faces its opponents bravely, using a bone as a weapon.
column23: deAls es seine Trauer überwand, entwickelte es sich und wurde stärker. Mit seinem Knochen als Waffe stellt sich Knogga mutig seinen Feinden.
column24: deEs ist klein und war ursprünglich sehr schwach. Erst als es anfing Knochen einzusetzen, wurde es wild.
column25: enThey thump their bones rhythmically to communicate among themselves. There are nearly 50 different rhythmic patterns.
column26: enIt collects bones from an unknown place. Some whisper that a MAROWAK graveyard exists somewhere in the world.
column27: enFrom its birth, this savage Pokémon constantly holds bones. It is skilled in using them as weapons.
column28: deEinst war es klein und schwach, doch jetzt ist es geschickt im Umgang mit seinem Knochen und verhält sich äußerst brutal.
column29: enWhen this Pokémon evolved, the skull of its mother fused to it. Marowak’s temperament also turned vicious at the same time.
column30: deKnogga ist eine entwickelte Form von Tragosso, die den Verlust der Mutter überwunden hat und stärker geworden ist. Die gestählte, abgehärtete Seele dieses Pokémon ist nur schwer zu brechen.
column31: deDer Knochen, den dieses Pokémon hält, ist seine Primärwaffe. Es wirft ihn wie einen Bumerang.
column32: deEs wirft seinen Knochen bumeranggleich nach Grypheldis, dem Erzfeind seiner Vorstufe, um es vom Himmel zu holen und sich an ihm zu rächen.
column33: enMAROWAK is the evolved form of a CUBONE that has overcome its sadness at the loss of its mother and grown tough. This POKéMON’s tempered and hardened spirit is not easily broken.
column34: enIt throws bones at Mandibuzz to knock it down. It’s thought that Marowak is trying to avenge its parent.
column35: enThe bone it holds is its key weapon. It throws the bone skillfully like a boomerang to KO targets.

column8: enHITMONLEE’s legs freely contract and stretch. Using these springlike legs, it bowls over foes with devastating kicks. After battle, it rubs down its legs and loosens the muscles to overcome fatigue.
column9: enWhen in a hurry, its legs lengthen progressively. It runs smoothly with extra long, loping strides.
column10: enThe legs freely contract and stretch. The stretchy legs allow it to hit a distant foe with a rising kick.
column11: enHitmonlee’s legs freely contract and stretch. Using these springlike legs, it bowls over foes with devastating kicks. After battle, it rubs down its legs and loosens the muscles to overcome fatigue.
column12: deDieses Pokémon besitzt einen formidablen Gleichgewichtssinn. Es kann in jeder Position pausenlos zutreten.
column13: deKicklee kann seine Beine ausfahren und einziehen. Es verblüfft seine Gegner mit brutalen Tritten. Nach einem Kampf massiert es seine Beine und lockert die Muskeln, um der Erschöpfung vorzubeugen.
column14: enIf it starts kicking repeatedly, both legs will stretch even longer to strike a fleeing foe.
column15: enThis amazing POKéMON has an awesome sense of balance. It can kick in succession from any position.
column16: enWhen in a hurry, its legs lengthen progressively. It runs smoothly with extra-long, loping strides.
column17: deDie Beine ziehen und strecken sich unabhängig voneinander. Mit seinen dehnbaren Beinen kann es einen entfernten Gegner treffen.
column18: enIt is also called the Kick Master. It uses its elastic legs to execute every known kick.
column19: enIts legs can stretch double. First-time foes are startled by its extensible reach.
column20: enThis amazing Pokémon has an awesome sense of balance. It can kick in succession from any position.
column21: deWenn es anfängt pausenlos zu treten, kann es seine Beine ausfahren, um fliehende Feinde zu treffen.
column22: deTritt ein Kicklee zu, wird die Unterseite seines Fußes im Moment des Aufpralls hart wie ein Diamant. So zerschmettert es seinen Gegner.
column23: deEs kann seine Beine nach Belieben ausfahren und einziehen und so auch entfernte Gegner treffen.
column24: enWhen kicking, the sole of its foot turns as hard as a diamond on impact and destroys its enemy.
column25: enIts legs freely stretch and contract. Using these springlike limbs, it bowls over foes with devastating kicks. After battle, it rubs down its tired legs.

column8: enPunches in corkscrew fashion. It can punch its way through a concrete wall like a drill.
column9: deMit seinen rotierenden Hieben vermag es, sich wie eine Bohrmaschine Stück für Stück durch Betonwände zu bohren.
column10: enThe spirit of a pro boxer has infused this POKéMON. It throws punches that are faster than a bullet train.
column11: deDie Schläge, die es austeilt, können Beton pulverisieren. Es muss sich im Kampf alle drei Minuten ausruhen.
column12: enIts punches slice the air. They are launched at such high speed, even a slight graze could cause a burn.
column13: enPunches in corkscrew fashion. It can punch its way through a concrete wall in the same way as a drill.
column14: enWhile apparently doing nothing, it fires punches in lightning-fast volleys that are impossible to see.
column15: deNockchan besitzt das Herz eines Boxers, der sich auf eine Weltmeisterschaft vorbereitet. Dieses Pokémon hat einen unbeugsamen Willen und gibt niemals auf.
column16: enHitmonchan is said to possess the spirit of a boxer who had been working toward a world championship. This Pokémon has an indomitable spirit and will never give up in the face of adversity.
column17: enHITMONCHAN is said to possess the spirit of a boxer who had been working towards a world championship. This POKéMON has an indomitable spirit and will never give up in the face of adversity.
column18: deDer Geist eines Profi-Boxers hat dieses Pokémon inspiriert. Seine Faustschläge sind schneller als ein Hochgeschwindigkeitszug.
column19: enTo increase the strength of all its punch moves, it spins its arms just before making contact.
column20: deSeine Fäuste zerschneiden regelrecht die Luft. Sie sind so schnell, dass selbst die geringste Berührung Verbrennungen verursacht.
column21: enIts punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.
column22: enThe spirit of a pro boxer has infused this Pokémon. It throws punches that are faster than a bullet train.
column23: enWhile apparently doing nothing, it fires punches in lightning fast volleys that are impossible to see.
column24: enThe arm-twisting punches it throws pulverize even concrete. It rests after three minutes of fighting.
column25: deSeine Fäuste zerschneiden die Luft. Es muss jedoch alle drei Minuten eine kurze Pause einlegen.
column26: enA HITMONCHAN is said to possess the spirit of a boxer who aimed to become the world champion. Having an indomitable spirit means that it will never give up.

column8: enIts tongue is twice the length of its body. It can be moved like an arm for grabbing food and attacking.
column9: enBeing licked by its long, saliva-covered tongue leaves a tingling sensation. Extending its tongue retracts its tail.
column10: enIt checks out whatever’s around it by licking everything. If you don’t clean off a spot where it’s licked you, you’ll break out in a rash!
column11: enWhenever Lickitung comes across something new, it will unfailingly give it a lick. It does so because it memorizes things by texture and by taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.
column12: enWhenever LICKITUNG comes across something new, it will unfailingly give it a lick. It does so because it memorizes things by texture and by taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.
column13: enIts tongue spans almost 7 feet and moves more freely than its forelegs. Its licks can cause paralysis.
column14: deSeine Zunge leckt jede Unreinheit sauber. Danach haftet allerdings ein übler Geruch daran, was wiederum am Ergebnis zweifeln lässt.
column15: deWäscht man sich nach der Berührung mit seinem klebrigen Speichel nicht, setzt bald ein starker Juckreiz ein, der nicht mehr zu stoppen ist.
column16: deEs ernährt sich vorwiegend von Käfer-Pokémon. Hat es diese mit der langen Zunge abgeschleckt und dadurch gelähmt, verschlingt es sie im Nu.
column17: enWhenever it sees something unfamiliar, it always licks the object because it memorizes things by texture and taste. It is somewhat put off by sour things.
column18: enInstead of hands, it uses its tongue, which is twice its height. Its sticky saliva grips anything.
column19: enIts tongue spans almost seven feet and moves more freely than its forelegs. Its licks can cause paralysis.
column20: enIts tongue can be extended like a chameleon's. It leaves a tingling sensation when it licks enemies.
column21: enIt licks filth clean with its tongue. Whatever it licks always stinks afterward, so whether it’s really clean is...questionable.
column22: deSeine Zunge ist doppelt so lang wie sein Körper. Es kann sie wie einen Arm bewegen, um damit Nahrung zu greifen oder zu attackieren.
column23: deSchlurps Zunge ist bis zu zwei Meter lang. Es kann sie geschickter bewegen als seine Vorderbeine und andere damit paralysieren.
column24: enBug Pokémon are Lickitung’s main food source. This Pokémon paralyzes its prey with a lick from its long tongue, then swallows the prey whole.
column25: enWhen it extends its over-six-foot-long tongue, its tail quivers. There is a possibility they are connected.
column26: enWields its long tongue deftly, as though it were an arm. The Pokémon's viscous saliva, once it has been collected and boiled down, yields a strong and highly useful adhesive.
column27: deSeine Zunge ist mit klebrigem Speichel bedeckt, der überall haftet. Dies ist sehr nützlich.
column28: enIts long tongue, slathered with a gooey saliva, sticks to anything, so it is very useful.
column29: enIts tongue has well-developed nerves that run to the very tip, so it can be deftly manipulated.
column30: enIt has a tongue that is over 6’6’’ long. It uses this long tongue to lick its body clean.
column31: enIts tongue can be extended like a chameleon’s. It leaves a tingling sensation when it licks enemies.
column32: enIf this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.
column33: deWenn Schlurp etwas Neues entdeckt, leckt es daran. Es merkt sich Gegenstände anhand der Struktur und des Geschmacks. Saure Sachen schrecken es eher ab.
column34: deMit seiner langen Zunge leckt es an allem, um es zu prüfen. Lässt man eine abgeleckte Stelle unbehandelt, entwickelt sich ein Ausschlag.

column8: deBei diesem Pokémon ist Vorsicht geboten, da sich die Gase in seinem Innern an warmen Orten ausbreiten und es explodieren lassen.
column9: enIts body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.
column10: deSein dünner, ballonartiger Körper ist mit schrecklichem Giftgas gefüllt. Es verbreitet einen heftigen Gestank, wenn es in der Nähe ist.
column11: enIf Koffing becomes agitated, it raises the toxicity of its internal gases and jets them out from all over its body. This Pokémon may also overinflate its round body, then explode.
column12: enIt adores polluted air. Some claim that Koffing used to be more plentiful in the Galar region than they are now.
column13: deSein Körper ist zum Bersten voll mit Giftgas. Angelockt vom fauligen Geruch verrottender Abfälle, lungert es auf Müllhalden herum.
column14: enIf one gets close enough to it when it expels poisonous gas, the gas swirling inside it can be seen.
column15: enKOFFING embodies toxic substances. It mixes the toxins with raw garbage to set off a chemical reaction that results in a terribly powerful poison gas. The higher the temperature, the more gas is concocted by this POKéMON.
column16: deAngeblich waren diese nach verpesteter Luft gierenden Pokémon früher weitaus häufiger in der Galar-Region anzutreffen als heute.
column17: enKoffing embodies toxic substances. It mixes the toxins with raw garbage to set off a chemical reaction that results in a terribly powerful poison gas. The higher the temperature, the more gas is concocted by this Pokémon.
column18: enLighter-than-air gases in its body keep it aloft. The gases not only smell, they are also explosive.
column19: enToxic gas is held within its thin, balloon-shaped body, so it can cause massive explosions.
column20: enIts thin, filmy body is filled with gases that cause constant sniffles, coughs and teary eyes.
column21: enIts thin, balloon-like body is inflated by horribly toxic gases. It reeks when it is nearby.
column22: enThe poisonous gases it contains are a little bit lighter than air, keeping it slightly airborne.
column23: deWenn Smogon sich aufregt, lässt es das giftige Gas in seinem Inneren entweichen. Wenn es sich zu sehr aufbläht, explodiert dieses Pokémon.
column24: deSmogons Körper enthält giftige Substanzen. Es vermischt die Toxine mit Müll, um eine chemische Reaktion in Gang zu bringen, die ein unheimlich starkes Giftgas hervorbringt. Je höher die Temperatur, desto mehr Gas produziert es.
column25: enIn hot places, its internal gases could expand and explode without any warning. Be very careful!
column26: enGetting up close to a KOFFING will give you a chance to observe, through its thin skin, the toxic gases swirling inside. It blows up at the slightest stimulation.
column27: enIf KOFFING becomes agitated, it raises the toxicity of its internal gases and jets them out from all over its body. This POKéMON may also overinflate its round body, then explode.
column28: enBecause it stores several kinds of toxic gases in its body, it is prone to exploding without warning.
column29: deGase, die leichter als Luft sind, lassen es schweben. Diese Gase stinken und sind explosiv.

column8: enInhaling toxic fumes from trash and mixing them inside its body lets it spread an even fouler stench.
column9: enVery rarely, a sudden mutation can result in two small Koffing twins becoming conjoined as a Weezing.
column10: enWEEZING loves the gases given off by rotted kitchen garbage. This POKéMON will find a dirty, unkempt house and make it its home. At night, when the people in the house are asleep, it will go through the trash.
column11: dePumpt sich eines der zwei Smogon auf, lässt das andere Luft ab. So findet ein Giftgasaustausch statt.
column12: enWEEZING alternately shrinks and inflates its twin bodies to mix together toxic gases inside. The more the gases are mixed, the more powerful the toxins become. The POKéMON also becomes more putrid.
column13: enIf one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.
column14: deSehr selten führt eine plötzliche Mutation eines Zwillings-Smogon zu einer Verbindung zu Smogmog.
column15: enBy diluting its toxic gases with a special process, the highest grade of perfume can be extracted. To WEEZING, gases emanating from garbage are the ultimate feast.
column16: enIf one of the twin KOFFING inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.
column17: enThis Pokémon lives and grows by absorbing poison gas, dust, and germs that exist inside garbage.
column18: enWhere two kinds of poison gases meet, 2 KOFFINGs can fuse into a WEEZING over many years.
column19: deSmogmog bläst seine beiden Körper abwechselnd auf und lässt sie wieder zusammenschrumpfen, um die giftigen Gase darin zu vermischen. Je mehr Gase vermischt werden, desto stärker werden die Toxine.
column20: enIt mixes gases between its two bodies. It’s said that these Pokémon were seen all over the Galar region back in the day.
column21: enWhere two kinds of poison gases meet, two KOFFING can fuse into a WEEZING over many years.
column22: deEs kann zwar nicht so viel Luft einsaugen wie die Smogmog aus Galar, dafür sind aber die Toxine, die es produziert, umso stärker.
column23: enIt lives and grows by absorbing dust, germs and poison gases that are contained in toxic waste and garbage.
column24: enVery rarely, a sudden mutation can result in two small KOFFING twins becoming conjoined as a WEEZING.
column25: deSmogmog liebt die Gase verrottender, fauler Küchenabfälle. Dieses Pokémon sucht sich ein vernachlässigtes, schmutziges Gebäude und richtet sich dort häuslich ein. Nachts, wenn die Menschen schlafen, wühlt es im Müll.
column26: enTop-grade perfume is made using its internal poison gases by diluting them to the highest level.
column27: enWhen it inhales poisonous gases from garbage, its body expands, and its insides smell much worse.
column28: enIt grows by feeding on gases released by garbage. Though very rare, triplets have been found.
column29: enWeezing loves the gases given off by rotted kitchen garbage. This Pokémon will find a dirty, unkempt house and make it its home. At night, when the people in the house are asleep, it will go through the trash.
column30: enIt can’t suck in air quite as well as a Galarian Weezing, but the toxins it creates are more potent than those of its counterpart.
column31: deIn seinen beiden Körpern vermischt es Gase. Man sagt, dass es früher überall in der Galar-Region anzutreffen gewesen sei.
column32: deSmogmog ernährt sich von allerlei Müll, aus dem es Köstlichkeiten wie giftige Gase, Bakterien und Staub absorbiert.
column33: enWeezing alternately shrinks and inflates its twin bodies to mix together toxic gases inside. The more the gases are mixed, the more powerful the toxins become. The Pokémon also becomes more putrid.

column8: deAufgrund seiner kurzen Beine kann es sich nicht umdrehen. Es kann immer nur in eine Richtung laufen.
column9: enRHYHORN’s brain is very small. It is so dense, while on a run it forgets why it started running in the first place. It apparently remembers sometimes if it demolishes something.
column10: enIt doesn’t care if there is anything in its way. It just charges and destroys all obstacles.
column11: enA POKéMON with a one-track mind. Once it charges,  it won't stop running until it falls asleep.
column12: enRhyhorn runs in a straight line, smashing everything in its path. It is not bothered even if it rushes headlong into a block of steel. This Pokémon may feel some pain from the collision the next day, however.
column13: enIt doesn't care if there is anything in its way. It just charges and destroys all obstacles.
column14: deDieses Pokémon ist zwar nicht besonders klug, aber dafür so stark, dass es durch Rammen sogar Hochhäuser zum Einsturz bringen kann.
column15: enIt can remember only one thing at a time. Once it starts rushing, it forgets why it started.
column16: enOnce it starts running, it doesn’t stop. Its tiny brain makes it so stupid that it can’t remember why it started running in the first place.
column17: deRihorn ist sehr einfach gestrickt und kann nur eingleisig denken. Stürmt es einmal los, hört es erst auf zu laufen, wenn es ermattet einschläft.
column18: enIts body is clad in a thick hide, and its tackles topple buildings. Unfortunately, it is not smart.
column19: enA Pokémon with a one-track mind. Once it charges, it won’t stop running until it falls asleep.
column20: enStrong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging Tackles.
column21: deRihorn läuft immer geradeaus und zerstampft alles, was ihm in die Quere kommt. Es macht ihm auch nichts aus, wenn es mit dem Kopf voran gegen einen Stahlklotz rennt. Am nächsten Tag allerdings könnte es Schmerzen haben.
column22: enStrong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.
column23: enIts massive bones are 1,000 times harder than human bones. Its TACKLE can knock a semitrailer flying.
column24: deRihorns Hirn ist sehr klein. Es ist so beschränkt, dass es während des Laufens vergisst, warum es überhaupt losgelaufen ist. Manchmal erinnert es sich aber daran, dass es etwas zerstört hat.
column25: enIts massive bones are 1000 times harder than human bones. It can easily knock a trailer flying.
column26: enIt is inept at turning because of its four short legs. It can only charge and run in one direction.
column27: deStark, aber nicht allzu klug, kann dieses Pokémon sogar Hochhäuser mit seinem Tackle-Angriff zum Einsturz bringen.
column28: enStrong, but not too bright, this POKéMON can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging TACKLES.
column29: enLudicrously strong—when it butts heads with a mountain, it is the mountain that shatters. But its short legs struggle with turns, and it is incapable of stopping unless it collides with something.
column30: deEs kann sich immer nur eine Sache merken. Sobald es losgestürmt ist, vergisst es augenblicklich den Auslöser dafür.
column31: enIts powerful tackles can destroy anything. However, it is too slow witted to help people work.
column32: enRhyhorn’s brain is very small. It is so dense, while on a run it forgets why it started running in the first place. It apparently remembers sometimes if it demolishes something.
column33: enRHYHORN runs in a straight line, smashing everything in its path. It is not bothered even if it rushes headlong into a block of steel. This POKéMON may feel some pain from the collision the next day, however.

column8: deNach seiner Entwicklung geht es aufrecht auf seinen Hinterbeinen. Mit seinem Horn kann es Löcher in Felsen bohren.
column9: enRHYDON’s horn can crush even uncut diamonds. One sweeping blow of its tail can topple a building. This POKéMON’s hide is extremely tough. Even direct cannon hits don’t leave a scratch.
column10: enProtected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.
column11: enIts rugged hide protects it from even the heat of lava. However, the hide also makes it insensitive.
column12: enRHYDON has a horn that serves as a drill. It is used for destroying rocks and boulders. This POKéMON occasionally rams into streams of magma, but the armor-like hide prevents it from feeling the heat.
column13: enBy lightly spinning its drill-like horn, it can easily shatter even a diamond in the rough.
column14: enIts brain developed when it began walking on its hind legs. Its armor-like hide even repels molten lava.
column15: enWalks on its hind legs. Shows signs of intelligence. Its armor-like hide even repels molten lava.
column16: deNach seiner Entwicklung geht es aufrecht auf seinen Hinterfüßen. Mit seinem Horn kann es Löcher in Felsen bohren.
column17: enIts brain developed after it stood up on its hind legs. Its drill horn bores tunnels through solid rock.
column18: enProtected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees.
column19: deDurch seine panzerähnliche Körperhülle kann es in bis zu 2 000 °C heißer Lava leben.
column20: enStanding on its hind legs freed its forelegs and made it smarter. It is very forgetful, however.
column21: enIts horn, which rotates like a drill, destroys tall buildings with one strike. It stands on its hind legs, and its brain is well developed.
column22: deRizeros hat ein Horn, das es als Bohrer benutzen kann. Damit zerstört es Felsblöcke und Steine. Dieses Pokémon rammt sein Horn manchmal in Ströme von Magma. Seine panzerähnliche Haut schützt es dabei vor der Hitze.
column23: enRapidly rotates its horn to bore through bedrock. It swaggers around volcanic regions, protected from the lava's heat by its tough, armorlike hide.
column24: enRhydon’s horn can crush even uncut diamonds. One sweeping blow of its tail can topple a building. This Pokémon’s hide is extremely tough. Even direct cannon hits don’t leave a scratch.
column25: enIt begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.
column26: deMit seinem Horn kann Rizeros selbst Diamanten zerbrechen. Ein einziger Hieb mit seinem Schweif kann ein Haus zum Einsturz bringen. Die Haut dieses Pokémon ist so hart, dass nicht einmal Kanonenkugeln Kratzer hinterlassen.
column27: enRhydon has a horn that serves as a drill. It is used for destroying rocks and boulders. This Pokémon occasionally rams into streams of magma, but the armor-like hide prevents it from feeling the heat.
column28: enIts brain developed when it began walking on hind legs. Its thick hide protects it even in magma.
column29: deSein ganzer Körper ist von einer panzerähnlichen Haut geschützt. Es kann sogar in bis zu 2000 şC heißer Lava leben.
column30: deEs hat gelernt, auf seinen Hinterbeinen zu laufen und komplex zu denken. Seine Haut gleicht einer Rüstung und widersteht sogar heißer Lava.

column8: enCHANSEY lay nutritionally excellent eggs every day. The eggs are so delicious, they are eagerly devoured by even those people who have lost their appetite.
column9: enPeople try to catch it for its extremely nutritious eggs, but it rarely can be found.
column10: enThe eggs laid by Chansey are rich in nutrients and a favorite food of many Pokémon.
column11: enChansey lays nutritionally excellent eggs on an everyday basis. The eggs are so delicious, they are easily and eagerly devoured by even those people who have lost their appetite.
column12: deEs ist äußerst selten und kann zudem sehr schnell davonlaufen. Schafft man es trotzdem, eins zu fangen, bringt es angeblich Glück.
column13: deEinst wurde es exzessiv von Wilddieben gejagt, die es auf das Ei in seinem Beutel abgesehen hatten. Mittlerweile ist es daher sehr selten.
column14: enBeing few in number and difficult to capture, it is said to bring happiness to the Trainer who catches it.
column15: deSeine Eier sind äußerst nahrhaft und schmecken köstlich, weshalb sie als erlesene Kochzutat gelten.
column16: enA kindly Pokémon that lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.
column17: enIt lays several eggs a day and won’t share them with those who have evil in their hearts.
column18: deMan sagt, es bringe Glück. Es ist sehr mitfühlend und teilt seine Eier mit Verletzten.
column19: enA rare and elusive POKéMON that is said to bring happiness to those who manage to catch one.
column20: enIt seems that other Pokémon’s efforts to take its delicious, nutritious egg away from it caused Chansey to get faster at fleeing.
column21: deEs legt mehrere Eier am Tag. Sie sind sehr nahrhaft und außerordentlich schmackhaft.
column22: enA gentle and kindhearted Pokémon that shares its nutritious eggs if it sees an injured Pokémon.
column23: enFew in number and difficult to capture, it is said to bring happiness to the trainer who catches it.
column24: deDie Eier, die es legt, sind ausgesprochen nahrhaft. Sie sind die Leibspeise vieler Pokémon.
column25: enCHANSEY lays nutritionally excellent eggs on an everyday basis. The eggs are so delicious, they are easily and eagerly devoured by even those people who have lost their appetite.
column26: deChaneira legt täglich ausgesprochen nahrhafte Eier. Sie sind so köstlich, dass sie sogar von Menschen verspeist werden, die gar keinen Appetit haben.
column27: enThis purehearted Pokémon shares its eggs with the injured.
column28: deDieses ursprünglich eher träge Pokémon hat gelernt, schnell davonzulaufen, um seine Eier vor Räubern schützen zu können.
column29: enA rare and elusive POKéMON that is said to bring happiness to those who manage to get it.
column30: deEs hat gelernt, rasch davonzulaufen, da es viele Pokémon auf die nahrhaften und köstlichen Eier abgesehen haben, die es legt.
column31: enBecause the eggs on their bellies have been overharvested by people in the past, the Chansey population remains very small.
column32: deChaneira ist sehr warmherzig und freundlich. Es kümmert sich liebevoll um verletzte Lebewesen, indem es seine nahrhaften Eier mit ihnen teilt.
column33: enIt is said to deliver happiness. Being compassionate, it shares its eggs with injured people.
column34: enIt walks carefully to prevent its egg from breaking. However, it is extremely fast at running away.
column35: enThis species was once very slow. To protect their eggs from other creatures, these Pokémon became able to flee quickly.
column36: enA gentle and kindhearted POKéMON that shares its nutritious eggs if it sees an injured POKéMON.
column37: enIt lays several eggs a day. The eggs are apparently rich in nutrients and extremely delicious.
column38: enThe egg Chansey carries is not only delicious but also packed with nutrition. It’s used as a high-class cooking ingredient.
column39: enNot only are these Pokémon fast runners, they’re also few in number, so anyone who finds one must be lucky indeed.

column8: enTangela’s vines snap off easily if they are grabbed. This happens without pain, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by newly grown vines the very next day.
column9: enIts vines snap off easily and painlessly if they are grabbed, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by new growth the very next day.
column10: deSeine Ranken verströmen einen eigentümlichen Duft, weshalb sie in bestimmten Gegenden von Galar als aromatische Kräuter verwendet werden.
column11: enTANGELA’s vines snap off easily if they are grabbed. This happens without pain, allowing it to make a quick getaway. The lost vines are replaced by newly grown vines the very next day.
column12: enBlue plant vines cloak the POKéMON’s identity in a tangled mass. It entangles anything that gets close.
column13: enIt tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.
column14: enThe whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to seaweed. Its vines shake as it walks.
column15: deSeine wahre Gestalt ist weiterhin ein Mysterium, da sie von Ranken verdeckt wird, die unaufhörlich nachwachsen, selbst wenn sie abreißen.
column16: deWenn man Tangelas Ranken berührt, fallen diese oft ab. Dabei empfindet es keine Schmerzen und es kann sich schnell in Sicherheit bringen. Verliert es Ranken, werden diese am nächsten Tag durch neue ersetzt.
column17: deWenn man Tangelas Ranken berührt, fallen diese sofort ab. Das tut ihm aber nicht weh und es kann sich schnell in Sicherheit bringen. Verliert es Ranken, werden diese am nächsten Tag durch neue ersetzt.
column18: enIt is shrouded by blue vines. No one has seen the face hidden behind this growth of vines.
column19: deEs berührt alles, was sich bewegt, mit seinen Ranken. Diese Berührungen kitzeln sehr.
column20: deAufgrund der Ranken, die seinen Körper bedecken, kennt keiner seine wahre Form. Die blauen Ranken wachsen immer weiter.
column21: enDuring battle, it constantly moves the vines that cover its body in order to annoy its opponent.
column22: enThe blue vines shrouding its body are covered in a growth of fine hair. It is known to be ticklish.
column23: deSein Körper liegt unter dichten, blauen Ranken verborgen, die niemals aufhören zu wachsen.
column24: enThe vines that cloak its entire body are always jiggling. They effectively unnerve its foes.
column25: enHidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.
column26: enIts identity is obscured by masses of thick blue vines. The vines are said to never stop growing.
column27: enMany writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.
column28: enIt is cloaked entirely in blue vines, preventing any glimpse of its true identity. The vines impart a refreshing sensation when chewed—they're useful as a spice.
column29: enIts identity is obscured by masses of thick, blue vines. The vines are said to never stop growing.
column30: enThe whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to seaweed. The vines sway as it walks.
column31: enThe vines of a Tangela have a distinct scent. In some parts of Galar, Tangela vines are used as herbs.

column8: deEs liegen Berichte vor, laut denen Kangama ohne eigenen Nachwuchs stattdessen Menschenkinder in Not aufgezogen haben.
column9: enAlthough it’s carrying its baby in a pouch on its belly, Kangaskhan is swift on its feet. It intimidates its opponents with quick jabs.
column10: deObwohl es sein Junges im Beutel trägt, bewegt sich dieses Pokémon äußerst leichtfüßig. Gegner schreckt Kangama mit blitzschnellen Schlägen ab.
column11: deEs zieht sein Junges in seiner Bauchtasche auf. Um es zu schützen, nimmt es mit jedem Gegner den Kampf auf.
column12: deFühlt es sich sicher, dann verlässt das Junge den Beutel. Die Mutter behält es ständig im Auge.
column13: enIf it is safe, the young gets out of the belly pouch to play. The adult keeps a close eye on the youngster.
column14: enThe female raises its offspring in a pouch on its belly. It is skilled at attacking using COMET PUNCH.
column15: deWer ein Kangama-Junges sieht, das zum Spielen den Beutel verlassen hat, sollte sich ihm nicht nähern. Seine Mutter behält es stets im Auge.
column16: enRaises its young in its belly pouch. Won't run from any fight to keep its young protected.
column17: enRaises its young in its belly pouch. Won’t run from any fight to keep its young protected.
column18: enTo avoid crushing the baby it carries in its pouch, it always sleeps standing up.
column19: enThere are records of a lost human child being raised by a childless Kangaskhan.
column20: enIf you come across a young KANGASKHAN playing by itself, never try to catch it. The baby’s parent is sure to be in the area, and it will become violently enraged.
column21: deDas Junge verlässt den Beutel der Mutter im Alter von drei Jahren. Dies ist der einzige Zeit-punkt, zu dem man die Mutter laut weinen hört.
column22: deSein Junges beschützt es in seinem Beutel. Wer dieses verwundet, den erwarten Kangamas Groll und eine rücksichtslose Tracht Prügel.
column23: deWenn du auf ein junges, spielendes Kangama stößt, solltest du es nicht stören oder versuchen, es einzufangen. Die Mutter dieses Baby-Pokémon ist bestimmt in Reichweite und könnte sehr wütend auf dich werden.
column24: enThe child in its pouch leaves home after roughly three years. That is the only time the mother is heard to cry wildly.
column25: enIt raises its offspring in its belly pouch. It lets the baby out to play only when it feels safe.
column26: enKangaskhan’s maternal love is so deep that it will brave death to protect its offspring.
column27: enYou shouldn’t get close to the child when it’s playing outside its mother’s pouch. Its mother is always nearby watching over it.
column28: enIf you come across a young KANGASKHAN playing by itself, you must never disturb it or attempt to catch it. The baby POKéMON’s parent is sure to be in the area, and it will become violently enraged at you.
column29: enThe infant rarely ventures out of its mother’s protective pouch until it is three years old.
column30: enKangaskhan protects its child by keeping it in its pouch. It has zero forgiveness for those who harm its child and will beat them down.
column31: enIf you come across a young Kangaskhan playing by itself, you must never disturb it or attempt to catch it. The baby Pokémon’s parent is sure to be in the area, and it will become violently enraged at you.
column32: enThe infant rarely ventures out of its mother's protective pouch until it is 3 years old.
column33: enTo protect its young, it will never give up during battle, no matter how badly wounded it is.
column34: deDie Mutterliebe des ausgewachsenen Kangama ist tief. Es wäre sogar bereit, sein eigenes Leben zu opfern, um sein Junges zu beschützen.
column35: deSein Nachwuchs wächst in seinem Beutel heran. Nur wenn es sicher ist, darf das Junge aus dem Beutel.

column8: enIf it senses any danger, it will vigorously spray water or a special type of ink from its mouth.
column9: enIf attacked by a larger enemy, it quickly swims to safety by adeptly controlling its well-developed dorsal fin.
column10: enIt maintains balance using its tail, which is wound up like a coil. It may spray ink from its mouth.
column11: enIts big, developed fins move rapidly, allowing it to swim backward while still facing forward.
column12: deEs schwimmt elegant wie ein Tänzer und erzeugt dabei Wasserstrudel. Mit Artgenossen wetteifert es darum, wer den größten Strudel formen kann.
column13: enIf attacked by a larger enemy, it quickly swims to safety by adeptly controlling its dorsal fin.
column14: enIf Horsea senses danger, it will reflexively spray a dense black ink from its mouth and try to escape. This Pokémon swims by cleverly flapping the fin on its back.
column15: deIm Schatten von Korallen legt es sein Nest an. Bei Gefahr versprüht es Tinte und flieht.
column16: deEs lebt in Meeren mit ruhigem Gezeitenstrom. Wird es angegriffen, versprüht es tiefschwarze Tinte und ergreift daraufhin die Flucht.
column17: enThey swim with dance-like motions and cause whirlpools to form. Horsea compete to see which of them can generate the biggest whirlpool.
column18: enHORSEA eats small insects and moss off of rocks. If the ocean current turns fast, this POKéMON anchors itself by wrapping its tail around rocks or coral to prevent being washed away.
column19: enHorsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.
column20: enWhen they're in a safe location, they can be seen playfully tangling their tails together.
column21: deSeeper ernährt sich von kleinen Insekten und dem Moos auf Steinen. Bei unruhiger See verankert sich dieses Pokémon mit seinem Schweif an Steinen oder Korallen, damit es nicht weggespült wird.
column22: deWenn Seeper sich in Gefahr wähnt, sprüht es zur Verteidigung entweder Wasser oder eine spezielle Tinte kraftvoll aus seinem Mund.
column23: enBy cleverly flicking the fins on its back side to side, it moves in any direction while facing forward. It spits ink to escape if it senses danger.
column24: enKnown to shoot down flying bugs with precision blasts of ink from the surface of the water.
column25: deWenn Seeper Gefahr wittert, versprüht es reflexartig eine tiefschwarze Tinte aus seinem Mund und ergreift die Flucht. Dieses Pokémon kann schwimmen, indem es geschickt mit der Flosse auf seinem Rücken schlägt.
column26: enIt makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.
column27: enHorsea eats small insects and moss off of rocks. If the ocean current turns fast, this Pokémon anchors itself by wrapping its tail around rocks or coral to prevent being washed away.
column28: deDieses Pokémon schießt mit Tinte auf über der Wasseroberfläche fliegende Insekten.
column29: enIf HORSEA senses danger, it will reflexively spray a dense black ink from its mouth and try to escape. This POKéMON swims by cleverly flapping the fins on its back.

column8: deSobald sich Seemon um eine Koralle gewickelt hat, schläft es. Von Zeit zu Zeit werden Menschen durch die Widerhaken dieses Pokémon verletzt, wenn sie versuchen, Korallen zu ernten und es dabei nicht bemerken.
column9: enIts spines provide protection. Its fins and bones are prized as traditional medicine ingredients.
column10: deSeemon erzeugt Strudel, indem es sich schnell um die eigene Achse dreht. Diese Strudel können sogar Fischerboote in die Tiefe reißen. Damit schwächt es Beute, bevor es sie im Ganzen verschlingt.
column11: deMännlichen Seemon obliegt es, den Nachwuchs großzuziehen. In diesem Zeitraum ist das Gift in ihren Rückenstacheln stärker und dickflüssiger.
column12: enIts body bristles with sharp spikes. Carelessly trying to touch it could cause fainting from the spikes.
column13: enSEADRA generates whirlpools by spinning its body. The whirlpools are strong enough to swallow even fishing boats. This POKéMON weakens prey with these currents, then swallows it whole.
column14: enIts fin-tips leak poison. Its fins and bones are highly valued as ingredients in herbal medicine.
column15: enThe poisonous barbs all over its body are highly valued as ingredients for making traditional herbal medicine. It shows no mercy to anything approaching its nest.
column16: deEine Berührung seiner Rückenflossen betäubt. Es klammert sich mit dem Schwanz an Korallen fest, um sich im Schlaf vor der Strömung zu schützen.
column17: deSelbst Beute, die größer als sein kleiner Mund ist, kann es dank dessen starker Saugkraft im Nu einsaugen und verspeisen.
column18: deDieses Pokémon kann rückwärts schwimmen, indem es Flügel und Schwanz als Flossen einsetzt.
column19: enIt is capable of swimming backwards by rapidly flapping its winglike pectoral fins and stout tail.
column20: enSeadra’s mouth is slender, but its suction power is strong. In an instant, Seadra can suck in food that’s larger than the opening of its mouth.
column21: enCapable of swimming backwards by rapidly flapping its wing-like pectoral fins and stout tail.
column22: enTouching the back fin causes numbness. It hooks its tail to coral to stay in place while sleeping.
column23: enSEADRA sleeps after wriggling itself between the branches of coral. Those trying to harvest coral are occasionally stung by this POKéMON’s poison barbs if they fail to notice it.
column24: deSein Körper ist mit scharfen Stacheln gespickt. Wenn man sorglos ist und es berührt, kann man durch die Stacheln bewusstlos werden.
column25: enIt’s the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.
column26: enThe male raises the young. If it is approached, it uses its toxic spikes to fend off the intruder.
column27: enIts spines provide protection. Its fins and bones are prized as traditional-medicine ingredients.
column28: enAn examination of its cells revealed the presence of a gene not found in HORSEA. It became a hot topic.
column29: enSeadra sleeps after wriggling itself between the branches of coral. Those trying to harvest coral are occasionally stung by this Pokémon’s poison barbs if they fail to notice it.
column30: enSeadra generates whirlpools by spinning its body. The whirlpools are strong enough to swallow even fishing boats. This Pokémon weakens prey with these currents, then swallows it whole.

column8: deBeim Schwimmen flattern seine Flossen wie ein Kleid, was ihm eine märchenhafte Ausstrahlung verleiht. Man nennt es auch „Wasserprinzessin“.
column9: enIts dorsal and pectoral fins are strongly developed like muscles. It can swim at a speed of five knots.
column10: enIts dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.
column11: deGoldini liebt es, in Teichen und Flüssen zu schwimmen. Wenn ein solches Pokémon in ein Aquarium gebracht wird, wird es selbst das dickste Glas mit einem Stoß seines Horns zum Zerbersten bringen und die Flucht ergreifen.
column12: enA strong swimmer, it is capable of swimming nonstop up fast streams at a steady speed of five knots.
column13: enThe way it swims along fluttering its dress-like fins has earned it the name “princess of the water.”
column14: deSeine Brust-, Rücken- und Schweifflossen sehen anmutig aus. Daher nennt man es den „Wassertänzer“.
column15: enIts dorsal, pectoral and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the water dancer.
column16: deEs schwimmt mit fünf Knoten. Wird es angegriffen, wehrt es sich mit seinem scharfen Horn.
column17: deWenn es warm wird, bilden sie Schwärme und schwimmen stromaufwärts. Dies ist ein Zeichen dafür, dass der Frühling herannaht.
column18: enIn the springtime, schools of GOLDEEN can be seen swimming up falls and rivers. It metes out staggering damage with its single horn.
column19: enThough it appears very elegant when swimming with fins unfurled, it can jab powerfully with its horn.
column20: enIts tail fin billows like an elegant ballroom dress, giving it the nickname of “The Water Queen.”
column21: deEinige Trainer sind von seinem langem Horn und seiner Eleganz so entzückt, dass sie ausschließlich Goldini trainieren.
column22: deSeine Rücken- und Brustflossen sind muskelähnlich entwickelt. Es erreicht beim Schwimmen eine Geschwindigkeit von bis zu fünf Knoten.
column23: enIt swims at a steady 5 knots. If it senses danger, it will strike back with its sharp horn.
column24: enGOLDEEN is a very beautiful POKéMON with fins that billow elegantly in water. However, don’t let your guard down around this POKéMON - it could ram you powerfully with its horn.
column25: enGoldeen is a very beautiful Pokémon with fins that billow elegantly in water. However, don’t let your guard down around this Pokémon—it could ram you powerfully with its horn.
column26: enIts tail fin billows like an elegant ballroom dress, giving it the nickname of the Water Queen.
column27: enA strong swimmer, it is capable of swimming nonstop up fast streams at a steady speed of five knots per hour.
column28: deSeine Brust-, Rücken- und Schwanzflossen bewegen sich anmutig. Daher nennt man es den „Wassertänzer“.
column29: enIts dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the water dancer.
column30: enWhen the weather grows warm, they form groups and swim upriver. This sight serves as a poetic reminder that spring has arrived.
column31: enGOLDEEN loves swimming wild and free in rivers and ponds. If one of these POKéMON is placed in an aquarium, it will shatter even the thickest glass with one ram of its horn and make its escape.
column32: enGoldeen loves swimming wild and free in rivers and ponds. If one of these Pokémon is placed in an aquarium, it will shatter even the thickest glass with one ram of its horn and make its escape.
column33: enAlthough known for their splendid tail fins, Goldeen apparently compete among themselves to see whose horn is thickest and sharpest.
column34: deWährend der Laichzeit kann man Schwärme von Goldini dabei beobachten, wie sie gegen den Strom von Flüssen und Wasserfällen schwimmen.
column35: enIt swims elegantly by flittering its tail fin as if it were a dress. It has the look of a queen.
column36: enDuring spawning season, they swim gracefully in the water, searching for their perfect mate.
column37: enWhen it is time for them to lay eggs, they can be seen swimming up rivers and falls in large groups.
column38: deGoldini ist ein wunderschönes Pokémon, dessen Flossen sich elegant im Wasser wiegen. Aber man muss auf dieses Pokémon achtgeben. Es könnte einen sonst heftig mit seinem Horn rammen.
column39: deEs ist für seine schöne Schwanzflosse bekannt. Miteinander wetteifern sie aber darum, wer das dickste und spitzeste Horn auf dem Kopf hat.
column40: enSpellbound by the length of its horn and the beauty of its fins, many strange Trainers raise Goldeen and nothing but Goldeen.

column8: enIts horn spins like a drill to steadily hollow out rocks—even harder ones. The coloration of the male is more vivid.
column9: enSeaking is very protective of its eggs. The male and female will take turns patrolling around their nest and eggs. The guarding of eggs by these Pokémon goes on for over a month.
column10: deGolkings Fangemeinde spaltet sich in zwei Lager, die sich gegenseitig nicht riechen können: die Hornenthusiasten und die Flossenfans.
column11: enIn the autumn, Seaking males can be seen performing courtship dances in riverbeds to woo females. During this season, this Pokémon’s body coloration is at its most beautiful.
column12: enIt makes its nest by hollowing out boulders in streams with its horn. It defends its eggs with its life.
column13: enDuring spawning season, SEAKING gather from all over, causing rivers to appear a brilliant red.
column14: deIm Herbst legt es an Gewicht zu und erscheint in prächtigen Farben, um so einen Partner anzulocken.
column15: deGolking gibt sehr viel acht auf seine Brut. Es patrouilliert um sein Nest und die Eier herum, wobei sich das männliche und das weibliche Exemplar abwechseln. Das Bewachen der Eier beschäftigt dieses Pokémon für über einen Monat.
column16: enDuring spawning season, SEAKING gather from all over, coloring the rivers a brilliant red.
column17: enUsing its horn, it bores holes in riverbed boulders, making nests to prevent its eggs from washing away.
column18: deMännliche Golking tanzen in Flussbetten, um die weiblichen Exemplare zu beeindrucken, die sich dann um den allerbesten Tänzer scharen.
column19: deIm Herbst, zur Paarungszeit, sieht man diese Pokémon kraftvoll Bäche und Flüsse hinaufschwimmen.
column20: enSEAKING is very protective of its eggs. The male and female will take turns patrolling around their nest and eggs. The guarding of eggs by these POKéMON goes on for over a month.
column21: deSein Horn rotiert wie ein Bohrer und höhlt selbst die härtesten Felsen aus. Männliche Golking erstrahlen in besonders prächtigen Farben.
column22: enIn the autumn spawning season, they can be seen swimming powerfully up rivers and creeks.
column23: enIn autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.
column24: enTrainers who are crazy for Seaking are divided into horn enthusiasts and fin enthusiasts. The two groups do not get along well.
column25: enThe horn on its head is sharp like a drill. It bores a hole in a boulder to make its nest.
column26: enIt punches holes in boulders on streambeds. This is a clever innovation that prevents its eggs from being attacked or washed away by the current.
column27: enIn the autumn, SEAKING males can be seen performing courtship dances in riverbeds to woo females. During this season, this POKéMON’s body coloration is at its most beautiful.
column28: enIt is the male's job to make a nest by carving out boulders in a stream using the horn on its head.
column29: enWhen the weather grows cold, its whole body flushes a deep red. This sight serves as a poetic reminder that autumn has arrived.
column30: deIm Herbst kann man männliche Golking dabei beobachten, wie sie Balztänze in Flussbetten aufführen, um die weiblichen Exemplare zu beeindrucken. Während dieser Zeit ist die Farbe dieser Pokémon am schönsten.
column31: deEs baut Nester, indem es Steine im Fluss mit seinem Horn aushöhlt. Es verteidigt seine Eier mit dem Leben.
column32: enTo protect its family, it will fight with its drill-sharp horn. It lives in hollowed rocks in streams.
column33: deMännliche Exemplare dieses Pokémon höhlen mit dem Horn an ihrem Kopf Steine im Flussbett aus, um sich so ein Nest zu bauen.
column34: enIt is the male’s job to make a nest by carving out boulders in a stream using the horn on its head.
column35: enWhen autumn comes, the males patrol the area around their nests in order to protect their offspring.
column36: deEs laicht in Löchern, die es mit seinem Horn in Felsen des Flussbettes gebohrt hat, damit seine Eier nicht vom Wasser fortgespült werden.
column37: deWenn es kälter wird, färbt sich sein ganzer Körper tiefrot. Dieser Anblick versetzt die Leute in Herbststimmung.
column38: enTo attract females, males dance on the river’s floor. The females gather around the male that dances most gracefully.

column8: enNo number of injuries can bother Staryu. Its amazing regenerative powers return it to its previous state in half a day!
column9: deIn Sterndus Körpermitte befindet sich ein hellrot leuchtendes Organ – sein Kern. Wenn du im Spätsommer an einen Strand gehst, sehen die leuchtenden Kerne dieser Pokémon aus wie Sterne.
column10: enAs long as its red core remains, it can regenerate its body instantly, even if it’s torn apart.
column11: enSTARYU’s center section has an organ called the core that shines bright red. If you go to a beach toward the end of summer, the glowing cores of these POKéMON look like the stars in the sky.
column12: enIf you visit a beach at the end of summer, you’ll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.
column13: enIn many places, there are folktales of stardust falling into the ocean and becoming Staryu.
column14: enWhen the stars twinkle at night, it floats up from the sea floor, and its body's center core flickers.
column15: enAt night, the center of its body slowly flickers with the same rhythm as a human heartbeat.
column16: enLarge numbers of these Pokémon make their home at the seaside. At night, a strange red glow radiates from the center of their bodies.
column17: deVerletzungen machen diesem Pokémon nichts aus. Dank seiner Regenerationsfähigkeit ist es einen halben Tag später schon wieder wie neu.
column18: enIt appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.
column19: enAs long as the center section is unharmed, this Pokémon can grow back fully even if it is chopped to bits.
column20: enAs long as the center section is unharmed, it can grow back fully even if it is chopped to bits.
column21: enAt night, the middle of its body slowly flickers with the same rhythm as a human heartbeat.
column22: enFish Pokémon nibble at it, but Staryu isn’t bothered. Its body regenerates quickly, even if part of it is completely torn off.
column23: deBei spätsommerlichen Strandbesuchen kann man Gruppen von Sterndu begegnen, deren Kerne in gleichmäßigem Rhythmus aufblinken.
column24: enThis Pokémon gets nibbled on by Lumineon and others. Thanks to its red core, it regenerates fast, so it’s unconcerned by their snack attacks.
column25: deEs taucht in großer Zahl an Ufern auf. Nachts erstrahlt der Kern in seiner Mitte in einem unheimlichen Rot.
column26: enIf its body is torn, it can grow back if the red core remains. The core flashes at midnight.
column27: enSTARYU apparently communicates with the stars in the night sky by flashing the red core at the center of its body. If parts of its body are torn, this POKéMON simply regenerates the missing pieces and limbs.
column28: deEs wird ab und zu von Lumineon angeknabbert, aber solange es seinen roten Kern hat, kann es den abgebissenen Teil sofort wiederherstellen.
column29: enStaryu’s center section has an organ called the core that shines bright red. If you go to a beach toward the end of summer, the glowing cores of these Pokémon look like the stars in the sky.
column30: enAn enigmatic POKéMON that can effortlessly regenerate any appendage it loses in battle.
column31: deSelbst wenn es zerstückelt wird, kann es seinen Körper immer wieder regenerieren, solange sein Kernstück unversehrt bleibt.
column32: enIt gathers with others in the night and makes its red core glow on and off with the twinkling stars. It can regenerate limbs if they are severed from its body.
column33: deIhm macht es nichts aus, von Fisch-Pokémon angeknabbert zu werden, da sich sein Körper bei Verletzungen im Nu regeneriert.
column34: enStaryu apparently communicates with the stars in the night sky by flashing the red core at the center of its body. If parts of its body are torn, this Pokémon simply regenerates the missing pieces and limbs.
column35: enEven if its body is torn, it can regenerate as long as the glowing central core remains intact.
column36: deSterndu kommuniziert mit den Sternen am Nachthimmel, indem es den roten Kern in seiner Körpermitte blinken lässt. Wenn Teile seines Körpers beschädigt wurden, stellt es die fehlenden Teile selbst wieder her.
column37: deEs taucht in großer Anzahl an Ufern auf. Nachts leuchtet der Kern in seiner Mitte rot auf.
column38: deEiner weitverbreiteten Volkssage zufolge ist Sterndu aus Sternennebel entstanden, der ins Meer gefallen war.
column39: deAuch wenn sein Körper nicht mehr intakt ist, kann es sich regenerieren, wenn der Kern leuchtet.

column8: enStarmie swims through water by spinning its star-shaped body as if it were a propeller on a ship. The core at the center of this Pokémon’s body glows in seven colors.
column9: deDen Mittelteil seines Körpers nennt man „Kern“. Man sagt, es würde damit kommunizieren, wenn dieser in den Regenbogenfarben leuchtet.
column10: enStarmie swims by spinning its body at high speed. As this Pokémon cruises through the ocean, it absorbs tiny plankton.
column11: deStarmie bewegt sich im Wasser fort, indem es seinen Körper mit hoher Geschwindigkeit rotieren lässt. Unterwegs absorbiert es winziges Plankton.
column12: enThis Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.
column13: enRegardless of the environment it lives in, its body grows to form a symmetrical geometric shape.
column14: deStarmie kann schwimmen, indem es seinen sternförmigen Körper wie eine Schiffsschraube dreht. Der Kern in seiner Körpermitte leuchtet in sieben Farben.
column15: enIts shining core is thought to receive and transmit enigmatic signals. It has been known to cause headaches in those who approach it.
column16: deSein leuchtender Kern, das „Juwel des Meeres“, ist so wertvoll, dass manche ihn zu Schmuck verarbeiten und unter der Hand verkaufen.
column17: enThe center section is named the core. People think it is communicating when it glows in seven colors.
column18: enIts unusual body shape, reminiscent of abstract art, led local people to spread rumors that this Pokémon may be an invader from outer space.
column19: enIt rotates its geometrically shaped body to swim through the water. It always seems to be sending out mysterious radio waves.
column20: enThe middle section of its body is called the core. It glows in a different color each time it is seen.
column21: enSTARMIE’s center section - the core - glows brightly in seven colors. Because of its luminous nature, this POKéMON has been given the nickname “the gem of the sea.”
column22: deEs rotiert seinen geometrischen Körper, um sich im Wasser fortzubewegen. Wie es scheint, sendet es immerzu mysteriöse Radiowellen aus.
column23: enIts sparkling core is called “the gem of the sea.” This core can be made into high-priced accessories that are traded in secret.
column24: enPeople in ancient times imagined that STARMIE were transformed from the reflections of stars that twinkled on gentle waves at night.
column25: enIt is said that it uses the seven-colored core of its body to send electric waves into outer space.
column26: enStarmie’s center section—the core—glows brightly in seven colors. Because of its luminous nature, this Pokémon has been given the nickname “the gem of the sea.”
column27: enSTARMIE swims through water by spinning its star-shaped body as if it were a propeller on a ship. The core at the center of this POKéMON’s body glows in seven colors.
column28: deStarmies Mittelteil, sein Kern, leuchtet in sieben Farben. Aufgrund dieser Eigenschaft wird es auch „Juwel des Meeres“ genannt.
column29: enThis POKéMON has a geometric body. Because of its body, the locals suspect that it is an alien creature.
column30: deOffenbar sendet und empfängt sein leuchtender Kern mysteriöse Radiowellen. Kommt man ihm zu nahe, löst es eventuell Kopfschmerzen aus.
column31: deDas als Kern bezeichnete Organ dieses Pokémon erstrahlt in den sieben Farben des Regenbogens, wenn Starmie mächtige Psycho-Kräfte entfesselt.
column32: enThe center section of its body is called the core. It glows in a different color each time it is seen.
column33: enThe center section is named the core. People think it is communicating when it glows in 7 colors.
column34: enIts core shines in many colors and sends radio signals into space to communicate with something.
column35: deAufgrund seiner ungewöhnlichen Körperform hegen viele Einheimische den Verdacht, es sei in Wirklichkeit ein Eindringling aus dem All.
column36: enAt the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.
column37: deDer Kern dieses Pokémon leuchtet in den Farben des Regenbogens. Sein Kern gilt als Edelstein.
column38: enIts central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value the core as a gem.
column39: deIn der Körpermitte befindet sich ein roter Kern, der mysteriöse Radiowellen in die Nacht sendet.
