column8: enIt shapes an invisible wall in midair by minutely vibrating its fingertips to stop molecules in the air.
column9: enIf interrupted while it is miming, it will suddenly DOUBLESLAP the offender with its broad hands.
column10: enEmanations from its fingertips solidify the air into invisible walls that repel even harsh attacks.
column11: enMr. Mime is a master of pantomime. Its gestures and motions convince watchers that something unseeable actually exists. Once the watchers are convinced, the unseeable thing exists as if it were real.
column12: enAlways practicing its pantomime act. It makes enemies believe something exists that really doesn’t.
column13: deViele Forscher glauben, seine Hände hätten nur deshalb so eine beachtliche Größe angenommen, damit es Pantomime praktizieren kann.
column14: dePantimos ist begabt im Mienenspiel und trainiert dieses jederzeit. Es kann andere dazu verleiten, Dinge zu sehen, die gar nicht existieren.
column15: enA MR. MIME is a master of pantomime. It can convince others that something unseeable actually exists. Once believed, the imaginary object does become real.
column16: enIt is adept at conning people. It is said to be able to create walls out of thin air by miming.
column17: enIt creates invisible walls with its pantomiming. If you don’t act impressed, it will attack you with a double slap!
column18: enIts pantomime skills are wonderful. You may become enraptured while watching it, but next thing you know, Mr. Mime has made a real wall.
column19: deDurch Pantomime lässt es unsichtbare Wände entstehen. Wer davon nicht beeindruckt ist, wird mit einem Duplexhieb bestraft.
column20: enIts fingertips emit a peculiar force field that hardens air to create an actual wall.
column21: enIf interrupted while it is miming, it will slap around the offender with its broad hands.
column22: enIt’s known for its top-notch pantomime skills. It protects itself from all sorts of attacks by emitting auras from its fingers to create walls.
column23: deEs ist geschickt darin, Menschen zu beeinflussen. Man sagt, es könne dünne Wände aus Luft nur durch Mimen entstehen lassen.
column24: enAlways practices its pantomime act. It makes enemies believe something exists that really doesn't.
column25: enA skilled mime from birth, it gains the ability to create invisible objects as it matures.
column26: enThe behavior of this clown-like Pokémon reminds one of pantomime. It creates invisible walls using a force emitted from its fingertips.
column27: dePantimos ist ein Meister der Pantomime. Seine Gesten und Bewegungen machen den Zuschauer glauben, dass etwas Unsichtbares tatsächlich existiert. Wenn ein Objekt für real gehalten wird, beginnt es, wirklich zu existieren.
column28: deEs ist für die Pantomime geboren. Zur Abwehr unterschiedlichster Angriffe erzeugt es mit einem Kraftfeld aus seinen Fingerspitzen Schutzwände.
column29: enMR. MIME is a master of pantomime. Its gestures and motions convince watchers that something unseeable actually exists. Once it is believed, it will exist as if it were a real thing.
column30: deSeine Fingerspitzen sondern etwas ab, das die Luft zu einer schützenden Wand werden lässt.
column31: enIt uses the mysterious power it has in its fingers to solidify air into an invisible wall.
column32: deSein Können als Pantomime ist verblüffend. Vor lauter Bewunderung merkt man gar nicht, wie plötzlich eine echte Wand vor einem erscheint.
column33: enThe broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.
column34: enIt is a pantomime expert that can create invisible but solid walls using miming gestures.

column8: enWhile young, they live together deep in the mountains, training themselves in how to fight with their scythes and move at high speeds.
column9: enIts two sharp scythes are more than just weapons. It uses them with dexterity to dress its prey before eating.
column10: deEs zerreißt und zerkleinert seine Beute mit seinen unglaublich scharfen Sicheln. Selten breitet es seine Flügel aus, um zu fliegen.
column11: enIts blindingly fast speed adds to the sharpness of its twin forearm scythes. The scythes can slice through thick logs in one wicked stroke.
column12: enSCYTHER is blindingly fast. Its blazing speed enhances the effectiveness of the twin scythes on its forearms. This POKéMON’s scythes are so effective, they can slice through thick logs in one wicked stroke.
column13: deMit jedem Kampf werden seine Sicheln schärfer. Es kann selbst einen massiven Baumstamm mit nur einem Schnitt zerteilen.
column14: deSeine schnellen, Ninja-artigen Reflexe irritieren seine Beute. Findet es eine Lücke in ihrer Defensive, schlitzt es sie mit seinen Sicheln auf.
column15: enThe sharp scythes on its forearms become increasingly sharp by cutting through hard objects.
column16: enScyther is blindingly fast. Its blazing speed enhances the effectiveness of the twin scythes on its forearms. This Pokémon’s scythes are so effective, they can slice through thick logs in one wicked stroke.
column17: deMit enormer Schnelligkeit zerkleinert es alles in seinem Umfeld. Unter dem Spitznamen „Ninja“ erfreut es sich in Alola großer Beliebtheit.
column18: deSichlor gleicht einem Ninja, wenn es plötzlich aus dem hohen Gras springt und mit seinen scharfen, sichelförmigen Armen angreift.
column19: deSichlor ist unheimlich schnell. Dadurch werden die beiden Sensen an seinen Unterarmen noch effektiver. Dieses Pokémon kann mit seinen Sensen in Sekundenschnelle Baumstämme zerteilen.
column20: enWith ninja-like agility and speed, it can create the illusion that there is more than one.
column21: enSome call it a ninja. Its movements— imperceptibly quick—are sufficient to cleave the air in two. It’s very popular in Alola.
column22: enIt tears and shreds prey with its wickedly sharp scythes. It very rarely spreads its wings to fly.
column23: enIt is nearly impossible to parry its attacking scythes. Its movements are like a ninja’s.
column24: enWith ninja-like agility and speed, it can create the illusion that there is more than one of itself.
column25: enThe large, wickedly sharp scythes on its forearms are truly fearsome weapons. Prey's attempts to flee are unfailingly thwarted by this Pokémon's nimble motions.
column26: enIt's very proud of its speed. It moves so fast that its opponent does not even know what knocked it down.
column27: deEntdeckt man im Wald ein Gebiet, in dem besonders viele Bäume gefällt wurden, befindet man sich im Revier eines Sichlors.
column28: enLeaps out of tall grass and slices prey with its scythes. The movement looks like that of a ninja.
column29: deJunge Sichlor wohnen in Gruppen tief in den Bergen. Dort trainieren sie ihre Schnelligkeit und üben den Kampf mit Sicheln.
column30: enIf you come across an area in a forest where a lot of the trees have been cut down, what you’ve found is a Scyther’s territory.
column31: deEs bewegt sich mit seinen Sensen so schnell durch das Gras, dass es kein Mensch sehen kann.
column32: enWhen it moves, it leaves only a blur. If it hides in grass, its protective coloration makes it invisible.
column33: enAs Scyther fights more and more battles, its scythes become sharper and sharper. With a single slice, Scyther can fell a massive tree.
column34: enWhen it moves, it leaves only a blur. If it hides in grass, its protective colors make it invisible.
column35: enIt confuses its prey with its quick, ninja-like movements. Then, in an instant, it cleaves them with its scythes.
column36: deSeine scharfen Sicheln sind nicht nur Waffen. Vor einer Mahlzeit filetiert es damit auch geschickt seine Beute.
column37: enIt slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

column8: enIt has several different cry patterns, each of which seems to have its own meaning.
column9: enIt rocks its body rhythmically. It appears to alter the rhythm depending on how it is feeling.
column10: enA JYNX sashays rhythmically as if it were dancing. Its motions are so bouncingly alluring, people seeing it are compelled to shake their hips without noticing.
column11: deEs bewegt seine Hüften in einem eigentümlichen Rhythmus. Rossana in Alola beherrschen einen besonders anmutigen Hüftschwung.
column12: deIn einer bestimmten Gegend von Galar wurde Rossana einst als „Königin des Eises“ gleichermaßen gefürchtet wie verehrt.
column13: deEs scheint sich mit einem besonderen Rhythmus zu bewegen. Beim Laufen schwingt es die Hüften und sieht so aus, als würde es tanzen.
column14: enIt wiggles its hips as it walks. It can cause people to dance in unison with it.
column15: enIt speaks using a language that sounds human. Research is under way to determine what is being said.
column16: enIts cries sound like human speech. However, it is impossible to tell what it is trying to say.
column17: enThe Jynx of Galar often have beautiful and delicate voices. Some of these Pokémon have even gathered a fan base.
column18: deViele Rossana in Galar verfügen über solch eine schöne und zarte Stimme, dass sie sogar eine Fangemeinde haben.
column19: enAppears to move to a rhythm of its own, as if it were dancing. It wiggles its hips as it walks.
column20: enJynx walks rhythmically, swaying and shaking its hips as if it were dancing. Its motions are so bouncingly alluring, people seeing it are compelled to shake their hips without giving any thought to what they are doing.
column21: deSein sonderbarer Ruf klingt fast wie die Sprache der Menschen. Manche Musiker schreiben sogar eigens Lieder, damit Rossana sie singen kann.
column22: enIt speaks a language similar to that of humans. However, it seems to use dancing to communicate.
column23: deEs spricht und benutzt dabei eine Sprache, die der des Menschen sehr ähnelt. Noch wird erforscht, was es wirklich spricht.
column24: enIts strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.
column25: enJYNX walks rhythmically, swaying and shaking its hips as if it were dancing. Its motions are so bouncingly alluring, people seeing it are compelled to shake their hips without giving any thought to what they are doing.
column26: deRossana bewegt sich rhythmisch fort. Es wiegt und schwingt seine Hüften, als würde es tanzen. Seine Bewegungen wirken so anziehend auf Menschen, dass diese beginnen, mit den Hüften zu schwingen, ohne darüber nachzudenken.
column27: enIt seductively wiggles its hips as it walks. It can cause people to dance in unison with it.
column28: enIt sways its hips to a rhythm all its own. The precise movements of Jynx living in Alola are truly wonderful.
column29: deDer beschwingte Gang dieses Pokémon bezaubert Zuschauer und lässt sie im Takt dazu tanzen.
column30: enIn certain parts of Galar, Jynx was once feared and worshiped as the Queen of Ice.

column8: deDie Menge an Elektrizität, die aus seinem Körper austritt, ist sehr viel größer als jene, die es über seine Nahrung zu sich nimmt.
column9: enNormally found near power plants, they can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.
column10: deEs wird oft für Stromausfälle verantwortlich gemacht, doch viel häufiger handelt es sich um eine Folge menschlichen Fehlverhaltens.
column11: deEs konsumiert am liebsten Elektrizität. Gelegentlich sieht man es in der Nähe von Kraftwerken.
column12: enHalf of all sudden blackouts are caused by Electabuzz gathering at electric power plants and gobbling up electricity.
column13: enIts body constantly discharges electricity. Getting close to it will make your hair stand on end.
column14: deIn vielen Elektrizitätswerken werden Pokémon vom Typ Boden eingesetzt, um Elektek davon abzuhalten, den dortigen Strom anzuzapfen.
column15: enIt windmills its arms to slightly boost its punches. Foes have been known to escape in the meantime.
column16: enWhen a storm arrives, gangs of this POKéMON compete with each other to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning bolts. Some towns use ELECTABUZZ in place of lightning rods.
column17: deFür die Hälfte aller plötzlichen Stromausfälle sind Elektek verantwortlich. Sie belagern gerne Kraftwerke und zapfen ihnen Strom ab.
column18: deBei Gewittern versammeln sich Elektek in der Nähe von hohen Bäumen, wo sie regungslos auf Blitzeinschläge warten.
column19: enElectricity runs across the surface of its body. In darkness, its entire body glows a whitish-blue.
column20: enElectricity permeates its body. It swings its arms round and round to charge up electricity before unleashing a punch.
column21: enWith the coming of a storm, many of these Pokémon will gather under tall trees and sit there waiting for lightning to strike.
column22: enIf a major power outage occurs, it is certain that this Pokémon has eaten electricity at a power plant.
column23: enFeeds on electrical energy. During sudden showers beneath looming thunderclouds, one can observe Electabuzz scaling tall trees, where the Pokémon will then wait for lightning to strike.
column24: deElektrizität bedeckt seinen Körper. Im Dunkeln erstrahlt es in einem bläulichen Ton.
column25: enWhile it’s often blamed for power outages, the truth is the cause of outages is more often an error on the part of the electric company.
column26: enIf a major power outage occurs, it is certain that this POKéMON has eaten electricity at a power plant.
column27: enResearch is progressing on storing lightning in Electabuzz so this energy can be used at any time.
column28: enWhen a storm arrives, gangs of this Pokémon compete with each other to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning bolts. Some towns use Electabuzz in place of lightning rods.
column29: enIt loves to feed on strong electricity. It occasionally appears around large power plants and so on.
column30: enHalf of all blackouts occur when this Pokémon appears at power plants and eats electricity.
column31: deBei Gewitter entbrennt unter Elektek ein Wettstreit um höher gelegene Orte, an denen ein Blitzeinschlag wahrscheinlich ist. Einige Ortschaften benutzen diese Pokémon anstelle von Blitzableitern.
column32: enWhen a storm approaches, it competes with others to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning. Some towns use ELECTABUZZ in place of lightning rods.
column33: enWhen two ELECTABUZZ touch, they control the electric currents to communicate their feelings.
column34: enElectricity leaks from it in amounts far greater than the amount of electricity it eats.
column35: deSein ganzer Körper speichert Elektrizität. Vor einem Schlag lädt es seine Arme mit kreisförmigen Bewegungen elektrisch auf.
column36: enMany power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.
column37: deGrößere Stromausfälle sind fast immer darauf zurückzuführen, dass ein Elektek den Strom eines Kraftwerks einfach gefressen hat.
column38: enElectricity runs across the surface of its body. In darkness, its entire body glows a whitish blue.

column8: enThe scorching fire exhaled by Magmar forms heat waves around its body, making it hard to see the Pokémon clearly.
column9: enWhen it’s tired, it leaps into the mouth of a volcano and soaks its body in magma to ease its weariness. Its body burns at 2,192 degrees F.
column10: deMagmar erlegt seine Beute mit Feuer. Manchmal röstet es diese zu seinem Bedauern so stark, dass sie versehentlich verkohlt.
column11: deIst es wütend, stößt es glühend heiße Flammen aus. Es hört damit erst auf, wenn von seinem Gegner nur noch Asche übrig ist.
column12: enIt dislikes cold places, so it blows scorching flames to make the environment suitable for itself.
column13: enIn battle, Magmar blows out intensely hot flames from all over its body to intimidate its opponent. This Pokémon’s fiery bursts create heat waves that ignite grass and trees in its surroundings.
column14: enIt moves more frequently in hot areas. It can heal itself by dipping its wound into lava.
column15: enFound near the mouth of a volcano. This fire-breather’s body temperature is nearly 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit.
column16: enIts body always burns with an orange glow that enables it to hide perfectly amidst flames.
column17: deJe heißer die Umgebung, desto besser ist Magmar in Form. Im Vergleich zu anderen Regionen ist es in Alola besonders stark.
column18: deMit seinem brennenden Körper und glühenden Atem heizt es seine Umgebung auf. Beim Niesen stößt es Flammen aus.
column19: enIn battle, MAGMAR blows out intensely hot flames from all over its body to intimidate its opponent. This POKéMON’s fiery bursts create heat waves that ignite grass and trees in its surroundings.
column20: deWenn es erschöpft ist, springt es in einen Vulkankrater und entspannt sich in der Lava. Sein brennender Körper ist 1200 °C heiß.
column21: deEs wurde bei einem Vulkanausbruch geboren. Sein Körper ist von hellen Flammen umgeben.
column22: enWhen it breathes deeply, heat waves form around its body, making it hard to see clearly.
column23: enIts entire body is burning. When it breathes, the temperature rises. When it sneezes, flames shoot out!
column24: enThe fiery surface of its body gives off a wavering, rippling glare that is similar to the sun.
column25: enThese Pokémon’s bodies are constantly burning. Magmar are feared as one of the causes behind fires.
column26: enMagmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.
column27: deMagmar werden in Vulkankratern geboren. Sie sehen aus wie Feuerbälle, da ihr ganzer Körper in Flammen gehüllt ist.
column28: enThe hotter the place, the better they feel. Magmar in Alola are said to be hardier than those in other areas.
column29: deSein Körper ist ständig in Flammen gehüllt, wodurch es als eine Ursache von Bränden gefürchtet wird.
column30: deIm Kampf stößt Magmar glühend heiße Flammen aus, um seinen Gegner einzuschüchtern. Diese Feuersalven erzeugen Hitzewellen, die das Gras und die Bäume der Umgebung in Brand setzen.
column31: deEs wurde in der Nähe eines Vulkans gefunden. Die Körpertemperatur dieses Feuerspuckers liegt bei fast 1 200 °C.
column32: enBorn in the spout of a volcano, its body is covered by flames that shimmer like the sun.
column33: enWhen angered, it spouts brilliant fire from all over its body. It doesn’t calm down until its opponent has burned to ash.
column34: enIn battle, it blows out intense flames from all over its body to intimidate its foe. These fiery bursts create heat waves that ignite grass and trees in the area.
column35: enIts body always burns with an orange glow that enables it to hide perfectly among flames.
column36: enBorn in an active volcano. Its body is always cloaked in flames, so it looks like a big ball of fire.
column37: enLegend has it that this Pokémon was born from the crater of a volcano. When wounded, it bathes in lava to heal its body, much as one would soak in a hot spring.

column8: enGrips its prey in its pincers and squeezes hard! It can't move if it's cold, so it lives in warm places.
column9: enIf it fails to crush the foe in its pincers, it will swing around and toss the opponent.
column10: enWith its horns, it digs burrows to sleep in at night. In the morning, damp soil clings to its body.
column11: enIt swings its long pincer horns wildly to attack. During cold periods, it hides deep in forests.
column12: dePinsir nimmt Beute mit seinen Hörnern in die Zange, um sie anschließend entzweizureißen oder mit Wucht fortzuschleudern.
column13: enPINSIR has a pair of massive horns  Protruding from the surface of these horns are thorns. These thorns are driven deeply into the foe’s body when the pincer closes, making it tough for the foe to escape.
column14: enThis Pokémon clamps its pincers down on its prey and then either splits the prey in half or flings it away.
column15: enPINSIR is astoundingly strong. It can grip a foe weighing twice its weight in its horns and easily lift it. This POKéMON’s movements turn sluggish in cold places.
column16: enOne solid blow from its horns is enough to split apart a large tree. Its greatest rival in Alola is Vikavolt.
column17: enTheir pincers are strong enough to shatter thick logs. Because they dislike cold, PINSIR burrow and sleep under the ground on chilly nights.
column18: enIts two long pincer horns are powerful. Once they grip an enemy, they won’t release until the foe is torn.
column19: enGrips its prey in its pincers and squeezes hard! It can’t move if it’s cold out, so it lives in warm places.
column20: enThese Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.
column21: deEin Stoß mit seinen Hörnern reicht, um große Bäume zu zersplittern. In Alola ist Donarion sein größter Rivale.
column22: deDer Status eines Pinsirs hängt von seinen Hörnern ab. Je dicker und stattlicher diese sind, desto beliebter ist es beim anderen Geschlecht.
column23: dePinsir ist erstaunlich stark. Es kann einen Feind, der doppelt so viel wiegt wie es selbst, mit seinen Hörnern packen und hochheben. Bei niedrigen Temperaturen werden die Bewegungen dieses Pokémon schwerfällig.
column24: enIt grips prey with its powerful pincers and will not let go until the prey is torn in half.
column25: enIf it fails to crush the victim in its pincers, it will swing it around and toss it hard.
column26: deEs nimmt Gegner mit seinen langen Hörnern in die Zange und reißt sie damit entzwei. Es ist sehr stark, aber es verabscheut Kälte.
column27: enAlthough it’s tough, it can’t handle cold well. When night falls, it buries itself in leafage and sleeps.
column28: deEs liefert sich Revierkämpfe mit Donarion. Aus noch unbekannten Gründen versteht es sich in Alola ausgesprochen gut mit Skaraborn.
column29: enWhen the temperature drops at night, it sleeps on treetops or among roots where it is well hidden.
column30: enIt grips prey with its pincers until the prey is torn in half. What it can’t tear, it tosses far.
column31: enSwings its long antlers wildly to attack. During cold periods, it hides deep in forests.
column32: deBeim Angriff verdreht es sein Geweih. In Kälteperioden versteckt es sich tief im Wald.
column33: dePinsir hat ein Paar massive Hörner. Aus diesen ragen Dornen hervor, die sich in den Körper seines Gegners hineinbohren, wenn sich die Zange schließt, und ihm das Entkommen schwer machen.
column34: enWith its pincer horns, it digs burrows to sleep in at night. In the morning, damp soil clings to its body.
column35: deEs hält seine Beute mit seiner Zange fest und teilt sie dann. Was es nicht teilen kann, wirft es fort.
column36: enIt grips its prey in its pincers and splits them apart. Although it is a powerful Pokémon, it can’t deal with the cold.
column37: dePinsir nimmt Gegner mit seinen Hörnern in die Zange und drückt kräftig zu. Es lebt an warmen Orten, da es bei niedrigen Temperaturen erstarrt.
column38: deEs ist zwar robust, verträgt jedoch keine Kälte. Bei Anbruch der Nacht vergräbt es sich im Laub und schläft.
column39: enIt gets into territorial disputes with Vikavolt. For some reason, it apparently gets along well with Heracross in Alola.
column40: enPinsir has a pair of massive horns. Protruding from the surface of these horns are thorns. These thorns are driven deeply into the foe’s body when the pincer closes, making it tough for the foe to escape.
column41: enPinsir is astoundingly strong. It can grip a foe weighing twice its weight in its horns and easily lift it. This Pokémon’s movements turn sluggish in cold places.

column8: enWhen it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.
column9: enThey live in groups. The one with the longest, thickest, and most-scarred horns is the boss of the herd.
column10: deEs kämpft mit vollem Einsatz, nachdem es sich mit seinen drei Schweifen auspeitschte, um sich anzustacheln.
column11: enHistorically, people in areas all over the world have ridden Tauros, but the practice is said to have started in Alola.
column12: enThis POKéMON is not satisfied unless it is rampaging at all times. If there is no opponent for TAUROS to battle, it will charge at thick trees and knock them down to calm itself.
column13: enA rowdy Pokémon with a lot of stamina. Once running, it won’t stop until it hits something.
column14: enWhen Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.
column15: enThey fight each other by locking horns. The herd's protector takes pride in its battle-scarred horns.
column16: deEs ist ungestüm und strotzt nur so vor Kraft. Wenn es erst einmal losprescht, rennt es so lange weiter, bis es mit etwas zusammenstößt.
column17: enIt is not satisfied unless it is rampaging at all times. If there is no opponent for TAUROS to battle, it will charge at thick trees and knock them down to calm itself.
column18: enThis Pokémon is not satisfied unless it is rampaging at all times. If there is no opponent for Tauros to battle, it will charge at thick trees and knock them down to calm itself.
column19: enA rowdy POKéMON with a lot of stamina. Once running, it won't stop until it hits something.
column20: deDie Tauros in Galar haben ein ungestümes Temperament und gestatten es Menschen nicht, auf ihrem Rücken zu reiten.
column21: enAlthough it’s known to be a fierce Pokémon, Tauros in the Alola region are said to possess a measure of calmness.
column22: deDieses Pokémon ist nur zufrieden, wenn es kämpfen kann. Wenn Tauros keinen Gegner findet, sucht es sich robuste Bäume und haut sie um, um sich abzureagieren.
column23: enThese violent POKéMON fight with other members of their herd in order to prove their strength.
column24: deObwohl es sonst für seine ungestüme Art bekannt ist, gelten Exemplare aus Alola als vergleichsweise gelassen.
column25: enWhen it is about to tackle, it whips its body repeatedly with its three long tails.
column26: deWenn es Tackle einsetzt, geißelt es mit seinen drei Schwänzen wiederholt seinen Körper.
column27: enThe Tauros of the Galar region are volatile in nature, and they won’t allow people to ride on their backs.
column28: enThe climate seems to be related to the reason Tauros in Alola are a little calmer than those in other regions.
column29: deTauros in Alola sind etwas sanftmütiger als in anderen Regionen. Dies ist offenbar auf das Klima zurückzuführen.
column30: dePeitscht es seinen Körper mit seinen Schweifen aus, ist Vorsicht geboten, denn es steht kurz davor, mit Karacho auf sein Ziel loszustürmen.
column31: enThey fight each other by locking horns. The herd’s protector takes pride in its battle-scarred horns.
column32: deSchon seit vielen Jahren reiten Menschen überall auf der Welt auf Tauros. Diese Tradition hat angeblich in Alola ihren Ursprung genommen.
column33: enAfter heightening its will to fight by whipping itself with its three tails, it charges at full speed.
column34: enWhen it is about to TACKLE, it whips its body repeatedly with its three long tails.
column35: enOnce it takes aim at its foe, it makes a headlong charge. It is famous for its violent nature.
column36: deSie leben in Herden. Ihr Anführer ist das Tauros mit den kräftigsten und längsten Hörnern sowie den meisten Kampfspuren darauf.

column8: enThis weak and pathetic POKéMON gets easily pushed along rivers when there are strong currents.
column9: enAn underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions, but usually not more than seven feet.
column10: enIt is said to be the world’s weakest Pokémon. No one knows why it has managed to survive.
column11: enIts reckless leaps make it easy pickings for predators. On the bright side, many Pokémon enjoy longer life spans, thanks to Magikarp.
column12: deKarpador ist im Kampf praktisch nutzlos, da es nur platschen kann. Daher wird es als schwach eingeschätzt. Es ist aber tatsächlich sehr robust und kann in jedem Gewässer überleben, egal, wie verseucht dieses ist.
column13: deDieses Pokémon ist so schwach und erbärmlich, dass es sich in Gewässern mit starker Strömung einfach vom Wasser treiben lässt.
column14: enIts swimming muscles are weak, so it is easily washed away by currents. In places where water pools, you can see many MAGIKARP deposited there by the flow.
column15: enMagikarp is virtually useless in battle as it can only splash around. As a result, it is considered to be weak. However, it is actually a very hardy Pokémon that can survive in any body of water no matter how polluted it is.
column16: enIt is virtually worthless in terms of both power and speed. It is the most weak and pathetic POKéMON in the world.
column17: enMAGIKARP is virtually useless in battle as it can only splash around. As a result, it is considered to be weak. However, it is actually a very hardy POKéMON that can survive in any body of water no matter how polluted it is.
column18: enMagikarp is a pathetic excuse for a Pokémon that is only capable of flopping and splashing. This behavior prompted scientists to undertake research into it.
column19: enFamous for being very unreliable. It can be found swimming in seas, lakes, rivers and shallow puddles.
column20: enIn the distant past, they were fairly strong, but they have become gradually weaker over time.
column21: deDa es immer nur leichtsinnig herumplatscht, ist es leichte Beute für hungrige Pokémon. Für viele ist es daher ein Lebensretter.
column22: enFamous for being very unreliable. It can be found swimming in seas, lakes, rivers, and shallow puddles.
column23: enIt is unable to swim against even slow-moving currents. It always splashes about for some reason.
column24: deEs ist schwach und unzuverlässig, aber es vermehrt sich unglaublich schnell. Deshalb trifft man es auch öfter an, als einem lieb ist.
column25: enIt is virtually worthless in terms of both power and speed. It is the most weak and pathetic Pokémon in the world.
column26: deKarpador ist ein armseliges Pokémon, das nur platschen kann. Sein merkwürdiges Verhalten hat so manchen Wissenschaftler zum Forschen angeregt.
column27: enA feeble, pitiful imbecile of a Pokémon that is nonetheless very hardy. Unperturbed by turbid water, it can be found living in all sorts of places.
column28: enThis weak and pathetic Pokémon gets easily pushed along rivers when there are strong currents.
column29: deKarpador ist für seine Nutzlosigkeit bekannt. Ob Meer, Fluss, See oder Pfütze, es schwimmt durch die verschiedensten Gewässer.
column30: enAn underpowered, pathetic POKéMON. It may jump high on rare occasions, but never more than seven feet.
column31: enIn the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.
column32: deDie urzeitlichen Vorfahren dieses Pokémon waren sehr viel stärker als ihre heutigen Nachkommen.
column33: enMAGIKARP is a pathetic excuse for a POKéMON that is only capable of flopping and splashing. This behavior prompted scientists to undertake research into it.
column34: deEs ist nutzlos, was Kraft und Geschwindigkeit angeht. Dieses ist das schwächste und erbärmlichste Pokémon der Welt.
column35: enA Magikarp living for many years can leap a mountain using Splash. The move remains useless, though.
column36: enFor no reason, it jumps and splashes about, making it easy for predators like PIDGEOTTO to catch it mid-jump.
column37: enThanks to their strong hold on life, dirty water doesn’t bother them at all. They live in waters all over the world!
column38: enA MAGIKARP living for many years can leap a mountain using Splash. The move remains useless, though.
column39: deEs ist nutzlos, was Kraft und Geschwindigkeit angeht. Dieses Pokémon ist das schwächste und erbärmlichste der Welt.
column40: deUnbekümmert strotzt es selbst in verseuchten Gewässern nur so vor Lebenskraft. Man findet es in allen Teilen der Welt in Ufernähe.
column41: enAlthough weak and helpless, this Pokémon is incredibly fertile. They exist in such multitudes, you’ll soon grow tired of seeing them.
column42: deVor langer, langer Zeit war es anscheinend ziemlich stark, doch es wurde zusehends schwächer und schwächer.

column8: enIt appears whenever there is world conflict, burning down any place it travels through.
column9: enIt has an extremely aggressive nature. The HYPER BEAM it shoots from its mouth totally incinerates all targets.
column10: enOnce it appears, its rage never settles until it has razed the fields and mountains around it.
column11: deDie bei der Entwicklung freigesetzte Energie stimuliert seine Gehirnzellen und verleiht ihm eine sehr zügellose, gewalttätige Natur.
column12: deWütet ein Garados, wird es alles niederbrennen. Dies geschieht selbst während eines Sturms.
column13: deWenn Garados in Rage gerät, ist sein wildes Blut kaum zu beruhigen, bis es alles niedergebrannt hat. Aufzeichnungen belegen, dass die Zerstörungswut dieses Pokémon einen ganzen Monat anhalten kann.
column14: enThey say that during past strife, GYARADOS would appear and leave blazing ruins in its wake.
column15: deEs ist von Natur aus sehr aggressiv. Der Hyperstrahl, den es aus seinem Maul verschießt, äschert seine Gegner ein.
column16: enOnce it begins to rampage, a Gyarados will burn everything down, even in a harsh storm.
column17: enBrutally vicious and enormously destructive. Known for totally destroying cities in ancient times.
column18: enIn ancient literature, there is a record of a Gyarados that razed a village when violence flared.
column19: deEin gigantisches, sehr kraftvolles Pokémon. Es ist fähig, ganze Städte zu zerstören.
column20: enOnce it begins to rampage, a GYARADOS will burn everything down, even in a harsh storm.
column21: enOnce it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.
column22: enIt is an extremely vicious and violent POKéMON. When humans begin to fight, it will appear and burn everything to the ground with intensely hot flames.
column23: enIt has an extremely aggressive nature. The Hyper Beam it shoots from its mouth totally incinerates all targets.
column24: deIn alten Schriften wird von einem Garados berichtet, das in einem Wutanfall ein Dorf zerstörte.
column25: deManche Menschen sind fest davon überzeugt, dass Orte, an denen ein Garados erscheint, dem Untergang geweiht sind.
column26: enOnce GYARADOS goes on a rampage, its ferociously violent blood doesn’t calm until it has burned everything down. There are records of this POKéMON’s rampages lasting a whole month.
column27: enIt fires hyper beams in all directions, burning the surrounding area to ash. There are some regions where it’s called “the deity of destruction.”
column28: enThe energy from evolution stimulated its brain cells strongly, causing it to become very ferocious.
column29: enA tale is told of a town that angered Gyarados. Before the sun rose the next day, flames utterly consumed the town, leaving not a trace behind.
column30: deWenn sich Karpador zu Garados entwickelt, durchlaufen seine Gehirnzellen eine strukturelle Veränderung, was wohl der Grund für die zügellose, gewalttätige Natur dieses Pokémon ist.
column31: enRarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.
column32: deEs heißt, es habe eine ganze Stadt, deren Bewohner es verärgert hatten, über Nacht vollständig in Schutt und Asche gelegt.
column33: enIn ancient literature, there is a record of a GYARADOS that razed a village when violence flared.
column34: enWhen MAGIKARP evolves into GYARADOS, its brain cells undergo a structural transformation. It is said that this transformation is to blame for this POKéMON’s wildly violent nature.
column35: enThey say that during past wars, GYARADOS would appear and leave blazing ruins in its wake.
column36: deMit seinem Hyperstrahl legt es die Umgebung in Schutt und Asche. Manchmal wird es auch als „Gottheit der Zerstörung“ bezeichnet.
column37: enOnce Gyarados goes on a rampage, its ferociously violent blood doesn’t calm until it has burned everything down. There are records of this Pokémon’s rampages lasting a whole month.
column38: enI suspect this Pokémon to be the true identity of a dragon written of in ancient texts, which claimed that it razed an entire village with white-hot beams from its maw.
column39: deGarados ist brutal und zerstörungswütig. Es hat Aufzeichnungen zufolge in alten Zeiten ganze Städte in Schutt und Asche gelegt.
column40: enThere are people who swear that any place Gyarados appears is fated for destruction.
column41: enWhen Magikarp evolves into Gyarados, its brain cells undergo a structural transformation. It is said that this transformation is to blame for this Pokémon’s wildly violent nature.

column13: enPeople have driven LAPRAS almost to the point of extinction. In the evenings, this POKéMON is said to sing plaintively as it seeks what few others of its kind still remain.
column14: enIt loves crossing the sea with people and Pokémon on its back. It understands human speech.
column15: deEin intelligentes und herzensgutes Pokémon. Während es auf dem Meer schwimmt, lässt es seinen herrlichen Gesang erklingen.
column16: deEs wäre einst durch übermäßiges Fischen fast ausgestorben. Durch ein strenges Abkommen zum Artenschutz gibt es nun fast zu viele.
column17: enPeople have driven Lapras almost to the point of extinction. In the evenings, this Pokémon is said to sing plaintively as it seeks what few others of its kind still remain.
column18: enIts high intelligence enables it to comprehend human speech. When it’s in a good mood, it sings in its beautiful voice.
column19: enThey’ve been so cherished that there’s now an overabundance. The fish Pokémon population has declined in waters with too many Lapras.
column20: enA POKéMON that has been overhunted almost to extinction. It can ferry people across the water.
column21: deSie sind gutmütig. Da sie selten kämpfen, wurden sie oft gefangen. Ihre Anzahl ist stark reduziert.
column22: deDieses freundliche Pokémon besitzt ein sanftes Gemüt und versteht die Sprache der Menschen. Auf seinem Rücken trägt es sie über das Meer.
column23: deDie Menschen sind dafür verantwortlich, dass Lapras fast ausgestorben wäre. Abends singt es traurige Klagelieder, da nur noch wenige seiner Artgenossen übrig sind.
column24: enThey have gentle hearts. Because they rarely fight, many have been caught. Their number has dwindled.
column25: deAufgrund seiner hohen Intelligenz kann es die menschliche Sprache verstehen. Es mag es, Menschen auf seinem Rücken zu transportieren.

column35: enA smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.
column36: enThis gentle POKéMON loves to give people rides and provides a very comfortable way to get around.

column13: enIt can reconstitute its entire cellular structure to change into what it sees, but it returns to normal when it relaxes.
column14: enDitto rearranges its cell structure to transform itself into other shapes. However, if it tries to transform itself into something by relying on its memory, this Pokémon manages to get details wrong.
column15: enWhile it can transform into anything, each Ditto apparently has its own strengths and weaknesses when it comes to transformations.

column8: deEs kann sich auf verschiedene Weise entwickeln. Evolis genetischer Code ist der Schlüssel für das Geheimnis der Entwicklung von Pokémon.
column9: deNach derzeitigem Forschungsstand kann es sich zu acht verschiedenen Pokémon entwickeln.
column10: enPossessing an unbalanced and unstable genetic makeup, it conceals many possible evolutions.
column11: enHarbors the potential to evolve into manifold forms. Within Eevee lies the key to the mysteries of Pokémon evolution—I'm certain of it.
column12: enEEVEE has an unstable genetic makeup that suddenly mutates due to the environment in which it lives. Radiation from various STONES causes this POKéMON to evolve.
column13: enCurrent studies show it can evolve into an incredible eight different species of Pokémon.
column14: deEvolis genetisches Erbmaterial ist so instabil, dass die Umgebung plötzliche Mutationen auslösen kann. Unter anderem führt die Wirkung verschiedener Steine zur Entwicklung dieses Pokémon.
column15: enIt has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.
column16: enIts genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element STONEs.

column22: enAn OMASTAR uses its tentacles to capture its prey. It is believed to have become extinct because its shell grew too large, making its movements slow and ponderous.
column23: enIts sharp beak rings its mouth. Its shell was too big for it to move freely, so it became extinct.
column24: deAmoroso benutzt seine Tentakel, um Beute einzufangen. Man nimmt an, dass es ausgestorben ist, weil seine Schale zu groß und zu schwer geworden war, sodass seine Bewegungen immer langsamer und schwerfälliger wurden.
column25: enIts tentacles are highly developed as if they are hands and feet. As soon as it ensnares prey, it bites.
column26: enOnce wrapped around its prey, it never lets go. It eats the prey by tearing at it with sharp fangs.
column27: enOMASTAR uses its tentacles to capture its prey. It is believed to have become extinct because its shell grew too large and heavy, causing its movements to become too slow and ponderous.
column28: enDespite having strong fangs and tentacles, it went extinct when its heavy shell made it unable to catch prey.
column29: deMit seinen Tentakeln umklammerte es fest seine Beute, bevor es ihr mit seinen scharfen Zähnen den Garaus machte.
column30: enIt wraps its prey in its tentacles to immobilize them and then finishes them off with its sharp fangs.
column31: enApparently, it cracked SHELLDER's shell with its sharp fangs and sucked out the insides.
column32: deMit seinen scharfen Zähnen ist es imstande, selbst Felsen zu zermalmen. Angreifen kann es jedoch nur Beute in Reichweite seiner Tentakel.
column33: deHat es die Beute erst eingewickelt, lässt es nicht mehr los. Es verspeist sie, indem es sie zerreißt.

column8: enIt is a POKéMON that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in rare cases, living examples have been discovered. KABUTO have not changed for 300 million years.
column9: deKabuto erlebte seine Blütezeit vor 300 Millionen Jahren. Doch in einer bestimmten Region gibt es angeblich noch einige lebende Exemplare.
column10: enThis Pokémon was regenerated from the fossil of an ancient creature. It protects itself with a hard shell.
column11: deDieses Pokémon wurde aus dem Fossil einer altertümlichen Kreatur geschaffen. Es schützt sich mittels eines harten Panzers.
column12: enA POKéMON that was recovered from a fossil. It uses the eyes on its back while hiding on the sea floor.
column13: deDieses Pokémon ist so gut wie ausgestorben. Sein Panzer wird immer härter, indem es sich alle drei Tage häutet.
column14: deEs wurde aus einem Fossil zurückverwandelt. Aus Verstecken am Meeresboden spähte es mit den Augen auf seinem Rücken die Gegend aus.
column15: enKABUTO is a POKéMON that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in extremely rare cases, living examples have been discovered. The POKéMON has not changed at all for 300 million years.
column16: deEs heißt zwar, dass Kabuto ausgestorben sei, doch in einer bestimmten Gegend trifft man es noch relativ häufig an.
column17: enThis POKéMON lived in ancient times. On rare occasions, it has been discovered as a living fossil.
column18: deMan geht davon aus, dass dieses Pokémon vor 300 Millionen Jahren die Strände bevölkerte.
column19: enA POKéMON that was resurrected from a fossil found in what was once the ocean floor eons ago.
column20: enA POKéMON that was regenerated from a fossil found in what was once the ocean floor long ago.
column21: enThis Pokémon became extinct everywhere, except in a few areas. It protects itself with its hard shell.
column22: enThis Pokémon thrived 300 million years ago. It’s said that living specimens can still be seen in a certain region—a rare sight.
column23: enWhile some say this species has gone extinct, Kabuto sightings are apparently fairly common in some places.
column24: enA Pokémon that was recovered from a fossil. It used the eyes on its back while hiding on the seafloor.
column25: deDieses Pokémon ist überall ausgestorben, mit Ausnahme einer bestimmten Region. Es schützt sich mit einem harten Panzer.
column26: deKabuto ist ein Pokémon, das aus einem Fossil reproduziert wurde. Höchst selten wurden lebende Exemplare dieser Art entdeckt. Dieses Pokémon hat sich seit 300 Millionen Jahren überhaupt nicht verändert.
column27: enOn rare occasions, some have been found as fossils which they became while hiding on the ocean floor.
column28: enThree hundred million years ago, it hid on the sea floor. It also has eyes on its back that glow.
column29: enThis POKéMON was regenerated from the fossil of an ancient creature. It protects itself with a hard shell.
column30: enThis species is almost entirely extinct. Kabuto molt every three days, making their shells harder and harder.
column31: enKabuto is a Pokémon that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in extremely rare cases, living examples have been discovered. The Pokémon has not changed at all for 300 million years.
column32: enIt is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.
column33: enThis Pokémon lived in ancient times. On rare occasions, it has been discovered as a living fossil.

column8: deVor langer Zeit jagte Kabutops unter Wasser nach Beute. Es hat sich von einem Meeresbewohner zu einem Landbewohner entwickelt, was man an den Veränderungen seiner Kiemen und Beine sehen kann.
column9: deIm Wasser zieht es seine Beine an und bewegt seinen Panzer, um so schneller schwimmen zu können.
column10: enIt swims at speeds of roughly 29 knots, quickly closing in on its prey and slashing into them with its scythes to finish them off.
column11: enIt is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.
column12: enKabutops swam underwater to hunt for its prey in ancient times. The Pokémon was apparently evolving from being a water dweller to living on land as evident from the beginnings of change in its gills and legs.
column13: enIts sleek shape is perfect for swimming. It slashes prey with its claws and drains the body fluids.
column14: enKABUTOPS once swam underwater to hunt  for prey. It was apparently evolving from being a water dweller to living on land as evident from changes in its gills and legs.
column15: enA slim and fast swimmer. It sliced its prey with its sharp sickles and drank the body fluids.
column16: deMit einem Tempo von etwa 29 Knoten schwamm es zu seiner Beute und schlitzte sie mit seinen Sicheln auf, bevor es ihr den Gnadenstoß gab.
column17: deDieses Pokémon ist ein sehr guter Schwimmer. Es attackiert Gegner mit seinen Sichelarmen.
column18: enWith sharp claws, this ferocious, ancient POKéMON rips apart prey and sucks their body fluids.
column19: deKabutops schlitzt seine Beute auf und saugt ihre Körpersäfte aus. Die Überreste dienen als Nahrung für andere Pokémon.
column20: enIn the water, it tucks in its limbs to become more compact, then it wiggles its shell to swim fast.
column21: enA slim and fast swimmer. It slices its prey with its sharp sickles and drinks the body fluids.
column22: enIts body had begun to change so it could function on land. But it didn’t adapt in time and went extinct.
column23: enThe cause behind the extinction of this species is unknown. Kabutops were aggressive Pokémon that inhabited warm seas.
column24: deSein dünner Körper ließ es rasant schwimmen. Offenbar schlitzte es seine Beute mit seinen Sicheln auf und saugte ihr die Körpersäfte aus.
column25: enIt swims freely through water. It catches prey with its scythe-like arms and drains the victim’s fluids.
column26: deDieses Pokémon hatte begonnen, sich an das Leben an Land anzupassen, starb aber aus, bevor es den Wandel vollziehen konnte.
column27: enWith sharp claws, this ferocious, ancient Pokémon rips apart prey and sucks their body fluids.
column28: deEs ist unklar, warum dieses brutale urzeitliche Pokémon, das in warmen Meeren lebte, letztendlich ausstarb.
column29: enIt was able to swim quickly through the water by compactly folding up its razor-sharp sickles.
column30: enKabutops slices its prey apart and sucks out the fluids. The discarded body parts become food for other Pokémon.
column31: enIts sleek shape is perfect for swimming. It slashes prey with its claws and drains their fluids.
column32: enKABUTOPS swam underwater to hunt for its prey in ancient times. The POKéMON was apparently evolving from being a water-dweller to living on land as evident from the beginnings of change in its gills and legs.

column8: deAerodactyl ist ein Pokémon aus dem Zeitalter der Dinosaurier. Es wurde aus genetischem Material reproduziert, das aus Bernstein gewonnen wurde. Man geht davon aus, dass es in grauer Vorzeit der König der Lüfte war.
column9: enA ferocious, prehistoric POKéMON that goes for the enemy's throat with its serrated saw-like fangs.
column10: deDieses wilde Pokémon aus der Urzeit wurde mithilfe von in Bernstein eingeschlossenem Genmaterial wieder zum Leben erweckt.
column11: enRestored from DNA found in amber, this Pokémon exhibited ferocity that was greater than expected. Some casualties resulted.
column12: enAerodactyl’s sawlike fangs can shred skin to tatters—even the skin of Steel-type Pokémon.
column13: enA Pokémon that roamed the skies in the dinosaur era. Its teeth are like saw blades.
column14: enAERODACTYL is a POKéMON from the age of dinosaurs. It was regenerated from DNA extracted from amber. It is imagined to have been the king of the skies.
column15: enA savage POKéMON that died out in ancient times. It was resurrected using DNA taken from amber.
column16: enAerodactyl is a Pokémon from the age of dinosaurs. It was regenerated from genetic material extracted from amber. It is imagined to have been the king of the skies in ancient times.
column17: enA ferocious, prehistoric POKéMON that goes for the enemy’s throat with its serrated, sawlike fangs.
column18: enAERODACTYL is a POKéMON from the age of dinosaurs. It was regenerated from genetic material extracted from amber. It is imagined to have been the king of the skies in ancient times.
column19: enA vicious POKéMON from the distant past, it appears to have flown by spreading its wings and gliding.
column20: deDieses grausame, antike Pokémon flog durch die Luft und stieß sehr hohe und laute Schreie aus.
column21: deSelbst mit moderner Technologie scheint es unmöglich, ein perfektes Exemplar von diesem wilden urzeitlichen Pokémon zu reproduzieren.
column22: enIn ancient times, it ruled the skies. A widely accepted theory is that it went extinct due to a large meteor impact.
column23: enIn prehistoric times, this POKéMON flew freely and fearlessly through the skies.
column24: deZu Urzeiten war es der Herrscher der Lüfte. Es wird allgemein angenommen, dass es infolge eines Kometenabsturzes ausstarb.
column25: deEs stammt aus dem Zeitalter der Dinosaurier. Mit seinen Zähnen, die Sägeblättern gleichen, zerreißt es seine Beute, bevor es sie frisst.
column26: deMit seinen Zähnen, die Sägeblättern gleichen, kann es selbst die Haut von Stahl-Pokémon zerfetzen.
column27: deEin grausames Pokémon, das vielen das Leben schwer gemacht hat. Es wurde aus Genmaterial reproduziert, das in Bernstein enthalten war.
column28: enA vicious Pokémon from the distant past, it appears to have flown by spreading its wings and gliding.
column29: deDieses antike Pokémon flog einst gebieterisch durch die Lüfte. Auf dem Boden hingegen war es schwach und konnte nur langsam gehen.
column30: enThis vicious POKéMON is said to have flown in ancient skies while shrieking high-pitched cries.
column31: enA Pokémon from the age of the dinosaurs. It used its sawlike fangs to shred its prey before eating them.
column32: enIt was regenerated from a dinosaur’s genetic matter that was found in amber. It flies with high-pitched cries.
column33: enA savage Pokémon that died out in ancient times. It was resurrected using DNA taken from amber.
column34: enIt flew through the open skies over the ancient continent as if they were its own. When it touched ground, its walk was weak and slow.
column35: enThis is a ferocious Pokémon from ancient times. Apparently even modern technology is incapable of producing a perfectly restored specimen.
column36: deIn Bernstein eingeschlossenes genetisches Material eines Dinosauriers war der Ursprung dieses Pokémon. Beim Fliegen schreit es schrill.
column37: enThis vicious Pokémon is said to have flown in ancient skies while shrieking high-pitched cries.

column8: deRelaxo verschlingt sogar Nahrung, auf der sich etwas Schimmel gebildet hat. Trotzdem verdirbt es sich nie den Magen.
column9: enVery lazy. Just eats and sleeps. As its rotund bulk builds, it becomes steadily more slothful.
column10: deDer typische Tagesablauf von Relaxo besteht lediglich aus Essen und Schlafen. Es ist so zutraulich, dass Kinder seinen dicken Bauch als Platz zum Spielen nutzen.
column11: enSnorlax’s typical day consists of nothing more than eating and sleeping. It is such a docile Pokémon that there are children who use its expansive belly as a place to play.
column12: enIt is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.
column13: enIt doesn’t do anything other than eat and sleep. When prompted to make a serious effort, though, it apparently displays awesome power.
column14: enSNORLAX’s typical day consists of nothing more than eating and sleeping. It is such a docile POKéMON that there are children who use its big belly as a place to play.
column15: deSein Magen kann jede Art von Nahrung verdauen, selbst wenn sie verschimmelt und verdorben ist.
column16: enIts stomach’s digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. It can even eat things off the ground.
column17: enIt has no interest in anything other than eating. Even if you climb up on its stomach while it’s napping, it doesn’t seem to mind at all!
column18: deEs heißt, sein Magen sei an Widerstandsfähigkeit nicht zu übertreffen. Für Relaxo ist selbst das Gift eines Sleimok nicht mehr als ein Würzstoff.
column19: enIt eats nearly 900 pounds of food every day. It starts nodding off while eating—and continues to eat even while it’s asleep.
column20: enWhen its belly is full, it becomes too lethargic to even lift a finger, so it is safe to bounce on its belly.
column21: deEs ist erst satt, wenn es über 400 kg Nahrung am Tag gefressen hat. Ist es mit dem Essen fertig, schläft es sofort ein.
column22: enIts stomach can digest any kind of food, even if it happens to be moldy or rotten.
column23: deWenn es nicht gerade frisst, dann schläft es. Macht es aber einmal Ernst, ist Schluss mit lustig.
column24: deEs nimmt jeden Tag 400 kg an Nahrung zu sich. Es schläft beim Essen ein und frisst selbst im Schlaf noch weiter.
column25: enSNORLAX’s typical day consists of nothing more than eating and sleeping. It is such a docile POKéMON that there are children who use its expansive belly as a place to play.
column26: enIts stomach's digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. It can even eat things off the ground.
column27: deEs muss über 400 kg Nahrung am Tag fressen, um satt zu werden. Ist es mit dem Essen fertig, schläft es sofort ein.
column28: enWhat sounds like its cry may actually be its snores or the rumblings of its hungry belly.
column29: enIt stops eating only to sleep. It doesn’t feel full unless it eats nearly 900 pounds a day.
column30: deDer Magen dieses Pokémon ist so resistent, dass es sogar Verschimmeltes oder Verdorbenes essen kann.
column31: deBei ihm geht es immer nur ums Fressen. Macht man es sich auf seinem Bauch bequem, während es döst, ist es ihm daher egal.
column32: enThis POKéMON's stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.
column33: enWill eat anything, even if the food happens to be a little moldy. It never gets an upset stomach.
column34: enThis Pokémon’s stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.
column35: enIts stomach is said to be incomparably strong. Even Muk’s poison is nothing more than a hint of spice on Snorlax’s tongue.
column36: enThis glutton appears in villages without warning and devours the entirety of their rice granaries—such occurrences have long been counted among the gravest of disasters.

column8: enOne of the legendary bird POKéMON, it chills moisture in the atmosphere to create snow while flying.
column9: enA legendary bird Pokémon. It freezes water that is contained in winter air and makes it snow.
column10: enOne of the legendary bird POKéMON. With its long tail trailing behind, its flying form is magnificent.
column11: enIt’s said that this Pokémon’s beautiful blue wings are made of ice. Articuno flies over snowy mountains, its long tail fluttering along behind it.
column12: enLegendary bird POKéMON. As it flies through the sky, it cools the air, causing snow to fall.
column13: enOne of the legendary bird Pokémon, it chills moisture in the atmosphere to create snow while flying.
column14: enARTICUNO is a legendary bird POKéMON that can control ice. The flapping of its wings chills the air. As a result, it is said that when this POKéMON flies, snow will fall.
column15: enA legendary bird POKéMON that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.
column16: deArktos ist ein sagenumwobenes Vogel-Pokémon, das Eis manipulieren kann. Das Schlagen seiner Flügel bringt die Luft zum Gefrieren. Man nimmt an, dass es zu schneien beginnt, wenn dieses Pokémon fliegt.
column17: enA legendary bird POKéMON. It freezes water that is contained in winter air and makes it snow.
column18: deEin Legendäres Vogel-Pokémon, das im Winter die Wasserpartikel in der Luft gefrieren lässt, um Schnee zu erzeugen.
column19: enArticuno is a legendary bird Pokémon that can control ice. The flapping of its wings chills the air. As a result, it is said that when this Pokémon flies, snow will fall.
column20: enThis Pokémon can control ice at will. Articuno is said to live in snowy mountains riddled with permafrost.
column21: enThe magnificent, seemingly translucent wings of this legendary bird Pokémon are said to be made of ice.
column22: enA legendary bird Pokémon. It can create blizzards by freezing moisture in the air.
column23: enThe magnificent, seemingly translucent wings of this legendary bird POKéMON are said to be made of ice.
column24: deEin Legendäres Vogel-Pokémon, das angeblich in Gletschern verirrten Wanderern den Weg weist.
column25: deArktos ist ein sagenumwobenes Vogel-Pokémon, das Eis manipulieren kann. Das Schlagen seiner Flügel bringt die Luft zum Gefrieren. Es wird behauptet, dass es zu schneien beginnt, wenn dieses Pokémon fliegt.
column26: deSeine wunderschönen blauen Flügel sollen aus Eis bestehen. Es fliegt über schneebedeckte Berge, während sein langer Schweif hinter ihm flattert.
column27: deEin Legendäres Vogel-Pokémon. Es kann Blizzards verursachen, indem es Feuchtigkeit gefriert.
column28: deArktos ist imstande, Eis zu manipulieren. Man sagt, es lebe in schneebedeckten Bergen, wo ewiger Winter herrscht.
column29: enA legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

column8: deEs heißt, wenn dieses Legendäre Vogel-Pokémon seine Flügel aneinanderreibt, brechen unmittelbar danach Gewitter los.
column9: enThis legendary bird POKéMON is said to appear when the sky turns dark and lightning showers down.
column10: enA legendary bird POKéMON that is said to appear from clouds while dropping enormous lightning bolts.
column11: enA legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear from clouds while dropping enormous lightning bolts.
column12: deZapdos ist ein Legendäres Vogel-Pokémon, das die Fähigkeit besitzt, Elektrizität zu manipulieren. Es lebt in Gewitterwolken. Dieses Pokémon nimmt Energie auf, wenn es vom Blitz getroffen wird.
column13: enThis legendary bird Pokémon is said to appear only when a thundercloud parts into two halves.
column14: enThis legendary bird Pokémon causes savage thunderstorms by flapping its glittering wings.
column15: enZapdos is a legendary bird Pokémon. It’s said that when Zapdos rubs its feathers together, lightning will fall immediately after.
column16: enA legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.
column17: deEin Legendäres Vogel-Pokémon, das in Gewitterwolken leben soll. Es kontrolliert Blitze.
column18: enThis Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.
column19: enThis legendary bird POKéMON causes savage thunderstorms by flapping its glittering wings.
column20: enZAPDOS is a legendary bird POKéMON that has the ability to control electricity. It usually lives in thunderclouds. It gains power if it is stricken by lightning bolts.
column21: enOne of the legendary bird POKéMON. While it is flying, it makes crackling and snapping sounds.
column22: enThis legendary bird Pokémon is said to appear when the sky turns dark and lightning showers down.

column12: deVor dem Kampf dreht es dem Feind den Rücken zu, um ihm zu zeigen, wie Furcht erregend sein Feuer lodert.

column9: enA FURRET has a very slim build. When under attack, it can squirm through narrow spaces and get away. In spite of its short limbs, it is very nimble and fleet.

column16: enIt makes a nest to suit its long and skinny body. The nest is impossible for other Pokémon to enter.

column20: enThere is no telling where the tail begins. Despite its short legs, it is quick at hunting RATTATA.

column8: deHoothoot besitzt ein inneres Organ, das die Erdumdrehung wahrnimmt. Dieses besondere Organ ermöglicht es diesem Pokémon, jeden Tag zur selben Zeit zu schreien.
column9: enIt begins to hoot at the same time every day. Some trainers use them in place of clocks.
column10: enIt switches its standing foot so quickly, it can’t be seen. It cries at the same time every day.
column11: enIt has an internal organ that senses the earth’s rotation. Using this special organ, a HOOTHOOT begins hooting at precisely the same time every day.
column12: enIt always stands on one foot. Even when attacked, it does not brace itself using both feet.
column13: enIt always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.
column14: enHOOTHOOT has an internal organ that senses and tracks the earth’s rotation. Using this special organ, this POKéMON begins hooting at precisely the same time every day.
column15: enIt begins to hoot at the same time every day. Some Trainers use them in place of clocks.
column16: deEs steht immer auf einem Bein. Es wechselt sein Standbein so schnell, dass man es kaum sieht.
column17: deEs stößt immer zur gleichen Uhrzeit seinen Ruf aus. Einige Trainer nehmen es deswegen statt einer Armbanduhr mit.
column18: enEvery day, it tilts its head in the same rhythm. A long time ago, people raised these Pokémon to serve as clocks.
column19: deHoothoot ruft jeden Tag zur exakt gleichen Zeit. Früher wurde es daher als Bote der Zeitgottheit verehrt und geschätzt.
column20: enIt has a perfect sense of time. Whatever happens, it keeps rhythm by precisely tilting its head in time.
column21: deSein Zeitgefühl ist perfekt. Was auch immer passiert, es behält den Rhythmus, da sein Kopf wackelt.
column22: enIt cries out at the same time every day. A long time ago, people cherished it and considered it a divine messenger sent to tell the time.
column23: deEs neigt seinen Kopf tagein, tagaus in einem festgelegten Rhythmus. Früher verwendete man es als Uhr.
column24: enIt marks time precisely. Some countries consider it to be a wise friend, versed in the world’s ways.
column25: enHoothoot has an internal organ that senses and tracks the earth’s rotation. Using this special organ, this Pokémon begins hooting at precisely the same time every day.

column8: enIt unfailingly catches prey in darkness. NOCTOWL owe their success to superior vision that allows them to see in minimal light, and to their supple and silent wings.
column9: deDank seines hervorragenden Sehvermögens, mit dem es auch bei geringer Beleuchtung sehen kann, und dank seiner geschmeidigen Flügel, mit denen es geräuschlos fliegt, entgeht Noctuh keine Beute.
column10: deSein Sehvermögen ist hervorragend. Selbst bei schwachem Licht kann es jedes Detail erkennen.
column11: enWith eyes that can see in pitch-darkness, it never lets its prey escape. Some even call it “the emperor of dark nights.”
column12: enIts eyes are specially adapted. They concentrate even faint light and enable it to see in the dark.
column13: enIts eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.
column14: enIf it flips its head upside down, it’s a sign that it is engaged in very complex thinking.
column15: enIts extremely soft feathers make no sound in flight. It silently sneaks up on prey without being detected.
column16: deSeine Augen sind so gut entwickelt, dass es selbst in fast kompletter Dunkelheit so klar sehen kann, als wäre es Tag.
column17: enWhen it needs to think, it rotates its head 180 degrees to sharpen its intellectual power.
column18: deMuss es nachdenken, dreht es seinen Kopf um 180 Grad, um so seinen Intellekt zu schärfen.
column19: enNoctowl never fails at catching prey in darkness. This Pokémon owes its success to its superior vision that allows it to see in minimal light, and to its soft, supple wings that make no sound in flight.
column20: enIts eyes are special. They can pick out objects as long as there is the tiniest amount of light.
column21: enNOCTOWL never fails at catching prey in darkness. This POKéMON owes its success to its superior vision that allows it to see in minimal light, and to its soft,  supple wings that make no sound in flight.
column22: deDreht es seinen Kopf um 180 Grad, ist es aus irgendeinem Grund besorgt. Man lässt es dann zur eigenen Sicherheit am besten in Ruhe.
column23: deDank seines perfekten Sehvermögens entgeht ihm selbst in der Dunkelheit keine Beute. Man nennt es auch den „Herrscher der Nacht“.
column24: deDank seines hervorragenden Sehvermögens, mit dem es auch bei geringer Beleuchtung sehen kann, und dank seiner geschmeidigen Flügel, mit denen es geräuschlos fliegen kann, entgeht Noctuh keine Beute.
column25: enWhen it turns its head entirely upside down, you know it’s troubled by something. If you don’t leave it be, it will peck you.

column8: enThey are timid and grow uneasy when not in a swarm with others of their kind. The pattern on their backs differs slightly from one to another.
column9: enIt is very timid. It will be afraid to move if it is alone. But it will be active if it is in a group.
column10: deEs ist scheu und stets mit Artgenossen zusammen. Das Sekret an seinen Füßen führt zu seinem Standort.
column11: deEs ist scheu und fürchtet sich, wenn es nicht Teil des Schwarms ist. Das Muster auf seinem Rücken ist bei jedem Exemplar minimal anders.
column12: enThey communicate with one another using bodily fluids that give off odors. When they’re angry, their odor smells sour.
column13: deDieses Pokémon reagiert sehr empfindlich auf Kälte. Im warmen Klima Alolas strotzt es nur so vor Lebensfreude.
