column30: enIts fur is so sensitive, it can feel minute shifts in the air and predict the weather...and its foes’ thoughts.
column31: deDie Spitze seines geteilten Schweifs bebt, wenn es die nächste Attacke seines Feindes voraussagt.
column32: deEs heißt, dass die Fähigkeit der Prophezeiung erst im Laufe der Zeit in ihm erwacht sei, damit es sich selbst vor Unheil bewahren könne.

column8: enWhen this Pokémon becomes angry, its pores secrete a poisonous sweat, which it sprays at its opponent’s eyes.
column9: enWith its black fur, it blends into the darkness. It bides its time, and when prey appears, this Pokémon goes for its throat, and then eats it.
column10: deSein schwarzes Fell verschmilzt mit der Nacht. Es lauert seiner Beute geduldig auf und beißt ihr bei erster Gelegenheit die Kehle durch.
column11: enThe light of the moon changed EEVEE’s genetic structure. It lurks in darkness for prey.
column12: deWird es der Aura des Mondes ausgesetzt, glühen seine Ringe und es wird von einer mysteriösen Kraft durchströmt.
column13: enUmbreon evolved as a result of exposure to the moon’s waves. It hides silently in darkness and waits for its foes to make a move. The rings on its body glow when it leaps to attack.
column14: enWhen darkness falls, the rings on the body begin to glow, striking fear in the hearts of anyone nearby.
column15: enThis Pokémon is nocturnal. Even in total darkness, its large eyes can spot its prey clearly!
column16: deBei Vollmond oder bei Aufregung leuchten die ringförmigen Muster auf seinem Körper gelb auf.
column17: enUMBREON evolved as a result of exposure to the moon’s waves. It hides silently in darkness and waits for its foes to make a move. The rings on its body glow when it leaps to attack.
column18: enIt lurks in the dark of night looking for prey. At the moment it pounces, the rings on its body glow dimly but ominously.
column19: enWhen exposed to the moon’s aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it’s filled with a mysterious power.
column20: enWhen darkness falls, the rings on its body begin to glow, striking fear in the hearts of anyone nearby.
column21: enWhen agitated, this Pokémon protects itself by spraying poisonous sweat from its pores.
column22: enIt is most active in the wee hours of the night, when moonlight bathes the land. Its large eyes can pierce the darkness and perceive prey with absolute clarity.
column23: deIst es wütend, feuert es giftigen Schweiß aus all seinen Poren auf die Augen seines Gegners ab.
column24: deEs lauert seiner Beute im Schutz der Dunkelheit auf. Greift es an, erzeugen die Ringe an seinem Körper ein schwaches, ominöses Leuchten.
column25: deMondlicht hat die genetische Struktur von Evoli verändert. Im Dunkeln wartet es auf Beute.
column26: deNachtara hat sich dadurch entwickelt, dass es den Mondwellen ausgesetzt war. Es versteckt sich im Schutze der Dunkelheit und wartet darauf, dass sich ein Feind bewegt. Die Ringe an seinem Körper leuchten auf, wenn es angreift.
column27: enUMBREON evolved from exposure to the moon’s energy pulses. It lurks in darkness and waits for its foes to move. The rings on its body glow when it leaps to attack.
column28: enThe light of the moon changed Eevee’s genetic structure. It lurks in darkness for prey.
column29: deIst es wütend, feuert es tödlichen Giftschweiß aus all seinen Poren auf die Augen seines Gegners ab.
column30: deDie Pupillen dieses nachtaktiven Pokémon sind groß genug, um auch in der schwärzesten Nacht noch seine Beute deutlich erkennen zu können.
column31: enWhen agitated, this POKéMON protects itself by spraying poisonous sweat from its pores.
column32: enOn the night of a full moon, or when it gets excited, the ring patterns on its body glow yellow.
column33: enWhen exposed to the moon’s aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it gains a mysterious power.

column8: enFeared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.
column9: enMURKROW were feared as the alleged bearers of ill fortune. It shows strong interest in anything that sparkles. It will even try to steal rings from women.
column10: enIt hides any shiny object it finds in a secret location. MURKROW and MEOWTH loot one another's stashes.
column11: enWidely shunned as a bearer of ill fortune. Upon crossing paths with this creature, I've been told one must chant ”Workrum, Workrum—bad luck, don't come” as a protective incantation.
column12: deViele verabscheuen und hassen es, da sie glauben, es bringe Verderben, wenn sie ihm nachts begegnen.
column13: deEs wacht bei Dämmerung auf und fliegt durch die Nacht. Daher das Sprichwort: „Flugs nach Haus, mein Kind, wenn Kramurx am Himmel sind!“
column14: deEs versteckt jeden schimmernden Gegenstand. Kramurx und Mauzi rauben sich gegenseitig die Beute.
column15: enThey awaken at dusk and take wing in the twilight, leading to the expression, “Get home before the Murkrow fly.”
column16: enMurkrow was feared and loathed as the alleged bearer of ill fortune. This Pokémon shows strong interest in anything that sparkles or glitters. It will even try to steal rings from women.
column17: enIf spotted, it will lure an unwary person into chasing it, then lose the pursuer on mountain trails.
column18: deFür seinen Chef geht es auf die Jagd nach glitzernden Objekten. Von vielen Menschen wird es als Überbringer des Unheils verabscheut.
column19: enIt hides any shiny object it finds in a secret location. Murkrow and Meowth loot one another’s stashes.
column20: enIt appears near travelers to lure them into deep forests. It is said to carry misfortune.
column21: enSeen as a symbol of bad luck, it’s generally disliked. Yet it gives presents—objects that sparkle or shine—to Trainers it’s close to.
column22: enIf spotted, it will lure an unwary person into chasing it then lose the pursuer on mountain trails.
column23: enIt searches for shiny things for its boss. Murkrow’s presence is said to be unlucky, so many people detest it.
column24: deKramurx wurde als vermeintlicher Überbringer des Unheils verabscheut und gefürchtet. Dieses Pokémon hat ein großes Interesse an allem, was funkelt und glitzert. Es stiehlt auch Ringe von Frauen.
column25: enIt is believed that seeing this Pokémon at night will bring about ominous occurrences.
column26: enIt is said that when chased, it lures its attacker onto dark mountain trails where the foe will get lost.
column27: enIt has a weakness for shiny things. It’s been known to sneak into the nests of Gabite—noted collectors of jewels—in search of treasure.
column28: enMURKROW was feared and loathed as the alleged bearer of ill fortune. This POKéMON shows strong interest in anything that sparkles or glitters. It will even try to steal rings from women.
column29: deEs ist als Unglücksbringer verschrien, aber wenn es sich mit seinem Trainer gut versteht, schenkt es ihm allerlei funkelnde und glitzernde Objekte.
column30: deEs hat eine Schwäche für glitzernde Objekte. Hin und wieder schleicht es sich, in Erwartung schillernder Schätze, ins Nest von Knarksel.

column8: enThis Pokémon is so famed for its intellect that a proverb still persists in some regions: “When in doubt, ask Slowking.”
column9: deDas Gift von Muschas drang in Flegmons Gehirn ein und erweckte dabei wie durch ein Wunder bisher verborgene Intelligenz in ihm.
column10: deDurch einen giftigen Biss wurde es zu einer wahren Intelligenzbestie. Es verfügt über Psycho-Kräfte.
column11: enA poisonous bite reacted with its system, blessing it with the enhanced intellect of a genius. It has full control of its psychic powers.
column12: enWhen its head was bitten, toxins entered SLOWPOKE’s head and unlocked an extraordinary power.
column13: enMiraculously, this former Slowpoke’s latent intelligence was drawn out when Shellder poison raced through its brain.
column14: enIt has incredible intellect and intuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.
column15: enBeing bitten by Shellder gave it intelligence comparable to that of award-winning scientists.
column16: enIt’s called “the sage of the sea.” It engages in battles of wits with Oranguru, but the result is usually a draw.
column17: enWhen its head was bitten, toxins entered Slowpoke’s head and unlocked an extraordinary power.
column18: deLaschoking betreibt jeden Tag Forschung, um die Geheimnisse der Welt zu lüften. Dieses Pokémon vergisst aber alles, was es bereits herausgefunden hat, wenn das Muschas auf seinem Kopf abfällt.
column19: deBittet man es darum, löst es jedes noch so schwere Problem für einen. Jedoch ist niemand in der Lage, Laschokings Sprache zu verstehen.
column20: deIn seinem Kopf kommen ihm ständig große Ideen, die die Welt verändern könnten. Leider vergisst es sie jedoch viel zu schnell wieder.
column21: enBeing bitten by SHELLDER gave it intelligence comparable to that of award-winning scientists.
column22: deEs ist so berühmt für seine Intelligenz, dass sich vielerorts das Sprichwort „Nicht verzagen, Laschoking fragen!“ durchgesetzt hat.
column23: deSein feines Gespür und Intellekt zeichnen es aus. Es bleibt in jeder Situation gelassen und besonnen.
column24: enIt’s constantly coming up with new ideas that would change the world, but as soon as it hits upon a new idea, it forgets it.
column25: deAls Flegmon in den Kopf gebissen wurde, floss Gift in seinen Kopf und setzte eine gewaltige Kraft frei.
column26: enSlowking can solve any problem presented to it, but no one can understand a thing Slowking says.
column27: enEvery time it yawns, SHELLDER injects more poison into it. The poison makes it more intelligent.
column28: enIt undertakes research every day to solve the mysteries of the world. However, it apparently forgets everything if the SHELLDER on its head comes off.
column29: enWhen its head was bitten, toxins entered SLOWPOKE's head and unlocked an extraordinary power.
column30: deMan nennt es auch den „Weisen des Meeres“. Es liefert sich mit Kommandutan epische Quiz-Duelle, die zumeist im Gleichstand enden.
column31: enSlowking undertakes research every day in an effort to solve the mysteries of the world. However, this Pokémon apparently forgets everything it has learned if the Shellder on its head comes off.
column32: enSLOWKING undertakes research every day in an effort to solve the mysteries of the world. However, this POKéMON apparently forgets everything it has learned if the SHELLDER on its head comes off.

column8: enA MISDREAVUS frightens people with a creepy, sobbing cry. It apparently uses its red spheres to absorb the fear of foes as its nutrition.
column9: enIf you hear a sobbing sound emanating from a vacant room, it’s undoubtedly a bit of mischief from Misdreavus.
column10: enIt likes playing mischievous tricks such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.
column11: enWhat makes it happy is imitating the voices of weeping people and scaring everyone. It doesn’t deal well with folks who aren’t easily frightened.
column12: enMisdreavus frightens people with a creepy, sobbing cry. The Pokémon apparently uses its red spheres to absorb the fearful feelings of foes and turn them into nutrition.
column13: deAndere zu erschrecken ist sein Lebenssinn. Wenn man das Ohr auf die roten Kugeln an seinem Hals legt, kann man Hilfeschreie hören.
column14: enMISDREAVUS frightens people with a creepy, sobbing cry. The POKéMON apparently uses its red spheres to absorb the fearful feelings of foes and turn them into nutrition.
column15: deTraunfugil erschreckt Menschen mit einem markerschütternden Schrei. Dieses Pokémon verwendet seine roten Kraftfelder dazu, die Ängste seiner Feinde aufzunehmen und sie in Nahrung umzuwandeln.
column16: enIt loves to bite and yank people’s hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.
column17: deEs nährt sich von der Angst anderer Wesen und nimmt diese in roten Kugeln auf. Schläft am Tage.
column18: deEs liebt Streiche und erschreckt nachts Menschen, indem es heult oder ihnen im Dunkeln auflauert.
column19: deEs lässt nichts unversucht, um Menschen zu erschrecken und ihnen Lebensenergie abzusaugen. Dafür übt es regelmäßig.
column20: enA Pokémon that startles people in the middle of the night. It gathers fear as its energy.
column21: deHört man ein Schluchzen in einem leeren Zimmer, handelt es sich dabei eindeutig um einen Streich von Traunfugil.
column22: enIt will use any means necessary to frighten people and absorb their life energy. It practices constantly to hone its skill in causing fear.
column23: enIt conceals itself in darkness, sending chills up travelers' spines with its childlike weeping. As it observes the frightened travelers with glee, the red orbs upon its chest let off an eerie light.
column24: enIt gets nourishment from fear that it absorbs into its red orbs. In daytime, it sleeps in darkness.
column25: enWhat gives meaning to its life is surprising others. If you set your ear against the red orbs around its neck, you can hear shrieking.
column26: enIt loves to bite and yank people's hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.
column27: enIt loves to watch people it's scared. It frightens them by screaming loudly or appearing suddenly.
column28: enIt likes playing mischievous tricks, such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.
column29: enIt loves to sneak up on people late at night, then startle them with its shrieklike cry.
column30: deEs genießt es, das Schluchzen eines Menschen nachzuahmen und andere damit zu erschrecken. Wen das kaltlässt, den kann es nicht leiden.
column31: enIt likes playing mischievous tricks such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.

column8: enWhen alone, nothing happens. However, if there are two or more, an odd power is said to emerge.
column9: enThis POKéMON is shaped like ancient text characters. Although research is ongoing, it is a mystery as to which came first, the ancient writings or the various UNOWN.
column10: deSein flacher, dünner Körper hängt immer an Wänden. Seine Form scheint eine Bedeutung zu haben.
column11: enThey seem to communicate among each other telepathically. They are always found stuck on walls.
column12: enThis POKéMON is shaped like ancient writing. It is a mystery as to which came first, the ancient writings or the various UNOWN. Research into this topic is ongoing but nothing is known.
column13: enBecause different types of UNOWN exist, it is said that they must have a variety of abilities.
column14: enShaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.
column15: enShaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.
column16: deSeine Form sieht aus wie antike Schrift. Was war zuerst da? Die Sprache oder Icognito?
column17: enIts flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.
column18: enIt is hard to believe these strangely shaped Pokémon are truly living creatures. I've pointed out that the species' many forms resemble writing from other lands; no one will take me seriously.
column19: deIcognito hat die Form antiker Schriftzeichen. Bis heute ist unklar, was zuerst da war – die antiken Schriftzeichen oder die vielen unterschiedlichen Icognito. Trotz umfangreicher Forschung bleiben Fragen offen.
column20: enTheir shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.
column21: enThis Pokémon is shaped like ancient writing. It is a mystery as to which came first, the ancient writings or the various Unown. Research into this topic is ongoing but nothing is known.

column8: deWoingenau tut nichts anderes als Angriffe auszuhalten. Von sich aus würde es nie angreifen. Eine Attacke auf seinen Schweif würde es allerdings niemals dulden. Wenn das passiert, setzt es Abgangsbund ein.
column9: enWOBBUFFET does nothing but endure attacks - it won’t attack on its own. However, it won’t endure an attack on its tail. When that happens, the POKéMON will try to take the foe with it using DESTINY BOND.
column10: deEs hasst Licht und Schläge. Wird es angegriffen, pumpt es sich auf, um einen Gegenschlag vorzubereiten.
column11: enTo keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.
column12: enIf two or more WOBBUFFET meet, they will turn competitive and try to outdo each other’s endurance. However, they may try to see which one can endure the longest without food. TRAINERS need to beware of this habit.
column13: deWenn zwei oder mehr Woingenau aufeinandertreffen, versuchen sie, gegenseitig ihre Geduld zu übertreffen. Sie möchten herausfinden, welches Pokémon es am längsten ohne Nahrung aushält. Darauf müssen Trainer achten.
column14: enIt hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to build up its counterstrike.
column15: enIn order to conceal its black tail, it lives in a dark cave and only moves about at night.
column16: enIt desperately tries to keep its black tail hidden. It is said to be proof the tail hides a secret.
column17: enIt hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to pump up its counterstrike.
column18: enWobbuffet does nothing but endure attacks—it won’t attack on its own. However, it won’t endure an attack on its tail. When that happens, the Pokémon will try to take the foe with it using Destiny Bond.
column19: deEs hasst Licht und Schocks. Wird es angegriffen, pumpt es sich auf, um einen Gegenschlag vorzubereiten.
column20: deEs lebt in der Dunkelheit, um seinen schwarzen Schweif zu verstecken. Es greift nie zuerst an.
column21: enIf two or more Wobbuffet meet, they will turn competitive and try to outdo each other’s endurance. However, they may try to see which one can endure the longest without food. Trainers need to beware of this habit.
column22: enUsually docile, a WOBBUFFET strikes back ferociously if its black tail is attacked. It makes its lair in caves where it waits for nightfall.

column8: enThe head on its tail contains a small brain. It can instinctively fight even while facing backward.
column9: enIts tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent and bite.
column10: enGirafarig’s rear head contains a tiny brain that is too small for thinking. However, the rear head doesn’t need to sleep, so it can keep watch over its surroundings 24 hours a day.
column11: enWhen it is in danger, its tail uses some sort of mysterious powers to drive away the enemy.
column12: enGIRAFARIG’s rear head contains a tiny brain that is too small for thinking. However, the rear head doesn’t need to sleep, so it can keep watch over its surroundings 24 hours a day.
column13: deSein Schweif hat ein eigenes Gehirn. Achtung! Kommst du ihm zu nahe, kann es dich riechen und beißt.
column14: enA GIRAFARIG is an herbivore--it eats grass and tree shoots. While it is eating, its tail makes chewing and swallowing motions as if it were also eating.
column15: enGIRAFARIG’s rear head also has a brain, but it is small. The rear head attacks in response to smells and sounds. Approaching this POKéMON from behind can cause the rear head to suddenly lash out and bite.
column16: deDer Kopf an Girafarigs Hinterteil verfügt nur über ein kleines Gehirn, mit dem es nicht denken kann. Dieses Gehirn benötigt jedoch keinen Schlaf und ist somit in der Lage, die Umgebung rund um die Uhr im Auge zu behalten.
column17: enIts tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent by biting.
column18: enWhile it sleeps, the head on its tail keeps watch. The tail doesn’t need to sleep.
column19: enIts tail also has a small brain. It bites to repel any foe trying to sneak up on it from behind.
column20: deWährend es schläft, hält sein Schweif Wache. Dieser benötigt keinen Schlaf.
column21: enGirafarig’s rear head also has a brain, but it is small. The rear head attacks in response to smells and sounds. Approaching this Pokémon from behind can cause the rear head to suddenly lash out and bite.
column22: deDer Kopf an Girafarigs Hinterteil hat ein eigenes Gehirn. Er reagiert mit Angriffen auf Gerüche und Geräusche. Wenn man sich diesem Pokémon von hinten nähert, könnte der hintere Kopf plötzlich vorschnellen und zubeißen.
column23: enIts tail, which also contains a small brain, may bite on its own if it notices an alluring smell.

column8: enPineco hangs from a tree branch and patiently waits for prey to come along. If the Pokémon is disturbed while eating by someone shaking its tree, it drops down to the ground and explodes with no warning.
column9: enPINECO hangs from a tree branch and patiently waits for prey to come along. If the POKéMON is disturbed while eating by someone shaking its tree, it drops down to the ground and explodes with no warning.
column10: enA PINECO hangs from a tree branch and waits for prey. While eating, if it is disturbed by someone shaking its tree, it falls on the ground and suddenly explodes.
column11: enIt sticks tree bark to itself with its saliva, making itself thicker and larger. Elderly Pineco are ridiculously huge.
column12: deEs macht seinen Körper breiter und größer, indem es sich mit Speichel Baumrinde anklebt. Ältere Tannza sind deshalb unfassbar groß.
column13: deEs sieht aus wie ein Tannenzapfen. Seine Schale schützt es vor Vogel-Pokémon, die an ihm picken wollen.
column14: enIt spits out a fluid that it uses to glue tree bark to its body. The fluid hardens when it touches air.
column15: enIt looks just like a pinecone. Its shell protects it from bird Pokémon that peck it by mistake.
column16: enIt hangs and waits for flying insect prey to come near. It does not move about much on its own.
column17: deTannza hängt an Ästen und wartet auf Beute. Wenn man dieses Pokémon bei einer Mahlzeit stört, indem man den Baum, auf dem es sich befindet, schüttelt, fällt es herunter und explodiert ohne Vorwarnung.
column18: enIt likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn’t bother it.
column19: enIt likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The extra weight doesn’t bother it.
column20: enIt likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.
column21: deTannza hängt an Ästen und wartet auf Beute. Wenn man dieses Pokémon bei einer Mahlzeit stört, indem man seinen Baum schüttelt, fällt es herunter und explodiert ohne Vorwarnung.
column22: enIt hangs and waits for flying-insect prey to come near. It does not move about much on its own.
column23: deEs hängt an Bäumen und wartet regungslos auf seine Beute, Käfer-Pokémon. In Alola ernährt es sich bevorzugt von Wommel.
column24: deEs fügt seiner Schale schichtenweise Baumrinde hinzu. Die zusätzliche Belastung ist ihm gleich.
column25: enMotionless, it hangs from trees, waiting for its bug Pokémon prey to come to it. Its favorite in Alola is Cutiefly.

column8: enIn the moment that it gulps down its prey, the inside of its shell is exposed, but to this day, no one has ever seen that sight.
column9: enIt remains immovably rooted to its tree. It scatters pieces of its hard shell to drive its enemies away.
column10: deEs bleibt stets bei seinem Baum. Es verstreut Teile seiner Schale, um so seine Feinde zu verjagen.
column11: enIt is encased in a steel shell. Its peering eyes are all that can be seen of its mysterious innards.
column12: deForstellka versteckt sich in seiner stahlharten Schale. Die Schale öffnet sich, wenn es nach Beute schnappt. Dies geht jedoch so schnell, dass man das Innere der Schale nicht zu Gesicht bekommt.
column13: deKommt ihm irgendetwas zu nahe, feuert es Teile seiner stählernen Schale ab. Das geschieht jedoch aus Reflex, nicht aus eigenem Willen.
column14: enIts entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.
column15: enUsually found hanging on to a fat tree trunk. It shoots out bits of its shell when it sees action.
column16: enIt keeps itself inside its steel shell. The shell is opened when it is catching prey, but it is so quick that the shell’s inside cannot be seen.
column17: enFORRETRESS conceals itself inside its hardened steel shell. The shell is opened when the POKéMON is catching prey, but it does so at such a quick pace that the shell’s inside cannot be seen.
column18: enWhen something approaches it, it fires off fragments of its steel shell in attack. This is not a conscious action but a conditioned reflex.
column19: enIts entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside the armor is a total mystery.
column20: enForretress conceals itself inside its hardened steel shell. The shell is opened when the Pokémon is catching prey, but it does so at such a quick pace that the shell’s inside cannot be seen.
column21: deSein Inneres ist nur kurz sichtbar, wenn es flugs seine Beute verschlingt. Bisher hat es noch kein Mensch geschafft, einen Blick hineinzuwerfen.
column22: deDieses Pokémon ist von einer Stahlhülle umgeben. Seine stechenden Augen sind alles, was man von ihm sieht.

column8: enIt travels by digging through the ground. Diglett and Dunsparce share one another’s tunnels happily.
column9: enWhen spotted, this POKéMON escapes backward by furiously boring into the ground with its tail.
column10: enDunsparce has a drill for its tail. It uses this tail to burrow into the ground backward. This Pokémon is known to make its nest in complex shapes deep under the ground.
column11: enDUNSPARCE has a drill for its tail. It uses this tail to burrow into the ground backwards. This POKéMON is known to make its nest in complex shapes deep under the ground.
column12: enIts drill-tipped tail is used to burrow into the ground backwards. This POKéMON is known to make its nest in complex shapes deep under the ground.
column13: deSie bewegen sich fort, indem sie sich durch den Boden graben. Da Dummisel sich mit Digda gut verstehen, teilen sie ihre Tunnel miteinander.
column14: enThe nests Dunsparce live in are mazes of tunnels. They never get lost in their own nests—they can tell where they are by the scent of the dirt.
column15: enWhen spotted, this Pokémon escapes backward by furiously boring into the ground with its tail.
column16: enIf spotted, it escapes by burrowing with its tail. It can hover just slightly using its wings.
column17: deWird es entdeckt, flüchtet dieses Pokémon, indem es sich mit seinem Schweif in den Boden gräbt.
column18: enIf spotted, it escapes by burrowing with its tail. It can float just slightly using its wings.
column19: enIt digs into the ground with its tail and makes a mazelike nest. It can fly just a little.
column20: deEs gräbt sich mit seinem Schweif in die Erde und baut ein labyrinthartiges Nest. Es kann kaum fliegen.
column21: deEs lebt in einem labyrinthartigen Nestkomplex. Anhand des Geruchs der Erde weiß Dummisel genau, wo es sich befindet und verirrt sich nie.
column22: enIt hides deep inside caves where no light ever reaches it and remains virtually motionless there.
column23: enThis Pokémon’s tiny wings have some scientists saying that Dunsparce used to fly through the sky in ancient times.
column24: deErblickt es einen Menschen, bohrt es mit seinem Schweif ein Loch und ergreift sofort die Flucht. Man braucht viel Glück, um ihm zu begegnen.
column25: enWhen it sees a person, it digs a hole with its tail to make its escape. If you happen to find one, consider yourself lucky.
column26: deDa es kleine Flügel hat, behaupten manche Wissenschaftler, dass es vor langer Zeit fliegen konnte.
column27: enIt creates mazes in dark locations. When spotted, it flees into the ground by digging with its tail.
column28: deDummisel hat einen Bohrer als Schweif. Diesen benutzt es, um sich rückwärts in den Boden einzugraben. Dieses Pokémon baut seinen Nestkomplex tief unter der Erdoberfläche auf.

column8: enGLIGAR glides through the air without a sound as if it were sliding. This POKéMON hangs on to the face of its foe using its clawed hind legs and the large pincers on its forelegs, then injects the prey with its poison barb.
column9: enIt flies straight at its target’s face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.
column10: enGligar glides through the air without a sound as if it were sliding. This Pokémon hangs on to the face of its foe using its clawed hind legs and the large pincers on its forelegs, then injects the prey with its poison barb.
column11: enIt sails on the winds with its limbs extended to strike from the sky. It aims for the prey’s face.
column12: enIt usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.
column13: enIt clamps on to its chosen prey then jabs the stinger on its tail into the prey while it’s stunned with surprise.
column14: enIt glides without making a single sound. It grasps the face of its foe using its hind and large front claws, then stabs with its poison barb.
column15: deSkorgla segelt lautlos durch die Lüfte. Es verankert sich mithilfe der Krallen an seinen Hinterbeinen und der riesigen Scheren an seinen Vorderbeinen am Gesicht eines Feindes und vergiftet ihn mit seinem Giftstachel.
column16: enIt builds its nest on a steep cliff. When it is done gliding, it hops along the ground back to its nest.
column17: enIt glides as if sliding. It startles foes by clamping on to their faces, then jabs with its poison stinger.
column18: deEs hängt meist an Klippen. Erspäht es Beute, spreizt es seine Flügel und greift diese sofort an.
column19: enIt flies straight at its target's face then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.
column20: enIts tail is tipped by a thick, horrible stinger. To bring down prey, it will first obscure their vision by covering their faces with its body, and then it will use the stinger to inject them with venom.
column21: deEs fliegt direkt auf das Gesicht seiner Feinde zu und injiziert ihnen eine Überdosis Gift.

column8: enIts body has been compressed deep under the ground. As a result, it is even harder than diamond.
column9: enIt is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.
column10: enThis Pokémon evolved through use of a strange item. Its body is coated with steel powder and notably hard—not even diamond can leave so much as a scratch.
column11: enIt’s said that if an ONIX lives for 100 years, its composition becomes diamondlike as it evolves into a STEELIX.
column12: enThe many small metal particles that cover this POKéMON's body reflect bright light well.
column13: enIt is said that if an ONIX lives for over 100 years, its composition changes to become diamond-like.
column14: enSTEELIX lives even further underground than ONIX. This POKéMON is known to dig towards the earth’s core. There are records of this POKéMON reaching a depth of over six-tenths of a mile underground.
column15: deEs beißt sich mit seinen Kiefern seinen Weg durch Felsen. Es kann auch im Dunkeln sehen.
column16: deMan glaubt, dass sich sein Körper aufgrund des Schluckens von eisenhaltiger Erde verwandelt habe.
column17: enSTEELIX live even further underground than ONIX. This POKéMON is known to dig toward the earth’s core, reaching a depth of over six-tenths of a mile underground.
column18: deEs heißt, dass der Körper von Onix so hart wie ein Diamant werde, wenn es mehr als 100 Jahre lebt.
column19: enIts body has been compressed deep under the ground. As a result, it is even harder than a diamond.
column20: enIt chews its way through boulders with its sturdy jaws. Its eyes can see in the darkness underground.
column21: deStahlos lebt noch tiefer unter der Erde als Onix. Dieses Pokémon gräbt immer in Richtung des Erdkerns. Nachweislich hat dieses Pokémon bereits Tiefen von 1 km erreicht.
column22: enIt is said that if an Onix lives for over 100 years, its composition changes to become diamond-like.
column23: enTempered underground under high pressure and heat, its body is harder than any metal.
column24: enThe iron it ingested with the soil it swallowed transformed its body and made it harder than diamonds.
column25: deHoher Druck und hohe Temperaturen haben seinen Körper härter als Stahl werden lassen.
column26: enSteelix lives even further underground than Onix. This Pokémon is known to dig toward the earth’s core. There are records of this Pokémon reaching a depth of over six-tenths of a mile underground.

column8: deEs wird schnell zahm und wird gerne verwöhnt. Viele lieben den Kontrast zwischen seiner mürrischen Miene und dem putzigen Charakter.
column9: enIt grows close to others easily and is also easily spoiled. The disparity between its face and its actions makes many young people wild about it.
column10: enBy baring its fangs and making a scary face, it sends smaller POKéMON scurrying in terror. The SNUBBULL does seem a little sad at making its foes flee.
column11: enSmall Pokémon flee from its scary face. It is, however, considered by women to be cute.
column12: deEs ist von Natur aus verspielt. Es tollt mit vielen Frauen herum, da es ihnen zugeneigt ist.
column13: enIn truth, it is a cowardly POKéMON. It growls eagerly in order to hide its fear from its opponent.
column14: enAlthough it looks frightening, it is actually kind and affectionate. It is very popular among women.
column15: deEs macht ein furchterregendes Gesicht, ist aber an sich recht feige. Sein Bemühen, bedrohlich zu wirken, macht es bei vielen beliebt.
column16: enDespite its intimidating face, it’s quite cowardly. Its desperate attempts to appear threatening make it popular with people.
column17: enBy baring its fangs and making a scary face, SNUBBULL sends smaller POKéMON scurrying away in terror. However, this POKéMON seems a little sad at making its foes flee.
column18: deIndem es seine Zähne fletscht und ein furchterregendes Gesicht macht, schlägt Snubbull kleinere Pokémon in die Flucht. Es scheint darüber jedoch etwas traurig zu sein.
column19: enBy baring its fangs and making a scary face, Snubbull sends smaller Pokémon scurrying away in terror. However, this Pokémon seems a little sad at making its foes flee.
column20: enIt has an active, playful nature. Many women like to frolic with it because of its affectionate ways.
column21: deKleine Pokémon fliehen beim Anblick seines Gesichts. Frauen aber finden es niedlich.
column22: deEs brüllt, um seine Feinde zu verunsichern. Normalerweise geht es die Dinge gemütlich an und schläft den halben Tag.
column23: deMan sieht es ihm zwar nicht an, aber es ist recht feige. Lässt man es mit anderen kleinen Hunde-Pokémon spielen, wird es oft gehänselt.
column24: enIts growls make its opponents uneasy. This laid-back Pokémon tends to sleep half the day.
column25: enIn contrast to its appearance, it’s quite timid. When playing with other puppy Pokémon, it sometimes gets bullied.

column8: enGranbull has a particularly well-developed lower jaw. The enormous fangs are heavy, causing the Pokémon to tip its head back for balance. Unless it is startled, it will not try to bite indiscriminately.
column9: enAlthough it’s popular with young people, Granbull is timid and sensitive, so it’s totally incompetent as a watchdog.
column10: enIt is timid in spite of its looks. If it becomes enraged, however, it will strike with its huge fangs.
column11: enGRANBULL has a particularly well-developed lower jaw. The enormous fangs are heavy, causing the POKéMON to tip its head back for balance. Unless it is startled, it will not try to bite indiscriminately.
column12: deEs ist noch schüchterner als Snubbull. Der Kontrast zwischen seinem Äußeren und seinem Wesen macht es bei jungen Leuten so beliebt.
column13: enIt can make most any POKéMON run away simply by opening its mouth wide to reveal its big fangs.
column14: enIt has a particularly well-developed lower jaw. The huge fangs are heavy, causing it to tilt its head. Unless it is startled, it will not try to bite.
column15: enIt is actually timid and easily spooked. If attacked, it flails about to fend off its attacker.
column16: enIt can make most any Pokémon run away simply by opening its mouth wide to reveal its big fangs.
column17: enIts well-developed lower jaw and heavy fangs are its weapons. As long as it’s not provoked, it rarely bites.
column18: deEs ist bei jungen Leuten sehr beliebt. Aufgrund seines feigen und sensiblen Charakters ist es als Wache allerdings völlig ungeeignet.
column19: deSein Unterkiefer ist sehr stark. Da es jedoch den Kampf scheut, kommt es nur selten dazu, dies zu demonstrieren.
column20: enBecause its fangs are too heavy, it always keeps its head tilted down. However, its BITE is powerful.
column21: enWhile it has powerful jaws, it doesn’t care for disputes, so it rarely has a chance to display their might.
column22: enMore timid than Snubbull, this Pokémon is doted on by young people amused at the contrast between its looks and its attitude.
column23: deGranbull besitzt einen besonders ausgeprägten Unterkiefer. Wegen seiner schweren Zähne wackelt es mit dem Kopf. Es beißt nur zu, wenn es erschreckt wird.
column24: deEs kann fast jedes Pokémon vertreiben, indem es sein Maul weit öffnet und seine scharfen Reißzähne zeigt.
column25: deEs ist trotz seines Äußeren schüchtern. Wird es wütend, schnappt es mit seinen Fängen zu.
column26: enBecause its fangs are too heavy, it always keeps its head tilted down. However, its bite is powerful.
column27: deEin ausgeprägter Unterkiefer und schwere Zähne sind seine Waffen. Es beißt nur zu, wenn es verärgert wird.

column8: enTo fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of water all at once.
column9: enQWILFISH sucks in water, inflating itself. This POKéMON uses the pressure of the water it swallowed to shoot toxic quills all at once from all over its body. It finds swimming somewhat challenging.
column10: enA QWILFISH uses the pressure of water it swallows to shoot toxic quills all at once from all over its body. It finds swimming to be somewhat challenging.
column11: deBaldorfish saugt Wasser ein, um sich aufzublasen. Dieses Pokémon nutzt den Druck des Wassers dazu, giftige Stacheln zu verschießen. Es betrachtet Schwimmen als eine echte Herausforderung.
column12: deSieht es sich einem größeren Feind gegenüber, schluckt es massenweise Wasser, um seinen Körper aufzublähen und größer zu wirken.
column13: deUm seine Giftstacheln abzufeuern, muss es seinen Körper aufpumpen, indem es 10 l trinkt.
column14: enThe small spikes covering its body developed from scales. They inject a toxin that causes fainting.
column15: enFishers detest this troublesome Pokémon because it sprays poison from its spines, getting it everywhere. A different form of Qwilfish lives in other regions.
column16: deDie kleinen Stacheln an seinem Körper sind aus Schuppen entstanden. Damit injiziert es Gift.
column17: enIt shoots the poison spines on its body in all directions. Its round form makes it a poor swimmer.
column18: enWhen faced with a larger opponent, it swallows as much water as it can to match the opponent's size.
column19: enWhen faced with a larger opponent, it swallows as much water as it can to match the opponent’s size.
column20: enTo fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of  water all at once.
column21: deDie kleinen Stacheln an seinem Körper sind aus Schuppen entstanden. Damit injiziert es Gift, das zu Ohnmacht führt.
column22: enQwilfish sucks in water, inflating itself. This Pokémon uses the pressure of the water it swallowed to shoot toxic quills all at once from all over its body. It finds swimming somewhat challenging.
column23: deBaldorfish saugt Wasser ein, um sich aufzublasen. Dieses Pokémon nutzt den Druck des Wassers dazu, giftige Stacheln aus seinem Körper zu verschießen. Es betrachtet Schwimmen als eine echte Herausforderung.

column8: enIt raises its pincers with eyelike markings for intimidation. It also swings them down dangerously.
column9: enIt uses its wings to adjust its body temperature. Otherwise, its metal body would become too hot and melt in the heat of battle.
column10: enScizor has a body with the hardness of steel. It is not easily fazed by ordinary sorts of attacks. This Pokémon flaps its wings to regulate its body temperature.
column11: enBulky pincers account for one third of Scizor’s body weight. A single swing of one of these pincers will crush a boulder completely.
column12: enSCIZOR has a body with the hardness of steel. It is not easily fazed by ordinary sorts of attacks. This POKéMON flaps its wings to regulate its body temperature.
column13: enOnce it has identified an enemy, this Pokémon smashes it mercilessly with pincers hard as steel.
column14: deMit einer seiner großen Scheren, die ein Drittel seines Körpergewichts ausmachen, kann es Felsen mit einem einzigen Schlag zermalmen.
column15: enThis POKéMON's pincers, which contain steel, can crush any hard object it gets a hold of into bits.
column16: enIt swings its eye-patterned pincers up to scare its foes. This makes it look like it has three heads.
column17: deSein Körper ist hart wie Stahl. Es nutzt seine großen, schweren Scheren weniger, um Gegner zu packen, als um sie niederzuschlagen.
column18: enIts wings are not used for flying. They are flapped at high speed to adjust its body temperature.
column19: deScherox’ Körper ist hart wie Stahl. Gewöhnliche Angriffe jagen ihm keine Angst ein. Dieses Pokémon schlägt mit den Flügeln, um seine Körpertemperatur zu regulieren.
column20: enThough its body is slim, Scizor has tremendous attacking power. Even Scizor’s muscles are made of metal.
column21: deSein Körper ist stahlhart. Es bedroht seine Gegner, indem es die Augen auf seinen Scheren zeigt.
column22: deDieses Pokémon kann mit seinen stählernen Scheren jeden harten Gegenstand mühelos zermalmen.
column23: enEvolved by way of a curious item. The shell covering its body has been shown to be stronger than forged steel.
column24: enOnce it has identified something as an enemy, it will continue beating them with its steel-hard pincers until there’s nothing left but scraps.
column25: deSobald es ein anderes Pokémon als Feind erkennt, schlägt es dieses mit seinen stahlharten Scheren gnadenlos in Stücke.
column26: deSobald es einen Gegner bemerkt, schlägt es ihn mit seinen stahlharten Scheren erbarmungslos nieder.
column27: enIt has a steel-hard body. It intimidates foes by upraising its eye-patterned pincers.
column28: enIts body is like steel. Its tough, heavy pincers are more suited to smashing enemies than grabbing them.
column29: deEs schlägt mit den Flügeln, um seine Körper-temperatur zu regulieren. So überhitzt und schmilzt sein stählerner Körper im Kampf nicht.
column30: enA SCIZOR has a body with the hardness of steel. It is not easily fazed by ordinary sorts of attacks. It flaps its wings to regulate its body temperature.
column31: deTrotz seines dünnen Körpers verfügt Scherox über eine gewaltige Angriffsstärke. Selbst seine Muskeln bestehen aus Metall.
column32: enThis Pokémon’s pincers, which contain steel, can crush any hard object it gets ahold of into bits.

column8: enThe berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.
column9: deIn seinem topfförmigen Panzer gelagerte Beeren verwandeln sich im Nu zu einem dickflüssigen Saft.
column10: enThe BERRIES it stores in its vase-like shell decompose and become a gooey liquid.
column11: enIt stores BERRIES inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.
column12: enIt stores berries in its shell. The berries eventually ferment to become delicious juices.
column13: enShuckle quietly hides itself under rocks, keeping its body concealed inside its hard shell while eating berries it has stored away. The berries mix with its body fluids to become a juice.
column14: enIt stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.
column15: enA SHUCKLE hides under rocks, keeping its body concealed inside its shell while eating stored berries. The berries mix with its body fluids to become a juice.
column16: deEs sammelt Beeren in seiner Schale. Um sich vor Attacken zu schützen, versteckt es sich unter Steinen.
column17: enThe BERRIES it stores in its vaselike shell decompose and become a gooey liquid.
column18: deIn seinem vasenförmigen Panzer gelagerte Beeren verwandeln sich mit der Zeit zu einem dickflüssigen Saft.
column19: deEs sammelt Beeren in seiner Schale. Um sich vor Attacken zu schützen, versteckt es sich unter Steinen und verharrt dort regungslos.
column20: dePottrott versteckt sich leise unter Steinen und verbirgt seinen Körper in seiner harten Schale, wenn es gesammelte Beeren zu sich nimmt. Die Beeren vermischen sich mit seinen Körperflüssigkeiten zu einem Saft.
column21: enThe berries stored in its jar-like shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.
column22: enSHUCKLE quietly hides itself under rocks, keeping its body concealed inside its hard shell while eating berries it has stored away. The berries mix with its body fluids to become a juice.
column23: dePottrott versteckt sich leise unter Steinen und verbirgt seinen Körper in seiner harten Schale, wenn es Beeren zu sich nimmt. Die Beeren vermischen sich mit seinen Körperflüssigkeiten zu einem Saft.
column24: enThe berries it stores in its vase-like shell decompose and become a gooey liquid.
column25: enThe fluid secreted by its toes carves holes in rocks for nesting and can be mixed with BERRIES to make a drink.

column8: enIt roams through forests searching for sweet nectar. Although it boasts fantastic physical strength, it’s not that good at flying.
column9: enWith its Herculean powers, it can easily throw around an object that is 100 times its own weight.
column10: enUsually docile, but if disturbed while sipping honey, it chases off the intruder with its horn.
column11: deDieses kräftige Pokémon rammt sein stolzes Horn unter den Rumpf des Gegners und wirft ihn um.
column12: enHERACROSS has sharp claws on its feet. These are planted firmly into the ground or the bark of a tree, giving the POKéMON a secure and solid footing to forcefully fling away foes with its proud horn.
column13: deDieses Pokémon ist stolz auf seine Stärke. Es kann mit Leichtigkeit das Hundertfache seines eigenen Körpergewichts stemmen.
column14: enHERACROSS charges in a straight line at its foe, slips beneath the foe’s grasp, and then scoops up and hurls the opponent with its mighty horn. This POKéMON even has enough power to topple a massive tree.
column15: enIt is usually docile, but if it is disturbed while sipping honey, it chases off the intruder with its horn.
column16: enHeracross loves sweet sap and will go looking through forests for it. The Pokémon uses its two antennae to pick up scents as it searches.
column17: enHeracross has sharp claws on its feet. These are planted firmly into the ground or the bark of a tree, giving the Pokémon a secure and solid footing to forcefully fling away foes with its proud horn.
column18: enIt’s proud of its thick horn. In Alola, its biggest rival is Vikavolt, which it’s always fighting with.
column19: enHeracross charges in a straight line at its foe, slips beneath the foe’s grasp, and then scoops up and hurls the opponent with its mighty horn. This Pokémon even has enough power to topple a massive tree.
column20: enThis Pokémon has an unparalleled horn. Heracross itself demonstrates tremendous power—it's capable of throwing several people trained in the traditional arts of war at once.
column21: deAuf der Suche nach süßem Honig durchstreift es die Wälder. Es ist stolz auf seine Stärke, aber fliegen kann es nicht besonders gut.
column22: deMit seiner herkulischen Kraft kann es Objekte, die hundertmal sein Körpergewicht haben, mühelos umwerfen.
column23: enThis powerful POKéMON thrusts its prized horn under its enemies’ bellies, then lifts and throws them.
column24: deSkaraborn geht direkt auf seinen Gegner los, rutscht auf ihn zu, um ihn auszuhebeln und mit seinem mächtigen Horn wegzuschleudern. Dieses Pokémon hat genug Kraft, um einen massigen Baum umzustoßen.
column25: enNo matter how heavy its opponents, it flings them far away with its prized horn.
column26: enThey gather in forests seeking the sweet sap of trees. It is completely clad in a steel-hard shell. It is proud of its horn, which it uses to fling foes.
column27: deSkaraborn hat scharfe Krallen an den Füßen. Diese bohren sich fest in den Boden oder in die Rinde eines Baumes und geben diesem Pokémon einen sicheren Halt. So kann es seine Gegner mit seinem imposanten Horn wegschleudern.
column28: enIt gathers in forests to search for tree sap, its favorite food. It’s strong enough to hurl foes.
column29: enIt loves sweet honey. To keep all the honey to itself, it hurls rivals away with its prized horn.
column30: enThis powerful Pokémon thrusts its prized horn under its enemies’ bellies, then lifts and throws them.
column31: enWith powerful legs and claws, it generates enough power to hurl foes over great distances.
column32: deSkaraborn verspeist am liebsten süßen Baumsaft. Mit seinen zwei Antennen nimmt es die Witterung auf, während es den Wald danach absucht.
column33: enThis Pokémon takes pride in its strength, which allows it to lift things 100 times heavier than itself with no trouble at all.
column34: enThis powerful POKéMON thrusts its prized horn under its enemies’ bellies then lifts and throws them.
column35: deSein mächtiges Horn ist sein ganzer Stolz. Es streitet sich ständig mit Donarion, seinem größten Rivalen in Alola.
