column17: enHeracross has sharp claws on its feet. These are planted firmly into the ground or the bark of a tree, giving the Pokémon a secure and solid footing to forcefully fling away foes with its proud horn.
column18: enIt’s proud of its thick horn. In Alola, its biggest rival is Vikavolt, which it’s always fighting with.
column19: enHeracross charges in a straight line at its foe, slips beneath the foe’s grasp, and then scoops up and hurls the opponent with its mighty horn. This Pokémon even has enough power to topple a massive tree.
column20: enThis Pokémon has an unparalleled horn. Heracross itself demonstrates tremendous power—it's capable of throwing several people trained in the traditional arts of war at once.
column21: deAuf der Suche nach süßem Honig durchstreift es die Wälder. Es ist stolz auf seine Stärke, aber fliegen kann es nicht besonders gut.
column22: deMit seiner herkulischen Kraft kann es Objekte, die hundertmal sein Körpergewicht haben, mühelos umwerfen.
column23: enThis powerful POKéMON thrusts its prized horn under its enemies’ bellies, then lifts and throws them.
column24: deSkaraborn geht direkt auf seinen Gegner los, rutscht auf ihn zu, um ihn auszuhebeln und mit seinem mächtigen Horn wegzuschleudern. Dieses Pokémon hat genug Kraft, um einen massigen Baum umzustoßen.
column25: enNo matter how heavy its opponents, it flings them far away with its prized horn.
column26: enThey gather in forests seeking the sweet sap of trees. It is completely clad in a steel-hard shell. It is proud of its horn, which it uses to fling foes.
column27: deSkaraborn hat scharfe Krallen an den Füßen. Diese bohren sich fest in den Boden oder in die Rinde eines Baumes und geben diesem Pokémon einen sicheren Halt. So kann es seine Gegner mit seinem imposanten Horn wegschleudern.
column28: enIt gathers in forests to search for tree sap, its favorite food. It’s strong enough to hurl foes.
column29: enIt loves sweet honey. To keep all the honey to itself, it hurls rivals away with its prized horn.
column30: enThis powerful Pokémon thrusts its prized horn under its enemies’ bellies, then lifts and throws them.
column31: enWith powerful legs and claws, it generates enough power to hurl foes over great distances.
column32: deSkaraborn verspeist am liebsten süßen Baumsaft. Mit seinen zwei Antennen nimmt es die Witterung auf, während es den Wald danach absucht.
column33: enThis Pokémon takes pride in its strength, which allows it to lift things 100 times heavier than itself with no trouble at all.
column34: enThis powerful POKéMON thrusts its prized horn under its enemies’ bellies then lifts and throws them.
column35: deSein mächtiges Horn ist sein ganzer Stolz. Es streitet sich ständig mit Donarion, seinem größten Rivalen in Alola.

column8: deSie bilden ein Team, um gemeinsam Eier aus den Nestern von Vogel-Pokémon zu klauen. Dann streiten sie sich darüber, wer sie fressen darf.
column9: deDieses durchtriebene und gewissenlose Pokémon beobachtet Nester, bis die Eltern ausgeflogen sind, und stiehlt dann deren Eier.
column10: enThey will cooperate to steal eggs from the nests of bird Pokémon, but fights break out to determine which one gets to eat the eggs.
column11: enVicious in nature, it drives PIDGEY from their nests and feasts on the eggs that are left behind.
column12: enSNEASEL scales trees by punching its hooked claws into the bark. This POKéMON seeks out unguarded nests and steals eggs for food while the parents are away.
column13: enIts sturdy, curved claws are ideal for traversing precipitous cliffs. From the tips of these claws drips a venom that infiltrates the nerves of any prey caught in Sneasel's grasp.
column14: enSneasel scales trees by punching its hooked claws into the bark. This Pokémon seeks out unguarded nests and steals eggs for food while the parents are away.
column15: enA smart and sneaky Pokémon, it makes its opponents flinch by suddenly showing the claws hidden in its paws.
column16: enIts paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it suddenly extends the claws and startles its enemy.
column17: deEs ernährt sich von Eiern, die es aus Nestern stiehlt. Beute greift es mit seinen scharfen Krallen an.
column18: enIt has a cunning yet savage disposition. It waits for parents to leave their nests, and then it sneaks in to steal their eggs.
column19: enA SNEASEL scales trees by punching its hooked claws into the bark. It seeks out unguarded nests and steals eggs for food while the parents are away.
column20: deIn seinen Pfoten stecken scharfe Krallen. Es fährt sie blitzschnell aus, um Gegner zu überraschen.
column21: deSniebel klettert an Bäumen hoch, indem es seine hakenförmigen Krallen in die Rinde schlägt. Dieses Pokémon sucht nach unbewachten Nestern und stiehlt die Eier. Davon ernährt es sich.
column22: enIt feeds on eggs stolen from nests. Its sharply hooked claws rip vulnerable spots on prey.
column23: enVicious in nature, it drives PIDGEY from their nests and scavenges any leftovers it can find.
column24: deEs lauert seiner Beute im Dunkeln auf. Wenn es Sandan mit seinen Krallen angreift, brechen diese manchmal an dessen robuster Haut ab.
column25: enThis cunning POKéMON hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.
column26: deIn seinen Pfoten stecken scharfe Krallen. Es fährt sie blitzschnell aus, um die Gegner zu überraschen.
column27: enIt uses its claws to poke holes in eggs so it can slurp out the insides. Breeders consider it a scourge and will drive it away or eradicate it.
column28: enIt blends into the dark of night to ambush its prey. Sneasel will attack Sandshrew, but its hard skin can cause Sneasel’s claws to snap off.
column29: deDieses Pokémon hat ein durchtriebenes, grausames Wesen. Es sucht nach unbewachten Nestern und stiehlt die Eier.
column30: deEs rammt seine Krallen in Eier und schlürft ihren Inhalt aus. Pokémon-Züchter können es aus diesem Grund nicht ausstehen.
column31: enA smart and sneaky Pokémon. A pair may work together to steal eggs by having one lure the parents away.
column32: enIt is extremely vicious and will not stop attacking until its foe is incapable of moving.

column8: deSteht der Winter vor der Türe, hortet es Nahrung an vielen verschiedenen, nur ihm bekannten Orten.
column9: enBefore food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.
column10: deFindet es Honig, leuchtet die Sichel auf seinem Kopf. Es leckt oft seine mit Honig bedeckten Pfoten.
column11: enIf it finds honey, its crescent mark glows. It always licks its paws because they are soaked with honey.
column12: deTeddiursa leckt gern seine mit Honig bedeckten Pranken ab. Dieses Pokémon bereitet seinen eigenen Honig zu, indem es die von Bibor gesammelten Früchte und Pollen miteinander vermengt.
column13: enIt licks its paws because of the sweet honey that has soaked into them. It is cunning, stealing into the nests of Combee and taking for itself the honey that the Combee have amassed.
column14: deTeddiursa leckt gern seine mit Honig bedeckten Pfoten ab. Dieses Pokémon bereitet seinen eigenen Honig zu, indem es die von Bibor gesammelten Früchte und Pollen vermengt.
column15: enIf it finds honey, its crescent mark glows. It always licks its paws because they’re soaked with honey.
column16: enIt lets honey soak into its paws so it can lick them all the time. Every set of paws tastes unique.
column17: enIt licks its palms that are sweetened by being soaked in honey. A TEDDIURSA makes its own honey by blending fruits and pollen collected by BEEDRILL.
column18: enIt always licks honey. Its palm tastes sweet because of all the honey it has absorbed.
column19: enThis POKéMON likes to lick its palms that are sweetened by being soaked in honey. TEDDIURSA concocts its own honey by blending fruits and pollen collected by BEEDRILL.
column20: enThis Pokémon likes to lick its palms that are sweetened by being soaked in honey. Teddiursa concocts its own honey by blending fruits and pollen collected by Beedrill.

column8: enIn the forests inhabited by Ursaring, it is said that there are many streams and towering trees where they gather food. This Pokémon walks through its forest gathering food every day.
column9: deIn den von Ursaring bewohnten Wäldern gibt es viele Flüsse und hohe Bäume, in denen es Nahrung findet. Dieses Pokémon läuft täglich durch den Wald, um nach Essbarem zu suchen.
column10: enIn forests, it is said that there are many streams and towering trees where an URSARING gathers food. It walks through its forest collecting food every day.
column11: enIn its territory, it leaves scratches on trees that bear delicious berries or fruits.
column12: enAlthough it is a good climber, it prefers to snap trees with its forelegs and eat fallen BERRIES.
column13: enAlthough it has a large body, it is quite skilled at climbing trees. It eats and sleeps in the treetops.
column14: enAlthough it is a good climber, it prefers to snap trees with its forelegs and eat fallen berries.
column15: enWith its ability to distinguish any smell, it unfailingly finds all food buried deep underground.
column16: enIn the forests inhabited by URSARING, it is said that there are many streams and towering trees where they gather food. This POKéMON walks through its forest gathering food every day.
column17: deTrotz seines riesigen Körpers ist es ein guter Kletterer. Es frisst und schläft in Baumkronen.
column18: deDa es alle Gerüche perfekt unterscheiden kann, findet es sogar Nahrung, die tief im Erdreich ist.
column19: enWhen the cold season arrives in Hisui, this Pokémon will wander fields and mountains alike in search of its favorite berries. Ursaring's hunger during this time makes it a ferocious danger.
column20: enAlthough it is a good climber, it prefers to snap stout trees with its forelegs and eat fallen BERRIES.
column21: enWith its ability to distinguish any aroma, it unfailingly finds all food buried deep underground.

column8: enMolten magma courses throughout SLUGMA’s circulatory system. If this POKéMON is chilled, the magma cools and hardens. Its body turns brittle and chunks fall off, reducing its size.
column9: deSchneckmag hat kein Blut in seinem Körper. Stattdessen fließt glühend heißes Magma in diesem Pokémon, das seine Organe mit lebenswichtigen Nährstoffen und Sauerstoff versorgt.
column10: enSlugma does not have any blood in its body. Instead, intensely hot magma circulates throughout this Pokémon’s body, carrying essential nutrients and oxygen to its organs.
column11: enA common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.
column12: enSLUGMA does not have any blood in its body. Instead, intensely hot magma circulates throughout this POKéMON’s body, carrying essential nutrients and oxygen to its organs.
column13: enIts body is made of magma. If it doesn’t keep moving, its body will cool and harden.
column14: deEs hält sich ständig bei Vulkanen auf und ist stets kriechend auf der Suche nach warmen Aufenthaltsorten.
column15: enThese group together in areas that are hotter than normal. If it cools off, its skin hardens.
column16: enIt never sleeps. It has to keep moving because if it stopped, its magma body would cool and harden.
column17: enMolten magma courses throughout Slugma’s circulatory system. If this Pokémon is chilled, the magma cools and hardens. Its body turns brittle and chunks fall off, reducing its size.
column18: deDurch Schneckmags Kreislaufsystem fließt Magma. Wenn dieses Pokémon erkältet ist, kühlt das Magma aus und erhärtet. Sein Körper wird spröde und es brechen Stücke heraus, sodass es kleiner wird.
column19: enIt is a species of POKéMON that lives in volcanic areas. If its body cools, its skin hardens and immobilizes it. To avoid that, it sleeps near magma.
column20: deSein Körper besteht aus Magma. Bleibt es nicht ständig in Bewegung, kühlt es aus und verhärtet sich.

column8: deSein Körper ist heißer als Lava und wogt stets. Manchmal schießen Flammen aus seinem Haus.
column9: enIts body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.
column10: enThe shell on its back is just skin that has cooled and hardened. It breaks easily with a slight touch.
column11: deMagcargos Schale ist eigentlich seine Haut, die erhärtet ist, als es auskühlte. Seine Schale ist sehr spröde und zerbrechlich. Schon bei einer leichten Berührung bricht sie auseinander. Dann muss es ein Bad in Magma nehmen.
column12: enMagcargo’s body temperature is approximately 18,000 degrees Fahrenheit. Water is vaporized on contact. If this Pokémon is caught in the rain, the raindrops instantly turn into steam, cloaking the area in a thick fog.
column13: deAus seinem porösen Schneckengehäuse sprudeln Feuerfontänen, die seinen Körper durchfluten.
column14: enThe shell on its back is made of hardened magma. Tens of thousands of years spent living in volcanic craters have turned MAGCARGO’s bodies into magma.
column15: enMAGCARGO’s body temperature is approximately 18,000 degrees F. Water is vaporized on contact. If this POKéMON is caught in the rain, the raindrops instantly turn into steam, cloaking the area in a thick fog.
column16: enIts brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that  circulate throughout its body.
column17: deMagcargos Körpertemperatur liegt bei etwa 10 000 °C. Dieses Pokémon bringt Wasser sofort zum Verdampfen. Wenn dieses Pokémon im Regen steht, werden die Tropfen augenblicklich zu Dampf und es entsteht dichter Nebel.
column18: enIts body temperature is roughly 18,000 degrees F. Flames spout from gaps in its hardened shell.
column19: enIts brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.
column20: enMagcargo’s shell is actually its skin that hardened as a result of cooling. Its shell is very brittle and fragile—just touching it causes it to crumble apart. This Pokémon returns to its original size by dipping itself in magma.
column21: enMAGCARGO’s shell is actually its skin that hardened as a result of cooling. Its shell is very brittle and fragile - just touching it causes it to crumble apart. This POKéMON returns to its original size by dipping itself in magma.

column8: deWenn es etwas Vielversprechendes erschnuppert, sprintet es Hals über Kopf darauf los, ohne nachzudenken.
column9: deWenn es etwas Vielversprechendes erschnuppert, dann stürzt es in Windeseile darauf zu.
column10: enIt has a very sensitive nose. It can locate mushrooms, berries, and even hot springs buried under ice.
column11: enIt roots for food by rubbing its snout against the ground. Its favorite food is a mushroom that grows under dried grass. It occasionally roots out hot springs.
column12: enIt rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food. It sometimes discovers hot springs.
column13: enIf it smells something enticing, it dashes off headlong to find the source of the aroma.
column14: enIt uses the tip of its nose to dig for food. Its nose is so tough that even frozen ground poses no problem.
column15: deAuf Nahrungssuche schnüffelt es am Boden entlang. Es entdeckt dabei manchmal auch heiße Quellen.
column16: enSWINUB roots for food by rubbing its snout against the ground. Its favorite food is a mushroom that grows under the cover of dead grass. This POKéMON occasionally roots out hot springs.
column17: enIt loves eating mushrooms that grow under dead grass. It also finds hot springs while foraging.
column18: enRooting the tip of its snout into the ground, it searches for food. Sometimes it even digs up a hot spring.
column19: deAuf Nahrungssuche schnüffelt Quiekel am Boden entlang. Seine Lieblingsspeise ist ein Pilz, der unter verwesendem Gras wächst. Manchmal spürt dieses Pokémon heiße Quellen auf.
column20: deAuf Nahrungssuche wühlt es sich mit der Nase durch den Erdboden. Manchmal entdeckt es dabei auch heiße Quellen.
column21: deAuf Nahrungssuche schnüffelt Quiekel am Boden entlang. Seine Lieblingsspeise ist ein Pilz, der unter abgestorbenem Gras wächst. Manchmal spürt dieses Pokémon heiße Quellen auf.
column22: enSwinub excels at sniffing out mushrooms buried beneath grass or snow. Since ancient times, the people of Hisui have often relied upon this skill.
column23: enIf it smells something enticing, it dashes headlong off to find the source of the aroma.
column24: enSwinub roots for food by rubbing its snout against the ground. Its favorite food is a mushroom that grows under the cover of dead grass. This Pokémon occasionally roots out hot springs.

column8: deTrotz kurzer Beine rutscht es auf eisigen Flächen nicht aus, da seine Hufe ihm genügend Halt bieten.
column9: enBecause the long hair all over its body obscures its sight, it just keeps charging repeatedly.
column10: deDa sein haariges Fell seine Sicht enorm beeinträchtigt, greift es ständig an, um den Gegner zu treffen.
column11: enIf it charges at an enemy, the hairs on its back stand up straight. It is very sensitive to sound.
column12: enThe long fur of this Pokémon covers its eyes, ears, and even limbs, allowing Piloswine to resist harshly frigid conditions. The Pokémon's white tusks can be used to defeat its enemies.
column13: deObwohl es sehr kurze Beine hat, rutscht es auf eisigen Flächen nicht aus, da seine gespaltenen Hufe ihm genügend Halt bieten.
column14: enA PILOSWINE is covered by a thick coat of long hair for enduring freezing cold. It uses its tusks to dig up food that has been buried under ice.
column15: enWith its excellent sense of smell, it’s even able to find mushrooms that are buried under frozen ground.
column16: deBeim Angriff auf einen Gegner stellen sich seine Rückenhaare auf. Es hört außergewöhnlich gut.
column17: enPiloswine is covered by a thick coat of long hair that enables it to endure the freezing cold. This Pokémon uses its tusks to dig up food that has been buried under ice.
column18: enAlthough its legs are short, its rugged hooves prevent it from slipping, even on icy ground.
column19: enCovered by a shaggy coat, it is strong against the cold. Its tusks of ice thicken when it snows.
column20: deKeifel hat ein dichtes Fell aus langen Haaren. Dadurch ist es gegen eisige Kälte gewappnet. Dieses Pokémon benutzt seine Stoßzähne, um an Nahrung zu gelangen, die unter dem Eis eingeschlossen ist.
column21: deKeifel hat ein dichtes Fell aus langen Haaren. Dadurch ist es gegen eisige Kälte gewappnet. Dieses Pokémon benutzt seine Hauer, um an Nahrung zu gelangen, die unter dem Eis eingeschlossen ist.
column22: enIts shaggy coat makes it unable to see. It checks surroundings with its sensitive nose instead.
column23: enPILOSWINE is covered by a thick coat of long hair that enables it to endure the freezing cold. This POKéMON uses its tusks to dig up food that has been buried under ice.

column8: enIt will regrow any branches that break off its head. People keep particularly beautiful Corsola branches as charms to promote safe childbirth.
column9: deHält sich lieber in den sauberen Seen im Süden auf. Seine Korallenarme verlieren in verschmutztem Wasser Farbe und Form.
column10: enIn Alola, where their natural enemies Mareanie are plentiful, many Corsola have stubby branches on their heads.
column11: deIn Alola gibt es viele Corasonn mit kurzen Korallenarmen, da es dort von ihren Fressfeinden Garstella nur so wimmelt.
column12: enCorsola’s branches glitter very beautifully in seven colors when they catch sunlight. If any branch breaks off, this Pokémon grows it back in just one night.
column13: enIt continuously sheds and grows. The tip of its head is prized as a treasure because of its beauty.
column14: enIt continuously sheds and grows. The tip of its head is prized as a treasure for its beauty.
column15: deEs lebt in warmen Meeren. Vor langer Zeit konnte man große Scharen dieses Pokémon auch an den Küsten Galars antreffen.
column16: deWenn Corasonns Arme Sonnenlicht abbekommen, glitzern sie wunderschön in sieben Farben. Wenn ein Arm abbricht, wächst über Nacht ein neuer nach.
column17: enCORSOLA’s branches glitter very beautifully in seven colors when they catch sunlight. If any branch breaks off, this POKéMON grows it back in just one night.
column18: enClusters of CORSOLA congregate in warm seas where they serve as ideal hiding places for smaller POKéMON. When the water temperature falls, this POKéMON migrates to the southern seas.
column19: enThese Pokémon live in warm seas. In prehistoric times, many lived in the oceans around the Galar region as well.
column20: deDie Äste auf seinem Kopf wachsen nach, wenn sie abbrechen. Besonders schöne abgebrochene Äste gelten als Glücksbringer für Geburten.
column21: enCoral branches grow from its head. They break off quite easily, but they grow back in about three days.
column22: enIn a south-sea nation, the people live in communities that are built on groups of these Pokémon.
column23: enCORSOLA live in warm southern seas. If the sea becomes polluted, the beautiful coral stalks become discolored and crumble away in tatters.
column24: enMany live in the clean seas of the south. They apparently can’t live in polluted waters.
column25: deDrohen die Arme auf seinem Kopf von einem Garstella verschlungen zu werden, wirft es diese ab und sucht das Weite.
column26: enThey prefer unpolluted southern seas. Their coral branches lose their color and deteriorate in dirty water.
column27: enThe pink of Corsola that live in Alola is deep and vibrant, thanks to seas filled with nutrition.
column28: enThe points on its head absorb nutrients from clean water. They cannot survive in polluted water.
column29: deAufgrund des nährstoffreichen Meeres ist das Pink der in Alola lebenden Corasonn besonders strahlend und kräftig.
column30: deAuf seinem Kopf wachsen Korallenarme, die ziemlich leicht abbrechen, allerdings innerhalb von etwa drei Tagen wieder nachwachsen.
column31: deEs häutet sich ständig und wächst. Seine Kopfspitze wurde als Schatz der Schönheit ausgezeichnet.
column32: deScharen von Corasonn sammeln sich im warmen Meer und dienen kleineren Pokémon als ideale Plätze zum Verstecken. Wenn die Wassertemperatur sinkt, wandert dieses Pokémon in die südlichen Meere ab.
column33: enIn a south sea nation, the people live in communities that are built on groups of these POKéMON.
column34: enClusters of Corsola congregate in warm seas where they serve as ideal hiding places for smaller Pokémon. When the water temperature falls, this Pokémon migrates to the southern seas.
column35: enPursued by Mareanie for the branches on its head, this Pokémon will sometimes snap its own branches off as a diversion while it escapes.

column8: enREMORAID sucks in water, then expels it at high velocity using its abdominal muscles to shoot down flying prey. When evolution draws near, this POKéMON travels downstream from rivers.
column9: enThe water they shoot from their mouths can hit moving prey from more than 300 feet away.
column10: enIt squirts water forcefully from its mouth to shoot down flying prey.
column11: deEs wird auch „Scharfschütze des Meeres“ genannt. Mit seinem Wasserstrahl kann es Beute aus 100 m Entfernung treffen.
column12: enIt has superb accuracy. The water it shoots out can strike even moving prey from more than 300 feet.
column13: enUsing its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a MANTINE’s underside to scavenge for leftovers.
column14: enIt forcefully squirts water. The water jet never misses prey even if the REMORAID is deep in the sea.
column15: enIt clings to MANTINE to feed on the big Pokémon’s scraps. This is an adaptation to avoid foes.
column16: enAlso known as the “sniper of the seas,” its water gun can hit prey without fail from over 300 feet away.
column17: enUsing its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a MANTINE's underside to scavenge for leftovers.
column18: deSelbst aus 100 m Entfernung treffen die Wassersalven, die es mit seinem Maul abfeuert, ihr Ziel.
column19: enThis Pokémon clings to Mantine and shares in its prosperity. When its Mantine is attacked, Remoraid will fight alongside it!
column20: enIt has superb accuracy. The water it shoots out can strike moving prey from more than 300 feet away.
column21: enIt forcefully squirts water. The water jet never misses prey even if the Remoraid is deep in the sea.
column22: deMit seiner Rückenflosse saugt es sich an der Unterseite eines Mantax fest und lebt von Speiseresten.
column23: deRemoraid saugt Wasser ein und spritzt es unter Einsatz seiner Bauchmuskeln mit Hochdruck wieder heraus. So schießt es fliegende Beute ab. Wenn seine Entwicklung näher rückt, wandert dieses Pokémon flussabwärts.
column24: enSpits water from its mouth with incredible accuracy. It captures Burmy by shooting them down off the branches from which they dangle.
column25: deEs ist äußerst präzise. Es kann mit seinem Wasserschuss Beute erlegen, die 100 m entfernt ist.
column26: enUsing its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine’s underside to scavenge for leftovers.
column27: enA REMORAID uses its abdominal muscles to forcefully expel swallowed water, then shoot down flying prey. When evolution approaches, it travels down rivers.
column28: deMit seiner Rückenflosse saugt es sich an der Unterseite eines Mantax’ fest und lebt von dessen Speiseresten.
column29: deEs heftet sich an Mantax und ernährt sich von dessen Essensresten. Wird Mantax angegriffen, kämpft es gemeinsam mit ihm.
column30: enRemoraid sucks in water, then expels it at high velocity using its abdominal muscles to shoot down flying prey. When evolution draws near, this Pokémon travels downstream from rivers.
column31: enTo escape from an attacker, it may shoot water out of its mouth, then use that force to swim backward.
column32: enIt has superb accuracy. The water it shoots out can strike even moving prey from more than 100 yards.

column8: deEs verkriecht sich gerne in Löchern, von wo aus es Gegner mit Tinte beschießt. Es bevorzugt Felsspalten und Vasen.
column9: deEs fühlt sich instinktiv in Löchern am wohlsten und schläft auch am liebsten in solchen, wobei es mitunter die Schlafplätze von anderen einnimmt.
column10: enIt lives in the gaps of boulders and in holes on the seafloor. Its suction cups grip prey tightly.
column11: enIt ensnares its foe with its suction-cupped tentacles before delivering the finishing blow. If the foe turns out to be too strong, it spews ink to escape.
column12: deDie Tinte, die es während der Flucht ausspeit, vermindert den Geruchssinn und verwirrt selbst Pokémon mit einer guten Nase.
column13: enIt traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.
column14: enOCTILLERY grabs onto its foe using its tentacles. This POKéMON tries to immobilize it before delivering the finishing blow. If the foe turns out to be too strong, OCTILLERY spews ink to escape.
column15: enWhile Octillery still shoots water from its mouth, the drastic anatomical difference between it and Remoraid meant that for a long time, no one believed the former evolved from the latter.
column16: deBeim Beutefang setzt es seine Tentakel ein. Es zermalmt seine Beute mit seinem steinharten Kopf.
column17: enThe ink it spits when escaping is special. It contains a substance that dulls the sense of smell, so Pokémon with keen noses get lost.
column18: enIt usually sleeps in caves. The pitch-black ink that Octillery spits is also used for cooking.
column19: enIt obscures its foe’s vision by spitting a cloud of murky ink. It makes its nest among boulders.
column20: enIt traps foes with the suction cups on its tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.
column21: enIt loves to lurk inside holes in rocks. It sometimes sprays ink on prey by sticking out only its mouth.
column22: enOctillery grabs onto its foe using its tentacles. This Pokémon tries to immobilize it before delivering the finishing blow. If the foe turns out to be too strong, Octillery spews ink to escape.
column23: enIts instinct is to bury itself in holes. It often steals the nesting holes of others to sleep in them.
column24: deEs schläft hauptsächlich in Felshöhlen. Die schwarze Tinte, die es ausspeit, wird auch als Kochzutat verwendet.
column25: enIt has a tendency to want to be in holes. It prefers rock crags or pots and sprays ink from them before attacking.
column26: deEs umschlingt Beute mit seinen Tentakeln und verpasst ihr dann eine beherzte Kopfnuss mit seinem steinharten Schädel.
column27: enIt traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles then smashes them with its rock-hard head.
column28: enIt instinctively sneaks into rocky holes. If it gets sleepy, it steals the nest of a fellow OCTILLERY.
column29: deOctillery saugt sich mit seinen Tentakeln an einem Feind fest. Dieses Pokémon betäubt seinen Gegner, bevor es ihm den Rest gibt. Wenn ein Feind zu stark ist, speit es Tinte aus und flüchtet.

column8: enIt shares its food with people who are stranded. Because Delibird is omnivorous, sometimes it gives those people bug Pokémon.
column9: enIt carries food rolled up in its tail. It has the habit of sharing food with people lost in mountains.
column10: enDelibird carries its food bundled up in its tail. There once was a famous explorer who managed to reach the peak of the world’s highest mountain, thanks to one of these Pokémon sharing its food.
column11: enIt wraps food in its tail to carry it around. The Delibird with the biggest tail is the boss of the flock!
column12: deSie bevorzugen eigentlich kühle Gegenden, aber die Botogel Alolas können bis zu einem gewissen Grad auch heißes Klima ertragen.
column13: enIt carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.
column14: deBotogel sammelt seine Nahrung in seinem Schweif. Es gab einmal einen berühmten Entdecker, der es dank eines Botogels geschafft hat, einen der höchsten Berge der Welt zu besteigen. Es hatte seine Nahrung mit ihm geteilt.
column15: enIt carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by its stored food.
column16: deEs teilt sein Futter mit Menschen in Not. Da es ein Allesfresser ist, kann es passieren, dass sie auch mal Käfer-Pokémon bekommen.
column17: deIm eingerollten Schweif transportiert es Futter, das es mit denen teilt, die sich verlaufen haben.
column18: deEs trägt immer Lebensmittel bei sich. Man sagt, dass viele Menschen in Not nur dank seiner Notration überlebt hätten.
column19: enDELIBIRD carries its food bundled up in its tail. There once was a famous explorer who managed to reach the peak of Mt. Everest thanks to one of these POKéMON sharing its food.
column20: deDieses Pokémon sammelt seine Nahrung in seinem Schweif. Das Botogel mit dem größten Schweif ist der Anführer der Gruppe.
column21: enIt carries food all day long. When someone is lost in the mountains, it shares that food.
column22: enIt nests at the edge of sharp cliffs. It spends all day carrying food to its awaiting chicks.
column23: enIt carries food bundled up in its tail. There was a famous explorer who managed to scale Mt. Everest thanks to a DELIBIRD sharing its food.
column24: enAlthough it naturally prefers colder locales, Delibird in Alola seem able to withstand the heat to a certain extent.
column25: enIt always carries its food with it, wherever it goes. If attacked, it throws its food at the opponent.
column26: deEs hat die Angewohnheit, seine eigene Nahrung mit Menschen und Pokémon zu teilen, und ist deshalb stets unterwegs auf Futtersuche.
column27: deBotogel sammelt seine Nahrung in seinem Schweif. Es gab einmal einen berühmten Entdecker, der es dank eines Botogels geschafft hat, einen der höchsten Berge der Welt zu besteigen. Es hat seine Nahrung mit ihm geteilt.
column28: deEs trägt den ganzen Tag Essen mit sich herum, das es mit Menschen teilt, die in den Bergen feststecken.
column29: enIt has a generous habit of sharing its food with people and Pokémon, so it’s always scrounging around for more food.

column8: enIt swims along freely, eating things that swim into its mouth. Its whole body is very coarse.
column9: enIf it builds up enough speed swimming, it can fly over 300 feet out of the water from the surface of the ocean.
column10: deAn schönen Tagen sieht man Scharen von Mantax elegant über die Wellen des Ozeans springen. Durch Remoraid, die an ihm haften und es begleiten, fühlt sich dieses Pokémon nicht belästigt.
column11: enOn sunny days, schools of MANTINE can be seen elegantly leaping over the sea’s waves. This POKéMON is not bothered by the REMORAID that hitches rides.
column12: enIt swims elegantly, mindless of REMORAID hitching on to its broad fins. It has a docile nature.
column13: enThis calm and gentle Pokémon swims gracefully through the sea. After building speed, it can leap out of the water. It is often misidentified as a bird Pokémon due to this behavior.
column14: enPostcards and posters featuring Mantine leaping elegantly above the waves are popular souvenirs of Alola.
column15: enAs it majestically swims, it doesn’t care if Remoraid attach to it to scavenge for its leftovers.
column16: deSchwimmt es schnell genug, kann es über die Wellen springen und bis zu 100 m durch die Lüfte gleiten.
column17: enSwimming freely in open seas, it may fly out of the water and over the waves if it builds up enough speed.
column18: enIf it builds up enough speed swimming, it can jump out above the waves and glide for over 300 feet.
column19: deEs gibt viele Menschen, die mit dem Surfen angefangen haben, weil sie die wunderschön dahinschwimmenden Mantax so bewundern.
column20: deEs schwimmt erhaben durch die Meere und duldet dabei etwaige Remoraid, die sich in der Hoffnung auf Futterreste an es heften.
column21: deAn schönen Tagen sieht man Schwärme von Mantax elegant über die Wellen des Ozeans springen. Durch Remoraid, die an ihm haften und es begleiten, fühlt sich dieses Pokémon nicht belästigt.
column22: dePostkarten und Poster, auf denen Mantax elegant über die Wellen springt, sind beliebte Mitbringsel aus Alola.
column23: enAs it majestically swims, it doesn’t care if REMORAID attach to it for scavenging its leftovers.
column24: enMany people have taken up surfing because they admire how magnificently Mantine swims.
column25: enWhile elegantly swimming in the sea, it ignores REMORAID that cling to its fins seeking food scraps.
column26: enOn sunny days, schools of MANTINE can be seen elegantly leaping over the waves. It is not bothered by the REMORAID that hitches rides.
column27: enAs it majestically swims, it doesn’t care if REMORAID attach to it to scavenge for its leftovers.
column28: enWhen the waves are calm, one may encounter a swarm of MANTINE swimming as if they are in flight.
column29: enOn sunny days, schools of Mantine can be seen elegantly leaping over the sea’s waves. This Pokémon is not bothered by the Remoraid that hitches rides.
column30: deZieht elegant durch die Meere und ignoriert dabei Remoraid, die an seinen Flossen haften.
column31: enAs it majestically swims, it doesn't care if REMORAID attach to it for scavenging its leftovers.
column32: enWhile elegantly swimming in the sea, it ignores Remoraid that cling to its fins seeking food scraps.

column8: deSein stählerner Körper ist robust, aber anfällig für Rost. Regnerische Tage verbringt es daher geduldig in seinem Nest.
column9: enIts sturdy wings look heavy, but they are actually hollow and light, allowing it to fly freely in the sky.
column10: deDie dornigen Zweige seines Nests bewirken, dass die Flügel seiner Jungen fest und hart werden.
column11: enIts metal body is sturdy, but it does rust rather easily. So on rainy days, this Pokémon prefers to stay put in its nest.
column12: deEs wird komplett von einer eisenharten Rüstung geschützt. Wenn es fliegt, erreicht es bis zu 300 km/h.
column13: deDie im Zuge seines Wachstums abgefallenen Federn sind sehr scharf. Krieger verwendeten sie früher als Schwerter.
column14: deDurch seinen stählernen Panzer sieht es zwar schwer aus, aber dennoch kann es bis zu 300 km/h schnell fliegen.
column15: enPeople fashion swords from Skarmory’s shed feathers, so this Pokémon is a popular element in heraldic designs.
column16: enSKARMORY is entirely encased in hard, protective armor. This POKéMON flies at close to 190 mph. It slashes foes with its wings that possess swordlike cutting edges.
column17: dePanzaeron ist komplett in einen harten, schützenden Panzer eingehüllt. Dieses Pokémon fliegt bis zu 300 km/h schnell. Es greift seine Feinde mit seinen messerscharfen Flügeln an.
column18: enIts feathers, which fall off as it grows, are thin and sharp. In times long past, warriors used them as swords.
column19: enThe pointed feathers of these Pokémon are sharper than swords. Skarmory and Corviknight fight viciously over territory.
column20: enThe wing feathers it sheds can be processed and made into knives whose sharpness is recognized by the finest chefs.
column21: enA POKéMON that has a body and wings of steel. People in the past used feathers fallen from SKARMORY to make swords and knives.
column22: deSeine spitzen Federn sind schärfer als jedes Schwert. Es liefert sich erbitterte Revierkämpfe mit Krarmor.
column23: enSKARMORY’s steel wings become tattered and bashed in from repeated battles. Once a year, the battered wings grow back completely, restoring the cutting edges to their pristine state.
column24: enIts sturdy wings look heavy, but its bones are hollow and light, allowing it to fly freely in the sky.
column25: enSkarmory is entirely encased in hard, protective armor. This Pokémon flies at close to 190 mph. It slashes foes with its wings that possess swordlike cutting edges.
column26: enIts heavy-looking iron body is actually thin and light, so it can fly at speeds over 180 mph.
column27: deEs wird gern als Vorlage für Wappenmotive genutzt, da aus den Federn, die ihm ausfallen, Schwerter hergestellt werden.
column28: enDespite being clad entirely in iron-hard armor, it flies at speeds over 180 mph.
column29: enAfter nesting in bramble bushes, the wings of its chicks grow hard from scratches by thorns.
column30: enThe feathers that it sheds are very sharp. It is said that people once used the feathers as swords.
column31: enIts body is draped in steel armor. It looks heavy, but it can fly at speeds of up to 185 miles an hour!
column32: enSkarmory’s steel wings become tattered and bashed in from repeated battles. Once a year, the battered wings grow back completely, restoring the cutting edges to their pristine state.
column33: dePanzaerons Stahlflügel bekommen in den vielen Kämpfen, die es austrägt, Risse und Beulen. Einmal im Jahr wachsen seine Flügel komplett nach und werden wiederhergestellt.
column34: deDie Federn, die ihm ausfallen, werden zu Küchenmessern verarbeitet, mit deren Schärfe auch erstklassige Köche zufrieden sind.

column8: deHunduster jagen in Gruppen. Sie kommunizieren miteinander über verschiedene Arten von Geheul und treiben ihre Feinde in die Enge. Die Zusammenarbeit dieser Pokémon ist einzigartig.
column9: enIt uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing prey.
column10: enIt uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.
column11: enIt conveys its feelings using different cries. It works in a pack to cleverly take down prey.
column12: enTo corner prey, they check each other's location using barks that only they can understand.
column13: enHoundour hunt as a coordinated pack. They communicate with each other using a variety of cries to corner their prey. This Pokémon’s remarkable teamwork is unparalleled.
column14: enIt is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.
column15: enHOUNDOUR communicate with each other using a variety of cries to corner their prey. This POKéMON’s remarkable teamwork is simply unparalleled.
column16: deMit wiederholtem unheimlichen Heulen vor Tagesanbruch machen sie auf die Existenz ihres Rudels aufmerksam.
column17: deSie treiben ihre Beute in die Enge und orientieren sich dabei anhand ihres Bellens, das nur sie verstehen.
column18: enThey make repeated eerie howls before dawn to call attention to their pack.
column19: enIt cooperates with others skillfully. When it becomes your partner, it’s very loyal to you as its Trainer and will obey your orders.
column20: deEs ist auf Zusammenarbeit spezialisiert und folgt den Befehlen seines Trainers aufs Wort.
column21: deIm Morgengrauen schallt sein ominöses Geheule über das Gebiet, das es für sich beansprucht.
column22: enTo corner prey, they check each other’s location using barks that only they can understand.
column23: enHOUNDOUR hunt as a coordinated pack. They communicate with each other using a variety of cries to corner their prey. This POKéMON’s remarkable teamwork is unparalleled.
column24: enAround dawn, its ominous howl echoes through the area to announce that this is its territory.

column8: enLong ago, people imagined its eerie howls to be the call of the grim reaper.
column9: enIf you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.
column10: enIn a Houndoom pack, the one with its horns raked sharply toward the back serves a leadership role. These Pokémon choose their leader by fighting among themselves.
column11: enIn a HOUNDOOM pack, the one with its horns raked sharply towards the back serves a leadership role. These POKéMON choose their leader by fighting amongst themselves.
column12: enThe pungent-smelling flame that shoots from its mouth results from toxins burning in its body.
column13: enIdentifiable by its eerie howls, people a long time ago thought it was the grim reaper and feared it.
column14: enUpon hearing its eerie howls, other POKéMON get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.
column15: enThey spew flames mixed with poison to finish off their opponents. They divvy up their prey evenly among the members of their pack.
column16: deEs bekämpft seine Gegner mit giftigen Flammen. Erlegte Beute wird im Rudel gerecht aufgeteilt.
column17: deIst es wütend, spuckt es giftige Flammen. Die Schmerzen der Verbrennungen dauern ewig an.
column18: enIn a HOUNDOOM pack, the one with its horns raked sharply back serves a leadership role. They choose their leader by fighting among themselves.
column19: enUpon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.
column20: deSein unheimliches Heulen ist unverkennbar. Früher wurde es von den Menschen als Bote des Todes gefürchtet.
column21: deIn einer Gruppe von Hundemon ist dasjenige mit den nach hinten gebogenen Hörnern der Anführer. Diese Pokémon bestimmen ihren Anführer durch das Ausfechten von Kämpfen untereinander.
column22: deIn alten Zeiten glaubte man, das Heulen dieses Pokémon sei der Ruf des Todes.
column23: enThe flames it breathes when angry contain toxins. If they cause a burn, it will hurt forever.

column8: enKingdra lives at extreme ocean depths that are otherwise uninhabited. It has long been believed that the yawning of this Pokémon creates spiraling ocean currents.
column9: enIt sleeps quietly, deep on the seafloor. When it comes up to the surface, it creates a huge whirlpool that can swallow even ships.
column10: deSeine abgefallenen Schuppen tragen einen tiefen Glanz in sich und sind so hochwertig, dass selbst Königshäuser mit ihnen beschenkt werden.
column11: deSeedraking schläft auf dem Meeresboden, wo ansonsten niemand lebt. Wenn ein Sturm aufkommt, wacht es auf und macht sich auf die Suche nach Beute.
column12: deKein anderes Lebewesen kann in den Tiefen existieren, in denen es schläft und so Energie speichert.
column13: enIt stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life-forms can survive.
column14: deWenn ein Sturm aufzieht, schwimmt es an die Meeresoberfläche. Stößt es auf ein Dragoran, bricht ein erbitterter Kampf zwischen ihnen aus.
column15: enWith the arrival of a storm at sea, this Pokémon will show itself on the surface. When a Kingdra and a Dragonite meet, a fierce battle ensues.
column16: enScales shed by this Pokémon have such a splendorous gleam to them that they’ve been given to royalty as gifts.
column17: deMan sagt, es hause in Unterwasserhöhlen. Es kann mächtige Strudel generieren, wenn es gähnt.
column18: enKINGDRA sleeps on the seafloor where it is otherwise devoid of life. When a storm arrives, the POKéMON is said to awaken and wander about in search of prey.
column19: deSeedraking lebt in den Tiefen des Ozeans, die ansonsten verwaist sind. Lange hat man geglaubt, dass das Gähnen dieses Pokémon Strudel auslöst.
column20: enIt stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life forms can survive.
column21: enIt sleeps deep on the ocean floor to build its energy. It is said to cause tornadoes as it wakes.
column22: enKINGDRA lives at extreme ocean depths that are otherwise uninhabited. It has long been believed that the yawning of this POKéMON creates spiraling ocean currents.
column23: enIt is said that it usually hides in underwater caves. It can create whirlpools by yawning.
column24: enIt lives in caves on the seafloor and creates giant whirlpools every time it moves.
column25: enKingdra sleeps on the seafloor where it is otherwise devoid of life. When a storm arrives, the Pokémon is said to awaken and wander about in search of prey.

column8: enPHANPY’s big ears serve as broad fans. When it becomes hot, it flaps the ears busily to cool down. Even the young are very strong.
column9: enDuring the deserted morning hours, it comes ashore where it deftly uses its trunk to take a shower.
column10: deAls Zeichen seiner Zuneigung stupst es dich mit dem Rüssel, was dich aber buchstäblich umwerfen könnte.
column11: enFor its nest, PHANPY digs a vertical pit  in the ground at the edge of a river. It marks the area around its nest with its trunk to let the others know that the area has been claimed.
column12: enPHANPY uses its long nose to shower itself. When others gather around, they thoroughly douse each other with water. These POKéMON can be seen drying their soaking-wet bodies at the edge of water.
column13: enIt swings its long snout around playfully, but because it is so strong, this can be dangerous.
column14: deUm sich ein Nest zu bauen, gräbt Phanpy am Flussufer ein Loch ins Erdreich. Es markiert die Umgebung seines Nests mithilfe seines Rüssels, damit seine Artgenossen wissen, dass dieses Gebiet vergeben ist.
column15: enFor its nest, Phanpy digs a vertical pit in the ground at the edge of a river. It marks the area around its nest with its trunk to let the others know that the area has been claimed.
column16: enIt swings its long snout around playfully, but because it is so strong, that can be dangerous.
column17: enIt is strong despite its compact size. It can easily pick up and carry an adult human on its back.
column18: enPhanpy uses its long nose to shower itself. When others gather around, they thoroughly douse each other with water. These Pokémon can be seen drying their soaking-wet bodies at the edge of water.
column19: deTrotz seiner geringen Größe ist es stark. Es kann einen Erwachsenen mühelos auf dem Rücken tragen.
column20: dePhanpy kann sich mit seiner Nase selbst duschen. Wenn andere dabei sind, begießen sie sich gegenseitig mit Wasser. Danach trocknet es seinen durchnässten Körper am Ufer.
column21: enAs a sign of affection, it bumps with its snout. However, it is so strong, it may send you flying.

column8: enIf Donphan were to tackle with its hard body, even a house could be destroyed. Using its massive strength, the Pokémon helps clear rock and mud slides that block mountain trails.
column9: enDONPHAN’s favorite attack is curling its body into a ball, then charging at its foe while rolling at high speed. Once it starts rolling, this POKéMON can’t stop very easily.
column10: enIt has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its tackle is strong enough to knock down a house.
column11: enIt attacks by curling up, then rolling into its foe. It can blow apart a house in one hit.
column12: enA DONPHAN is so strong it can easily haul a dump truck. Its hide has toughened to a rock-hard state. An ordinary sort of attack won’t even leave a scratch.
column13: enIt attacks by curling up then rolling into its foe. It can blow apart a house in one hit.
column14: enThe longer and bigger its tusks, the higher its rank in its herd. The tusks take long to grow.
column15: deDonphan kann mit seinem harten Körper sogar ein Haus zerstören. Mit seiner imposanten Kraft hilft dieses Pokémon dabei, Erdrutsche aus dem Weg zu räumen, die Bergpfade versperren.
column16: enIf DONPHAN were to tackle with its hard body, even a house could be destroyed. Using its massive strength, the POKéMON helps clear rock and mud slides that block mountain trails.
column17: enIt has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its Tackle is strong enough to knock down a house.
column18: enIt has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its TACKLE is strong enough to knock down a house.
column19: deJe größer und länger die Stoßzähne, desto höher ist ihr Rang in der Herde, doch das dauert lange.
column20: enThe longer and bigger its tusks, the higher its rank in its herd. The tusks take a long time to grow.
column21: enBecause this POKéMON's skin is so tough, a normal attack won't even leave a scratch on it.
column22: deAufgrund seiner scharfen Stoßzähne und seiner rauen Haut könnte es mit Tackle ein Haus niederreißen.
column23: deDonphans Lieblingsattacke ist es, als Ball zusammengerollt mit voller Wucht gegen seine Gegner zu krachen. Wenn es erst einmal rollt, ist dieses Pokémon nur schwer aufzuhalten.
column24: enDonphan’s favorite attack is curling its body into a ball, then charging at its foe while rolling at high speed. Once it starts rolling, this Pokémon can’t stop very easily.

column8: dePorygon2 wurde in einem wissenschaftlichen Experiment von Menschen erzeugt. Es verfügt über künstliche Intelligenz, wodurch es selbstständig neue Gesten und Gefühle erlernen kann.
column9: enWith planetary development software installed, it became capable of working in space.
column10: enThis is a Porygon that was updated with special data. Porygon2 develops itself by learning about many different subjects all on its own.
column11: enAI has been installed in it. It learns various things all on its own, but it even remembers things it doesn’t need to know.
column12: enFurther research enhanced its abilities. Sometimes, it may exhibit motions that were not programmed.
column13: deSeine Fähigkeiten wurden verbessert. Es zeigt manchmal sogar Handlungen, die nicht einprogrammiert sind.
column14: enPorygon2 was created by humans using the power of science. The man-made Pokémon has been endowed with artificial intelligence that enables it to learn new gestures and emotions on its own.

column8: deEs wird unterirdisch geboren und frisst sich durchs Erdreich an die Oberfläche zu seinen Eltern. Es verabscheut grelles, blendendes Licht.

column8: enThis troublesome Pokémon can’t wait to evolve, so it relieves its stress by wildly propelling itself around using compressed gas.

column15: deTrotz seiner harten Schale kann es sich frei bewegen. Es ist flink und äußerst zerstörerisch.
column16: deEs ist sehr stark und sein Panzer ist steinhart. Setzt es Fuchtler ein, kann es einen Berg umstürzen.
column17: enPupitar creates a gas inside its body that it compresses and forcefully ejects to propel itself like a jet. The body is very durable—it avoids damage even if it hits solid steel.
column18: enIts shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.
column19: deSein Körper ist hart wie Fels. Es lässt mit Hochdruck Gas ab, um wie eine Rakete nach oben zu schießen.
column20: deObwohl es sich noch im Puppenstadium befindet, bewegt es sich rege. Es hat schon Arme und Beine unter seinem harten Panzer.
column21: deEs kann seine Entwicklung nicht erwarten und stört andere, wenn es zum Stressabbau mithilfe von komprimiertem Gas durch die Luft zischt.
column22: enIts body is as hard as bedrock. By venting pressurized gas, it can launch itself like a rocket.
column23: dePupitar erzeugt in seinem Körper ein Gas, das es komprimiert und kraftvoll ausstößt, um sich wie ein Düsenflugzeug anzutreiben. Sein Körper ist sehr robust und er hält auch massivem Stahl stand.
column24: enPUPITAR creates a gas inside its body that it compresses and forcefully ejects to propel itself like a jet. The body is very durable - it avoids damage even if it hits solid steel.
column25: enIt will not stay still, even while it's a pupa. It already has arms and legs under its solid shell.
column26: enA PUPITAR creates a gas inside its body that it ejects under compression to propel itself like a jet. Its body can withstand a collision with solid steel.
column27: enEven sealed in its shell, it can move freely. Hard and fast, it has outstanding destructive power.

column8: deDieses Pokémon ist ein wandelndes Desaster. Berge, die ihm im Weg stehen, reißt es ein und Häuser macht es dem Erdboden gleich.
column9: deEs besitzt so viel Kraft, dass es mit nur einer Hand die Erde beben lassen und Berge zerbröckeln kann.
column10: enExtremely strong, it can change the landscape. It has an insolent nature that makes it not care about others.
column11: deEs durchstreift die Berge auf der Suche nach Gegnern. Begegnet es einem, der ihm nicht ebenbürtig ist, ignoriert es ihn und zieht weiter.

column13: deSeine Schritte erzeugen Erdbeben, die selbst große Berge plätten und die Form ganzer Landstriche verändern.

column18: enIts body can’t be harmed by any sort of attack, so it is very eager to make challenges against enemies.
column19: enIt wanders through the mountains seeking opponents to fight. If it finds an opponent that’s not worthy, Tyranitar ignores it and wanders on.

column21: enIf it rampages, it knocks down mountains and buries rivers. Maps must be redrawn afterward.

column8: enTreecko is cool, calm, and collected—it never panics under any situation. If a bigger foe were to glare at this Pokémon, it would glare right back without conceding an inch of ground.

column10: enIt makes its nest in a giant tree in the forest. It ferociously guards against anything nearing its territory. It is said to be the protector of the forest’s trees.
column11: enTreecko has small hooks on the bottom of its feet that enable it to scale vertical walls. This Pokémon attacks by slamming foes with its thick tail.
column12: enTREECKO has small hooks on the bottom of its feet that enable it to scale vertical walls. This POKéMON attacks by slamming foes with its thick tail.
column13: enIt quickly scales even vertical walls. It senses humidity with its tail to predict the next day’s weather.
column14: deMit den kleinen Haken unter seinen Füßen kann Geckarbor an Wänden hochklettern. Dieses Pokémon greift Feinde an, indem es mit seinem mächtigen Schweif zuschlägt.

column18: enSmall hooks on the bottom of its feet catch on walls and ceilings. That is how it can hang from above.
column19: deDank seiner mit winzigen Stacheln besetzten Sohlen haftet es sogar kopfüber an Wänden und Decken.

column20: deIn seinem Bauch ist ein Flammensack, der stets brennt. Umarmt man es, fühlt es sich warm an.

column8: enOn land, it can powerfully lift large boulders by planting its four feet and heaving. It sleeps by burying itself in soil at the water’s edge.

column13: deIm Wasser atmet Hydropi durch die Kiemen an seinen Wangen. Wenn es im Kampf in eine brenzlige Situation gerät, setzt es seine verblüffende Kraft ein, mit der es Felsbrocken zerquetschen kann, die größer sind als es selbst.

column16: enIn water, MUDKIP breathes using the gills on its cheeks. If it is faced with a tight situation in battle, this POKéMON will unleash its amazing power - it can crush rocks bigger than itself.

column18: enIts power can crush boulders. It rests by covering itself with mud at the bottom of a river.
column19: deDie Flosse auf seinem Kopf prüft die Strömung des Wassers. Dieses Pokémon kann Felsen heben.

column8: enIt is at its best when on muddy ground with poor footing. It quickly overwhelms foes struggling in mud.
column9: deSeine kräftigen Beine geben ihm sicheren Halt. Es gräbt sich in Dreck ein, wenn es schlafen will.
column10: enThe surface of MARSHTOMP’s body is enveloped by a thin, sticky film that enables it to live on land. This POKéMON plays in mud on beaches when the ocean tide is low.
column11: enMarshtomp is much faster at traveling through mud than it is at swimming. This Pokémon’s hindquarters exhibit obvious development, giving it the ability to walk on just its hind legs.

column13: deMoorabbel kommt im Schlamm besser voran als im Wasser. Das Hinterteil dieses Pokémon ist gut entwickelt, sodass es auf seinen Hinterbeinen laufen kann.
column14: enLiving on muddy ground that provides poor footing has made its legs sturdy.
column15: deDer zähe Morast seiner Heimat hat seine Beine gestählt und ihnen gewaltige Kraft verliehen.
column16: deMoorabbels Körper ist mit einem klebrigen Film überzogen, durch den es an Land überleben kann. Dieses Pokémon spielt bei Ebbe in den Schlammpfützen am Strand.
column17: enMARSHTOMP is much faster at traveling through mud than it is at swimming. This POKéMON’s hindquarters exhibit obvious development, giving it the ability to walk on just its hind legs.
column18: enIts sturdy legs give it sure footing, even in mud. It burrows into dirt to sleep.
column19: enIts toughened hind legs enable it to stand upright. Because it weakens if its skin dries out, it replenishes fluids by playing in mud.

column9: enSWAMPERT is very strong. It has enough power to easily drag a boulder weighing more than a ton. This POKéMON also has powerful vision that lets it see even in murky water.
column10: enIt can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.
column11: deSumpex ist sehr stark. Es kann mit Leichtigkeit einen Felsblock bewegen, der über eine Tonne wiegt. Weiterhin ist es mit einem exzellenten Sehvermögen ausgestattet, wodurch es auch in trüben Gewässern sehen kann.
column12: deSumpex kann Stürme voraussagen, indem es feine Veränderungen der Geräusche von Wellen und Wind mit seinen Flossen wahrnimmt. Wenn eine Sturmfront aufzieht, häuft es Felsbrocken an, um sich zu schützen.
column13: deEs kann im Schwimmen ein großes Schiff ziehen. Seine Gegner schlägt es mit Schlägen seiner Arme zurück.
column14: deSchon ein Hieb seiner steinharten Arme schlägt selbst den stärksten Felsen zu Bruch.

column16: enIts arms are hard as rock. With one swing, it can break a boulder into pieces.

column19: enIf it senses the approach of a storm and a tidal wave, it protects its seaside nest by piling up boulders. It swims as fast as a jet ski.

column8: deSobald Fiffyen eine Bewegung wahrnimmt, schnappt es zu. Es jagt seine Beute so lange, bis sie vollkommen erschöpft ist. Wenn sich die Beute allerdings wehrt, kann es passieren, dass Fiffyen klein beigibt und flieht.
column9: deEin beharrliches Pokémon, das seine Beute jagt, bis diese erschöpft ist.
column10: enPOOCHYENA is an omnivore - it will eat anything. A distinguishing feature is how large its fangs are compared to its body. This POKéMON tries to intimidate its foes by making the hair on its tail bristle out.
column11: enAt first sight, POOCHYENA takes a bite at anything that moves. This POKéMON chases after prey until the victim becomes exhausted. However, it may turn tail if the prey strikes back.
column12: enAt first sight, Poochyena takes a bite at anything that moves. This Pokémon chases after prey until the victim becomes exhausted. However, it may turn tail if the prey strikes back.
column13: enA Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.
column14: enIt has a very tenacious nature. Its acute sense of smell lets it chase a chosen prey without ever losing track.
column15: enIt chases its prey until the victim becomes exhausted. However, it turns tail if the prey strikes back.

column17: enIt savagely threatens foes with bared fangs. It chases after fleeing targets tenaciously. It turns tail and runs, however, if the foe strikes back.

column19: deEs hat ein hartnäckiges Wesen. Sein präziser Geruchssinn ermöglicht ihm, seiner Beute nachzujagen, ohne die Fährte zu verlieren.

column9: deMagnayen zieht in Gruppen durch die Wildnis. Durch seine Erfahrungen in der Wildnis hört es nur auf Trainer, die außergewöhnliche Fähigkeiten haben.
column10: enIt will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.
column11: enIt will always obey the commands of a skilled TRAINER. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.
column12: enMIGHTYENA gives obvious signals when it is preparing to attack. It starts to growl deeply and then flattens its body. This POKéMON will bite savagely with its sharply pointed fangs.
column13: enMightyena gives obvious signals when it is preparing to attack. It starts to growl deeply and then flattens its body. This Pokémon will bite savagely with its sharply pointed fangs.
column14: enIt chases down prey in a pack. It will never disobey the commands of a skilled Trainer.
column15: deEs wird stets die Befehle eines begabten Trainers befolgen. Dieses Verhalten geht darauf zurück, dass es früher im Rudel lebte.
column16: enIt chases down prey in a pack of around ten. They defeat foes with perfectly coordinated teamwork.
column17: deMagnayen sendet deutliche Signale aus, wenn es sich auf einen Angriff vorbereitet. Es knurrt heftig und macht sich flach. Dieses Pokémon kann mit seinen spitzen Zähnen brutal zubeißen.
column18: enMIGHTYENA travel and act as a pack in the wild. The memory of its life in the wild compels the POKéMON to obey only those TRAINERS that it recognizes to possess superior skill.
column19: enMightyena travel and act as a pack in the wild. The memory of its life in the wild compels the Pokémon to obey only those Trainers that it recognizes to possess superior skill.
column20: deEs jagt in Rudeln von ungefähr zehn Magnayen. Beim Einkreisen der Beute arbeiten sie perfekt zusammen.

column8: enA POKéMON with abundant curiosity. It shows an interest in everything, so it always zigs and zags.
column9: enIt gets interested in everything, which is why it zigs and zags. It is good at finding items.
column10: enThe hair on ZIGZAGOON’s back is  bristly. It rubs the hard back hair against trees to leave its territorial markings. This POKéMON may play dead to fool foes in battle.
column11: enRubbing its nose against the ground, it always wanders about back and forth in search of something. It is distinguished by the zigzag footprints it leaves.
column12: deSie markieren ihr Revier, indem sie ihr hartes Fell an Bäumen reiben. Sie sind weniger aggressiv als die Zigzachs der Galar-Region.
column13: enIt marks its territory by rubbing its bristly fur on trees. This variety of Zigzagoon is friendlier and calmer than the kind native to Galar.
column14: deDiese Form nahmen die Zigzachs Galars an, um sich an andere Regionen anzupassen. Sie haben ein Talent dafür, Dinge aufzuspüren.
column15: deDas Haar auf Zigzachs’ Rücken ist struppig. Es reibt seine Rückenborsten an Bäumen, um sein Revier zu markieren. Dieses Pokémon stellt sich im Kampf schon einmal tot, um seine Feinde reinzulegen.
column16: enZIGZAGOON restlessly wanders everywhere at all times. This POKéMON does so because it is very curious. It becomes interested in anything that it happens to see.
column17: deZigzachs ist ein rastloser Wanderer. Dieses Pokémon ist nämlich sehr neugierig und es inspiziert alles, was ihm zufällig begegnet.
column18: enA Pokémon with abundant curiosity. It shows an interest in everything, so it always zigzags.
column19: deEs läuft im Zickzack. Es hat das Talent, Items im Gras, aber auch im Boden, zu finden.
column20: enIt walks in zigzag fashion. It’s good at finding items in the grass and even in the ground.
column21: deEin sehr neugieriges Pokémon. Es zeigt an allem Interesse, daher läuft es zickzack.
column22: enIt walks in zigzag fashion. It is good at finding items in the grass and even in the ground.
column23: enZigzagoon that adapted to regions outside Galar acquired this appearance. If you’ve lost something, this Pokémon can likely find it.
column24: enThe hair on Zigzagoon’s back is bristly. It rubs the hard back hair against trees to leave its territorial markings. This Pokémon may play dead to fool foes in battle.
column25: enZigzagoon restlessly wanders everywhere at all times. This Pokémon does so because it is very curious. It becomes interested in anything that it happens to see.

column8: enWhen hunting, Linoone will make a beeline straight for the prey at a full run. While this Pokémon is capable of topping 60 mph, it has to come to a screeching halt before it can turn.
column9: enLinoone always runs full speed and only in straight lines. If facing an obstacle, it makes a right-angle turn to evade it. This Pokémon is very challenged by gently curving roads.
column10: enLINOONE always runs full speed and only in straight lines. If facing an obstacle, it makes a right-angle turn to evade it. This POKéMON is very challenged by gently curving roads.
column11: deGeradaks rennt immer mit vollem Tempo und nie im Zickzack, sondern nur gerade. Wenn ihm ein Hindernis im Weg ist, läuft es rechtwinklig daran vorbei. Für dieses Pokémon stellen leichte Kurven eine große Herausforderung dar.
column12: enIt uses its explosive speed and razor-sharp claws to bring down prey. Running along winding paths is not its strong suit.
column13: enWhen running in a straight line, it can top 60 miles per hour. However, it has a tough time with curved roads.
column14: deEs erlegt seine Beute mit schnellen Bewegungen und scharfen Klauen. Kurven zu nehmen bereitet ihm aber große Schwierigkeiten.
column15: deAuf der Jagd rennt Geradaks so schnell es kann schnurstracks hinter seiner Beute her. Es erreicht zwar über 100 km/h, muss aber erst aus vollem Lauf zum Stehen kommen, bevor es die Richtung wechseln kann.
column16: deEs stürzt sich mit 100 km/h auf Beute. Aber es kann nur geradeaus laufen und die Jagd misslingt oft.
column17: enWhen hunting, LINOONE will make a beeline straight for the prey at a full run. While this POKéMON is capable of topping 60 mph, it has to come to a screeching halt before it can turn.
column18: deSein Fell ist kräftig und fest. Rasierpinsel, die aus seinen ausgefallenen Haaren hergestellt werden, sind absolute Luxusartikel.
column19: enIt charges prey at speeds over 60 mph. However, because it can only run straight, it often fails.
column20: deAuf gerader Strecke erreicht es mühelos 100 km/h. Nur die Kurven bereiten ihm große Schwierigkeiten.
column21: enIt is exceedingly fast if it only has to run in a straight line. When it spots pond-dwelling prey underwater, it quickly leaps in and catches it with its sharp claws.
column22: enWhen running in a straight line, it can easily top 60 miles an hour. It has a tough time with curved roads.
column23: enIts fur is strong and supple. Shaving brushes made with shed Linoone hairs are highly prized.

column8: deWaumpel wird von Schwalboss als Beute angesehen. Dieses Pokémon wehrt sich gegen angreifende Räuber, indem es die Stacheln an seinem Hinterteil auf sie richtet. Es schwächt seine Gegner mit dem Gift, das aus den Stacheln austritt.
column9: enUsing the spikes on its rear end, Wurmple peels the bark off trees and feeds on the sap that oozes out. This Pokémon’s feet are tipped with suction pads that allow it to cling to glass without slipping.
column10: enWURMPLE is targeted by SWELLOW as prey. This POKéMON will try to resist by pointing the spikes on its rear at the attacking predator. It will weaken the foe by leaking poison from the spikes.
column11: deEs isst am liebsten Blätter. Wird es von einem Staralili angegriffen, verteidigt es sich mit Stacheln.
column12: deEs haust in Wäldern und auf Wiesen. Bei Angriffen wehrt es sich mit den Giftstacheln auf seinem Hinterteil.
column13: enIt lives amidst tall grass and in forests. When attacked, it resists by pointing its venomous spikes at the foe.
column14: enUsing the spikes on its rear end,   WURMPLE peels the bark off trees and feeds on the sap that oozes out. This POKéMON’s feet are tipped with suction pads that allow it to cling to glass without slipping.
column15: enIt loves to eat leaves. If it is attacked by a Starly, it will defend itself with its spiked rear.
column16: enIt sticks to tree branches and eats leaves. The thread it spits from its mouth, which becomes gooey when it touches air, slows the movement of its foes.
column17: enWurmple is targeted by Swellow as prey. This Pokémon will try to resist by pointing the spikes on its rear at the attacking predator. It will weaken the foe by leaking poison from the spikes.
column18: enOften targeted by bird Pokémon, it desperately resists by releasing poison from its tail spikes.
column19: enIt lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels attacks by  raising up the spikes on its rear.
column20: enIt spits a white silk that turns sticky when it contacts air. It is used to immobilize foes.
column21: deWaumpel kann mit den Stacheln an seinem Hinterteil die Rinde von Bäumen entfernen und ernährt sich von dem austretenden Saft. An den Füßen dieses Pokémon befinden sich Saugnäpfe, mit denen es auch an Glas nicht abrutscht.
column22: enIt lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels attacks by raising up the spikes on its rear.
column23: enLikes sap and is abundant in the wild. Why it evolves into various different forms is unknown. One cannot tell from a Wurmple's appearance which form it will take when it evolves.
column24: enIt loves to eat leaves. If it is attacked by a STARLY, it will defend itself with its spiked rear.

column8: enSILCOON was thought to endure hunger and not consume anything before its evolution. However, it is now thought that this POKéMON slakes its thirst by drinking rainwater that collects on its silk.
column9: enWraps itself in thin strings of silk while it stores energy for evolution. It can't extend its limbs and its movement is slow, but its eyes keep a sharp lookout—Silcoon is always on guard.
column10: deEs trinkt den Tau, der sich in der Seide fängt, und wartet auf seine Entwicklung. Sein Kokon schützt es.
column11: enIt conserves its energy by moving as little as possible. It awaits evolution while drinking only a little rainwater.
column12: enIt prepares for evolution using the energy it stored while it was a WURMPLE. It keeps watch over the surroundings with its two eyes.
column13: enHaving wrapped silk around the branches of a tree, it quiescently awaits evolution.
column14: enIt anchors itself by wrapping twigs with the silk from its body. It motionlessly awaits evolution.
column15: enIt drinks dew that collects on its silk and waits for evolution. Its hard cocoon repels attacks.
column16: deBisher nahm man an, dass Schaloko vor seiner Entwicklung hungert und überhaupt nichts zu sich nimmt. Nun aber wird behauptet, dass es seinen Durst mit Regenwasser löscht, das sich auf seiner seidigen Hülle sammelt.
column17: enSilcoon tethers itself to a tree branch using silk to keep from falling. There, this Pokémon hangs quietly while it awaits evolution. It peers out of the silk cocoon through a small hole.
column18: deEs bindet sich mit Seide an Äste und trinkt Regenwasser, während es starr auf seine Entwicklung wartet.
column19: enSILCOON tethers itself to a tree branch using silk to keep from falling. There,  this POKéMON hangs quietly while it awaits evolution. It peers out of the silk cocoon through a small hole.
column20: enIt wraps silk around the branches of a tree. It drinks rainwater on its silk while awaiting evolution.
column21: enSilcoon was thought to endure hunger and not consume anything before its evolution. However, it is now thought that this Pokémon slakes its thirst by drinking rainwater that collects on its silk.
