column8: enThis Pokémon becomes more energetic the more sunlight there is. The part resembling a shell is similar to silt and is slightly damp and warm to the touch.
column9: enThe shell on its back is made of soil. On a very healthy TURTWIG, the shell should feel moist.
column10: enThe shell on its back is made of soil. On a very healthy Turtwig, the shell should feel moist.
column11: deSein Körper lebt von der Photosynthese, die Sauerstoff freisetzt. Ist es durstig, welkt sein Blatt.
column12: enIt undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.
column13: enMade from soil, the shell on its back hardens when it drinks water. It lives along lakes.
column14: deIm Sonnenlicht betreibt sein ganzer Körper Photosynthese. Sein Panzer besteht aus hartem Lehm.
column15: enPhotosynthesis occurs across its body under the sun. The shell on its back is actually hardened soil.

column8: enIt knows where pure water wells up. It carries fellow Pokémon there on its back.
column9: deEs weiß, wo es reinstes Quellwasser finden kann. Trägt andere Pokémon auf seinem Rücken dorthin.
column10: enAppears where there is clean spring water. The fruit that grows on the shrubs on its shell is sweet, nutritious, and truly delicious.
column11: enThe shell is hardened soil. Some Pokémon come to peck the berries growing on the trees on its back.
column12: enIt lives along water in forests. In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.
column13: deEs lebt in der Nähe von Wasser in Wäldern. Tagsüber verlässt es diese, um ein Sonnenbad zu nehmen.
column14: enA GROTLE that lives in the forest is said to have its own secret springwater.

column8: enSome Pokémon are born on a TORTERRA’s back and spend their entire life there.
column9: deKleine Pokémon fangen manchmal an, auf dem bewegungslosen Rücken Nester zu bauen.
column10: enSome Pokémon are born on a Torterra’s back and spend their entire life there.
column11: enThis remarkable, large-bodied Pokémon would serve beautifully as borrowed scenery for a garden, and its strength is peerless. Torterra roams the wilderness in search of clean water.
column12: deIn alten Zeiten malten die Menschen sich aus, die Erde ruhe auf dem Rücken eines riesigen Chelterrar.
column13: enAncient people imagined that beneath the ground, a gigantic TORTERRA dwelled.
column14: enGroups of this Pokémon migrating in search of water have been mistaken for “moving forests.”
column15: enSmall Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.
column16: enAncient people imagined that beneath the ground, a gigantic Torterra dwelled.

column8: enIts fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly. Even rain can’t extinguish the fire.
column9: enThe gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.
column10: enIt agilely scales sheer cliffs to live atop craggy mountains. Its fire is put out when it sleeps.
column11: enIt is very agile. Before going to sleep, it extinguishes the flame on its tail to prevent fires.
column12: enFull of vigor and always in high spirits. It was once known by the name ”Lantern-Tail” and feared as some kind of apparition.
column13: deAn seinem Rücken verbrennt es die Gase aus seinem Bauch. Geht es ihm schlecht, leuchtet es weniger hell.
column14: deDas Feuer an seinem Hinterteil wird durch Gase im Bauch genährt. Selbst Regen löscht es nicht.

column8: deEs stürzt sich von Decken und Wänden auf Beute. Sein feuriger Schweif ist nur eine seiner Waffen.
column9: deEs kontrolliert die Stärke des Feuers auf seinem Schweif geschickt, um Gegner auf Distanz zu halten.
column10: enIt uses ceilings and walls to launch aerial attacks. Its fiery tail is but one weapon.
column11: enThe deeper the blue on its face, the more powerful it will grow to become. It leaps about every which way and lands powerful blows against its opponents with the flame on its tail.
column12: enA bigger fire on its tail and a brighter blue pattern on its face means its rank in its pack is higher.
column13: enTo intimidate attackers, it stretches the fire on its tail to make itself appear bigger.
column14: enIt skillfully controls the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

column8: enIt tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style.
column9: deEs hält den Gegner mit flinken Bewegungen zum Narren. Im Kampf setzt es alle Gliedmaßen ein.
column10: enA tall, hardy Pokémon with a dazzling appearance. It shrouds itself in flame and battles as if engaged in dance—truly a sight to behold.
column11: enIts crown of fire is indicative of its fiery nature. It is beaten by none in terms of quickness.
column12: enIt uses a special kind of martial arts involving all its limbs. Its fire never goes out.
column13: enIt uses unique fighting moves with fire on its hands and feet. It will take on any opponent.
column14: deSeine Krone aus Feuer ist Zeichen seines feurigen Wesens. Niemand ist schneller im Kampf als dieses Pokémon.

column8: enBecause it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.
column9: enA poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.
column10: deEs ist sehr stolz und nimmt daher kein Futter von anderen an. Seine dicken Daunen schützen vor Kälte.
column11: enIt lives along shores in northern countries. A skilled swimmer, it dives for over 10 minutes to hunt.
column12: deEinmischung kann es gar nicht leiden. Es ist bockig und fasst nur schwer Zutrauen zu seinem Trainer.
column13: enIt doesn’t like to be taken care of. It’s difficult to bond with since it won’t listen to its Trainer.
column14: enPrefers cold climes and appears along coasts. It's an adorable little thing—as cute as any child—but it's also prideful, unwilling to accept handouts from people.

column8: dePliprin sind Einzelgänger. Ein Schlag ihrer kräftigen Flügel haut selbst große Bäume entzwei.
column9: enIt lives alone, away from others. Apparently, every one of them believes it is the most important.
column10: enIts wings deliver wicked blows that snap even the thickest of trees. It searches for prey in icy seas.
column11: enBecause every Prinplup considers itself to be the most important, they can never form a group.
column12: enIt swims gracefully through the frigid sea and sings with a voice like the roaring tide. It has powerful, sturdy wings and dignity to match.
column13: enBecause every PRINPLUP considers itself to be the most important, they can never form a group.
column14: enIt lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees.
column15: deEs lebt allein, entfernt von anderen. Jedes von ihnen denkt, es sei das Bedeutendste unter ihnen.

column8: enThe three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.
column9: enSince ancient times, it has been revered by the people of Hisui, who call it the Master of the Waves. Its wings are a match for even master-crafted blades.
column10: deDie drei Hörner, die aus dem Schnabel wachsen, stehen für Kraft. Ein Anführer hat die größten Hörner.
column11: enIt swims as fast as a jet boat. The edges of its wings are sharp and can slice apart drifting ice.
column12: deEs schwimmt so schnell wie ein Rennboot. Seine Flügel haben scharfe Seiten und können Packeis schneiden.
column13: enIf anyone were to hurt its pride, it would slash them with wings that can cleave through an ice floe.
column14: enIt avoids unnecessary disputes, but it will decimate anything that threatens its pride.

column8: deAuf der Suche nach Käfer-Pokémon fliegen Schwärme von ihnen durchs Land. Ihr Ruf erzeugt großen Lärm.
column9: deIhr Schwarm ist stets groß. Obwohl es kleine Pokémon sind, schwingen sie ihre Flügel mit enormer Kraft.
column10: enThey live in the fields and mountains, gathering in large flocks. Their cries are quite obnoxious. Though small, their wings are strong—a strike from them leaves pain that persists for a week.
column11: enUsually with a large flock, it is barely noticeable when alone. Its cries are very strident.
column12: enThey flock in great numbers. Though small, they flap their wings with great power.
column13: enThey flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.
column14: enBecause they are weak individually, they form groups. However, they bicker if the group grows too big.

column8: enIt lives in forests and fields. Squabbles over territory occur when flocks collide.
column9: deEs lebt in Wäldern und auf Wiesen. Treffen Schwärme aufeinander, streiten sie sich um das Revier.
column10: enThey maintain huge flocks, although fierce scuffles break out between various flocks.
column11: enRecognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.
column12: enThey form remarkably large flocks and are constantly fighting amongst themselves. I suspect that those with magnificent plumes on their heads are the strong ones.
column13: enRecognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a STARAVIA cries noisily.
column14: enIt flies around forests and fields in search of bug Pokémon. It stays within a huge flock.
column15: deEs neigt dazu, sich in großen Gruppen zu bewegen. Zwischen ihnen kommt es zu heftigen Kämpfen.

column8: enWhen Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.
column9: deWird Staravia zu Staraptor, verlässt es die Gruppe und lebt allein. Seine Spannweite ist gigantisch.
column10: enIt has a savage nature. It will courageously challenge foes that are much larger.
column11: enIt never stops attacking even if it is injured. It fusses over the shape of its comb.
column12: enThe muscles in its wings and legs are strong. It can easily fly while gripping a small Pokémon.
column13: enIt has left the flock, having gained strength enough to survive on its own. The astounding force with which Staraptor flies through the air allows it to carry away large, burly targets.
column14: deDie Muskeln in seinen Flügeln und Beinen sind stark. Es kann im Flug sogar ein anderes Pokémon tragen.
column15: enWhen STARAVIA evolve into STARAPTOR, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

column8: enWhen Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.
column9: deWird Staravia zu Staraptor, verlässt es die Gruppe und lebt allein. Seine Spannweite ist gigantisch.
column10: enIt has a savage nature. It will courageously challenge foes that are much larger.
column11: enIt never stops attacking even if it is injured. It fusses over the shape of its comb.
column12: enThe muscles in its wings and legs are strong. It can easily fly while gripping a small Pokémon.
column13: enIt has left the flock, having gained strength enough to survive on its own. The astounding force with which Staraptor flies through the air allows it to carry away large, burly targets.
column14: deDie Muskeln in seinen Flügeln und Beinen sind stark. Es kann im Flug sogar ein anderes Pokémon tragen.
column15: enWhen STARAVIA evolve into STARAPTOR, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

column8: deEs knabbert ständig an Holz und Steinen, um seine Vorderzähne zu kürzen. Es nistet am Wasser.
column9: enIt constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.
column10: enWith nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.
column11: enA comparison revealed that Bidoof’s front teeth grow at the same rate as Rattata’s.
column12: deEs hat Nerven wie Drahtseile, nichts kann es erschüttern. Es ist agiler und aktiver, als es scheint.
column13: enIt lives in groups by the water.  It chews up boulders and trees around its nest with its incisors.
column14: enA comparison revealed that BIDOOF’s front teeth grow at the same rate as RATTATA’s.
column15: enBidoof has an unsophisticated face and is rarely flustered by anything. There have been incidents involving Bidoof sauntering into villages and gnawing on the houses without a single care.

column8: enWhile its movements out of water are sluggish, it can swim as fast as any FEEBAS.
column9: enIt busily makes its nest with stacks of branches and roots it has cut up with its sharp incisors.
column10: enA river dammed by BIBAREL will never overflow its banks, which is appreciated by people nearby.
column11: enA river dammed by Bibarel will never overflow its banks, which is appreciated by people nearby.
column12: deEs baut sein Nest, indem es in Flüssen Dämme aus Schlamm und Ästen baut. Ein fleißiger Arbeiter.
column13: enBibarel fur repels water and is also a fantastic material for heat retention. These Pokémon create dams on rivers to live in.
column14: enIt makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.
column15: deEmsig durchbeißt es mit seinen scharfen Vorderzähnen Wurzeln und Äste und baut daraus sein Nest.

column8: enWhen the trees take on new hues, more of these Pokémon appear. The tone they create by striking their antennae together resembles that of the marimba, an instrument of foreign lands.
column9: enIt shakes its head back to front, causing its antennae to hit each other and sound like a xylophone.
column10: enIt chats with others using the sounds of its colliding antennae. These sounds are fall hallmarks.
column11: deWenn seine zwei Antennen sich berühren, erklingt ein Ruf wie das Spiel eines Xylophons.
column12: deDiese Pokémon unterhalten sich, indem sie ihre Antennen aneinanderschlagen. Der Ton ist ihr Kennzeichen.
column13: enIts legs are short. Whenever it stumbles, its stiff antennae clack with a xylophone-like sound.
column14: enWhen its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.

column8: enIt crosses its knifelike arms in front of its chest when it cries. It can compose melodies ad lib.
column9: enBy allowing its cry to resonate in the hollow of its belly, it produces a captivating sound.
column10: deÜber seine Melodien teilt es seine Gefühle mit. Wissenschaftler untersuchen das Muster der Melodien.
column11: enIt uses its cutlass-like arms to produce sound, the melody of which varies from individual to individual. It is a worthwhile endeavor to seek out one's favorite tunes.
column12: enThere is a village that hosts a contest based on the amazingly variable cries of this Pokémon.
column13: deWenn es ruft, verschränkt es seine messerartigen Arme vor der Brust. Es komponiert aus dem Stegreif.
column14: enIt signals its emotions with its melodies. Scientists are studying these melodic patterns.

column8: deEs erzeugt Elektrizität durch das Strecken und Zusammenziehen seiner Muskeln. Bei Bedrohung glüht es.
column9: deEs bringt sein Fell mit Elektrizität zum Glühen. Um seinen Artgenossen Signale zu übermitteln, wedelt es mit seiner leuchtenden Schweifspitze.
column10: enThe extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.
column11: deDurch Muskelkontraktionen erzeugt es Strom. Heftiges Zittern ist bei Sheinux ein Anzeichen dafür, dass es sehr viel Elektrizität generiert.
column12: enThe extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. Its fur glows when it’s in trouble.
column13: enShakes its body to generate electricity. Its stature belies its aggression—one must be patient to tame this Pokémon.
column14: enElectricity makes this Pokémon’s fur glow. Shinx sends signals to others of its kind by shaking the tip of its tail while the tail tip is shining brightly.
column15: deIn Gefahr blendet es seinen Gegner mit seinem Fell und flieht, während der Gegner einen Moment blind ist.
column16: enIts forelegs have a muscle-based system of generating electricity. Its body shines if endangered.
column17: enAll of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.
column18: enThis Pokémon generates electricity by contracting its muscles. Excited trembling is a sign that Shinx is generating a tremendous amount of electricity.

column8: deDurch die Spitzen seiner scharfen Krallen strömt Elektrizität. Selbst kleine Kratzer verursachen Ohnmacht.
column9: deSeine Krallen geben Elektrizität ab, die stark genug ist, jemanden bewusstlos zu machen.
column10: enBy joining its tail with that of another Luxio, this Pokémon can receive some of the other Luxio’s electricity and power up its own electric blasts.
column11: deVerbindet es seinen Schweif mit dem eines Artgenossen, absorbiert es einen Teil von dessen Elektrizität und teilt stärkere Stromschläge aus.
column12: enUpon encountering an opponent, this Pokémon prepares for battle by extending its claws, which can put out 1,000,000 volts of electricity.
column13: enBy gathering their tails together, they collectively generate powerful electricity from their claws.
column14: deSieht es Gegner, macht es sich kampfbereit, indem es die Krallen an den Pfoten ausfährt, welche eine Million Volt austeilen können.
column15: enIts claws loose electricity with enough amperage to cause fainting. They live in small groups.
column16: enIt rests its forelegs on others to communicate with rhythmic electric pulses from its claws.
column17: enProudly uses its electrified claws as weapons. It seems to be a gracious Pokémon, evenly sharing the spoils of the hunt with others of its kind.
column18: enStrong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

column8: enSeeing through solid objects uses up a lot of Luxray’s electricity, so the Pokémon sleeps for long periods of time to store up energy.
column9: enWhen its eyes gleam gold, it can spot hiding prey--even those taking shelter behind a wall.
column10: enIt can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.
column11: enWhen its eyes gleam gold, it can spot hiding prey—even those taking shelter behind a wall.
column12: enLuxray can see through solid objects. It will instantly spot prey trying to hide behind walls, even if the walls are thick.
column13: enLuxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.
column14: deBeim Aufspüren von Gefahren sind Luxtras hellseherische Fähigkeiten äußerst hilfreich.
column15: deLeuchten seine Augen golden auf, kann es Beute, die sich versteckt, sehen. Es kann durch Wände sehen.
column16: deDieses Pokémon verfügt über einen Röntgenblick. So kann es im Nu Beute aufspüren, selbst wenn sich diese hinter dicken Wänden versteckt.
column17: enThey form packs, each having one male as leader. Legends say that when Luxray's two eyes shimmer with gold, the Pokémon can see through anything.
column18: enLUXRAY’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.
column19: deDer Einsatz seines Röntgenblicks verbraucht sehr viel Elektrizität. Luxtra gewinnt diese zurück, indem es lange schläft.
column20: enIt has eyes that can see through anything. It spots and captures prey hiding behind objects.

column8: enThis Pokémon is highly sensitive to temperature changes. When its bud starts to open, that means spring is right around the corner.
column9: enThe pollen it releases contains poison. If this Pokémon is raised on clean water, the poison’s toxicity is increased.
column10: enSensitive to changing temperatures, the bud blooms when it’s warm, releasing toxic pollen.
column11: deEs hat ein feines Gespür für die kleinsten Temperaturveränderungen. Wenn sich seine Knospe öffnet, bricht bald der Frühling an.
column12: deEs verstreut giftigen Pollen. Je sauberer das Wasser war, mit dem es gegossen wurde, desto stärker ist der Giftstoff.
column13: deIm Winter schließt es die Knospe. Im Frühjahr öffnet es die Knospe und gibt Pollen ab.
column14: enIt lives alongside clear ponds. It scatters pollen that induces harsh sneezing and runny noses.
column15: enWhen it feels the sun’s warm touch, it opens its bud to release pollen. It lives alongside clear pools.
column16: enOver the winter, it closes its bud and endures the cold. In spring, the bud opens and releases pollen.
column17: enWhen the sun's light strengthens, the bud atop this Pokémon's head opens. This is a sign to the people that the harsh winter is over, and the season of budding has begun.
column18: deBei Sonnenlicht öffnet es seine Knospe und gibt Pollen ab. Es lebt in der Nähe von sauberem Wasser.
column19: enSensitive to changing temperature, the bud is said to bloom when it feels the sun’s warm touch.

column8: enLuring prey with a sweet scent, it uses poison whips on its arms to poison, bind, and finish off the prey.
column9: deMit den Bewegungen eines Tänzers holt es aus und schlägt mit Ranken um sich, die giftige Dornen haben.
column10: enIts sweet aroma attracts prey. Then it spews poison. The more toxic it is, the sweeter its aroma.
column11: enWith the movements of a dancer, it strikes with whips that are densely lined with poison thorns.
column12: enThe poison in its right hand is quick acting. The poison in its left hand is slow acting. Both are life threatening.
column13: deEs lockt seine Beute mit süßem Duft an, um sie danach mit seinen dornigen Ranken zu peitschen oder zu würgen.
column14: deDas Gift aus seiner rechten Hand wirkt sofort, das aus seiner linken langsam. Beide Gifte können tödlich sein.
column15: enHidden within the bouquet on each hand are thorned whips loaded with virulent poison. Roserade moves gracefully as it corners its prey and mercilessly lashes them with its whips.
column16: deMit dem süßlichen Duft seiner Blüten betört es seine Gegner, bevor es sie mit dornigen Ranken heftig auspeitscht.
column17: enIt attracts prey with a sweet aroma, then downs it with thorny whips hidden in its arms.
column18: enEach of its hands contains different toxins, but both hands can jab with near-fatal power.
column19: enAfter captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.

column8: enA lifelong jungle dweller from 100 million years ago, it would snap obstructing trees with headbutts.
column9: enA lifelong jungle dweller from 100 million years ago, it would snap obstructing trees with head butts.
column10: enIt was resurrected from an iron ball-like fossil. It downs prey with its head butts.
column11: enCRANIDOS toughen up their already rock-hard heads by headbutting one another.
column12: enAn incredibly rare sight. They duel each other by ramming their heads together, and the resulting sound echoes throughout the area like the pealing of a bell.
column13: enA primeval Pokémon, it possesses a hard and sturdy skull, lacking any intelligence within.
column14: enIts hard skull is its distinguishing feature. It snapped trees by headbutting them, and then it fed on their ripe berries.
column15: enIt lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its skull is as hard as iron.
column16: deSeine Besonderheit ist der harte Schädel. Mit seinem Kopfstoß fällte es Bäume, um deren Früchte fressen zu können.
column17: deIn den Erdschichten, in denen Fossilien von Koknodon entdeckt werden, finden sich auch Überreste abgebrochener Bäume.
column18: enIt lived in jungles around a hundred million years ago. It used its skillful headbutts to combat Aerodactyl.
column19: deEs lebt seit Urzeiten im Dschungel. Kann im Weg befindliche Bäume mit Kopfstößen aus dem Weg räumen.
column20: enIn rock layers where Cranidos fossils are found, the fossilized trunks of trees snapped in two are also often found.
column21: deEs wurde aus einem ballartigen, eisernen Fossil geschaffen. Es schlägt Beute mit einem Kopfstoß.
column22: deDieses urzeitliche Pokémon war trotz seines harten, robusten Schädels nicht sehr schlau.
column23: enIt was resurrected from an iron ball-like fossil. It downs prey with its headbutts.
column24: deVor Urzeiten lebte es im Dschungel. Mit seinem Kopfstoß setzt es sich gegen Aerodactyl zur Wehr.

column8: enVery little is known about its biology. Can knock down massive trees by smashing its beautiful, pearl-like crown against them.
column9: deDurch häufige Kopfstöße ist sein Schädel hart und dick geworden. Seine Hirnmasse litt darunter und ist kleiner als bei Koknodon.
column10: enIts skull withstands impacts of any magnitude. As a result, its brain never gets the chance to grow.
column11: deSein dicker Schädelknochen trotzt jedem Angriff. Gleichzeitig stoppt er jedoch sein Hirnwachstum.
column12: enIf two were to smash their heads together, their foot-thick skulls would keep them from fainting.
column13: deSein Schädel ist hart wie Eisen. Es reißt Dschungelbäume nieder, während es seine Beute jagt.
column14: deEs ist die Weiterentwicklung eines fossilen Pokémon. Einmal entkam ein Rameidon und zerstörte per Kopfstoß ein ganzes Hochhaus.
column15: enIn ancient times, people would dig up fossils of this Pokémon and use its skull, which is harder than steel, to make helmets.
column16: enThe result of repeated headbutts is a skull grown thick and hard. However, its brain has shrunk in size compared with Cranidos’s.
column17: deVor langer Zeit wurden die Fossilien dieses Pokémon ausgegraben und aus den stahlharten Schädeln Helme hergestellt.
column18: deDieses auf Kopfstöße spezialisierte urzeitliche Pokémon soll einer Theorie zufolge wegen seines kleinen Hirns aus Dummheit ausgestorben sein.
column19: enIts powerful head butt has enough power to shatter even the most durable things upon impact.
column20: enRecords exist of a revived fossil that evolved into Rampardos. It proceeded to escape and then destroy a skyscraper with a headbutt.
column21: enThis ancient Pokémon used headbutts skillfully. Its brain was really small, so some theories suggest that its stupidity led to its extinction.
column22: enIts skull is as hard as iron. It is a brute that tears down jungle trees while catching prey.

column8: deMan findet zwar in uralten Erdschichten Fossilien dieses Pokémon, aber meist handelt es sich dabei nur um Teile des Gesichts.
column9: enA Pokémon that lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its facial hide is extremely hard.
column10: enIt is outstandingly armored. As a result, it can eat grass and berries without having to fight.
column11: deEs wurden viele Fossilien dieses Pokémon gefunden und nur bei sehr wenigen war das Gesicht beschädigt oder fehlte ganz.
column12: enThis Pokémon lived in primeval jungles. Few enemies would have been willing to square off against its heavily armored face, so it’s thought.
column13: enIt was generated from a fossil dug out of a layer of clay that was older than anyone knows. It has a sturdy face.
column14: enIt habitually polishes its face by rubbing it against tree trunks. It is weak to attacks from behind.
column15: enMuch remains unknown about this Pokémon, as few have ever seen it. However, we know that it is calm and dislikes conflict, and it enjoys polishing its face against trees and rocks.
column16: enA mild-mannered, herbivorous Pokémon, it used its face to dig up tree roots to eat. The skin on its face was plenty tough.
column17: deMan nimmt an, dass es dank seines gepanzerten Gesichts kaum Feinde hatte. Es lebte vor Urzeiten im Dschungel.
column18: deEs reibt sein Gesicht an Baumstämmen, um es zu polieren. Es ist leicht, es von hinten anzugreifen.
column19: enAlthough its fossils can be found in layers of primeval rock, nothing but its face has ever been discovered.
column20: deDas Fossil, aus dem es entstand, wurde aus einer 100 Millionen Jahre alten Gesteinsschicht geborgen.
column21: enMany fossils of this Pokémon have been found, but almost none have shown signs of damage to the face.
column22: deEin friedliches, pflanzenfressendes Pokémon. Mit seiner harten Gesichtshaut wühlte es im Boden und fraß Baumwurzeln.

column8: deJeder Frontalangriff wird abgeschmettert. Dieses friedliche Pokémon ernährt sich von Gras und Beeren.
column9: enWhen attacked, they form a wall with their shieldlike faces to protect their young.
column10: enIts face is sturdy—as strong as diamond—and this hardness offers a very stable defense. Much about this species is still unknown, such as its natural habitat.
column11: enIt lived in the same environments as Rampardos. Their fossils have been found together— seemingly from after they’d fought to the finish.
column12: deAlle möglichen Angriffe von vorn kann es abwehren, aber von hinten ist es schutzlos.
column13: deEs lebte in denselben Gebieten wie Rameidon. Manche Fossilienfunde legen nahe, dass sie sich erbitterte Kämpfe geliefert haben.
column14: enWhen attacked, they form a wall. Their rock-hard faces serve to protect them from the attacks.
column15: deDieses Pokémon lebte vor etwa 100 Millionen Jahren. Sein unglaublich robustes Gesicht ist härter als Stahl.
column16: enAny frontal attack is repulsed. It is a docile Pokémon that feeds on grass and berries.
column17: enWhen they lined up side by side, no foe could break through. They shielded their young in that way.
column18: enThis Pokémon is from roughly 100 million years ago. Its terrifyingly tough face is harder than steel.
column19: enThe bones of its face are huge and hard, so they were mistaken for its spine until after this Pokémon was successfully restored.
column20: enWhile it can guard against any sort of attack from the front, it is left without recourse when attacked from behind.
column21: deStellen sie sich Seite an Seite, kann kein Gegner hindurchbrechen. So schützen sie auch ihre Jungen.
column22: deAls man Fossilien noch nicht zurückverwandeln konnte, war man der Ansicht, sein großer, harter Gesichtsknochen sei sein Rücken.

column8: enTo shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.
column9: enEven if it is born where there are no cocooning materials, it somehow always ends up with a cloak.
column10: enIf its cloak is even slightly damaged, Burmy will immediately repair it with whatever is close at hand. The Pokémon within the cloak is scrawny and vulnerable to the cold.
column11: deUm sich vor dem eisigen Winterwind zu schützen, legt es sich unter einen Umhang aus Ästen und Laub.
column12: deWird sein Umhang im Kampf zerstört, macht es sich schnell aus dem, was es findet, einen neuen.
column13: enIt covers itself with a cloak to shelter from the cold. When it’s hot, its cloak is thinner.
column14: enIf its cloak is broken in battle, it quickly remakes the cloak with materials nearby.

column8: deDie Umgebung, in der es sich entwickelt, bestimmt sein Aussehen. Es formt seinen Körper mithilfe der umliegenden Materialien.
column9: enIt is said that a WORMADAM that evolves on a cold day will have a thicker cloak.
column10: enWhen evolving, its body takes in surrounding materials. As a result, there are many body variations.
column11: enIts appearance changes depending on where it evolved. The materials on hand become a part of its body.
column12: enWhen BURMY evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.
column13: enWhen Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.
column14: deAls sich Burmy entwickelte, wurde sein Umhang Teil des Körpers. Es legt den Umhang niemals ab.

column8: enWhile it loves floral honey, it won’t gather any itself. Instead, it plots to steal some from COMBEE.
column9: enIt flutters around at night and steals honey from the COMBEE hive.
column10: enIt does not keep a nest. It flies over fields and mountains in constant search of floral honey.
column11: enScatters steel-colored scales as it flaps its wings. Despite being observed feeding primarily on the nectar of flowers, Mothim is not often seen around flower gardens.
column12: enIt loves the honey of flowers and steals honey collected by Combee.
column13: enWhile it loves floral honey, it won’t gather any itself. Instead, it plots to steal some from Combee.
column14: enIt loves the honey of flowers and steals honey collected by COMBEE.
column15: enIt flutters around at night and steals honey from the Combee hive.
column16: deNachts fliegt es emsig umher und stiehlt, während Wadribie schläft, Honig aus dessen Nest.
column17: deEs liebt Honig und stiehlt den Honig, der von Wadribie gesammelt wurde.

column8: enThey swear fealty to a queen Pokémon and work diligently to gather nectar. Each swarm produces a different flavor of honey.
column9: enIt ceaselessly gathers nectar from sunrise to sundown, all for the sake of Vespiquen and the swarm.
column10: enAt night, COMBEE sleep in a group of about a thousand, packed closely together in a lump.
column11: deEs sammelt Honig und bringt ihn in seine Kolonie. Nachts bilden sie einen Bienenstock und schlafen.
column12: enThe trio is together from birth. It constantly gathers honey from flowers to please VESPIQUEN.
column13: deDieses Trio ist von Geburt an zusammen. Fleißig bringt es Blütenhonig zu Honweisel.
column14: deDieses Trio ist immer zusammen, aber jedes von ihnen hat leicht andere Vorlieben, was Blütenhonig angeht.
column15: enIt collects and delivers honey to its colony. At night, they cluster to form a beehive and sleep.
column16: enA Pokémon formed by three others. It busily carries sweet floral honey to VESPIQUEN.
column17: enThis Pokémon is a set of three. When they sleep, they gather up and form a giant hive of 100 Combee.
column18: enThe members of the trio spend all their time together. Each one has a slightly different taste in nectar.
column19: enThe trio is together from birth. It constantly gathers honey from flowers to please Vespiquen.
column20: deSie sammeln von der Morgendämmerung bis zum Sonnenuntergang unentwegt Blütenhonig für Honweisel und die Kolonie.

column8: enIts abdomen is a honeycomb for grubs. It raises its grubs on honey collected by Combee.
column9: deSein Bauch ist die Wabe für die Larven. Es füttert seine Larven mit dem Honig, den Wadribie sammelt.
column10: enIt houses its colony in cells in its body and releases various pheromones to make those grubs do its bidding.
column11: enWhen endangered, grubs from its six-cell honeycomb strike back. There is only one in a colony.
column12: enIts abdomen is a honeycomb for grubs. It raises its grubs on honey collected by COMBEE.
column13: deEs hat die Kontrolle über seine Nachkommen. Diese würden im Kampf ihr Leben aufs Spiel setzen, um es vor Feinden zu schützen.
column14: enIt releases various pheromones to make the grubs in its body do its bidding while fighting foes.
column15: enIt raises grubs in the holes in its body. It secretes pheromones to control COMBEE.
column16: deJe mehr Pheromone ein Honweisel freisetzt, desto mehr Wadribie kann es kontrollieren.
column17: enCommands its subjects to build its hive. It will dispatch any interlopers who dare sneak into its nest and use them as nourishment for itself.
column18: deEs beherbergt Jung-Pokémon in seinem Rumpf, die es mithilfe von verschiedenen Pheromonen frei herumkommandieren kann.
column19: enIt skillfully commands its grubs in battles with its enemies. The grubs are willing to risk their lives to defend Vespiquen.
column20: enVespiquen that give off more pheromones have larger swarms of Combee attendants.

column8: enIt makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.
column9: enIt makes electricity with pouches in its cheeks and shoots charges from its tail. It lives atop trees.
column10: enA species related to the Pikachu line. Though Pachirisu is a calm Pokémon, it still presents a danger should one touch its electrified tail or cheeks.
column11: deUm gespeicherte Elektrizität zu teilen, reiben zwei von ihnen ihre Backentaschen aneinander.
column12: enA pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.
column13: enIt’s one of the kinds of Pokémon  with electric cheek pouches. It shoots charges from its tail.
column14: deEs bildet ein Fellknäuel, der vor statischer Energie knistert. Es speichert die Energie in Bäumen.

column8: deEs füllt den Schwimmreifen um seinen Hals mit Luft, um den Kopf über dem Wasser zu halten und die Umgebung zu überblicken.
column9: enIt inflates the flotation sac around its neck and pokes its head out of the water to see what is going on.
column10: enIt swims by rotating its two tails like a screw. When it dives, its flotation sac collapses.
column11: enIt spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.
column12: enIt moves freely in the water by spinning its forked tail for propulsion. The resemblance to the screw of a steamboat is coincidental.
column13: enIt inflates its flotation sac, keeping its face above water in order to watch for prey movement.
column14: enIt has a flotation sac that is like an inflatable collar. It floats on water with its head out.
column15: deEs schwimmt, indem es seine beiden Schweife wie eine Schiffsschraube rotieren lässt.

column8: enWith its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.
column9: enHas a long, rather splendid flotation sac, which prevents Floatzel from drowning even in stormy seas. One might glimpse this species around fishing hamlets from time to time.
column10: enIts flotation sac developed as a result of pursuing aquatic prey. It can double as a rubber raft.
column11: enIt is a common sight around fishing ports. It is known to rescue people and carry off prey.
column12: enIt floats using its well-developed flotation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.
column13: deEs treibt mithilfe einer Art Rettungsring auf dem Wasser und hilft dem, der zu ertrinken droht.
column14: deDa es seit jeher Beute im Wasser jagt, entwickelte es einen Rettungsring.

column8: enOnce the fruit growing alongside the main body is large and plump, Cherubi will use the nutrients within to evolve. The fruit then detaches, becoming nourishment for other creatures.
column9: deSonnenlicht färbt es rot. Verliert das kleine Bällchen Nährstoffe, welkt es und die Entwicklung beginnt.
column10: enThe deeper a Cherubi’s red, the more nutrients it has stockpiled in its body. And the sweeter and tastier its small ball!
column11: enThe small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. STARLY try to peck it off.
column12: deJe stärker die Rotfärbung seines Körpers ist, desto mehr Nährstoffe stecken in ihm und umso süßer und besser schmeckt sein Bällchen.
column13: deSein nährstoffreiches Bällchen erfreut sich bei Vogel-Pokémon großer Beliebtheit. Es ist ständig auf der Flucht, damit es nicht gefressen wird.
column14: enThe small ball holds the nutrients needed for evolution. Apparently, it is very sweet and tasty.
column15: enSunlight colors it red. When the small ball is drained of nutrients, it shrivels to herald evolution.
column16: enIt evolves by sucking the energy out of the small ball where it had been storing nutrients.
column17: enIt nimbly dashes about to avoid getting pecked by bird Pokémon that would love to make off with its small, nutrient-rich storage ball.
column18: deEs saugt Nährstoffe, die in seinem Bällchen enthalten sind, und nutzt die Energie für seine Entwicklung.
column19: enThe small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.

column8: enIt blooms during times of strong sunlight. It tries to make up for everything it endured as a bud.
column9: deAls Knospe verhält es sich ruhig und bewegt sich kaum, während es geduldig auf Sonnenschein wartet.
column10: enIf it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun’s rays with its whole body.
column11: enIt is docile as a bud, but turns cheerful when it blooms. It folds back into a bud if sunlight wanes.
column12: enDuring times of strong sunlight, its bud blooms, its petals open fully, and it becomes very active.
column13: enIts folded petals are pretty tough. Bird Pokémon can peck at them all they want, and Cherrim won’t be bothered at all.
column14: enAs a bud, it barely moves. It sits still, placidly waiting for sunlight to appear.
column15: deBei Sonnenschein entfaltet seine Knospe ihre Blütenblätter und es springt lebhaft umher.
column16: enMotionless, save for the occasional quiver. A rich array of Pokémon can be found gathered around it, drawn by the scent exuded from Cherrim's folded petals.
column17: deSpürt es Sonnenlicht, öffnet es seine Blütenblätter und nimmt die Energie der Sonnenstrahlen auf.
column18: deDie Blütenblätter, die es umschließen, sind hart. Deswegen macht es ihm absolut nichts aus, wenn Vogel-Pokémon es mit ihren Schnäbeln angreifen.

column8: deWenn man es fest drückt, sondert es eine seltsame lilafarbene Flüssigkeit ab. Diese ist zwar nicht schädlich, aber sehr schleimig.
column9: enSubjecting this Pokémon to a strong force causes it to secrete a strange purple fluid. Though harmless, the fluid is awfully sticky.
column10: enFound in abundance on seashores bordering warm waters. Shellos are unexpectedly friendly and will crawl toward any person they see. Take care not to get coated in mucus!
column11: deEs lebt zwar für gewöhnlich im Meer, kann sich aber auch an Land bewegen, bis seine Haut austrocknet.
column12: deEs lebt hauptsächlich in Küstengebieten. Seine Form und Farbe ändern sich je nach Lebensraum und Futter.
column13: enPurple mucus sticks to the hands of anyone who touches it. Take care, as the substance is troublesome to wash off.
column14: enWhen it senses danger, a purple liquid oozes out of it. The liquid is thought to be something like greasy sweat.
column15: enIts colors and shapes differ from region to region. In the Sinnoh region, two types are confirmed.
column16: deNicht seinen Körper zusammendrücken, sonst sickert eine seltsame lilafarbene Flüssigkeit aus!
column17: enWhile they normally live in the sea, they can function on land, too, until their skin dries out.
column18: deFasst man es an, schmiert man sich mit lilafarbenem Schleim voll, der nicht abgeht, egal, wie oft man sich auch die Hände wäscht.
column19: deSein Aussehen hängt von seiner Umgebung ab. Man geht davon aus, dass es diese Form in warmen Meeren annimmt.
column20: enIt lives along bodies of water. Its body shape has changed to suit its habitat.
column21: deSpürt es Gefahr, stößt es eine lilafarbene Flüssigkeit aus. Man geht davon aus, dass es sich dabei um eine Art Angstschweiß handelt.
column22: enFor the most part, it makes its home along the seashore. Its color and form differ according to its habitat and the quality of its food.
column23: enBeware of pushing strongly on its squishy body, as it makes a mysterious purple fluid ooze out.
column24: enIts shape and coloration vary, depending on its habitat.
column25: deForm und Farbe dieses Pokémon ändern sich je nach der Umgebung, in der es lebt.
column26: enThis Pokémon’s habitat shapes its physique. According to some theories, life in warm ocean waters causes this variation to develop.

column8: enLong ago, its entire back was shielded with a sturdy shell. There are traces of it left in its cells.
column9: deVor langer Zeit hatte es einen Panzer. In seinem Rücken ist davon noch eine harte, dünne Platte übrig.
column10: enPlankton, invisible to the naked eye, is its main food source. It comes onto the land periodically, but the reason for this is not known.
column11: deIn Urzeiten muss es einen großen Schutzpanzer besessen haben. Es lebt in kleinen Wasserstellen.
column12: enEats beach sand for nourishment. Should one Gastrodon encounter another of a different color, a fierce battle will inevitably ensue.
column13: enIt apparently had a huge shell for protection in ancient times. It lives in shallow tidal pools.
column14: enIts search for food sometimes leads it onto land, where it leaves behind a sticky trail of slime as it passes through.
column15: enThe softness of its body helps disperse the force of impacts, so although its body is uncommonly squishy, it’s also surprisingly resilient.
column16: enTheir shape and color change, depending on their environment and diet. There are many of them at beaches where the waves are calm.
column17: deSeine Farbe und Form ändern sich je nachdem, wo es lebt und was es frisst. Man trifft viele von ihnen an Küsten mit mäßiger Brandung an.
column18: enWhen its natural enemy attacks, it oozes purple fluid and escapes.
column19: enIt has a pliable body without any bones. If any part of its body is torn off, it grows right back.
column20: deWird es von einem natürlichen Feind angegriffen, flieht es, indem es ein lila Sekret über die Haut ausstößt.
column21: deEs hat starke Regenerationskräfte. Selbst wenn Fisch-Pokémon ein Stück von ihm abbeißen, wächst es innerhalb weniger Stunden nach.
column22: enIt has strong regenerative capabilities. Even if parts of it are bitten off by fish Pokémon, it will return to normal within a few hours.
column23: deEs ist weich, kann aber einiges einstecken, da sein biegsamer Körper die Wucht von Schlägen absorbiert.
column24: deEs ernährt sich hauptsächlich von Plankton, das man mit bloßem Auge nicht sehen kann. Es ist noch unklar, warum es an Land kommt.
column25: deAuf der Suche nach Futter kommt es bisweilen auch an Land. Wo es entlangkriecht, hinterlässt es eine klebrige Schleimspur.
column26: enLong ago, it had a shell on its back. There’s now a vestigial plate on its back that’s hard but thin.

column8: enLong ago, its entire back was shielded with a sturdy shell. There are traces of it left in its cells.
column9: deVor langer Zeit hatte es einen Panzer. In seinem Rücken ist davon noch eine harte, dünne Platte übrig.

column21: deEs hat starke Regenerationskräfte. Selbst wenn Fisch-Pokémon ein Stück von ihm abbeißen, wächst es innerhalb weniger Stunden nach.
