column8: deEs bewegt sich mit geöffnetem Maul und verschluckt seine Beute mit jeder Menge Sand, den es durch seine Nasenlöcher wieder ausstößt.
column9: deEs ist hauptsächlich tagsüber aktiv. Die Nächte in der Wüste sind kalt, deswegen vergräbt es sich zum Schlafen tief im Sand.
column10: enIt enshrouds itself with sand to protect itself from germs. It does not enjoy getting wet.
column11: enIt shrouds itself in sand to ward off germs. It travels easily through the sands of the desert.
column12: enThough large and languid, Hippopotas is difficult to detect due to its tendency to burrow into and lurk beneath the soil. When agitated or excited, it expels sand from its nostrils.
column13: enThis Pokémon is active during the day and passes the cold desert nights burrowed snugly into the sand.
column14: enIt shuts its nostrils tight then travels through sand as if walking. They form colonies of around ten.
column15: deEs lebt in ausgetrockneten Gebieten. Statt zu schwitzen, sondert sein Körper Sand ab.
column16: enIt moves through the sands with its mouth open, swallowing sand along with its prey. It gets rid of the sand by spouting it from its nose.
column17: enIt shuts its nostrils tight then travels through sand as if walking. They form colonies of around 10.
column18: deEs bedeckt sich mit Sand, um sich vor Keimen zu schützen. Es mag es gar nicht, wenn es nass wird.
column19: enIt lives in arid places. Instead of perspiration, it expels grainy sand from its body.

column8: deEs wirbelt im Körper gespeicherten Sand durch Öffnungen und generiert so einen Sandwirbelsturm.
column9: enShort-tempered and easily moved to violence. It whips up whirlwinds of sand to crush its foes' spirits, then goes in for the attack.
column10: deManchmal bleiben in den Öffnungen in seinem Körper Steine stecken. Lithomith entfernen diese Steine, deswegen genießen sie seinen Schutz.
column11: deDas weit aufgerissene Maul demonstriert seine Stärke. Es attackiert mit gebündeltem Sand.
column12: enStones can get stuck in the ports on their bodies. Dwebble help dislodge such stones, so Hippowdon look after these Pokémon.
column13: enIt brandishes its gaping mouth in a display of fearsome strength. It raises vast quantities of sand while attacking.
column14: deWenn man es wütend macht, wird es rabiat. Es stößt aufgenommenen Sand aus und verursacht damit einen Sandsturm.
column15: enWhen roused to violence by its rage, it spews out the quantities of sand it has swallowed and whips up a sandstorm.
column16: enIt is surprisingly quick to anger. It holds its mouth agape as a display of its strength.
column17: enIt blasts internally stored sand from ports on its body to create a towering twister for attack.
column18: enIts huge mouth is almost seven feet across. It has enough power to completely crush a car.

column8: enAfter burrowing into the sand, it waits patiently for prey to come near. This Pokémon and Sizzlipede share common descent.
column9: deEs greift seine Beute mit den Krallen an seinem Schweif und vergiftet sie. Dann wartet es ab...
column10: enIt grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.
column11: enIt burrows under the sand to lie in wait for prey. Its tail claws can inject its prey with a savage poison.
column12: enIts claws are not only razor-sharp but poisonous, making Skorupi a highly dangerous Pokémon. It seems to be weakened by cold temperatures, however.
column13: enAs soon as the tail claws close, its needle tips secrete poison. It can survive a year without food.
column14: enIt lives in arid regions and can go without food for a year while waiting for prey.
column15: enIt lives in arid lands. It buries itself in sand and lies in wait for unsuspecting prey.
column16: deDieses Pokémon vergräbt sich im Sand und lauert dort regungslos seiner Beute auf. Es ist artverwandt mit Thermopod.
column17: deEs wartet im Sand versteckt auf Beute, die es sich mit seinem giftigen Schweif krallt.
column18: deEs schlägt mit den giftigen Krallen an seinem Schweif zu. Wer von ihnen gestochen wird, bekommt ein lähmendes Gift injiziert.
column19: enIt attacks using the claws on its tail. Once locked in its grip, its prey is unable to move as this Pokémon’s poison seeps in.

column8: enIts body is encased in a sturdy shell. Its head rotates 180 degrees, eliminating blind spots.
column9: enIts poison is potent, but it rarely sees use. This Pokémon prefers to use physical force instead, going on rampages with its car-crushing strength.
column10: enIt’s so vicious that it’s called the Sand Demon. Yet when confronted by Hippowdon, Drapion keeps a low profile and will never pick a fight.
column11: deSein Gift ist stark, aber es setzt es nur selten ein. Ist es richtig wütend, schlägt es mit einer Wucht zu, die selbst Autos zu Schrott verarbeitet.
column12: enHas a brutish, ferocious temperament. With immense strength and a sturdy shell off which swords will bounce, it rampages about and wreaks havoc.
column13: enIt has the power in its clawed arms to make scrap of a car. The tips of its claws release poison.
column14: enIt attacks people and Pokémon that cross the desert. This has only furthered its bad reputation.
column15: enIt takes pride in its strength. Even though it can tear foes apart, it finishes them off with powerful poison.
column16: enPossessing a sturdy build, it takes pride in its strength, taking down foes without using toxins.
column17: deEs bringt seine Gegner mit starkem Gift zur Strecke, obwohl es weiß, dass es stark genug ist, um sie zu zerfetzen.
column18: deEs wird auch „Wüstendämon“ genannt, weil es so grausam ist. Nur mit Hippoterus legt es sich nie an, sondern verhält sich ruhig.
column19: deIn seinen Armen steckt so viel Kraft, dass es mit seinen giftigen Krallen Autos zerquetschen kann.

column8: enSwaying and dodging the attacks of its foes, it weaves its flexible body in close, then lunges out with its poisonous claws.
column9: enIt booms out a victory croak when its prey goes down in defeat. This Pokémon and Seismitoad are related species.
column10: deDie Gelenke an seinen Klauen geben ein so starkes Gift ab, dass selbst ein kleiner Kratzer fatal ist.
column11: enIts knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.
column12: deEs nähert sich springend seinem Gegner und bohrt seine giftigen Klauen in ihn hinein. Schon ein kleiner Kratzer lässt den Gegner K.O. gehen.
column13: deVerfügt über einen Giftsack an seiner Kehle. Quakt es, schäumt das Gift und wird so noch stärker.
column14: enIt has a poison sac at its throat. When it croaks, the stored poison is churned for greater potency.
column15: enIt bounces toward opponents and gouges them with poisonous claws. No more than a scratch is needed to knock out its adversaries.
column16: enThe croaking that Toxicroak produces before a battle is for churning the poison it has stored in its poison sac.
column17: enIts crimson claws contain a virulent toxin. This toxin can be made into a tonic by diluting it, mixing it with several types of wild grass, and boiling it down over two days.
column18: deWenn es seine Beute erlegt, quakt es seinen Triumph in die Welt hinaus. Es ist artverwandt mit Branawarz.
column19: enThe toxin made in its poison sacs is pumped to the knuckle claws through tubes down its arms.

column8: enSwaying and dodging the attacks of its foes, it weaves its flexible body in close, then lunges out with its poisonous claws.
column9: enIt booms out a victory croak when its prey goes down in defeat. This Pokémon and Seismitoad are related species.
column10: deDie Gelenke an seinen Klauen geben ein so starkes Gift ab, dass selbst ein kleiner Kratzer fatal ist.
column11: enIts knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.
column12: deEs nähert sich springend seinem Gegner und bohrt seine giftigen Klauen in ihn hinein. Schon ein kleiner Kratzer lässt den Gegner K.O. gehen.
column13: deVerfügt über einen Giftsack an seiner Kehle. Quakt es, schäumt das Gift und wird so noch stärker.
column14: enIt has a poison sac at its throat. When it croaks, the stored poison is churned for greater potency.
column15: enIt bounces toward opponents and gouges them with poisonous claws. No more than a scratch is needed to knock out its adversaries.
column16: enThe croaking that Toxicroak produces before a battle is for churning the poison it has stored in its poison sac.
column17: enIts crimson claws contain a virulent toxin. This toxin can be made into a tonic by diluting it, mixing it with several types of wild grass, and boiling it down over two days.
column18: deWenn es seine Beute erlegt, quakt es seinen Triumph in die Welt hinaus. Es ist artverwandt mit Branawarz.
column19: enThe toxin made in its poison sacs is pumped to the knuckle claws through tubes down its arms.

column8: enIt attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.
column9: enThough this is a plant Pokémon, it has a gluttonous and unruly temperament. Carnivine attacks its prey with its cavernous maw wide open.
column10: enIt walks around on its tentacles in search of a tree branch where it can dangle down and ambush prey.
column11: enIt binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.
column12: deKlammert sich an Bäume in Sümpfen. Lockt Beute mit seinem süßlichen Speichel an und schluckt sie dann.
column13: enHanging from branches using its tentacles, it looks like a plant. It awaits prey, mouth wide open.
column14: deSein süßlich riechender Speichel zieht Beute an, die es frisst. Es braucht einen Tag, sie zu fressen.
column15: enUsing its tentacles to lash itself to trees, it lies in wait for prey, luring it close with sweet-smelling drool.

column8: deSeine leuchtenden Flossen locken Beute an. Bei Tag bleibt es nahe an der Wasseroberfläche, während es abends in den Tiefen verschwindet.
column9: deWurden die Flossen lange dem Sonnenlicht ausgesetzt, leuchten sie in der Dunkelheit.
column10: enIts double tail fins propel its energetic jumps. When it breaks the surface of the sea, Wingull swoop down to grab it on the fly.
column11: deDer rosafarbene Teil seiner Haut speichert Sonnenlicht und leuchtet. Es wird angenommen, dass dies der Tarnung dient.
column12: enAfter long exposure to sunlight, the patterns on its tail fins shine vividly when darkness arrives.
column13: enWhat a gorgeous sight this Pokémon is as it swims with its long, pink-painted caudal fins fluttering behind it. Finneon's beautiful appearance has led to its nickname: ”finery fish.”
column14: enThe line running down its side can store sunlight. It shines vividly at night.
column15: deSeine rosafarbene Musterung leuchtet in der Nacht. Wegen seiner Popularität unter Tauchern ist es ein gern gesehener Gast in Feriengebieten.
column16: deMit ihren beiden Flossen können sie hoch in die Luft springen. Über der Meeresoberfläche lauern jedoch Wingull.
column17: enSwimming and fluttering its two tail fins, it looks like a BEAUTIFLY. At night, the patterns on its tail fins softly shine.
column18: enIt lures in prey with its shining tail fins. It stays near the surface during the day and moves to the depths when night falls.
column19: enWhen night falls, their pink patterns begin to shine. They’re popular with divers, so there are resorts that feed them to keep them close.
column20: enThe way its two tail fins flutter while it swims has earned it the nickname “BEAUTIFLY of the Sea.”
column21: deDie Linie an seiner Seite kann Sonnenlicht speichern. Nachts leuchtet es sehr intensiv.
column22: enIt absorbs sunlight with the pink areas of its skin, which then shine. This appears to be a form of camouflage.

column8: enWith its shining light, it lures its prey close. However, the light also happens to attract ferocious fish Pokémon—its natural predators.
column9: deDieses Tiefsee-Pokémon lebt am Meeresboden, über den es mit seinen Flossen krabbelt, um Starmie, seine Leibspeise, aufzuspüren.
column10: deAuf dem tiefen Meeresboden gibt es nur wenig Beute, weshalb es sich mit Lanturn heftige Kämpfe um das wertvolle Futter liefert.
column11: enThis deep-sea Pokémon lives at the bottom of the sea. Its fins haul it over the seabed in search of its favorite food—Starmie.
column12: deEs lebt tief auf dem Meeresboden. Das blinkende Muster auf seinen vier Rückenflossen zieht Beute an.
column13: enIt crawls along the seafloor using its long front fins like legs. It competes for food with Lanturn.
column14: deUm nicht von Fressfeinden entdeckt zu werden, krabbelt es auf zwei Brustflossen am Meeresboden.
column15: enLUMINEON swimming in the darkness of the deep sea look like stars shining in the night sky.
column16: enIt crawls along the seafloor using its long front fins like legs. It competes for food with LANTURN.
column17: enThey traverse the deep waters as if crawling over the seafloor. The fantastic lights of its fins shine like stars in the night sky.
column18: enTo avoid detection by predators, it crawls along the seafloor using the two fins on its chest.
column19: enDeep down at the bottom of the ocean, prey is scarce. Lumineon get into fierce disputes with Lanturn over food.
column20: enIt lives on the deep-sea floor. It attracts prey by flashing the patterns on its four tail fins.
column21: enUses its gleaming fins to hunt its prey. The view of Lumineon schooling near the surface of the sea at night is breathtaking— it's as though there were shining stars right there.
column22: deEs bewegt sich krabbelnd über den Boden der Tiefsee. Seine prachtvollen Flossen leuchten wie der Sternenhimmel.
column23: deMit seinem blinkenden Muster lockt es Beute an. Leider zieht es hierdurch auch seine Fressfeinde an.

column8: enWhen it swims close to the surface of the ocean, people aboard ships are able to observe the pattern on its back.
column9: enMantyke living in Galar seem to be somewhat sluggish. The colder waters of the seas in this region may be the cause.
column10: deIhre Rücken sind je nach Region unterschiedlich gemustert. Oft mischen sie sich unter Remoraid-Schwärme.
column11: deEs mischt sich unter Remoraid-Schwärme und schwimmt mit ihnen. Werden diese angegriffen, kämpft es gemeinsam an ihrer Seite.
column12: enIt’s highly friendly and easily tamed. Tours that take people swimming with Mantyke are a super-popular beach activity.
column13: enA friendly Pokémon that captures the subtle flows of seawater using its two antennae.
column14: enThough ball-like in shape, this Pokémon is a proficient swimmer. I have discovered that if a Mantyke spends much time with schools of Remoraid, it will eventually achieve evolution.
column15: enPeople organize tours to see this Pokémon frolic and skim the tops of waves with Remoraid.
column16: deVielleicht liegt es an der niedrigen Temperatur des Meeres, aber die Mantirps der Galar-Region bewegen sich etwas schwerfälliger als andere.
column17: enIt swims along with a school of Remoraid, and they’ll all fight together to repel attackers.
column18: enPeople organize tours to see this Pokémon frolic and skim the tops of waves with REMORAID.
column19: enMantyke are friendly toward people and will approach boats closely. The patterns on their backs differ depending on their habitat.
column20: deEs ist so zutraulich, dass es sich sogar Schiffen voller Menschen nähert. Die Rückenmuster von Mantirps sind in jeder Region unterschiedlich.
column21: enThe pattern on its back varies by region. It often swims in a school of Remoraid.
column22: deDa es nah an der Meeresoberfläche schwimmt, kann man sein Rückenmuster von Schiffen aus erspähen.
column23: deEin sehr zahmes, zutrauliches Pokémon. Touren, bei denen man Mantirps begegnen kann, sind eine beliebte Freizeitaktivität an Küstenorten.
column24: enScientists discovered that the distinctive patterns on its back differ by region.

column8: deEs lebt auf verschneiten Bergen und steckt seine Füße in den Schnee, um über sie Wasser und Kälte zu absorbieren.
column9: deIm Frühjahr wachsen Beeren, die wie gefrorene Süßigkeiten aussehen, um seinen Bauch herum.
column10: enIn the spring, it grows berries with the texture of frozen treats around its belly.
column11: enDuring cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain’s lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.
column12: deIm Winter steigt es bis zum Fuß der Berge herab. Im Frühjahr kehrt es auf den Gipfel zurück.
column13: enThe berries that grow around its belly are like ice pops. Galarian Darumaka absolutely love these berries.
column14: deDie an Speiseeis erinnernden Beeren, die an seinem Bauch wachsen, sind bei den Flampion Galars ein sehr beliebter Snack.
column15: enIt lives on snowy mountains. It sinks its legs into the snow to absorb water and keep its own temperature down.
column16: enOne is likely to encounter this Pokémon while out in the snow. There are stories of Snover appearing in human settlements but doing no harm—rather, they bond with the children.
column17: enSeemingly curious about people, they gather around footsteps they find on snowy mountains.
column18: enIt lives on snowy mountains. Having had little contact with humans, it is boldly inquisitive.

column8: enIt whips up blizzards in mountains that are always buried in snow. It is the abominable snowman.
column9: enIf it sees any packs of Darumaka going after Snover, it chases them off, swinging its sizable arms like hammers.
column10: enIt blankets wide areas in snow by whipping up blizzards. It is also known as “The Ice Monster.”
column11: deEs haust im Gebirge, wo ewiger Schnee liegt, und löst Blizzards aus, um sich zu verstecken.
column12: enIt lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.
column13: deDieses Pokémon löst Blizzards aus. Wenn es seinen großen Körper schüttelt, wird in seiner Umgebung alles sofort schneeweiß.
column14: enA powerful Pokémon that can split huge boulders with ease. Dislikes associating with others and chooses to live quietly deep within the mountains, playing with the snow.
column15: enThis Pokémon is known to bring blizzards. A shake of its massive body is enough to cause whiteout conditions.
column16: deEs schwingt seine großen Arme wie Hämmer und vertreibt damit Gruppen von Flampion, die es auf Shnebedeck abgesehen haben.
column17: deEs bedeckt weite Gebiete mit Schnee, indem es Blizzards auslöst. Man nennt es „Das Eismonster“.
column18: enThey appear when the snow flowers bloom. When the petals fall, they retreat to places unknown again.

column8: enIt whips up blizzards in mountains that are always buried in snow. It is the abominable snowman.
column9: enIf it sees any packs of Darumaka going after Snover, it chases them off, swinging its sizable arms like hammers.
column10: enIt blankets wide areas in snow by whipping up blizzards. It is also known as “The Ice Monster.”
column11: deEs haust im Gebirge, wo ewiger Schnee liegt, und löst Blizzards aus, um sich zu verstecken.
column12: enIt lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.
column13: deDieses Pokémon löst Blizzards aus. Wenn es seinen großen Körper schüttelt, wird in seiner Umgebung alles sofort schneeweiß.
column14: enA powerful Pokémon that can split huge boulders with ease. Dislikes associating with others and chooses to live quietly deep within the mountains, playing with the snow.
column15: enThis Pokémon is known to bring blizzards. A shake of its massive body is enough to cause whiteout conditions.
column16: deEs schwingt seine großen Arme wie Hämmer und vertreibt damit Gruppen von Flampion, die es auf Shnebedeck abgesehen haben.
column17: deEs bedeckt weite Gebiete mit Schnee, indem es Blizzards auslöst. Man nennt es „Das Eismonster“.
column18: enThey appear when the snow flowers bloom. When the petals fall, they retreat to places unknown again.

column8: enThey attack their quarry in packs. Prey as large as Mamoswine easily fall to the teamwork of a group of Weavile.
column9: deSie jagen ihre Beute in Gruppen. Im Team erledigen sie selbst große Ziele wie Mamutel mit Leichtigkeit.
column10: enThey travel in groups of four or five, leaving signs for one another on trees and rocks. They bring down their prey with coordinated attacks.
column11: enThanks to its increased intelligence, scrapping over food is a thing of the past. A scratch from its claws will give you a case of frostbite!
column12: enIt lives in snowy regions. It carves patterns in trees with its claws as a signal to others.
column13: enWith its claws, it leaves behind signs for its friends to find. The number of distinct signs is said to be over 500.
column14: deSie kommunizieren miteinander, indem sie Zeichen mit ihren Krallen hinterlassen. Es soll über 500 davon geben.
column15: deSie leben in kalten Gebieten und ernähren sich in Alola hauptsächlich von Vulpix und Sandan. Ihre Beute teilen sie gerecht untereinander auf.
column16: deEs hat gelernt, sich mit Artgenossen nicht mehr um seine Beute zu streiten. Kratzer durch seine Klauen hinterlassen Frostbeulen.
column17: deEs lebt in kalten Gebieten in Gruppen von vier oder fünf Pokémon, die bei der Jagd großes Geschick zeigen.
column18: enThey communicate by clawing signs in boulders and work together to surround enemies.
column19: enThey dwell in cold places. This Pokémon’s main food source in Alola is Vulpix and Sandshrew, which they carefully divide among their group.
column20: enOne Weavile will trip a Sandshrew and flip it over, and then another Weavile will deal the finishing blow with its sharp claws.
column21: enThey live in cold regions, forming groups of four or five that hunt prey with impressive coordination.
column22: deSie sind immer zu viert oder fünft unterwegs. Bei der Jagd arbeiten sie zusammen, indem sie Zeichen in Felsen und Bäume ritzen.
column23: deZieht es einem Sandan die Beine weg, gibt ein weiteres Snibunna der nun rücklings liegenden Beute mit seinen Krallen den Gnadenstoß.
column24: enThis species corners prey as a pack, under the guidance of a leader. Weavile displays increased cunning, leading me to speculate that its evolution caused further brain development.
column25: enIt sends signals to others by carving odd patterns in frost-covered trees and ice.
column26: deEs lebt in schneereichen Gebieten. Snibunna senden einander Signale, indem sie Zeichen in Rinde ritzen.
column27: enEvolution made it even more devious. It communicates by clawing signs in boulders.

column8: deMan vermutet, es habe sich entwickelt, nachdem es einem besonderen Magnetfeld ausgesetzt war, das seine Molekularstruktur veränderte.
column9: enThree units generate magnetism. There have been many mistaken reports of UFO sightings when Magnezone flies through the night sky.
column10: deEs überwacht sein Territorium mithilfe seines Radars. Entdeckt es Eindringlinge, greift es sie unvermittelt mit einem Hyperstrahl an.
column11: deEs heißt, dass es beim Herumfliegen mysteriöse Funkwellen aussende und unbekannte empfange.
column12: enAs it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin, while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.
column13: deGelegentlich erzeugen sie so starke Magnetfelder, dass sie sich gegenseitig anziehen und einander immobilisieren.
column14: deMan sagt, es werde von einem Wesen aus dem All kontrolliert und mit der Antenne auf seinem Kopf empfange es von ihm Signale.
column15: deSeine drei Bestandteile erzeugen ein Magnetfeld. Es kommt oft vor, dass am Himmel fliegende Magnezone für Ufos gehalten werden.
column16: enSome say that Magnezone receives signals from space via the antenna on its head and that it’s being controlled by some mysterious being.
column17: enIt evolved from exposure to a special magnetic field. Three units generate magnetism.
column18: enA group tried to use scientific means to make MAGNEZONE evolve, but their efforts ended in failure.
column19: enA group tried to use scientific means to make Magnezone evolve, but their efforts ended in failure.
column20: enIt uses radar to monitor its territory. Intruders are quickly disposed of with a hyper beam.
column21: enIt’s thought that a special magnetic field changed the molecular structure of this Pokémon’s body, and that’s what caused the Pokémon’s evolution.
column22: enSometimes the magnetism emitted by Magnezone is too strong, making them attract each other so they cannot move.
column23: deManche Menschen glauben immer noch, dass Magnezone von einem anderen Planeten stamme. Es geht ein starkes Magnetfeld von ihm aus.
column24: enExposure to a special magnetic field changed MAGNETON’s molecular structure, turning it into MAGNEZONE.
column25: enThere are still people who believe that this Pokémon came from outer space. It emanates a powerful magnetic field.
column26: deEs entwickelte sich, als es einem besonderen Magnetfeld ausgesetzt wurde.
column27: enI theorize that a special magnetic field influenced this Pokémon, changing its molecular structure and causing it to evolve. It emits strange radio waves toward space from its antenna.

column8: deEs umklammert Dinge mit seiner dehnbaren Zunge. Kommt man ihm zu nahe, wird man eingespeichelt.
column9: enTheir saliva contains lots of components that can dissolve anything. The numbness caused by their lick does not dissipate.
column10: enLickilicky’s strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body. No one has figured out how Lickilicky’s tongue can stretch so far.
column11: deMit seiner Zunge kann es alles tun und ist damit so geschickt wie Menschen mit ihren Händen. Diese verwendet Schlurplek nur sehr unbeholfen.
column12: enLickilicky can do just about anything with its tongue, which is as dexterous as the human hand. In contrast, Lickilicky’s use of its fingers is clumsy.
column13: enIt uses its tongue much more skillfully than its hands or its feet. It can deftly pick up a single small bean with its tongue.
column14: enA contest is under way to determine which one can stick its tongue out the farthest. The current record is...more than 82 feet.
column15: enIts tongue can extend and contract freely, and it is capable of reaching lengths over 10 times Lickilicky's height. Beware of the saliva, as it contains corrosive elements.
column16: enIt has space in its throat to store saliva. It can also roll up its tongue and store it in the same spot.
column17: deSeine sonderbare Zunge kann sich bis auf ein Vielfaches seiner Körpergröße ausdehnen. Niemand weiß, wie das möglich ist.
column18: deIhr Speichel enthält eine ätzende Substanz, die bei Kontakt mit der Haut anhaltende Taubheit erzeugt.
column19: enIts saliva can decompose anything. It wraps its long tongue around things to coat them with its sticky saliva.
column20: enIt wraps things with its extensible tongue. Getting too close to it will leave you soaked with drool.
column21: deEs gibt Wettbewerbe dazu, welches Schlurplek seine Zunge am weitesten herausstrecken kann. Der Rekord liegt bei 25 m.
column22: deEs kann mit seiner Zunge geschickter umgehen als mit seinen Armen und Beinen. Selbst winzige Böhnchen hebt es mit ihrer Hilfe problemlos auf.
column23: enThe long tongue is always soggy with slobber. The saliva contains a solvent that causes numbness.

column8: deEs legt Steine in die Löcher seiner Hände und wirft sie mit Muskelkraft. Sogar Georok werden verwendet.
column9: enFrom holes in its palms, it fires out GEODUDE. Its carapace  can withstand volcanic eruptions.
column10: enIt can launch a rock held in its hand like a missile by tightening then expanding muscles instantly.
column11: enIt can load up to three projectiles per arm into the holes in its hands. What launches out of those holes could be either rocks or Roggenrola.
column12: enIt puts rocks in holes in its palms and uses its muscles to shoot them. GEODUDE are shot at rare times.
column13: deEs feuert Kleinstein aus seinen Handflächen. Durch seinen Schützer erträgt es sogar Vulkanausbrüche.
column14: deMit seinem Schützer wehrt es Angriffe ab und durchsticht dann den verdutzten Gegner mit seinem Bohrer, auf den es sehr stolz ist.
column15: deEs bestückt die Vertiefungen in seinen Händen mit Felsen oder Kiesling und feuert sie daraus ab. In jeder Hand hat es Platz für drei Geschosse.
column16: enThis Pokémon evolved through use of a curious item. Its rocklike hide is composed of a mysterious substance and can withstand a blow from a masterwork sword with nary a scratch.
column17: enIt relies on its carapace to deflect incoming attacks and throw its enemy off balance. As soon as that happens, it drives its drill into the foe.
column18: enIt puts rocks in holes in its palms and uses its muscles to shoot them. Geodude are shot at rare times.
column19: enFrom holes in its palms, it fires out Geodude. Its carapace can withstand volcanic eruptions.

column8: enTangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.
column9: enIts vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can’t even see its eyes.
column10: deEs umwickelt Beute, indem es seine Arme, die aus Ranken bestehen, verlängert.
column11: enIt ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.
column12: deJüngsten Erkenntnissen zufolge bestehen seine zwei Arme, die es beliebig verlängern kann, aus gebündelten Ranken.
column13: enVine growth is accelerated for Tangrowth living in warm climates. If the vines grow long, Tangrowth shortens them by tearing parts of them off.
column14: enEven if one of its arms is eaten, it’s fine. The Pokémon regenerates quickly and will go right back to normal.
column15: deJe wärmer sein Lebensraum ist, desto schneller wachsen seine Ranken. Werden sie zu lang, kürzt Tangoloss sie, indem es sie abreißt.
column16: enIts arms are made of plants that bind themselves to things. They grow back right away if cut.
column17: enWhen it remains still, it appears to be a large shrub. Unsuspecting prey that wander near get ensnared by its vines.
column18: deIn warmen Jahreszeiten wuchern seine Ranken so dicht, dass man nicht einmal mehr seine Augen erkennt.
column19: enDraped with long vines, it resembles a shrub in appearance. It swings bundles of vines as though they were arms, wrapping them around prey to ensnare them.

column8: enIt pushes the tips of its tails against its foes and then lets loose a high-voltage current. Its foes are burned to a crisp in an instant.
column9: enHeedless of enemy attacks, it closes in, shoves its tails onto the foe, then looses high voltage.
column10: deElevoltek gehört zu jenen Elektro-Pokémon, die den meisten Strom erzeugen können. Mit seinen Schweifspitzen entlädt es Starkstrom.
column11: deEs berührt Gegner mit seinen Schweifspitzen und entlädt dann starke Elektrizität. So kann es sie im Nullkommanichts verkohlen.
column12: enIts evolution was induced by an unusual item, and its electrical output rises along with its heart rate. From its tails, it can unleash an electric current measuring 20,000 volts.
column13: deEs berührt den Gegner mit seinen beiden Schweifspitzen und entlädt dann über 20 000 V.
column14: enThe amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.
column15: enIt pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.
column16: deWie viel Strom es erzeugt, ist abhängig von seinem Puls. Im Kampf steigt Elevolteks elektrische Spannung schlagartig an.
column17: enWhen it gets excited, it thumps its chest. With every thud, thunder roars and electric sparks shower all around.
column18: enThe instant it presses the tips of its tails onto an opponent, it sends over 20,000 volts of electricity into the foe.
column19: deBei voller Ladung zucken feurige, blassweiße Funken zwischen seinen zwei Hörnern hin und her.
column20: deWenn es sich aufregt, schlägt es sich kräftig auf die Brust. Bei jedem Schlag sprühen Funken und es ertönt ein Donnern.
column21: enAs its electric charge amplifies, blue sparks begin to crackle between its horns.
column22: enIt grips its tail, which spews electricity, and then beats down opponents with the power of its electrified fist.
column23: enA single Electivire can provide enough electricity for all the buildings in a big city for a year.
column24: deEin einzelnes Elevoltek erzeugt genügend Elektrizität, um eine Großstadt ein ganzes Jahr lang mit Strom zu versorgen.
column25: deEs umfasst seine vor Elektrizität sprühenden Schweife, um seine Hände aufzuladen und dann mit aller Kraft zuzuschlagen.
column26: enIn terms of electrical-energy output, Electivire is one of the best among all Electric Pokémon. It discharges high-voltage currents from its tails.

column8: deDurch das Leben in Vulkankratern ähnelt Magbrants Körper seinem Umfeld. Es besitzt ein Organ, das einer Magmakammer gleicht.
column9: enThey dwell in volcanic craters. According to what is known, a single pair of male and female Magmortar lives in one volcano.
column10: enFrom its arm, it launches fireballs hotter than 3,500 degrees Fahrenheit. Its arm starts to melt when it fires a whole barrage.
column11: enWhen Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.
column12: enIt blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit out of its arms. Its breath also sears and sizzles.
column13: deEs schießt Feuerbälle auf seine Gegner. Da alles, was es damit trifft, verkohlt wird, sieht es auf der Jagd nach Beute von dieser Methode ab.
column14: deAus den Enden seiner Arme schießen Feuerbälle mit 2 000 °C. Es lebt in Vulkankratern.
column15: enIt blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees F from the ends of its arms. It lives in volcanic craters.
column16: deHolt es tief Luft, werden die Flammen in seinem Bauch stärker und erreichen eine Temperatur von 2000 şC.
column17: enLiving in the crater of a volcano has caused this Pokémon’s body to resemble its environment— it has an organ similar to a magma chamber.
column18: enMagmortar takes down its enemies by shooting fireballs, which burn them to a blackened crisp. It avoids this method when hunting prey.
column19: enWhen launching 3,600 degrees F fireballs, its body takes on a whitish hue from the intense heat.
column20: enIt blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit from the ends of its arms. It lives in volcanic craters.
column21: deAuch heute gibt es noch einige Fabriken, in denen die Flammen von Magbrant zur Verarbeitung von Metall verwendet werden.
column22: enUse of a strange item caused this Pokémon to evolve. Fireballs launched from the ends of its tubelike arms are hot enough to melt an iron pot in an instant.
column23: deAus den Enden seiner Arme feuert es 2000 °C heiße Feuerbälle ab. Dadurch verkohlen sie nach mehrmaligem Einsatz leicht.
column24: enThere are still quite a few factories that rely on the flames provided by Magmortar to process metals.
column25: deEs lebt in Vulkankratern. Angeblich gibt es pro Vulkan immer nur ein Magbrant-Paar.
column26: enWhen shooting 3,600 degree F fireballs from its arms, its body takes on a whitish hue from the intense heat.
column27: deEs schießt 2 000 °C heiße Feuerbälle aus den Enden seiner Arme. Selbst sein Atem steht in Flammen.

column8: enScant few have ever sighted this Pokémon. After studying what literature remains, I am certain Togekiss will reveal itself when peace reigns in the land.
column9: enIt shares many blessings with people who respect one another’s rights and avoid needless strife.
column10: enAs everyone knows, it visits peaceful regions, bringing them gifts of kindness and sweet blessings.
column11: enThese Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil. In recent times, they’ve hardly been seen at all.
column12: deMenschen, die unnötigen Streit vermeiden und sich gegenseitig respektieren, ist es wohlgesinnt.
column13: deEs heißt, Togekiss erscheine in friedlichen Zeiten und verteile alle möglichen Wohltaten.
column14: deEs zeigt sich nie an Orten, wo Streit und Zwietracht herrschen. In letzter Zeit wird es kaum noch gesehen.
column15: enIt will never appear where there is strife. Its sightings have become rare recently.
column16: enKnown as a bringer of blessings, it’s been depicted on good-luck charms since ancient times.
column17: deMan sagt, es verteile Wohltaten. Aus diesem Grund wird sein Abbild seit Urzeiten für Glücksbringer verwendet.

column8: enThe beat of its wings is so powerful that it accidentally dislodges full-grown trees when it takes off in flight.
column9: enIts jaw power is incredible. It is adept at biting apart foes while flying by at high speed.
column10: enBy churning its wings, it creates shock waves that inflict critical internal injuries to foes.
column11: deSeine bevorzugte Strategie besteht darin, seine Gegner aus dem Flug blitzartig zur Strecke zu bringen.
column12: enThis six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.
column13: enIt prefers to battle by biting apart foes’ heads instantly while flying by at high speed.
column14: enExtremely violent. When hunting, it wastes none of its energy, aiming only for prey's most vulnerable spots. Any who manage to tame this Pokémon must be of incredible bravery.
column15: deEs kann mühelos einen Erwachsenen umhertragen. Die Federn an seinem Hinterteil stabilisieren seinen Flug.

column8: enCells similar to those of plants have been found in its fur. Its hard tail can fell a large tree with one stroke, and the tail's sharpness exceeds even that of a sword crafted by a master.
column9: enThis Pokémon’s tail is blade sharp, with a fantastic cutting edge that can slice right though large trees.
column10: enThe younger they are, the more they smell like fresh grass. With age, their fragrance takes on the odor of fallen leaves.
column11: deMittels Photosynthese versorgt es sich mit Nährstoffen. Es lebt abgeschieden in dichten, von glitzernden Bächen durchzogenen Wäldern.
column12: deJunge Folipurba riechen stark nach frischem Gras, ältere hingegen verströmen den Geruch von herabgefallenem Laub.
column13: deDie Bewohner Galars mögen den eigentümlichen Geruch, den seine Blätter verströmen. Als Parfüm ist er sehr beliebt.
column14: enIt basically does not fight. With cells similar to those of plants, it can perform photosynthesis.
column15: enIts cellular composition is closer to that of a plant than an animal. It uses photosynthesis to produce its energy supply without eating food.
column16: deSeine Zellstruktur ähnelt jener von Pflanzen. Durch Photosynthese kann es ohne Nahrungsaufnahme Energie gewinnen.
column17: deSein Schweif ist scharf wie eine Klinge. Es ist stolz darauf, dass es damit selbst einen großen Baum sauber zweiteilen kann.
column18: enIt gets its nutrition from photosynthesis. It lives a quiet life deep in forests where clean rivers flow.
column19: enWhen you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you’ll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.
column20: enJust like a plant, it uses photosynthesis. As a result, it is always enveloped in clear air.
column21: deFolipurba kämpft nicht gerne, aber sollten sich seine Kameraden in Gefahr befinden, verwendet es seinen blattartigen Schweif wie eine Klinge.
column22: enWith cells similar to those of plants, it performs photosynthesis inside its body and creates pure air.
column23: deAn klaren Tagen erzeugt Folipurba saubere Luft, indem es Photosynthese betreibt.
column24: deGenau wie eine Pflanze führt es die Photosynthese aus. Deshalb ist es ständig von reiner Luft umgeben.
column25: enAlthough it doesn’t like disputes, it will sharpen the leaf on its tail into a blade and fight if it has to protect its friends.
column26: enGalarians favor the distinctive aroma that drifts from this Pokémon’s leaves. There’s a popular perfume made using that scent.
column27: enWhen you see LEAFEON asleep in a patch of sunshine, you’ll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

column8: deEs kann seine Körpertemperatur frei regulieren. Indem es die Wasserbestandteile der Luft gefrieren lässt, wirbelt es Diamantstaub auf.
column9: enAs a protective technique, it can completely freeze its fur to make its hairs stand like needles.
column10: enIt protects itself by freezing its fur into sharp needles. It can drop its body temperature below –75 degrees Fahrenheit.
column11: deEs lässt die Wasserbestandteile in der Luft in Sekundenschnelle zu Diamantstaub gefrieren und greift damit seine Beute an.
column12: enBy controlling its body heat, it can freeze the atmosphere around it to make a diamond-dust flurry.
column13: deEs lässt sein Fell gefrieren und macht es somit so spitz wie Eiszapfen. Seine Körpertemperatur kann es auf -60 °C senken.
column14: enThe coldness emanating from Glaceon causes powdery snow to form, making it quite a popular Pokémon at ski resorts.
column15: deEs lässt sein Fell gefrieren und macht es dadurch so spitz wie Eiszapfen, mit denen es dann seine Beute attackiert.
column16: enIt lowers its body heat to freeze its fur. The hairs then become like needles it can fire.
column17: enIt can instantaneously freeze any moisture that’s around it, creating ice pellets to shoot at its prey.
column18: enIt causes small ice crystals to form by lowering the temperature of the surrounding atmosphere.
column19: deGlaziola erzeugt ein Diamantstaubgestöber, um Beute mit seiner Schönheit so zu verzaubern, dass es sie unbemerkt einfrieren kann.
column20: deWenn es seine Körpertemperatur senkt, erstarren die Haare seines Fells zu Eis und dienen ihm als nadelartige Geschosse.
column21: enGlaceon is able to lower its body temperature very quickly. It freezes the atmosphere, creating diamond dust that glitters like gems while it flutters and dances around.
column22: deErzeugt ein Diamantstaubgestöber durch das Einfrieren der Luft um sich herum.
column23: enAny who become captivated by the beauty of the snowfall that Glaceon creates will be frozen before they know it.
column24: enIt freezes its fur into icicles, spiky and sharp, and tackles its prey.
column25: deDie kalte Luft, die Glaziola abgibt, erzeugt Pulverschnee. In Skigebieten erfreut es sich daher großer Beliebtheit.
column26: enIt can control its body temperature at will. This enables it to freeze the moisture in the atmosphere, creating flurries of diamond dust.

column8: enIt dances silently through the sky. When it approaches prey, it can land a critical hit in an instant.
column9: deEs fliegt völlig lautlos. Erst fängt es seine Beute mit seinem langen Schweif, dann saugt es sie aus.
column10: enIf it succeeds in catching even a faint breeze properly, it can circle the globe without flapping once.
column11: enIt glides soundlessly on pitch-black wings and sinks sharp fangs into the throat of its prey. It takes on a look of satisfaction once it has entirely drained its prey of blood.
column12: deEs hängt kopfüber von einem Ast und beobachtet seine Beute. Bei Gelegenheit stürzt es sich auf sie.
column13: enIt observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!
column14: enIts flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey... Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

column8: deEs existiert schon seit Urzeiten. Mamutel wurde sogar schon in 10 000 Jahre altem Eis gefunden.
column9: enThis species reached its zenith during the period known as the ice age. I suspect that Hisui's frigid climate is in harmony with Mamoswine's constitution, thus awakening hidden potential.
column10: enIt looks strong, and that’s exactly what it is. As the weather grows colder, its ice tusks grow longer, thicker, and more impressive.
column11: enIts impressive tusks are made of ice. The population thinned when it turned warm after the ice age.
column12: enA frozen Mamoswine was dug from ice dating back 10,000 years. This Pokémon has been around a long, long, long time.
column13: enThis Pokémon can be spotted in wall paintings from as far back as 10,000 years ago. For a while, it was thought to have gone extinct.
column14: deEs ist so stark, wie es aussieht. Je kälter es ist, desto dicker, länger und beeindruckender werden seine Stoßzähne aus Eis.
column15: enA frozen one was dug up from soil dating back 10,000 years. It woke up to much amazement.
column16: enIt flourished worldwide during the ice age but its population declined when the masses of ice began to dwindle.
column17: enA frozen MAMOSWINE was dug from ice dating back 10,000 years. This Pokémon has been around a long, long, long time.
column18: deDie beeindruckenden Stoßzähne dieser Pokémon sind aus Eis. Nach der Eiszeit nahm ihre Population ab.
column19: enWhen the temperature rose at the end of the ice age, most Mamoswine disappeared.
column20: deSie sind schon auf 10000 Jahre alten Wandmalereien abgebildet. Es gab Zeiten, in denen man sie für ausgestorben hielt.

column8: deSeit eine neue Software installiert wurde, die es zu interdimensionalen Reisen befähigen sollte, wurde sein Verhalten instabil.
column9: deSein seltsames Verhalten verdankt es offenbar der Unfähigkeit des Wissenschaftlers, der seine Software aktualisiert hat.
column10: deEine neue Software sollte es zu interdimensionalen Reisen befähigen, doch anscheinend ging etwas schief.
column11: enA faulty update was added to its programming. Its behavior is noticeably strange, so the experiment may have been a failure.
column12: enIts programming was modified to enable it to travel through alien dimensions. Seems there might have been an error...
column13: deZusätzliche Software wurde installiert, um das Pokémon zu verbessern. Etwas ging jedoch schief und seitdem benimmt es sich seltsam.
column14: enAdditional software was installed to make it a better Pokémon. It began acting oddly, however.
column15: enIts programming was modified to enable work in alien dimensions. It did not work as planned.
column16: enIts behavior is noticeably unstable, which is apparently due to the incompetence of the engineer who updated its programming.
column17: enA curious item induced this evolution. The Pokémon's offensive capabilities have greatly increased, but the strangeness of its behavior has magnified in equal measure. This worries me.
column18: enIts program was modified to facilitate extra-dimensional activities, but that led to noticeably strange behavior.
column19: enIn order to create a more advanced Pokémon, an additional program was installed, but apparently it contained a defect that makes it move oddly.
column20: enPorygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to move between dimensions, but the program also caused instability in Porygon-Z’s behavior.
column21: deEine fehlerhafte neue Software führte zu seinem auffällig seltsamen Verhalten. Vermutlich handelt es sich um ein verkorkstes Experiment.
column22: deEine neue Software sollte es zu interdimensionalen Reisen befähigen, doch seitdem verhält es sich seltsam.
column23: enSome say an additional program made this Pokémon evolve, but even academics can’t agree on whether Porygon-Z is really an evolution.
column24: deEs heißt, es habe sich durch eine neue Software entwickelt, doch Wissenschaftler sind sich nicht einig, ob Porygon-Z wirklich eine Entwicklung ist.
column25: deZusätzliche Software wurde installiert, um das Pokémon zu verbessern. Seitdem benimmt es sich seltsam.

column8: deEs spürt sofort, wenn jemand in Not ist, und eilt ihm unverzüglich zu Hilfe.
column9: enA master of courtesy and swordsmanship, it fights using extending swords on its elbows.
column10: enTrue to its honorable-warrior image, it uses the blades on its elbows only in defense of something or someone.
column11: deDa es selbst die leisesten Gedanken seines Gegners lesen kann, kann es immer zuerst angreifen.
column12: enSharply attuned to others’ wishes for help, this Pokémon seeks out those in need and aids them in battle.
column13: deEs kämpft, indem es die Schwerter an seinen Ellbogen verlängert. Es ist ein Meister des Kampfes.
column14: enWhen trying to protect someone, it extends its elbows as if they were swords and fights savagely.
column15: deDieses Pokémon wird als Krieger angesehen. Die Klingen an seinen Ellbogen benutzt es nur, um etwas zu beschützen.
column16: enThe blades extending from its elbows are sharper than the finest swords. Its swordsmanship, albeit self-taught, is astonishingly impressive.
column17: enBecause it can sense what its foe is thinking, its attacks burst out first, fast, and fierce.

column8: deEs spürt sofort, wenn jemand in Not ist, und eilt ihm unverzüglich zu Hilfe.

column10: enTrue to its honorable-warrior image, it uses the blades on its elbows only in defense of something or someone.

column15: deDieses Pokémon wird als Krieger angesehen. Die Klingen an seinen Ellbogen benutzt es nur, um etwas zu beschützen.
column16: enThe blades extending from its elbows are sharper than the finest swords. Its swordsmanship, albeit self-taught, is astonishingly impressive.
column17: enBecause it can sense what its foe is thinking, its attacks burst out first, fast, and fierce.

column8: deSein Körper selbst bewegt sich nicht. Um Beute zu fangen, steuert es drei kleine Einheiten, die Mininasen genannt werden.
column9: deDurch seinen starken Magnetismus macht es Elektrogeräte in seiner Nähe kaputt.
column10: enIt exudes strong magnetism from all over. It controls three small units called Mini-Noses.
column11: deEs gibt starken Magnetismus ab. Es steuert drei kleine Einheiten, die sich Mininasen nennen.
column12: enThe main body controls three mobile units called Mini-Noses, which it maneuvers to catch prey.
column13: deSein Körper selbst befehligt meist nur die drei kleinen Einheiten, die an seiner Stelle kämpfen und für die Nahrungssuche zuständig sind.
column14: enIt radiates such a powerful magnetic field that nearby electrical appliances become unusable.
column15: deEs steuert kleine Einheiten, die man Mininasen nennt. Leider verirren sie sich manchmal, und es heißt, dass einige von ihnen nie wiederkehren.
column16: deEs steuert drei kleine Einheiten mithilfe von starkem Magnetismus. Man nennt sie Mininasen.
column17: enIt freely controls three units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.
column18: enIt uses three small units to catch prey and battle enemies. The main body mostly just gives orders.
column19: enIt freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.
column20: enIt controls three units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force. With them, it can attack the foe from three directions.
column21: enAlthough it can control its units known as Mini-Noses, they sometimes get lost and don’t come back.
column22: enIt is able to emit powerful magnetism, allowing it control over the iron sand that forms its luscious mustache. Using this iron sand, Probopass forms hard stones with which it smites its prey.

column8: deEs verschluckt Beute im Ganzen mit dem Maul an seinem Bauch, verspeist aber nur ihre Seele. Den Körper spuckt es wieder aus.
column9: deNiemand weiß, ob es einen eigenen Willen hat. Über Radiowellen empfängt es Befehle aus dem Jenseits, Menschen und Pokémon zu entführen.
column10: enWith the mouth on its belly, Dusknoir swallows its target whole. The soul is the only thing eaten— Dusknoir disgorges the body before departing.
column11: enThis feared Pokémon is said to travel to worlds unknown. Some even believe that it takes lost spirits along with it.
column12: deMan sagt, dass es verlorene Seelen in seinem biegsamen Körper nach Hause geleite.
column13: deDie Antenne auf seinem Kopf empfängt Radiowellen aus einer anderen Dimension.
column14: enIt is said to take lost spirits into its pliant body and guide them home.
column15: enThe antenna on its head captures radio waves from the world of spirits that command it to take people there.
column16: enComes to those whose lives have come to an end and escorts their souls to the afterlife. Known to mistakenly take the souls of those who yet have life left in them, albeit rarely.
column17: enAt the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

column8: deEs friert seine Beute mit -50 şC kalter Luft ein, nimmt sie dann in seinen Unterschlupf mit und reiht sie dort fein säuberlich auf.
column9: enIt spits out cold air of nearly -60 degrees Fahrenheit to freeze its quarry. It brings frozen prey back to its lair and neatly lines them up.
column10: enIt freezes prey by blowing its -58 degrees F breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.
column11: enIt freezes foes with an icy breath nearly -60 degrees Fahrenheit. What seems to be its body is actually hollow.
column12: enWhen it finds humans or Pokémon it likes, it freezes them and takes them to its chilly den, where they become decorations.
column13: enThe soul of a woman lost on a snowy mountain possessed an icicle, becoming this Pokémon. The food it most relishes is the souls of men.
column14: enIt freezes foes with an icy breath nearly -60 degrees F. What seems to be its body is actually hollow.
column15: deEs ist aus dem Gram einer Frau entstanden, die auf schneebedeckten Bergen umgekommen ist. Am liebsten isst es gefrorene Seelen.
column16: enLegends in snowy regions say that a woman who was lost on an icy mountain was reborn as FROSLASS.
column17: enLegends in snowy regions say that a woman who was lost on an icy mountain was reborn as Froslass.
column18: deWanderer, die durch verschneite Berge stapfen, friert es ein und nimmt sie mit in seinen Bau. Allerdings nur jene, die es als schön erachtet.
column19: enIt freezes hikers who have come to climb snowy mountains and carries them back to its home. It only goes after men it thinks are handsome.
column20: deEs heißt, dass es am Abend eines Schneesturms Menschensiedlungen heimsuche. Wenn es an der Tür klopft, sollte man sie besser nicht öffnen.
column21: enAfter a woman met her end on a snowy mountain, her regrets lingered on. From them, this Pokémon was born. Its favorite food is frozen souls.
column22: deMenschen und Pokémon, die ihm gefallen, friert es mit eisiger Luft ein. Dann nimmt es sie mit in seinen Bau und stellt sie dort aus.
column23: deSein eisiger Atem mit -50 °C friert Gegner ein. Was aussieht wie sein Körper, ist tatsächlich hohl.
column24: enIt’s said that on nights of terrible blizzards, it comes down to human settlements. If you hear it knocking at your door, do not open it!
column25: enLegends in snowy regions say that a woman who was lost on an icy mountain was reborn as FROSLASS
column26: enA Pokémon inhabited by the soul of a woman who died bearing a grudge in the snowy mountains. Legends of Froslass placing deathly curses on misbehaving men send shivers down my spine.
column27: deIn schneereichen Gegenden erzählt man sich, es sei die Wiedergeburt einer in den Bergen Verunglückten.
column28: deMan sagt, die Seele einer in den Bergen verunglückten Frau sei in einen Eiszapfen gefahren. Es frisst am liebsten Männerseelen.

column8: enWith a body made of plasma, it can inhabit all sorts of machines. It loves to surprise others.
column9: deMit seinem aus Plasma bestehenden Körper kann es in verschiedene Geräte schlüpfen. Es liebt es, andere zu erschrecken.
column10: deDie Erfindung eines jungen Tüftlers hat zu der Herstellung verschiedener Geräte geführt, die das Potenzial von Rotom nutzen können.
column11: enThis bizarre Pokémon appears to be a will-o'-the-wisp powered by electricity. Be wary, as Rotom is both smart and mischievous.
column12: deDieses Pokémon wurde lange Zeit erforscht, um als Energiequelle für einen besonderen Motor zu dienen.
column13: enResearch continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.
column14: enIts electric-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.
column15: enIts body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.
column16: deSein Körper besteht aus Plasma. Mit ihm kann es in elektrische Geräte eindringen und für Chaos sorgen.
column17: enOne boy’s invention led to the development of many different machines that take advantage of Rotom’s unique capabilities.

column8: enA Pokémon feared but also respected for stealing away the memories of evildoers. I have found records that suggest Uxie holds dominion over knowledge.
column9: enIt is said that its emergence gave humans the intelligence to improve their quality of life.
column10: de„Das wissende Wesen“. Es soll die Erinnerungen derer löschen, die ihm in die Augen sehen.
column11: enWhen Uxie flew, people gained the ability to solve problems. It was the birth of knowledge.
column12: enAccording to some sources, this Pokémon provided people with the intelligence necessary to solve various problems.
column13: enWhen UXIE flew, people gained the ability to solve problems. It was the birth of knowledge.
column14: deMan sagt, dass durch sein Auftauchen Menschen die Intelligenz erhielten, ihr Leben zu verbessern.
column15: enKnown as “The Being of Knowledge.” It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

column8: enKnown as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.
column9: deEs schläft auf dem Grund eines Sees. Man sagt, sein Geist verlasse den Körper und fliege über den See.
column10: enKnown as the Being of Emotion. In legend, this Pokémon was feared, as any who showed disrespect would have their emotions thrown into disarray.
column11: de„Das fühlende Wesen“. Es lehrt die Menschen die Ideale von Trauer, Schmerz und Freude.
column12: enWhen Mesprit flew, people learned the joy and sadness of living. It was the birth of emotions.
column13: enWhen MESPRIT flew, people learned the joy and sadness of living. It was the birth of emotions.
column14: enIt sleeps at the bottom of a lake. Its spirit is said to leave its body to fly on the lake’s surface.
column15: enThis Pokémon is said to have endowed the human heart with emotions, such as sorrow and joy.

column8: enThe dreaded Being of Willpower. Legends tell of this Pokémon manipulating the will of its adversaries and turning them into puppets of its own.
column9: enIt is thought that UXIE, MESPRIT and AZELF all came from the same egg.
column10: de„Das starke Wesen“. Es schläft auf dem Grund eines Sees und hält so die Welt in Balance.
column11: deMan glaubt, dass Selfe, Vesprit und Tobutz alle aus demselben Ei kamen.
column12: enWhen Azelf flew, people gained the determination to do things. It was the birth of willpower.
column13: enWhen AZELF flew, people gained the determination to do things. It was the birth of willpower.
column14: enThis Pokémon is said to have endowed humans with the determination needed to face any of life’s difficulties.
column15: enKnown as “The Being of Willpower.” It sleeps at the bottom of a lake to keep the world in balance.
column16: enIt is thought that Uxie, Mesprit, and Azelf all came from the same egg.

column11: enA legendary Pokémon of Sinnoh. It is said that time flows when Dialga’s heart beats.
column12: enThis Pokémon completely controls the flow of time. It uses its power to travel at will through the past and future.
column13: enA Pokémon spoken of in legend. It is said that time began moving when DIALGA was born.

column8: enStories tell of this Pokémon being birthed from the boiling magma within Mount Coronet. Its molten-steel body holds many mysteries.
column9: enIts body is made of rugged steel. However, it is partially melted in spots because of its own heat.
column10: enBoiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.
column11: enIt dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.
column12: deEs lebt in vulkanischen Höhlen. Mit seinen kreuzförmigen Klauen kann es sogar an der Decke laufen.
column13: deDas Blut, das durch seinen Körper fließt, brodelt heiß wie Magma. Es lebt in vulkanischen Höhlen.

column8: enIt’s said that Regigigas created other Pokémon from many different materials before it moved the continents to where they are today.
column9: deMan sagt, es habe Pokémon aus einem Eisberg, Felsen und Magma nach seinem Abbild geschaffen.
column10: deEs gibt eine Legende, wonach dieses Pokémon die Kontinente mit einem Seil gezogen hat.
column11: deMan sagt, es habe aus verschiedenen Materialien Pokémon erschaffen und dann die Kontinente an ihren jetzigen Standort bewegt.
column12: enIt is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.
column13: enThere is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.
column14: enIt is believed to have shaped REGIROCK, REGICE, and REGISTEEL out of clay, ice, and magma.
column15: enAccording to legend, Regigigas pulled landmasses together and bound them with rope to create the continent of Hisui. Though I have my doubts, the story could well contain a shred of truth.
column16: deÜberall auf der Welt kursieren Legenden, denen zufolge Regigigas Pokémon nach seinem Abbild geschaffen habe.
column17: enAll over the world, there are legends that tell of Regigigas creating other Pokémon in its own image.

column8: deEs wurde aufgrund seines Verhaltens verbannt. Aus der Zerrwelt schaut es auf die alte Welt.
column9: deEs lebt in einer Zerrwelt, die auf der Kehrseite der unseren liegt und die sich aller Logik entzieht.
column10: enIt was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.
column11: enThere is one Hisuian verse that tells of a powerful light creating a deep shadow. I imagine that this deep shadow is Giratina.
column12: enThis Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange.
column13: enA Pokémon that is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours. It appears in an ancient cemetery.

column8: enShiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.
column9: enCresselia is reminiscent of the crescent moon. It leaves a brilliant line of light in its wake as it flies across the night sky. I daresay it resembles the heavenly maiden who created the Milky Way.
column10: deSeine Flügel geben schimmernde Partikel ab, die wie ein Schleier herabrieseln. Man sagt, es verkörpere die Mondsichel.
column11: enOn nights around the quarter moon, the aurora from its tail extends and undulates beautifully.
column12: enThose who sleep holding CRESSELIA’s feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.
column13: enThose who sleep holding Cresselia’s feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.
column14: deHält man eine seiner Federn, träumt man süß. Manche glauben, es sei die Verkörperung der Mondsichel.

column8: enA Pokémon that lives in warm seas. It inflates the flotation sac on its head to drift and search for food.
column9: enIt drifts in warm seas. It always returns to where it was born, no matter how far it may have drifted.
column10: enCan be seen floating offshore during seasons when the seas are warm. Its azure body blends in with the ocean waters—logic suggests this is a defense mechanism against natural predators.
column11: deSteigt die Meerestemperatur, bläst es seinen Schwimmbeutel auf und treibt in Schwärmen auf dem Wasser.
column12: enWhen the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs.
column13: deLässt sich in warmen Meeren treiben, kehrt aber immer an den Platz seiner Geburt zurück.

column8: deEs besitzt die wundersame Fähigkeit, das Herz eines jeden anderen Pokémon anzurühren.
column9: enIt starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.
column10: enIt is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.
column11: enBorn on a cold seafloor, it will swim great distances to return to its birthplace.
column12: deEs wird mit einer wundersamen Kraft geboren, die eine Bindung zu jedem anderen Pokémon möglich macht.
column13: enRumored to migrate across the oceans and visit Hisui's coastal waters only rarely. Although Manaphy resembles Phione, it is also quite different. The relation between the two is unclear.
column14: enWater makes up 80% of its body. This Pokémon is easily affected by its environment.

column8: enFolklore has it that on moonless nights, this Pokémon will make people see horrific nightmares.
column9: deEs vertreibt Eindringlinge aus seinem Revier, indem es sie in Schlaf versetzt und mit Alpträumen quält.
column10: enIt chases people and Pokémon from its territory by causing them to experience deep, nightmarish slumbers.
column11: enTo protect itself, it afflicts those around it with nightmares. However, it means no harm.
column12: deEs kann andere in Schlaf versetzen und ihnen Träume geben. Es ist nur bei Neumond aktiv.
column13: enOn a moonless night, a strange incident occurred in which every one of a village's inhabitants suffered nightmares. The villagers attested that Darkrai appeared before them in these nightmares.
column14: enIt can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

column9: enThe blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings  of gratitude are the message it delivers.
column10: enThe flowers all over its body burst into bloom if it is lovingly hugged and senses gratitude.
column11: enWhen the turning of seasons brings the cruel winter to its end and the joyous people give thanks to the heavens, Shaymin appears and covers the withered land with flowers.
column12: enIt lives in flower patches and avoids detection by curling up to look like a flowering plant.
column13: deEs kann die Luft von Giften reinigen und Ödland in ein üppig blühendes Blumenfeld verwandeln.
column14: enIt can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.
column15: enThe blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.
