column9: deEin warmherziges Pokémon, das über heilende und segnende Kräfte verfügt. In längst vergangenen Zeiten herrschte es über Galar.
column10: enCalyrex is a merciful Pokémon, capable of providing healing and blessings. It reigned over the Galar region in times of yore.
column11: enCalyrex is known in legend as a king that ruled over Galar in ancient times. It has the power to cause hearts to mend and plants to spring forth.

column8: enThe black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.
enA violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.
enI believe it was Hisui's swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.
enI believe it was Hisui's swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.
enSavage, violent Pokémon, red and blue Basculin are always fighting each other over territory.
column9: deEin wildes und brutales Pokémon. Rot gestreifte und blau gestreifte Barschuft tragen ständig Kämpfe um ihr Territorium aus.
column10: deRot gestreifte und blau gestreifte Exemplare sind sich feindlich gesonnen. Doch ist jeder Schwarm bunt gemischt.
column11: deFrüher standen sie oft auf dem Speiseplan. Das Fleisch rotliniger Exemplare war wegen seines hohen Fettanteils bei jungen Leuten sehr beliebt.
column12: deSie sind bei Anglern beliebt, weil sie ihnen harte Kämpfe liefern. Sie werden oft illegal in Teichen ausgesetzt, wodurch sie sich schneller verbreiten.
column13: deDieses Pokémon ist so brutal, dass in Seen neben einem Schwarm Barschuft nur noch Krebscorps und Krebutack bestehen können.
column14: enRed- and blue-striped Basculin are very violent and always fighting. They are also remarkably tasty.
column15: deSie gelten als Delikatesse. Aufgrund ihrer brutalen Natur liegen rot und blau gestreifte Exemplare immer im Clinch.
column16: enIn the past, it often appeared on the dinner table. The meat of red-striped Basculin is on the fatty side, and it’s more popular with the youth.
column17: enWhen a school of Basculin appears in a lake, everything else disappears, except for Corphish and Crawdaunt. That’s how violent Basculin are.
column18: enRed and blue Basculin get along so poorly, they’ll start fighting instantly. These Pokémon are very hostile.
column19: enAnglers love the fight this Pokémon puts up on the hook. And there are always more to catch— many people release them into lakes illicitly.
column20: enThough it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor—this one is gentle—I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.
column21: enRed and blue Basculin usually do not get along, but sometimes members of one school mingle with the other’s school.

column8: enClads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.
deEs rammt seine Krallen in Eier und schlürft ihren Inhalt aus. Pokémon-Züchter können es aus diesem Grund nicht ausstehen.
column9: deEs lauert seiner Beute im Dunkeln auf. Wenn es Sandan mit seinen Krallen angreift, brechen diese manchmal an dessen robuster Haut ab.
column10: enThey will cooperate to steal eggs from the nests of bird Pokémon, but fights break out to determine which one gets to eat the eggs.
column11: enSneasel scales trees by punching its hooked claws into the bark. This Pokémon seeks out unguarded nests and steals eggs for food while the parents are away.
column12: deIn seinen Pfoten stecken scharfe Krallen. Es fährt sie blitzschnell aus, um die Gegner zu überraschen.
column13: enA SNEASEL scales trees by punching its hooked claws into the bark. It seeks out unguarded nests and steals eggs for food while the parents are away.
column14: enIt feeds on eggs stolen from nests. Its sharply hooked claws rip vulnerable spots on prey.
column15: enVicious in nature, it drives PIDGEY from their nests and feasts on the eggs that are left behind.
column16: deDieses durchtriebene und gewissenlose Pokémon beobachtet Nester, bis die Eltern ausgeflogen sind, und stiehlt dann deren Eier.
column17: enSNEASEL scales trees by punching its hooked claws into the bark. This POKéMON seeks out unguarded nests and steals eggs for food while the parents are away.
column18: enVicious in nature, it drives PIDGEY from their nests and scavenges any leftovers it can find.
column19: enIt blends into the dark of night to ambush its prey. Sneasel will attack Sandshrew, but its hard skin can cause Sneasel’s claws to snap off.
column20: enIt uses its claws to poke holes in eggs so it can slurp out the insides. Breeders consider it a scourge and will drive it away or eradicate it.
column21: enThis cunning POKéMON hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.
column22: enA smart and sneaky Pokémon. A pair may work together to steal eggs by having one lure the parents away.
column23: deSniebel klettert an Bäumen hoch, indem es seine hakenförmigen Krallen in die Rinde schlägt. Dieses Pokémon sucht nach unbewachten Nestern und stiehlt die Eier. Davon ernährt es sich.
column24: deIn seinen Pfoten stecken scharfe Krallen. Es fährt sie blitzschnell aus, um Gegner zu überraschen.
column25: deSie bilden ein Team, um gemeinsam Eier aus den Nestern von Vogel-Pokémon zu klauen. Dann streiten sie sich darüber, wer sie fressen darf.
column26: deEs ernährt sich von Eiern, die es aus Nestern stiehlt. Beute greift es mit seinen scharfen Krallen an.
column27: deDieses Pokémon hat ein durchtriebenes, grausames Wesen. Es sucht nach unbewachten Nestern und stiehlt die Eier.
column28: enIt is extremely vicious and will not stop attacking until its foe is incapable of moving.
column29: enA smart and sneaky Pokémon, it makes its opponents flinch by suddenly showing the claws hidden in its paws.
column30: enIts paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it suddenly extends the claws and startles its enemy.
column31: enIts sturdy, curved claws are ideal for traversing precipitous cliffs. From the tips of these claws drips a venom that infiltrates the nerves of any prey caught in Sneasel's grasp.
column32: enIt has a cunning yet savage disposition. It waits for parents to leave their nests, and then it sneaks in to steal their eggs.

column8: enBecause of Sneasler's virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.
enWhen faced with a larger opponent, it swallows as much water as it can to match the opponent’s size.
column9: deSieht es sich einem größeren Feind gegenüber, schluckt es massenweise Wasser, um seinen Körper aufzublähen und größer zu wirken.
column10: enQwilfish sucks in water, inflating itself. This Pokémon uses the pressure of the water it swallowed to shoot toxic quills all at once from all over its body. It finds swimming somewhat challenging.
column11: deDie kleinen Stacheln an seinem Körper sind aus Schuppen entstanden. Damit injiziert es Gift.
column12: deBaldorfish saugt Wasser ein, um sich aufzublasen. Dieses Pokémon nutzt den Druck des Wassers dazu, giftige Stacheln aus seinem Körper zu verschießen. Es betrachtet Schwimmen als eine echte Herausforderung.
column13: enA QWILFISH uses the pressure of water it swallows to shoot toxic quills all at once from all over its body. It finds swimming to be somewhat challenging.
column14: enThe small spikes covering its body developed from scales. They inject a toxin that causes fainting.
column15: deBaldorfish saugt Wasser ein, um sich aufzublasen. Dieses Pokémon nutzt den Druck des Wassers dazu, giftige Stacheln zu verschießen. Es betrachtet Schwimmen als eine echte Herausforderung.
column16: enIt shoots the poison spines on its body in all directions. Its round form makes it a poor swimmer.
column17: enTo fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of water all at once.
column18: enQWILFISH sucks in water, inflating itself. This POKéMON uses the pressure of the water it swallowed to shoot toxic quills all at once from all over its body. It finds swimming somewhat challenging.
column19: enFishers detest this troublesome Pokémon because it sprays poison from its spines, getting it everywhere. A different form of Qwilfish lives in other regions.
column20: enWhen faced with a larger opponent, it swallows as much water as it can to match the opponent's size.
column21: deDie kleinen Stacheln an seinem Körper sind aus Schuppen entstanden. Damit injiziert es Gift, das zu Ohnmacht führt.
column22: enTo fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of  water all at once.
column23: deUm seine Giftstacheln abzufeuern, muss es seinen Körper aufpumpen, indem es 10 l trinkt.

column8: enIts lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname ”sea fiend.” It slurps up poison to nourish itself.
enWhen it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.
enWhen it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.
deVon alters her lebt es Seite an Seite mit den Menschen. In Ausgrabungsstätten steinzeitlicher Ruinen wurden Überreste von Fukano gefunden.
column9: enVery friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.
column10: enExtremely loyal, it will fearlessly bark at any opponent to protect its own Trainer from harm.
column11: deFukano hat einen ausgezeichneten Geruchssinn. Wenn dieses Pokémon einmal einen Geruch wahrgenommen hat, vergisst es ihn nicht mehr. Es benutzt diese Fähigkeit dazu, die Gefühlslage von Lebewesen auszuloten.
column12: enExtremely loyal, it will fearlessly bark at any opponent to protect its own trainer from harm.
column13: enIt is very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.
column14: deHat es sich erst einmal an Menschen gewöhnt, wird es sehr zutraulich. In der Wildnis liefert es sich jedoch heftige Revierkämpfe mit Wuffels.
column15: enIt looks cute, but when you approach another Trainer’s Growlithe, it will bark at you and bite.
column16: deEs ist klug und loyal. Sein Revier verteidigt es jedoch mit lautem Bellen gegen Fremde und Eindringlinge.
column17: deEs mag zwar niedlich aussehen, doch nähert man sich dem Fukano eines fremden Trainers, bellt es laut und schnappt nach einem.
column18: enExtremely loyal to its Trainer, it will bark at those who approach the Trainer unexpectedly and run them out of town.
column19: enA Pokémon with a friendly nature. However, it will bark fiercely at anything invading its territory.
column20: enIt has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.
column21: enIt controls a big territory. If it detects an unknown smell, it roars loudly to force out the intruder.
column22: deEs ist sehr loyal und bellt jeden Gegner furchtlos an, um seinen Trainer vor Schaden zu bewahren.
column23: enGrowlithe has a superb sense of smell. Once it smells anything, this Pokémon won’t forget the scent, no matter what. It uses its advanced olfactory sense to determine the emotions of other living things.
column24: enVery protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.
column25: deEs ist von Natur aus sehr zutraulich. Doch wenn jemand in sein Revier eindringt, wird dieser wild angebellt.
column26: deEs ist von Natur aus tapfer und vertrauenswürdig und scheut auch vor Gegnern nicht zurück, die größer und stärker sind als es selbst.
column27: enIts superb sense of smell ensures that this POKéMON won’t forget any scent, no matter what. It uses its sense of smell to detect the emotions of others.
column28: enWhile it’s quite friendly toward humans once it’s grown used to them, in the wild it must be quite fierce to defend its territory from Rockruff.
column29: enIt has lived alongside humans since ages ago. Its bones have been found in excavations of ruins from the Stone Age.
column30: enA POKéMON with a friendly nature. However, it will bark fiercely at anything invading its territory.
column31: deEs ist von Natur aus tapfer und vertrauenswürdig. Es scheut nicht vor starken Gegnern zurück.
column32: enGROWLITHE has a superb sense of smell. Once it smells anything, this POKéMON won’t forget the scent, no matter what. It uses its advanced olfactory sense to determine the emotions of other living things.
column33: enA Pokémon with a loyal nature. It will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.
column34: deEs ist sehr freundlich und bleibt den Menschen treu. Durch Bellen und Beißen versucht es, Gegner zu verscheuchen.
column35: enThey patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.
column36: enIt’s both clever and loyal, but if a stranger tries to invade its territory, it barks threateningly.
column37: deFukano hat einen ausgezeichneten Geruchssinn. Wenn dieses Pokémon einmal einen Geruch wahrgenommen hat, vergisst es ihn nicht mehr. Es benutzt diese Fähigkeit dazu, die Gefühlslage von Lebewesen zu erkennen.

column8: enThe fire burning inside its body serves as the energy to fuel it as it runs great distances. It appears in many legends.
column9: deArkani ist sehr schnell. Es kann an einem Tag fast 10 000 km zurücklegen. Das Feuer, das im Innern dieses Pokémon lodert, dient ihm als Energiequelle.
column10: deDieses Pokémon trägt ein wunderschönes Fell. Es ist obendrein schnell und sehr wendig.
column11: deViele Trainer sind davon fasziniert, dass dieses Pokémon innerhalb eines Tages 10000 km zurücklegen kann.
column12: enOverflowing with beauty and majesty, this strong Pokémon appears in ancient Eastern folklore.
column13: enA Pokémon that has long been admired for its beauty. It runs agilely as if on wings.
column14: deSein bezauberndes Bellen klingt majestätisch. Jeder, der es vernimmt, muss sich automatisch verbeugen.
column15: deDer Legende nach soll es vor langer Zeit an der Seite eines Kriegshelden gekämpft und so an der Eroberung eines Landes mitgewirkt haben.
column16: enThis legendary Chinese Pokémon is considered magnificent. Many people are enchanted by its grand mane.
column17: enIts magnificent bark conveys a sense of majesty. Anyone hearing it can’t help but grovel before it.
column18: enThis legendary Chinese POKEMON is considered magnificent. Many people are enchanted by its grand mane.
column19: deDieses Pokémon verzaubert die Menschen seit Urzeiten mit seiner Schönheit. Obendrein ist es schnell und sehr wendig.
column20: enSnaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.
column21: deWeit entfernt im Osten gilt Arkani als legendär. Wenn es rennt, zieht es mit seinen leichtfüßigen Bewegungen so manche in seinen Bann.
column22: enThere are so many old tales about them that they’re called legendary Pokémon, but there are way more of them around than you’d expect.
column23: enAn ancient picture scroll shows that people were attracted to its movement as it ran through prairies.
column24: deEin anmutiges Pokémon, das Kraft und Stolz ausstrahlt. Bereits in alten Legenden aus dem weit entfernten Osten wurde von ihm berichtet.
column25: enThe sight of it running over 6,200 miles in a single day and night has captivated many people.
column26: deEs ranken sich so viele Mythen um Arkani, dass es gemeinhin als legendär gilt. In Wirklichkeit  kommt es jedoch sehr häufig vor.
column27: enIts magnificent bark conveys a sense of majesty. Anyone hearing it can't help but grovel before it.
column28: enA POKéMON that is described in Chinese legends. It is said to race at an unbelievable speed.
column29: enThis fleet-footed POKéMON is said to run over 6,200 miles in a single day and night. The fire that blazes wildly within its body is its source of power.
column30: enARCANINE is known for its high speed. It is said to be capable of running over 6,200 miles in a single day and night. The fire that blazes wildly within this POKéMON’s body is its source of power.
column31: enLegends tell of its fighting alongside a general and conquering a whole country.
column32: enA POKéMON that has long been admired for its beauty. It runs agilely as if on wings.
column33: enIts proud and regal appearance has captured the hearts of people since long ago.
column34: enA legendary Pokémon in the East. Many people are charmed by the grace and beauty of its running.
column35: enArcanine is known for its high speed. It is said to be capable of running over 6,200 miles in a single day and night. The fire that blazes wildly within this Pokémon’s body is its source of power.
column36: deMithilfe der Energie, die das in ihm lodernde Feuer erzeugt, eilt es über Berg und Tal. Viele Legenden ranken sich um dieses Pokémon.
column37: enA POKéMON that has been admired since the past for its beauty. It runs agilely as if on wings.
column38: enA legendary POKéMON in China. Many people are charmed by its grace and beauty while running.

column8: enPonyta is very weak at birth. It can barely stand up. This Pokémon becomes stronger by stumbling and falling to keep up with its parent.
column9: enCapable of jumping incredibly high. Its hooves and sturdy legs absorb the impact of a hard landing.
column10: enPONYTA is very weak at birth. It can barely stand up. This POKéMON becomes stronger by stumbling and falling to keep up with its parent.
column11: deEs kann unglaublich hoch springen. Die Wucht der Landung absorbiert es mit seinen robusten Hufen und Beinen.
column12: enTraining by jumping over grass that grows longer every day has made it a world-class jumper.
column13: deSeine Beine werden kräftig, da es seinen Eltern hinterherläuft. Es rennt den ganzen Tag umher.
column14: deNach seiner Geburt ist Ponita sehr schwach. Es braucht sehr lange, bis es auf allen Vieren steht. Dieses Pokémon wird stärker, da es bei dem Versuch, mit seinen Eltern Schritt zu halten, immer wieder stolpert und hinfällt.
column15: enAs a newborn, it can barely stand. However, through galloping, its legs are made tougher and faster.
column16: enA PONYTA is very weak at birth. It can barely stand up. Its legs become stronger as it stumbles and falls while trying to keep up with its parent.
column17: deHat man erst einmal das Vertrauen eines Ponitas gewonnen, kann man seltsamerweise sogar seine feurige Mähne anfassen, ohne sich zu verbrennen.
column18: deNeugeboren kann es kaum stehen. Durch das Galoppieren werden seine Beine aber schneller und kräftiger.
column19: enThese Pokémon live in herds out in the grassland. Newborn foals lack their fiery manes, which will develop about an hour after birth.
column20: enIts hooves are ten times harder than diamond. It can trample anything completely flat in little time.
column21: enIts hind legs, which have harder-than-diamond hooves, kick back at any presence it senses behind it.
column22: enIt is a weak runner immediately after birth. It gradually becomes faster by chasing after its parents.
column23: enIt can’t run properly when it’s newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.
column24: deNach seiner Geburt ist Ponita so schwach, dass es kaum von alleine aufstehen kann. Dieses Pokémon wird dadurch schnell stärker, dass es sich beim Versuch, mit seinen Eltern Schritt zu halten, immer wieder aufrappeln muss.
column25: enAbout an hour after birth, its fiery mane and tail grow out, giving it an impressive appearance.
column26: enIts hind legs, which have hooves that are harder than diamond, kick back at any presence it senses behind it.
column27: deNach der Geburt fällt ihm das Laufen schwer. Die Wettrennen, die es sich mit seinen Freunden liefert, stärken jedoch seine Beinmuskulatur.
column28: enIts hooves are 10 times harder than diamonds. It can trample anything completely flat in little time.
column29: enIts body is light, and its legs are incredibly powerful. It can clear Ayers Rock in one leap.
column30: enIts legs grow strong while it chases after its parent. It runs in fields and mountains all day.
column31: enIf you’ve been accepted by Ponyta, its burning mane is mysteriously no longer hot to the touch.

column8: enIt just loves to gallop. The faster it goes, the longer the swaying flames of its mane will become.
column9: deMan kann Gallopa dabei beobachten, wie es lässig über Felder und Wiesen galoppiert. Wenn dieses Pokémon jedoch Ernst macht, erreicht es bis zu 240 km/h. Dann beginnt seine Mähne zu lodern.
column10: deSeine Mähne lodert auf, wenn es mit 240 km/h pfeilschnell galoppiert.
column11: enRapidash usually can be seen casually cantering in the fields and plains. However, when this Pokémon turns serious, its fiery manes flare and blaze as it gallops its way up to 150 mph.
column12: deDas schnellste Exemplar wird zum Anführer bestimmt und gibt als solcher den Zielort und die Geschwindigkeit der Herde vor.
column13: enJust loves to run. If it sees something faster than itself, it will give chase at top speed.
column14: enWith incredible acceleration, it reaches its top speed of 150 mph after running just ten steps.
column15: enIt has astounding acceleration. From a standstill, it can reach top speed within 10 steps.
column16: deMan kann Gallopa dabei beobachten, wie es fröhlich über Felder und Wiesen galoppiert. Wenn dieses Pokémon jedoch Ernst macht, erreicht es bis zu 240 km/h. Dann beginnt seine Mähne zu lodern.
column17: enVery competitive, this Pokémon will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.
column18: enWith incredible acceleration, it reaches its top speed of 150 mph after running just 10 steps.
column19: enRAPIDASH usually can be seen casually cantering in the fields and plains. However, when this POKéMON turns serious, its fiery manes flare and blaze as it gallops its way up to 150 mph.
column20: deEs galoppiert für sein Leben gern. Entdeckt es etwas, das schneller ist als es selbst, nimmt es blitzschnell die Verfolgung auf.
column21: enFiery mane aglow, Rapidash darts like an arrow across the land. This prodigiously swift creature can traverse the vast region of Hisui in a day and a half.
column22: enIt can gallop at a top speed of 150 miles per hour. It can race as fast as a bullet train while ablaze.
column23: enVery competitive, this POKéMON will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.
column24: deDie lodernde Mähne dieses Pokémon flattert im Wind, wenn es mit einer Geschwindigkeit von 240 km/h über Felder und Wiesen galoppiert.
column25: enAt full gallop, its four hooves barely touch the ground because it moves so incredibly fast.
column26: enIt usually canters casually in the fields and plains. But once a RAPIDASH turns serious, its fiery manes flare and blaze as it gallops its way up to 150 mph.
column27: enThis Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.
column28: enIt gallops at nearly 150 mph. With its mane blazing ferociously, it races as if it were an arrow.
column29: enThe fastest runner becomes the leader, and it decides the herd’s pace and direction of travel.
column30: deDieses Pokémon verfolgt schnelle Objekte in der Hoffnung, ein Wettrennen gegen sie zu gewinnen.
column31: enWhen at an all-out gallop, its blazing mane sparkles, enhancing its beautiful appearance.

column8: deEs faulenzt am Wasser. Wenn es in den Schweif gebissen wird, bemerkt es das erst am nächsten Tag.
column9: deEs ist so träge und unempfindlich, dass es keinen Schmerz verspürt, wenn seine Rute gegessen wird. Es bemerkt nicht einmal, dass sie nachwächst.
column10: enAlthough slow, it is skilled at fishing with its tail. It does not feel pain if its tail is bitten.
column11: deSeine Rute bricht sehr leicht ab. Dies kümmert es jedoch wenig, da es dabei keine Schmerzen spürt und sie sofort wieder nachwächst.
column12: deDieses Pokémon ist unglaublich faul und träge. Es faulenzt den ganzen Tag lang und schert sich nicht um die verstrichene Zeit.
column13: enThere are some places where Slowpoke is worshiped because of a long-standing belief that whenever Slowpoke yawns, it rains.
column14: enA sweet sap leaks from its tail’s tip. Although not nutritious, the tail is pleasant to chew on.
column15: enIt is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.
column16: enAlthough their tails, which fall off naturally, can easily be found lying around, they’re a precious ingredient for cooking.
column17: enA sweet sap leaks from its tail's tip. Although not nutritious, the tail is pleasant to chew on.
column18: enWhen this Pokémon’s tail is soaked in water, sweetness seeps from it. Slowpoke uses this trait to lure in and fish up other Pokémon.
column19: enIt is always so absent-minded that it won't react, even if its flavorful tail is bitten.
column20: enIt catches prey by dipping its tail in water at the side of a river. But it often forgets what it is doing and spends entire days just loafing at water’s edge.
column21: enIncredibly slow and sluggish. It is quite content to loll about without worrying about the time.
column22: deFlegmon benutzt seinen Schweif, um Beute zu fangen, indem es ihn an einem Flussufer ins Wasser taucht. Dieses Pokémon vergisst häufig, was es gerade tun wollte, und verbringt ganze Tage damit, am Ufer herumzutrödeln.
column23: deVon selbst abgefallene Flegmon-Ruten scheinen überall herumzuliegen. Dennoch gilt dieses einfache Nahrungsmittel als hochwertig.
column24: deEiner Legende zufolge soll Flegmons Gähnen Regen herbeiführen. In manchen Regionen wird ihm deswegen gehuldigt.
column25: enIts long tail often breaks off. It doesn’t really feel any pain, though, and the tail grows back, so Slowpoke isn’t particularly bothered.
column26: enIncredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.
column27: enSlow-witted and oblivious, this Pokémon won’t feel any pain if its tail gets eaten. It won’t notice when its tail grows back, either.
column28: enIt lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won't even notice for a whole day.
column29: deEs ist stets in Gedanken versunken und niemand weiß, worüber es nachdenkt. Es kann mit seiner Rute gut angeln.
column30: deTaucht es seine Rute ins Wasser, strömt sie ein süßes Aroma aus. Mit diesem Köder lockt es andere Pokémon an, die es dann an Land zieht.
column31: enIt lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won’t even notice for a whole day.
column32: enSLOWPOKE uses its tail to catch prey by dipping it in water at the side of a river. However, this POKéMON often forgets what it’s doing and often spends entire days just loafing at water’s edge.
column33: enIncredibly slow and dopey. It takes 5 seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.
column34: enSlowpoke uses its tail to catch prey by dipping it in water at the side of a river. However, this Pokémon often forgets what it’s doing and often spends entire days just loafing at water’s edge.
column35: enAlolan home cooking involves drying Slowpoke tails and then simmering them into a salty stew.
column36: deIn Alola bereiten viele Familien Flegmon-Ruten zu, indem sie diese zuerst trocknen und dann in Salzwasser kochen.

column8: enIts tail has a SHELLDER firmly attached with a bite. As a result, the tail can’t be used for fishing anymore. This forces it to reluctantly swim and catch prey.
column9: deEs entwickelte sich, als ein Muschas in seine Rute biss. Das süße Aroma, das aus der Rute strömt, hat Muschas in eine Art Trance versetzt.
column10: enWhenever Shellder bites down hard on its tail, it gives Slowbro a flash of inspiration...which it forgets a moment later.
column11: enSLOWBRO’s tail has a SHELLDER firmly attached with a bite. As a result, the tail can’t be used for fishing anymore. This causes SLOWBRO to grudgingly swim and catch prey instead.
column12: deEs führt ein ruhiges Leben in der Nähe des Meeres. Verliert es das Muschas an seinem Schwanz, wird es wieder zu einem Flegmon.
column13: enIf the tail-biting SHELLDER is thrown off in a harsh battle, it reverts to being an ordinary SLOWPOKE.
column14: enLives lazily by the sea. If the SHELLDER on its tail comes off, it becomes a SLOWPOKE again.
column15: deDas Muschas an seiner Rute bekommt von dem süßen Geschmack einfach nicht genug und mutiert für mehr Biss zu einer Spiralform.
column16: deAls Flegmon im Wasser jagen ging, wurde es von einem Muschas gebissen. Dadurch entwickelte es sich zu Lahmus.
column17: enSlowpoke became Slowbro when a Shellder bit on to its tail. Sweet flavors seeping from the tail make the Shellder feel as if its life is a dream.
column18: enWhen a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.
column19: deAn Lahmus’ Rute hat sich ein Muschas festgebissen. Daher kann es ihn nicht mehr zum Angeln benutzen und es ist dazu gezwungen, umherzuschwimmen und nach Beute Ausschau zu halten.
column20: enThe SHELLDER that is latched onto SLOWPOKE's tail is said to feed on the host's left over scraps.
column21: enBeing bitten by a Shellder shocked this Pokémon into standing on two legs. If the Shellder lets go, it seems Slowbro will turn back into a Slowpoke.
column22: enThough usually dim witted, it seems to become inspired if the SHELLDER on its tail bites down.
column23: deBeißt das Muschas an seinem Schweif beherzt zu, hat es eine geniale Idee, die es aber sofort wieder vergisst.
column24: deEs betrachtet oft mit leerem Blick das Meer. Das Gift, das Muschas in seinen Körper injiziert, erhöht seine Begriffsstutzigkeit noch weiter.
column25: enSlowbro’s tail has a Shellder firmly attached with a bite. As a result, the tail can’t be used for fishing anymore. This causes Slowbro to grudgingly swim and catch prey instead.
column26: deAn Lahmus’ Schweif hat sich ein Muschas festgebissen. Daher kann es ihn nicht mehr zum Fischen benutzen und ist dazu gezwungen, umherzuschwimmen und Ausschau nach Beute zu halten.
column27: enThe SHELLDER that latches onto SLOWPOKE’s tail is said to feed on the host’s leftover scraps.
column28: enShellder, in its greed to suck out more and more sweetness from Slowbro’s tail, has metamorphosed into a spiral-shaped shell.
column29: enAn attached SHELLDER won't let go because of the tasty flavor that oozes out of its tail.
column30: enNaturally dull to begin with, it lost its ability to feel pain due to SHELLDER's seeping poison.
column31: deDas Muschas an seiner Rute lässt nicht locker, da ein leckerer Geschmack aus seiner Rute strömt.
column32: deEin von Grund auf begriffsstutziges Pokémon. Verliert es seine Rute mitsamt Muschas, entwickelt es sich zu Flegmon zurück.
column33: enSpacing out is basically all it does. It turns back into Slowpoke if its tail, along with Shellder, breaks off.
column34: enNaturally dull to begin with, it lost its ability to feel pain due to SHELLDER’s seeping poison.
column35: deDer Schock, den Muschas’ Biss verursachte, ließ es auf zwei Beinen stehen. Löst das Muschas sich von ihm, wird es wieder zu einem Flegmon.
column36: enAn attached Shellder won’t let go because of the tasty flavor that oozes out of its tail.
column37: enWhen a SLOWPOKE went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a SHELLDER. That made it evolve into SLOWBRO.
column38: enIt spaces out while gazing at the sea. With Shellder’s poison flowing through its body, it becomes even spacier.
column39: enLives lazily by the sea. If the Shellder on its tail comes off, it becomes a Slowpoke again.
column40: enThough usually dim witted, it seems to become inspired if the Shellder on its tail bites down.

column8: deAls Flegmon in den Kopf gebissen wurde, floss Gift in seinen Kopf und setzte eine gewaltige Kraft frei.
column9: enWhen its head was bitten, toxins entered SLOWPOKE's head and unlocked an extraordinary power.
column10: deSein feines Gespür und Intellekt zeichnen es aus. Es bleibt in jeder Situation gelassen und besonnen.
column11: enBeing bitten by SHELLDER gave it intelligence comparable to that of award-winning scientists.
column12: enMiraculously, this former Slowpoke’s latent intelligence was drawn out when Shellder poison raced through its brain.
column13: enSlowking undertakes research every day in an effort to solve the mysteries of the world. However, this Pokémon apparently forgets everything it has learned if the Shellder on its head comes off.
column14: deDurch einen giftigen Biss wurde es zu einer wahren Intelligenzbestie. Es verfügt über Psycho-Kräfte.
column15: enEvery time it yawns, SHELLDER injects more poison into it. The poison makes it more intelligent.
column16: enBeing bitten by Shellder gave it intelligence comparable to that of award-winning scientists.
column17: enSlowking can solve any problem presented to it, but no one can understand a thing Slowking says.
column18: deEs ist so berühmt für seine Intelligenz, dass sich vielerorts das Sprichwort „Nicht verzagen, Laschoking fragen!“ durchgesetzt hat.
column19: deMan nennt es auch den „Weisen des Meeres“. Es liefert sich mit Kommandutan epische Quiz-Duelle, die zumeist im Gleichstand enden.
column20: deDas Gift von Muschas drang in Flegmons Gehirn ein und erweckte dabei wie durch ein Wunder bisher verborgene Intelligenz in ihm.
column21: enIt undertakes research every day to solve the mysteries of the world. However, it apparently forgets everything if the SHELLDER on its head comes off.
column22: enIt has incredible intellect and intuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.
column23: deLaschoking betreibt jeden Tag Forschung, um die Geheimnisse der Welt zu lüften. Dieses Pokémon vergisst aber alles, was es bereits herausgefunden hat, wenn das Muschas auf seinem Kopf abfällt.
column24: enSLOWKING undertakes research every day in an effort to solve the mysteries of the world. However, this POKéMON apparently forgets everything it has learned if the SHELLDER on its head comes off.
column25: deBittet man es darum, löst es jedes noch so schwere Problem für einen. Jedoch ist niemand in der Lage, Laschokings Sprache zu verstehen.
column26: enA poisonous bite reacted with its system, blessing it with the enhanced intellect of a genius. It has full control of its psychic powers.
column27: enWhen its head was bitten, toxins entered SLOWPOKE’s head and unlocked an extraordinary power.
column28: enIt’s constantly coming up with new ideas that would change the world, but as soon as it hits upon a new idea, it forgets it.
column29: enThis Pokémon is so famed for its intellect that a proverb still persists in some regions: “When in doubt, ask Slowking.”
column30: enWhen its head was bitten, toxins entered Slowpoke’s head and unlocked an extraordinary power.
column31: deIn seinem Kopf kommen ihm ständig große Ideen, die die Welt verändern könnten. Leider vergisst es sie jedoch viel zu schnell wieder.
column32: enIt’s called “the sage of the sea.” It engages in battles of wits with Oranguru, but the result is usually a draw.

column8: enVoltorb is extremely sensitive—it explodes at the slightest of shocks. It is rumored that it was first created when a Poké Ball was exposed to a powerful pulse of energy.
column9: enIt is said to camouflage itself as a POKé BALL. It will self-destruct with very little stimulus.
column10: enDuring the study of this POKéMON, it was discovered that its components are not found in nature.
column11: enVOLTORB is extremely sensitive - it explodes at the slightest of shocks. It is rumored that it was first created when a POKé BALL was exposed to a powerful pulse of energy.
column12: enUsually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.
column13: enIt rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.
column14: enIt looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on contact.
column15: enIt is said to camouflage itself as a Poké Ball. It will self-destruct with very little stimulus.
column16: enUsually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a POKé BALL, it has zapped many people.
column17: enIt bears an uncanny and unexplained resemblance to a POKé BALL. Because it explodes at the slightest shock, even veteran TRAINERS treat it with caution.
column18: deMan sagt, es tarne sich als Pokéball, um sich zu schützen. Allerdings zerstört es sich schon bei der kleinsten äußeren Einwirkung selbst.
column19: deDieses Pokémon wird oftmals mit einem Pokéball verwechselt. Es lebt vorwiegend in Kraftwerken.
column20: enIt looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on touch.
column21: enIt was discovered when Poké Balls were introduced. It is said that there is some connection.
column22: enVOLTORB was first sighted at a company that manufactures POKé BALLS. The link between that sighting and the fact that this POKéMON looks very similar to a POKé BALL remains a mystery.
column23: deEs wurde entdeckt, als man Pokébälle einführte. Es scheint, als gäbe es da einen Zusammenhang.
column24: enIt was discovered when POKé BALLS were introduced. It is said that there is some connection.
column25: deVoltobal wurde zuerst bei einer Firma beobachtet, die Pokébälle herstellt. Der Zusammenhang zwischen dieser Beobachtung und der Tatsache, dass dieses Pokémon wie ein Pokéball aussieht, ist noch ungeklärt.
column26: enAn enigmatic Pokémon that happens to bear a resemblance to a Poké Ball. When excited, it discharges the electric current it has stored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.
column27: enA life-form whose identity is unknown. It is said to SCREECH or suddenly SELFDESTRUCT.
column28: enVoltorb was first sighted at a company that manufactures Poké Balls. The link between that sighting and the fact that this Pokémon looks very similar to a Poké Ball remains a mystery.

column18: enStories say those who tried to catch Zoroark were trapped in an illusion and punished.
column19: enEach has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.

column8: deDank seiner dicken Haut hält es selbst das Gewicht eines Sumoringers aus. Wenn es Stromschläge verteilt, grinst es.
column9: deDieses Pokémon lebt im Morast. Dank der dortigen Bakterien hat es ein Organ entwickelt, mit dem es Strom erzeugen kann.
column10: deEs vergräbt sich im Morast am Meeresboden. Wird es von seiner Beute gestreift, lähmt es sie mit Strom.
column11: enIts skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.
column12: enIt conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.
column13: enFor some reason, this Pokémon smiles slightly when it emits a strong electric current from the yellow markings on its body.
column14: deEs verteilt über das gelbe Muster auf seinem Körper starke Stromstöße. Immer wenn es Strom absondert, grinst es. Niemand weiß, wieso.
column15: enWhen its opponent can’t be paralyzed, it contorts itself with unexpected speed and flops away.
column16: enThanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity.

column8: enThe more scars they have, the more respect these brave soldiers of the sky  get from their peers.
column9: enFor the sake of its friends, this brave warrior of the sky will not stop battling, even if injured.
column10: deEs ist ein furchtloses und stolzes Pokémon, das als Symbol des Mutes häufig auf Wappen abgebildet wird.
column11: deUnter Washakwil gelten Narben an der Stirnseite als Beweis für Mut. Diejenigen, die viele Narben auf der Rückseite vorweisen, werden verhöhnt.
column12: deFür Freunde stürzt es sich selbstlos in den Kampf. Es kann mit seinen Krallen Autos durch die Lüfte tragen.
column13: deEin tapferer Krieger der Lüfte, der zum Schutz seiner Kameraden auch mit Verletzungen immer weiterkämpft.
column14: enScreaming a bloodcurdling battle cry, this huge and ferocious bird Pokémon goes out on the hunt. It blasts lakes with shock waves, then scoops up any prey that float to the water's surface.
column15: enBecause this Pokémon is hotheaded and belligerent, it’s Corviknight that’s taken the role of transportation in Galar.
column16: deAls die Menschen einst ihren Lebensraum bedrohten, schlossen sich alle Washakwil zusammen und bekämpften sie.
column17: enThey fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.
column18: enThe more scars it has on its front, the more heroic it’s considered to be. Those with many scars on their back are mocked by the flock.
column19: enWith its brave disposition, it fears nothing—not even death. Ancient Alolan people respected it, referring to it as “the hero of the sky.”
column20: deDieses tapfere Pokémon fürchtet nicht einmal den Tod. Die Bewohner von Alola nannten es in grauer Vorzeit ehrfürchtig „Krieger der Lüfte“.
column21: deDa es hitzköpfig und aggressiv ist, hat es den Posten als Kurier in Galar an Krarmor verloren.
column22: enKnown as “the hero of the skies,” this Pokémon is so proud and so brave that it will never retreat, even when it’s injured.
column23: deManche nennen es auch „Krieger der Lüfte“. Ein stolzes und unerschrockenes Pokémon, das eher Verletzungen erleidet, als zu fliehen.
column24: enIt’s thought that people disturbed their habitats in the past, so Braviary banded together to fight back.
column25: enKnown for its bravery and pride, this majestic Pokémon is often seen as a motif for various kinds of emblems.

column8: enIt has trouble drawing a line between friends and food. It will calmly try to melt and eat even those it gets along well with.
column9: deDa es keine Zähne hat, sondert es einen alles zersetzenden Schleim auf seine Beute ab. Danach schlürft es diese genüsslich auf.
column10: deDa sein alles zersetzender Schleim es schützt, konnte es sich bis heute gegen seine Fressfeinde behaupten, obwohl es recht schwach ist.
column11: deEs bewegt sich gemächlich kriechend fort. Der spiralförmige Fortsatz beherbergt sein Gehirn und seine inneren Organe.
column12: enThis Pokémon’s mucous can dissolve anything. Toothless, it sprays mucous on its prey. Once they’re nicely dissolved, it slurps them up.
column13: deDie Grenze zwischen Freund und Fressen ist bei diesem Pokémon fließend. Als Freund geschätzt, zu Suppe zersetzt!
column14: enIt drives away opponents by excreting a sticky liquid that can dissolve anything. Its eyes devolved, so it can’t see anything.
column15: deSein Körper ist von einem alles zersetzenden Schleim bedeckt. Will man diesen berühren, sollte man vorher Schutzhandschuhe anziehen.
column16: deIn dem Fortsatz auf seinem Rücken befindet sich sein winziges Gehirn. Es kann nur ans Fressen und an die Flucht vor seinen Feinden denken.
column17: enIts entire body is covered with gooey mucus that can dissolve anything. Be sure to put gloves on if you’re going to touch it.
column18: enIts four horns are a high-performance radar system. It uses them to sense sounds and smells, rather than using ears or a nose.
column19: enAlthough this Pokémon isn’t very strong, its body is coated in a caustic slime that can melt through anything, so predators steer clear of it.
column20: enIt crawls along sluggishly. The swirly protrusion on its back is filled with its brain and other organs.
column21: deEs verjagt Gegner durch Absonderung eines alles zersetzenden Schleims. Die zurückgebildeten Augäpfel des Pokémon gewähren ihm keine Sicht.
column22: deSeine vier Fühler fungieren als hochleistungsfähiges Radarsystem. Es setzt sie anstelle von Nase und Ohren zur Wahrnehmung von Gerüchen und Geräuschen ein.
column23: enA creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui's water.
column24: enThe lump on its back contains its tiny brain. It thinks only of food and escaping its enemies.

column8: deUnter seiner schleimigen Haut befindet sich eine dicke Speckschicht, die gegnerische Angriffe abfängt.
column9: deEs greift mit seinen ausfahrbaren Fühlern an, deren Durchschlagskraft hundertmal so stark wie der Faustschlag eines Schwergewichtsboxers ist.
column10: enIt’s very friendly toward people. If you grow close to it, Goodra will hug you with its sticky, slime-covered body. Don’t get mad.
column11: enIt gets picked on because it’s meek. But then, whoever teased it gets to feel the full force of its horns and a good swatting from its thick tail.
column12: deDieses freundliche Pokémon hat die Eigenart, mit seinem schleimigen, klebrigen Körper all jene zu umarmen, die es mag.
column13: deWer dieses Pokémon nicht ernst nimmt, bekommt es mit seinen Fühlern zu tun oder wird von seinem Schwanz umgehauen.
column14: enAble to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.
column15: enAn amazingly friendly Pokémon, but if left to itself, loneliness overcomes it, and it oozes gooey tears.
column16: enIt attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that’s the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers.
column17: deEs greift den Gegner mit ausfahrbaren Fühlern an. Seine Stärke ist vergleichbar mit der Durchschlagskraft der Faustschläge von 100 Profiboxern.
column18: enUnderneath its slimy skin is a thick layer of fat, and the combination of the two makes enemies’ attacks slide right off.
column19: enThis very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.
column20: deDieses äußerst freundliche Drachen-Pokémon neigt dazu, seinen geliebten Trainer zu umarmen und so mit einer dicken Schleimschicht zu umhüllen.
column21: deEs kommt vor, dass es die Befehle seines Trainers nicht versteht und nur verblüfft dreinschaut. Dieser putzige Anblick hat es beliebt gemacht.
column22: enSometimes it misunderstands instructions and appears dazed or bewildered. Many Trainers don’t mind, finding this behavior to be adorable.
column23: deDieses äußerst freundliche Pokémon benötigt sehr viel Aufmerksamkeit, sonst fühlt es sich einsam und weint schleimige Tränen.
column24: enIts form of offense is forcefully stretching out its horns. The strikes land 100 times harder than any blow from a heavyweight boxer.

column8: enThe way several Bergmite huddle on its back makes it look like an aircraft carrier made of ice.
column9: deSein eisbedeckter Körper ist so hart wie Stahl. Es nutzt diese stahlharte Hülle, um Hindernisse zu zerschmettern und sich so seinen Weg zu bahnen.
column10: deDie Risse auf seinem Körper werden im Zuge seiner täglichen Aktivitäten tiefer. Ihm genügt jedoch eine Nacht, um sie wieder zu schließen.
column11: deMit den zahlreichen Arktip auf seinem Rücken sieht es aus wie ein Flugzeugträger aus Eis.
column12: enThe armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease. This Pokémon barrels along steep mountain paths, cleaving through the deep snow.
column13: enAt high latitudes, this Pokémon can be found with clusters of Bergmite on its back as it swims among the icebergs.
column14: deMit unzähligen Arktip auf dem Rücken schwimmt es wie ein Eisberg durch die kalten Meere hoher Breitengrade.
column15: enIts ice-covered body is as hard as steel. Its cumbersome frame crushes anything that stands in its way.
column16: enAs Avalugg moves about during the day, the cracks in its body deepen. The Pokémon’s body returns to a pristine state overnight.

column8: deEs braucht bloß 0,1 Sekunden, um eine Feder anzulegen und auf den Gegner zu schießen. Oft besiegt es einen, bevor man blinzeln kann.
column9: deIm Grunde ein vorsichtiges und gelassenes Pokémon, das jedoch in wahnsinnige Panik verfällt, wenn es überrascht wird.
column10: enAlthough basically cool and cautious, when it’s caught by surprise, it’s seized by panic.
column11: enDecidueye can nock and fire an arrow at an enemy in a tenth of a second, so its battles are decided in the blink of an eye.
column12: enIt nocks its arrow quills and shoots them at opponents. When it simply can’t afford to miss, it tugs the vine on its head to improve its focus.
column13: enThe air stored inside the rachises of Decidueye's feathers insulates the Pokémon against Hisui's extreme cold. This is firm proof that evolution can be influenced by environment.
column14: enAs if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye’s shots never miss.
column15: deIn 0,1 Sekunden spannt es eine Feder in seine Flügelranke und schießt sie wie einen Pfeil auf die Schwachstellen seines ahnungslosen Ziels.
column16: enIn a tenth of a second, it can nock and fire an arrow quill, piercing an opponent’s weak point before they notice what’s happening.
column17: deDieses Pokémon kann die Federn seiner Flügel wie Pfeile abschießen. Hat es sein Ziel angepeilt, trifft es garantiert.
column18: enIt fires arrow quills from its wings with such precision, they can pierce a pebble at distances over a hundred yards.
column19: deDieses Pokémon kann die Federn seiner Flügel wie Pfeile verschießen. Dabei ist es so präzise, dass es einen Kiesel auf 100 m durchbohrt.
column20: deEs legt seine Federpfeile an, um sie auf Gegner zu schießen. Muss ein Schuss sitzen, zieht es zur Konzentration an einer seiner Kopfranken.

column8: enIts fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants. This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.
column9: enThe sweet scent its body gives off mesmerizes those around it. The scent grows stronger when this Pokémon is in the sun.

column8: enThe hardness of Floragato’s fur depends on the Pokémon’s mood. When Floragato is prepared to battle, its fur becomes pointed and needle sharp.
column9: enFloragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

column8: enThis Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.
column9: enWith skillful misdirection, it rigs foes with pollen-packed flower bombs. Meowscarada sets off the bombs before its foes realize what’s going on.

column8: enIt lies on warm rocks and uses the heat absorbed by its square-shaped scales to create fire energy.
column9: enIts flame sac is small, so energy is always leaking out. This energy is released from the dent atop Fuecoco’s head and flickers to and fro.

column8: enThe combination of Crocalor’s fire energy and overflowing vitality has caused an egg-shaped fireball to appear on the Pokémon’s head.
column9: enThe valve in Crocalor’s flame sac is closely connected to its vocal cords. This Pokémon utters a guttural cry as it spews flames every which way.

column8: enSkeledirge’s gentle singing soothes the souls of all that hear it. It burns its enemies to a crisp with flames of over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit.
column9: enThe fiery bird changes shape when Skeledirge sings. Rumor has it that the bird was born when the fireball on Skeledirge’s head gained a soul.

column8: enThis Pokémon migrated to Paldea from distant lands long ago. The gel secreted by its feathers repels water and grime."
column9: enIts strong legs let it easily swim around in even fast-flowing rivers. It likes to keep things tidy and is prone to overthinking things.

column8: enThe hardworking Quaxwell observes people and Pokémon from various regions and incorporates their movements into its own dance routines.
column9: enThese Pokémon constantly run through shallow waters to train their legs, then compete with each other to see which of them kicks most gracefully.

column8: enA single kick from a Quaquaval can send a truck rolling. This Pokémon uses its powerful legs to perform striking dances from far-off lands.
column9: enDancing in ways that evoke far-away places, this Pokémon mesmerizes all that see it. Flourishes of its decorative water feathers slice into its foes.

column8: enIt searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.
column9: enThis Pokémon spurns all but the finest of foods. Its body gives off an herblike scent that bug Pokémon detest.

column8: enOinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.
column9: enIt entrances female Pokémon with the sweet, alluring scent that wafts from all over its body.

column8: enOinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.
column9: enIt entrances female Pokémon with the sweet, alluring scent that wafts from all over its body.

column8: enThe ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon’s natural enemy.
column9: enThe thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread’s strength is the topic of ongoing research.

column8: enSpidops covers its territory in tough, sticky threads to set up traps for intruders.
column9: enIt clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.

column8: enIt has its third set of legs folded up. When it’s in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.
column9: enIt’s highly skilled at a fighting style in which it uses its jumping capabilities to dodge incoming attacks while also dealing damage to opponents.

column8: enWhen it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.
column9: enIt uses its normally folded third set of legs when in Showdown Mode. This places a huge burden on its body, so it can’t stay in this mode for long.

column8: enThe pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.
column9: enIt has underdeveloped electric sacs on its cheeks. These sacs can produce electricity only if Pawmi rubs them furiously with the pads on its forepaws.

column8: enWhen its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.
column9: enPawmo uses a unique fighting technique in which it uses its forepaws to strike foes and zap them with electricity from its paw pads simultaneously.

column8: enPawmot’s fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.
column9: enThis Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.

column8: enExhibiting great teamwork, they use their incisors to cut pieces out of any material that might be useful for a nest, then make off with them.
column9: enThe pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

column8: enThe two little ones just appeared one day. The group might be a family of related Pokémon, but nobody knows for sure.
column9: enThe larger pair protects the little ones during battles. When facing strong opponents, the whole group will join the fight.

column8: enThe yeast in Fidough’s breath is useful for cooking, so this Pokémon has been protected by people since long ago.
column9: enThis Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough’s breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon’s vicinity.

column8: enThe surface of this Pokémon’s skin hardens when exposed to intense heat, and its body has an appetizing aroma.
column9: enThe pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon’s body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

column8: enThis Pokémon converts nutrients into oil, which it stores in the fruit on its head. It can easily go a whole week without eating or drinking.
column9: enIt protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

column8: enIt basks in the sun to its heart’s content until the fruits on its head ripen. After that, Dolliv departs from human settlements and goes on a journey.
column9: enDolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

column8: enThis Pokémon drives back enemies by launching its rich, aromatic oil at them with enough force to smash a boulder.
column9: enThis calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

column8: enThese Pokémon prefer to live in cities. They form flocks based on the color of their feathers, and they fight over territory.
column9: enGreen-feathered flocks hold the most sway. When they’re out searching for food in the mornings and evenings, it gets very noisy.

column8: enThe ground scrapes its body as it travels, causing it to leave salt behind. Salt is constantly being created and replenished inside Nacli’s body.
column9: enIt was born in a layer of rock salt deep under the earth. This species was particularly treasured in the old days, as they would share precious salt.

column8: enThis Pokémon dry cures its prey by spraying salt over them. The curing process steals away the water in the prey’s body.
column9: enIt compresses rock salt inside its body and shoots out hardened salt pellets with enough force to perforate an iron sheet.

column8: enMany Pokémon gather around Garganacl, hoping to lick at its mineral-rich salt.
column9: enGarganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.

column8: enBurnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.
column9: enIts firepower increases when it fights, reaching over 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. It likes berries that are rich in fat.

column8: enArmarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.
column9: enThis Pokémon clads itself in armor that has been fortified by psychic and fire energy, and it shoots blazing fireballs.

column8: enThe fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.
column9: enAn old set of armor steeped in grudges caused this Pokémon’s evolution. Ceruledge cuts its enemies to pieces without mercy.

column8: enIt floats using the electricity stored in its body. When thunderclouds are around, Tadbulb will float higher off the ground.
column9: enTadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

column8: enWhen this Pokémon expands and contracts its wobbly body, the belly-button dynamo in its stomach produces a huge amount of electricity.
column9: enWhat appear to be eyeballs are actually organs for discharging the electricity generated by Bellibolt’s belly-button dynamo.

column8: enThese Pokémon make their nests on coastal cliffs. The nests have a strange, crackling texture, and they’re a popular delicacy.
column9: enWhen its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

column8: enKilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.
column9: enIt uses its throat sac to store electricity generated by its wings. There’s hardly any oil in its feathers, so it is a poor swimmer.

column8: enIts well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.
column9: enIt always scowls in an attempt to make opponents take it seriously, but even crying children will burst into laughter when they see Maschiff’s face.

column8: enThis Pokémon can store energy in its large dewlap. Mabosstiff unleashes this energy all at once to blow away enemies.
column9: enMabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

column8: enTo keep enemies away from its territory, it paints markings around its nest using a poisonous liquid that has an acrid odor.
column9: enThough usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

column8: enEach Grafaiai paints its own individual pattern, and it will paint that same pattern over and over again throughout its life.
column9: enThe color of the poisonous saliva depends on what the Pokémon eats. Grafaiai covers its fingers in its saliva and draws patterns on trees in forests.

column8: enA soul unable to move on to the afterlife was blown around by the wind until it got tangled up with dried grass and became a Pokémon.
column9: enNot even Bramblin knows where it is headed as it tumbles across the wilderness, blown by the wind. It loathes getting wet.

column8: enBrambleghast wanders around arid regions. On rare occasions, mass outbreaks of these Pokémon will bury an entire town.
column9: enIt will open the branches of its head to envelop its prey. Once it absorbs all the life energy it needs, it expels the prey and discards it.

column8: enThough it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.
column9: enToedscool lives in muggy forests. The flaps that fall from its body are chewy and very delicious.

column8: enIt coils its 10 tentacles around prey and sucks out their nutrients, causing the prey pain. The folds along the rim of its head are a popular delicacy.
column9: enThese Pokémon gather into groups and form colonies deep within forests. They absolutely hate it when strangers approach.

column8: enThis Pokémon lives on sheer cliffs. It sidesteps opponents’ attacks, then lunges for their weak spots with its claws.
column9: enKlawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can’t remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.

column8: enThe more sunlight this Pokémon bathes in, the more spicy chemicals are produced by its body, and thus the spicier its moves become.
column9: enTraditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

column8: enThe green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.
column9: enThe red head converts spicy chemicals into fire energy and blasts the surrounding area with a super spicy stream of flame.

column8: enThe green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.
column9: enThe red head converts spicy chemicals into fire energy and blasts the surrounding area with a super spicy stream of flame.

column8: enThis Pokémon creates a mud ball by mixing sand and dirt with psychic energy. It treasures its mud ball more than its own life.
column9: enIt rolls its mud ball around while the energy it needs for evolution matures. Eventually the time comes for it to evolve.

column8: enAn infant sleeps inside the ball. Rabsca rolls the ball soothingly with its legs to ensure the infant sleeps comfortably.
column9: enThe body that supports the ball barely moves. Therefore, it is thought that the true body of this Pokémon is actually inside the ball.

column8: enIt spends its time running around wastelands. If anyone steals its beloved berries, it will chase them down and exact its revenge.
column9: enFlittle’s toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon’s belly.

column8: enIt immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.
column9: enIt emits psychic power from the gaps between its multicolored frills and sprints at speeds greater than 120 mph.

column8: enThis Pokémon pounds iron scraps together to make a hammer. It will remake the hammer again and again until it’s satisfied with the result.
column9: enIt swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

column8: enThis Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.
column9: enThese Pokémon make their homes in piles of scrap metal. They test the strength of each other’s hammers by smashing them together.

column8: enThe hammer tops 220 pounds, yet it gets swung around easily by Tinkaton as it steals whatever it pleases and carries its plunder back home.
column9: enThis intelligent Pokémon has a very daring disposition. It knocks rocks into the sky with its hammer, aiming for flying Corviknight.

column8: enThough it looks like Diglett, Wiglett is an entirely different species. The resemblance seems to be a coincidental result of environmental adaptation.
column9: enThis Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

column8: enA variety of fish Pokémon, Wugtrio was once considered to be a regional form of Dugtrio.
column9: enIt has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

column8: enIt gathers things up in an apron made from shed feathers added to the Pokémon’s chest feathers, then drops those things from high places for fun.
column9: enBombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

column8: enIts water ring is made from seawater mixed with a sticky fluid that Finizen secretes from its blowhole.
column9: enIt likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.

column8: enIts physical capabilities are no different than a Finizen’s, but when its allies are in danger, it transforms and powers itself up.
column9: enThis Pokémon changes its appearance if it hears its allies calling for help. Palafin will never show anybody its moment of transformation.

column8: enIts physical capabilities are no different than a Finizen’s, but when its allies are in danger, it transforms and powers itself up.
column9: enThis Pokémon changes its appearance if it hears its allies calling for help. Palafin will never show anybody its moment of transformation.

column8: enIt is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.
column9: enThe steel section is Varoom’s actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

column8: enIt creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders—now numbering eight—to generate energy.
column9: enRevavroom viciously threatens others with the sound of its exhaust. It sticks its tongue out from its cylindrical mouth and sprays toxic fluids.

column8: enIt can sprint at over 70 mph while carrying a human. The rider’s body heat warms Cyclizar’s back and lifts the Pokémon’s spirit.
column9: enApparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

column8: enThis Pokémon lives in arid deserts. It maintains its metal body by consuming iron from the soil.
column9: enWhen attacked, this Pokémon will wield the tendrils on its body like fists and pelt the opponent with a storm of punches.

column8: enGlimmet’s toxic mineral crystals look just like flower petals. This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder like pollen to protect itself.
column9: enIt absorbs nutrients from cave walls. The petals it wears are made of crystallized poison.

column8: enGlimmora’s petals are made of crystallized poison energy. It has recently become evident that these petals resemble Tera Jewels.
column9: enWhen this Pokémon detects danger, it will open up its crystalline petals and fire beams from its conical body.

column8: enGlimmora’s petals are made of crystallized poison energy. It has recently become evident that these petals resemble Tera Jewels.
column9: enWhen this Pokémon detects danger, it will open up its crystalline petals and fire beams from its conical body.

column8: enThis friendly Pokémon doesn’t like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.
column9: enIt is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.

column8: enHoundstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.
column9: enA lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn’t like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.

column8: enThis Pokémon apparently ties the base of its neck into a knot so that energy stored in its belly does not escape from its beak.
column9: enThanks to a behavior of theirs known as “synchronizing,” an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.

column8: enIt lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.
column9: enThis species left the ocean and began living on land a very long time ago. It seems to be closely related to Wailmer.

column8: enIce energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.
column9: enThis Pokémon wanders around snowy, icy areas. It protects its body with powerful muscles and a thick layer of fat under its skin.

column8: enWhen Veluza discards unnecessary flesh, its mind becomes honed and its psychic power increases. The spare flesh has a mild but delicious flavor.
column9: enVeluza has excellent regenerative capabilities. It sheds spare flesh from its body to boost its agility, then charges at its prey.

column8: enThis Pokémon is a glutton, but it’s bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.
column9: enIt treats Tatsugiri like its boss and follows it loyally. Though powerful, Dondozo is apparently not very smart.

column8: enTatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.
column9: enThis is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

column8: enTatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.
column9: enThis is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.
