column9: enThis is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

column8: enIt imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.
column9: enWhen its anger rose beyond a critical point, this Pokémon gained power that is unfettered by the limits of its physical body.

column8: deDieses Pokémon lebt in Eiswasser. Wird es an Land kalt, verlässt es das Wasser und sucht nach Futter.
column9: enIt lives in cold water, half burying itself in mud at the bottom to sleep.
column10: deWenn es am Abend kühler wird, kommen sie an Land, um nach Nahrung zu suchen.
column11: deFelino lebt eigentlich im Wasser. Manchmal kommt es jedoch an Land, um nach Nahrung zu suchen. An Land bedeckt es seinen Körper mit einem schleimigen Giftfilm.
column12: enWOOPER usually live in water but come out onto land seeking food occasionally. On land, they coat their bodies with a gooey, toxic film.
column13: enThis POKéMON lives in cold water. It will leave the water to search for food when it gets cold outside.
column14: deDieses Pokémon lebt in kaltem Wasser. Wenn die Temperaturen an Land tief genug fallen, verlässt es das Wasser und sucht dort nach Futter.
column15: deAn Land bedeckt es seine Haut mit einer giftigen Schicht, die es vor dem Austrocknen bewahrt.
column16: enThis Pokémon lives in cold water. It will leave the water to search for food when it gets cold outside.
column17: enWhen it walks around on the ground, it coats its body with a slimy, poisonous film.
column18: enWOOPER usually lives in water. However, it occasionally comes out onto land in search of food. On land, it coats its body with a gooey, toxic film.
column19: enWhen the temperature cools in the evening, they emerge from water to seek food along the shore.
column20: enWooper usually lives in water. However, it occasionally comes out onto land in search of food. On land, it coats its body with a gooey, toxic film.
column21: enWhen walking on land, it covers its body with a poisonous film that keeps its skin from dehydrating.
column22: enA mucous membrane covers its body. Touching it barehanded will cause a shooting pain.

column8: enWhen attacked, this Pokémon will retaliate by sticking thick spines out from its body. It’s a risky move that puts everything on the line.
column9: enIt lives at the bottom of ponds and swamps. It will carry Wooper on its back and ferry them across water from one shore to the other.

column8: enThe hardened head from the tail protects the head of the main body as Farigiraf whips its long neck around to headbutt enemies.
column9: enNow that the brain waves from the head and tail are synced up, the psychic power of this Pokémon is 10 times stronger than Girafarig’s.

column8: enThis Pokémon uses its hard tail to make its nest by boring holes into bedrock deep underground. The nest can reach lengths of over six miles.
column9: enIt drives enemies out of its nest by sucking in enough air to fill its long, narrow lungs, then releasing the air in an intense blast.

column8: enThough it commands a massive army in battle, it’s not skilled at devising complex strategies. It just uses brute strength to keep pushing.
column9: enOnly a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

column8: enSightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.
column9: enThis creature resembles a mysterious Pokémon that, according to a paranormal magazine, has lived since ancient times.

column8: enIt resembles a mysterious Pokémon described in a paranormal magazine as a Jigglypuff from one billion years ago.
column9: enThere has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.

column8: enIt bears a slight resemblance to a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a cross between a dinosaur and a mushroom.
column9: enIt is possible that the creature listed as Brute Bonnet in a certain book could actually be this Pokémon.

column8: enIt has similar features to a ghostly pterosaur that was covered in a paranormal magazine, but the two have little else in common.
column9: enThis Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.

column8: enThis mysterious Pokémon has some similarities to a creature that an old book introduced as Slither Wing.
column9: enThis Pokémon somewhat resembles an ancient form of Volcarona that was introduced in a dubious magazine.

column8: enNo records exist of this Pokémon being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon’s traits match up with a creature shown in an expedition journal.
column9: enIt slightly resembles a Magneton that lived for 10,000 years and was featured in an article in a paranormal magazine.

column8: enThis Pokémon closely resembles a scientific weapon that a paranormal magazine claimed was sent to this planet by aliens.
column9: enSightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. It resembles a mysterious object described in an old expedition journal.

column8: enIts shape is similar to a robot featured in a paranormal magazine article. The robot was said to have been created by an ancient civilization.
column9: enIt resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.

column8: enIt is very similar to a cyborg covered exclusively by a paranormal magazine. The cyborg was said to be the modified form of a certain athlete.
column9: enThis Pokémon shares many similarities with Iron Hands, an object mentioned in a certain expedition journal.

column8: enIt resembles a certain Pokémon introduced in a paranormal magazine, described as the offspring of a Hydreigon that fell in love with a robot.
column9: enIt’s possible that Iron Jugulis, an object described in an old book, may actually be this Pokémon.

column8: enThis Pokémon resembles an unknown object described in a paranormal magazine as a UFO sent to observe humanity.
column9: enNo records exist of this species being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon’s traits match up with an object described in an old book.

column8: enIt has some similarities to a Pokémon introduced in a dubious magazine as a Tyranitar from one billion years into the future.
column9: enSome of its notable features match those of an object named within a certain expedition journal as Iron Thorns.

column8: enFrigibax absorbs heat through its dorsal fin and converts the heat into ice energy. The higher the temperature, the more energy Frigibax stores.
column9: enThis Pokémon lives in forests and craggy areas. Using the power of its dorsal fin, it cools the inside of its nest like a refrigerator.

column8: enIt attacks with the blade of its frozen dorsal fin by doing a front flip in the air. Arctibax’s strong back and legs allow it to pull off this technique.
column9: enArctibax freezes the air around it, protecting its face with an ice mask and turning its dorsal fin into a blade of ice.

column8: enThis Pokémon blasts cryogenic air out from its mouth. This air can instantly freeze even liquid-hot lava.
column9: enIt launches itself into battle by flipping upside down and spewing frigid air from its mouth. It finishes opponents off with its dorsal blade.

column8: enThis Pokémon blasts cryogenic air out from its mouth. This air can instantly freeze even liquid-hot lava.
column9: enIt launches itself into battle by flipping upside down and spewing frigid air from its mouth. It finishes opponents off with its dorsal blade.

column8: enThis Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.
column9: enIt lives inside an old treasure chest. Sometimes it gets left in shop corners since no one realizes it’s actually a Pokémon.

column8: enIt has a sturdy body made up of stacked coins. Gholdengo overwhelms its enemies by firing coin after coin at them in quick succession.
column9: enIts body seems to be made up of 1,000 coins. This Pokémon gets along well with others and is quick to make friends with anybody.

column8: enThe grudge of a person punished for writing the king’s evil deeds upon wooden tablets has clad itself in dead leaves to become a Pokémon.
column9: enIt drains the life-force from vegetation, causing nearby forests to instantly wither and fields to turn barren.

column8: enThis Pokémon can control 100 tons of fallen snow. It plays around innocently by leaping in and out of avalanches it has caused.
column9: enThe hatred of those who perished by the sword long ago has clad itself in snow and become a Pokémon.

column8: enThe fear poured into an ancient ritual vessel has clad itself in rocks and dirt to become a Pokémon.
column9: enIt slowly brings its exceedingly heavy head down upon the ground, splitting the earth open with huge fissures that run over 160 feet deep.

column8: enThe envy accumulated within curved beads that sparked multiple conflicts has clad itself in fire and become a Pokémon.
column9: enIt controls flames burning at over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit. It casually swims through the sea of lava it creates by melting rock and sand.

column8: enIt is possible that this is the creature listed as Roaring Moon in an expedition journal that still holds many mysteries.
column9: enAccording to an article in a dubious magazine, this Pokémon has some connection to a phenomenon that occurs in a certain region.

column8: enIt has some similarities to a mad scientist’s invention covered in a paranormal magazine.
column9: enIt’s possible that this is the object listed as Iron Valiant in a certain expedition journal.

column8: enThis Pokémon resembles Cyclizar, but it is far burlier and more ferocious. Nothing is known about its ecology or other features.
column9: enThis seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

column8: enThis seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.
column9: enMuch remains unknown about this creature. It resembles Cyclizar, but it is far more ruthless and powerful.

column8: enThis ferocious creature is shrouded in mystery. It's named after an aquatic monster mentioned in an old expedition journal.
column9: enIt resembles an illustration published in a paranormal magazine, said to be a depiction of a super-ancient Suicune.

column8: enMany of its physical characteristics match those of a Virizion from the future that was covered in a paranormal magazine.
column9: enAccording to the few eyewitness accounts that exist, it used its shining blades to julienne large trees and boulders.

column8: enThe head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.
column9: enDipplin is two creatures in one Pokémon. Its evolution was triggered by a special apple grown only in one place.

column8: enPoltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.
column9: enSupposedly, the regrets of a tea ceremony master who died before perfecting his craft lingered in some matcha and became a Pokémon.

column8: enIt pretends to be tea, trying to fool people into drinking it so it can drain their life-force. Its ruse is generally unsuccessful.
column9: enIt prefers cool, dark places, such as the back of a shelf or the space beneath a home's floorboards. It wanders in search of prey after sunset.

column8: enAfter all its muscles were stimulated by the toxic chain around its neck, Okidogi transformed and gained a powerful physique.
column9: enOkidogi is a ruffian with a short temper. It can pulverize anything by swinging around the chain on its neck.

column8: enMunkidori keeps itself somewhere safe while it toys with its foes, using psychokinesis to induce intense dizziness.
column9: enThe chain is made from toxins that enhance capabilities. It stimulated Munkidori's brain and caused the Pokémon's psychic powers to bloom.

column8: enFezandipiti beats its glossy wings to scatter pheromones that captivate people and Pokémon.
column9: enFezandipiti owes its beautiful looks and lovely voice to the toxic stimulants emanating from the chain wrapped around its body.

column8: enThis Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.
column9: enThis mischief-loving Pokémon is full of curiosity. It battles by drawing out the type-based energy contained within its masks.

column8: enThis Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.
column9: enThis mischief-loving Pokémon is full of curiosity. It battles by drawing out the type-based energy contained within its masks.

column8: enThis Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.
column9: enThis mischief-loving Pokémon is full of curiosity. It battles by drawing out the type-based energy contained within its masks.

column8: enThis Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.
column9: enThis mischief-loving Pokémon is full of curiosity. It battles by drawing out the type-based energy contained within its masks.

column8: enIt digs holes on mountains, searching for food. It’s so durable that being caught in a cave-in won’t faze it.
column9: enIt gathers static electricity from its surroundings. The beams it launches when down on all fours are tremendously powerful.

column8: enThese capricious syrpents have banded together. On the rare occasion that their moods align, their true power is unleashed.
column9: enSeven syrpents live inside an apple made of syrup. The syrpent in the center is the commander.

column8: enIt resembles an eerie Pokémon once shown in a paranormal magazine. That Pokémon was said to be an Entei regenerated from a fossil.
column9: enThere are scant few reports of this creature being sighted. One short video shows it rampaging and spouting pillars of flame.

column8: enIt's said to incinerate everything around it with lightning launched from its fur. Very little is known about this creature.
column9: enIt bears resemblance to a Pokémon that became a hot topic for a short while after a paranormal magazine touted it as Raikou's ancestor.

column8: enIt was named after a mysterious object recorded in an old book. Its body seems to be metallic.
column9: enIt resembles a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a Terrakion that had been modified by an evil organization.

column8: enThere was supposedly an incident in which it launched shining blades to cut everything around it to pieces. Little else is known about it.
column9: enIt resembles a mysterious object introduced in a paranormal magazine as a cutting-edge weapon shaped like a Cobalion.

column8: enIt’s thought that this Pokémon lived in ancient Paldea until it got caught in seismic shifts and went extinct.
column9: enTerapagos protects itself using its power to transform energy into hard crystals. This Pokémon is the source of the Terastal phenomenon.

column8: enIt feeds others toxic mochi that draw out desires and capabilities. Those who eat the mochi fall under Pecharunt’s control, chained to its will.
column9: enIts peach-shaped shell serves as storage for a potent poison. It makes poisonous mochi and serves them to people and Pokémon.

column8: enThey live in groups. The one with the longest, thickest, and most-scarred horns is the boss of the herd.
column9: enWhen it is about to tackle, it whips its body repeatedly with its three long tails.
column10: enThe Tauros of the Galar region are volatile in nature, and they won’t allow people to ride on their backs.
column11: enAfter heightening its will to fight by whipping itself with its three tails, it charges at full speed.
column12: enThese violent POKéMON fight with other members of their herd in order to prove their strength.
column13: deEs kämpft mit vollem Einsatz, nachdem es sich mit seinen drei Schweifen auspeitschte, um sich anzustacheln.
column14: enWhen Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.
column15: enA rowdy Pokémon with a lot of stamina. Once running, it won’t stop until it hits something.
column16: enAlthough it’s known to be a fierce Pokémon, Tauros in the Alola region are said to possess a measure of calmness.
column17: enWhen it is about to TACKLE, it whips its body repeatedly with its three long tails.
column18: enIt is not satisfied unless it is rampaging at all times. If there is no opponent for TAUROS to battle, it will charge at thick trees and knock them down to calm itself.
column19: deObwohl es sonst für seine ungestüme Art bekannt ist, gelten Exemplare aus Alola als vergleichsweise gelassen.
column20: enThis POKéMON is not satisfied unless it is rampaging at all times. If there is no opponent for TAUROS to battle, it will charge at thick trees and knock them down to calm itself.
column21: enHistorically, people in areas all over the world have ridden Tauros, but the practice is said to have started in Alola.
column22: deSchon seit vielen Jahren reiten Menschen überall auf der Welt auf Tauros. Diese Tradition hat angeblich in Alola ihren Ursprung genommen.
column23: dePeitscht es seinen Körper mit seinen Schweifen aus, ist Vorsicht geboten, denn es steht kurz davor, mit Karacho auf sein Ziel loszustürmen.
column24: enWhen it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.
column25: enThis Pokémon is not satisfied unless it is rampaging at all times. If there is no opponent for Tauros to battle, it will charge at thick trees and knock them down to calm itself.
column26: deEs ist ungestüm und strotzt nur so vor Kraft. Wenn es erst einmal losprescht, rennt es so lange weiter, bis es mit etwas zusammenstößt.
column27: deSie leben in Herden. Ihr Anführer ist das Tauros mit den kräftigsten und längsten Hörnern sowie den meisten Kampfspuren darauf.
column28: enThey fight each other by locking horns. The herd's protector takes pride in its battle-scarred horns.
column29: enThe climate seems to be related to the reason Tauros in Alola are a little calmer than those in other regions.
column30: enOnce it takes aim at its foe, it makes a headlong charge. It is famous for its violent nature.
column31: deDieses Pokémon ist nur zufrieden, wenn es kämpfen kann. Wenn Tauros keinen Gegner findet, sucht es sich robuste Bäume und haut sie um, um sich abzureagieren.
column32: deTauros in Alola sind etwas sanftmütiger als in anderen Regionen. Dies ist offenbar auf das Klima zurückzuführen.
column33: deDie Tauros in Galar haben ein ungestümes Temperament und gestatten es Menschen nicht, auf ihrem Rücken zu reiten.
column34: enA rowdy POKéMON with a lot of stamina. Once running, it won't stop until it hits something.
column35: enThey fight each other by locking horns. The herd’s protector takes pride in its battle-scarred horns.
column36: deWenn es Tackle einsetzt, geißelt es mit seinen drei Schwänzen wiederholt seinen Körper.
